- A advances dialog/confirms; B cancels; START opens menu.

arguments.content:
# Strategy playbook

## Early boot / intro
- The harness may begin before the game has fully reached the title screen even if RAM reports Pallet Town. If the screen is blank white, `wait` a few seconds for the boot/logo sequence.
- Once at the title screen, use `start` or `a` to proceed to the main menu / intro.
- RAM location can be stale or meaningless during boot/title; trust the screenshot until actual gameplay begins.
- On the player naming screen, pressing `start` accepted the default name immediately (resulting default shown as AAAA in this ROM/state).
- On the rival naming screen, pressing `start` also accepted the default name; after that, mash `a` through Oak's remaining intro until gameplay resumes in the bedroom.

## General
- Default d-pad press moves about one tile.
- A advances dialog/confirms; B cancels; START opens menu.
- If a textbox is open, movement inputs do nothing; clear dialog before trying to walk.
- When stuck, trust RAM coordinates to map passable tiles. Probe systematically rather than repeating the same edge press.

## Current route
- Current bottleneck: Player's House 2F. Need to locate the downstairs warp/stairs from the bedroom, then exit house and head north to trigger Oak escort.

## Player's House 2F notes
- Verified walkable positions so far include roughly x=1..5 and y=6..7.
- Bed at left is solid; plant at right is solid; TV/desk area above is solid/interactable.
- Repeated `down` from row y=7 at x=2, x=3, and x=4 did **not** trigger stairs/warp.
- Repeated `down` holds also did not help, so this is not a timing issue.
- Need a genuinely different approach next: fully map exact passable tiles around the TV/bed edges, or try menu/state sanity checks rather than mashing bottom edge.

## Tooling / harness notes
- `save_checkpoint` may currently fail with a filesystem 500 error; don't rely on it until verified working.

# Strategy playbook

## Early boot / intro
- The harness may begin before the game has fully reached the title screen even if RAM reports Pallet Town. If the screen is blank white, `wait` a few seconds for the boot/logo sequence.

- If a textbox is open, movement inputs do nothing; clear dialog before trying to walk.
- When stuck, trust RAM coordinates to map passable tiles. Probe systematically rather than repeating the same edge press.
- If a map behaves impossibly for several turns, try a genuinely different interaction next: START menu, A on unusual tiles/objects, or checkpoint rewind if available.

## Current route
- Current bottleneck: Player's House 2F. Need to locate the downstairs warp/stairs from the bedroom, then exit house and head north to trigger Oak escort.
- Strong evidence now says the downstairs warp is NOT triggering from the visible bottom edge tiles we have tested.
- Next different approach should be non-movement-centric: open START menu to sanity-check that controls/state are normal, then probe A/interact on unusual floor/object positions or consider whether the room layout is offset/partially hidden.

## Early boot / intro
- The harness may begin before the game has fully reached the title screen even if RAM reports Pallet Town. If the screen is blank white, `wait` a few seconds for the boot/logo sequence.

- On the player naming screen, pressing `start` accepted the default name immediately (resulting default shown as AAAA in this ROM/state).
- On the rival naming screen, pressing `start` also accepted the default name; after that, mash `a` through Oak's remaining intro until gameplay resumes in the bedroom.

## General
- Default d-pad press moves about one tile.
- A advances dialog/confirms; B cancels; START opens menu.
- If a textbox is open, movement inputs do nothing; clear dialog before trying to walk.
- When stuck, trust RAM coordinates to map passable tiles. Probe systematically rather than repeating the same edge press.
- If a map behaves impossibly for several turns, try a genuinely different interaction next: START menu, A on unusual tiles/objects, or checkpoint rewind if available.

- Next turn should try truly different interactions: inspect the bed/bookshelf/PC area with A from multiple sides, or consider whether there is a scripted tile/object interaction rather than a standard stair warp.

## Player's House 2F notes
- Verified walkable positions so far include roughly x=1..5 and y=6..7.
- Bed at left is solid; plant at right is solid; TV/desk area above is solid/interactable.
- Repeated `down` from row y=7 at x=1 through x=5 did not trigger stairs/warp.

arguments.content:
# Strategy playbook

## Early boot / intro
- The harness may begin before the game has fully reached the title screen even if RAM reports Pallet Town. If the screen is blank white, `wait` a few seconds for the boot/logo sequence.
- Once at the title screen, use `start` or `a` to proceed to the main menu / intro.
- RAM location can be stale or meaningless during boot/title; trust the screenshot until actual gameplay begins.
- On the player naming screen, pressing `start` accepted the default name immediately (resulting default shown as AAAA in this ROM/state).
- On the rival naming screen, pressing `start` also accepted the default name; after that, mash `a` through Oak's remaining intro until gameplay resumes in the bedroom.
- After intro finishes, the player may spawn facing the TV/SNES and an `A` press can reopen the inspect textbox immediately. Clear textboxes completely before attempting movement.

## General
- Default d-pad press moves about one tile.
- A advances dialog/confirms; B cancels; START opens menu.
- If a textbox is open, movement inputs do nothing; clear dialog before trying to walk.
- When screenshot and RAM disagree during normal gameplay, trust RAM for coordinates/state and screenshot for UI/dialog/world geometry.
- When stuck, trust RAM coordinates to map passable tiles. Probe systematically rather than repeating the same edge press.
- If a map behaves impossibly for several turns, try a genuinely different interaction next: inspect unusual tiles with A, use menu open/close as a state refresh, or re-read memories/playbook before spending more movement calls.

## Current route
- Current bottleneck: Player's House 2F. Need to identify why the expected downstairs warp is not triggering from the apparent bottom-center tile.
- Strongest current evidence: on a clean screen at (3,7) facing down, repeated `down`, waiting, and even a 60-frame `down` hold do not move or warp.
- Next action turn should emphasize *new hypotheses*, not more bottom-edge shoving: inspect nearby floor/rug/TV/desk tiles with A from multiple facings; approach the center-bottom tile from adjacent columns/rows; consider whether the visible layout differs from expected bedroom assumptions.
- Save-state support may be unreliable, so don't depend on rewind unless checkpoint creation is confirmed working again.

## Player's House 2F notes
- Verified walkable positions so far include roughly x=1..5 and y=6..7.
- Bed at left is solid; plant at right is solid; TV/desk area above is solid/interactable.
- Repeated `down` from row y=7 at x=1 through x=5 did not trigger stairs/warp.
- Serpentine probing around the room never reached y>7.
- Long holds also did not help, so this is not a timing issue.
- Visual reminder from current state: at (3,7) facing down, the avatar stands centered near the bottom of the visible room with bed left, TV above, plant right, and no obvious visible stair graphic. Do not assume the warp is exactly where it would be in memory from standard play.

## Tooling / harness notes
- `save_checkpoint` may fail with a filesystem 500 error; treat checkpoints as unverified until a save succeeds.
- `list_checkpoints` returned none.
- Repeated mechanical sequences worth tooling:
  - intro progression / default-name acceptance
  - dialog mashing with moderate waits
  - systematic tile-probe paths that log coordinate changes

Player's House 2F consolidated wedge notes: current clean state is at (3,7) facing down with no textbox; bed left, TV/desk above, plant right, and no obvious visible stair graphic on the bottom edge.

## Verified observations
- Movement within the room works normally when no textbox is open.
- Walkable area discovered so far is roughly x=1..5, y=6..7.
- Bed on left and plant on right are solid obstacles.
- TV/desk area above is solid/interactable; pressing A there can produce the SNES/"Okay! It's time to go!" messages.
- START menu opens/closes normally, so controls are not frozen.
- From bottom row positions including center (3,7), repeated Down presses do not move or warp.
- Waiting in place and long-hold Down also do not trigger anything.

## Implications
- This is not merely a lingering-dialog problem.
- This is not a simple timing issue.
- The expected downstairs warp either is not on the apparent bottom-center tile, requires a different approach/interaction, or this map/state differs from standard expectations.

## Recommended next experiments
1. Use A to inspect floor/room features from several facings, especially around center-bottom and near TV/desk.
2. Re-approach the center-bottom tile from left/right columns and from the upper row.
3. Treat the screenshot layout literally instead of assuming standard house geometry.
4. If still impossible, gather a more explicit coordinate-to-screen map for every reachable tile.

## General

- Long-hold d-pad presses can move many tiles and are essential for discovering larger walkable areas when the visible local probe seems too small.

- When stuck, trust RAM coordinates to map passable tiles. Probe systematically rather than repeating the same edge press.
- If a map behaves impossibly, do not overfit to short-press geometry: test long holds in each direction before declaring corruption.

## Current route
- We have now escaped Player's House 2F and reached Player's House 1F.
- Immediate next goals: leave the house, trigger Mom if needed, go to Pallet Town, head north toward Oak/event flow, then obtain a starter in Oak's lab.

## Player's House 2F notes

## Tooling / harness notes

data.summary:
Turn 17 summary:

- I systematically mapped **Player’s House 1F** with `probe_path` instead of guessing.
- Key findings on **1F (map 0x25)**:
  - Starting area at bottom-left is walkable across the lower room.
  - I could move up the left side to about **(1,3)**, then across the top to about **(5,3)**.
  - The furniture blocks some center/right movement, but the room is much larger than the initial screen crop suggested.
  - The **staircase at the top-right is real and usable**: from around **(7,2)** pressing **up** warped to **Player’s House 2F (map 0x26)**.
- After warping, I explored **2F**

data.name: update_playbook

arguments.content:
# Strategy playbook

## Early boot / intro
- The harness may begin before the game has fully reached the title screen even if RAM reports Pallet Town. If the screen is blank white, `wait` a few seconds for the boot/logo sequence.
- Once at the title screen, use `start` or `a` to proceed to the main menu / intro.
- RAM location can be stale or meaningless during boot/title; trust the screenshot until actual gameplay begins.
- On the player naming screen, pressing `start` accepted the default name immediately (resulting default shown as AAAA in this ROM/state).
- On the rival naming screen, pressing `start` also accepted the default name; after that, mash `a` through Oak's remaining intro until gameplay resumes in the bedroom.
- After intro finishes, the player may spawn facing the TV/SNES and an `A` press can reopen the inspect textbox immediately. Clear textboxes completely before attempting movement.

## General
- Default d-pad press moves about one tile.
- Long-hold d-pad presses can move many tiles and are essential for discovering larger walkable areas when the visible local probe seems too small.
- A advances dialog/confirms; B cancels; START opens menu.
- If a textbox is open, movement inputs do nothing; clear dialog before trying to walk.
- `B` can also dismiss the SNES/TV textbox; useful sequence from that state: `b, b, down, down` to reach clean standing state at (3,7) facing down.
- When screenshot and RAM disagree during normal gameplay, trust RAM for coordinates/state and screenshot for UI/dialog/world geometry.
- When stuck, trust RAM coordinates to map passable tiles. Probe systematically rather than repeating the same edge press.
- If a map behaves impossibly, do not overfit to short-press geometry: test long holds in each direction before declaring corruption.

## Current route
- We have left Player's House, triggered Oak at the Pallet Town north exit, and reached Oak's Lab.
- Current state: in Oak's Lab at the starter scene, no Pokémon yet, textbox active with Oak speaking.
- Immediate next goals: finish Oak/rival dialog, choose a starter from the table, name/accept it if prompted, then win or finish the first rival battle and continue the opening.

## Pallet Town / opening event flow
- Player's House 1F exit: from the downstairs room, the lower corridor leads to the door warp; from about (2,7) on map 0x25, pressing `down` exits to Pallet Town.
- Pallet Town north exit / Oak trigger: the correct opening is on the east side of the north path. At about map 0x00, x=10, y=1, stepping north triggers Oak's interruption and escort to the lab.
- Oak escort scene ends in Oak's Lab (map 0x28) with Oak, rival, and three Poké Balls near the table.

## Oak's Lab expectations
- At the first lab visit, Oak speaks for multiple textboxes before the player can choose a starter. Expect to mash through dialog before movement/selection resumes.
- The starter selection likely requires walking to a Poké Ball and pressing `A`; the rival may intercept certain choices, and Oak may have additional text before/after pickup.
- Save-state/checkpoint would be valuable before the rival fight or before choosing a starter if harness checkpoints become reliable.

## Player's House 2F notes
- Prior wedge hypothesis was wrong.
- Correct fix: from bottom-left area, long-hold movement revealed a much larger room than short presses suggested.
- Verified successful escape path discovery:
  - from (1,7), long `right` reached about (5,7)
  - long `up` reached about (5,3)
  - short `right`, `up`, `right`, `up` reached about (6,1) near the visible staircase at top-right
  - pressing `right` there warped to Player's House 1F (map 0x25)
- Lesson: off-screen or partly off-screen walkable space can exist; visible bottom-edge dead ends are not sufficient evidence of corruption.

## Tooling / harness notes
- `save_checkpoint` may fail with a filesystem 500 error; treat checkpoints as unverified until a save succeeds.
- `list_checkpoints` previously returned none.
- Repeated mechanical sequences worth tooling:
  - intro progression / default-name acceptance
  - dialog mashing with moderate waits
  - systematic tile-probe paths that log coordinate changes
- `probe_path` is very useful for discovering large moves from long holds because it logs the resulting coordinates after each held direction.
- In-game SAVE is distinct from harness checkpoints and appears to work; it may allow `CONTINUE` after an external reset even when harness checkpoints are unavailable.

- I finished the first rival battle sequence, but **lost** the battle.

- After fainting, I advanced through the post-battle dialog:

data.summary:
Turn 25 summary:

- I made real progress instead of staying stuck at Oak’s Lab.
- From Oak’s Lab, I probed downward and confirmed the exit:
  - moving down from `(5,6)` to `(5,11)` reached the doorway area
  - one more `down` warped out to **Pallet Town** at about `(12,11)`
- I briefly re-entered Oak’s Lab by pressing `up` from the door, confirming the warp is reliable, then exited again.

What I learned in Pallet Town:
- Standing directly in front of Oak’s Lab door can be awkward for moving north; probing showed I needed to move away from the doorway alignment first.
- From outside Oak’s Lab,

arguments.content:
# Strategy playbook

## Early boot / intro
- The harness may begin before the game has fully reached the title screen even if RAM reports Pallet Town. If the screen is blank white, `wait` a few seconds for the boot/logo sequence.
- Once at the title screen, use `start` or `a` to proceed to the main menu / intro.
- RAM location can be stale or meaningless during boot/title; trust the screenshot until actual gameplay begins.
- On the player naming screen, pressing `start` accepted the default name immediately (resulting default shown as AAAA in this ROM/state).
- On the rival naming screen, pressing `start` also accepted the default name; after that, mash `a` through Oak's remaining intro until gameplay resumes in the bedroom.
- After intro finishes, the player may spawn facing the TV/SNES and an `A` press can reopen the inspect textbox immediately. Clear textboxes completely before attempting movement.

## General
- Default d-pad press moves about one tile.
- Long-hold d-pad presses can move many tiles and are essential for discovering larger walkable areas when the visible local probe seems too small.
- A advances dialog/confirms; B cancels; START opens menu.
- If a textbox is open, movement inputs do nothing; clear dialog before trying to walk.
- `B` can also dismiss the SNES/TV textbox; useful sequence from that state: `b, b, down, down` to reach clean standing state at (3,7) facing down.
- When screenshot and RAM disagree during normal gameplay, trust RAM for coordinates/state and screenshot for UI/dialog/world geometry.
- When stuck, trust RAM coordinates to map passable tiles. Probe systematically rather than repeating the same edge press.
- If a map behaves impossibly, do not overfit to short-press geometry: test long holds in each direction before declaring corruption.

## Current route
- We have Squirtle Lv5 and have already lost/completed the first rival battle; story can continue normally.
- Current priority is Route 1 northbound travel to Viridian City.
- Immediate next goals: finish clearing the current wild battle text, then continue north while using route edges/grass probes to find the passable path.

## Pallet Town / opening event flow
- Player's House 1F exit: from the downstairs room, the lower corridor leads to the door warp; from about (2,7) on map 0x25, pressing `down` exits to Pallet Town.
- Pallet Town north exit / Oak trigger: the correct opening is on the east side of the north path. At about map 0x00, x=10, y=1, stepping north triggers Oak's interruption and escort to the lab.
- Oak escort scene ends in Oak's Lab (map 0x28) with Oak, rival, and three Poké Balls near the table.

## Oak's Lab expectations
- At the first lab visit, Oak speaks for multiple textboxes before the player can choose a starter. Expect to mash through dialog before movement/selection resumes.
- The starter selection likely requires walking to a Poké Ball and pressing `A`; the rival may intercept certain choices, and Oak may have additional text before/after pickup.
- Save-state/checkpoint would be valuable before the rival fight or before choosing a starter if harness checkpoints become reliable.
- Verified starter choice taken: Squirtle.
- On the nickname screen, pressing `START` immediately declines naming and keeps the default species name.
- First rival battle can be started by pressing `A` while facing the rival in the lab.

## Route 1 notes
- At the south entrance around map 0x0c, (10,28), the north tile by the sign/fence is blocked; repeated `up` there does nothing.
- The bottom path is open to the east at least to about x=14.
- Stepping into grass on the east side can trigger wild encounters; confirmed Wild Rattata Lv3.
- In basic wild battles, repeated `A` is enough to progress when the cursor remains on FIGHT/TACKLE, but watch for menu state changes.

## Player's House 2F notes
- Prior wedge hypothesis was wrong.
- Correct fix: from bottom-left area, long-hold movement revealed a much larger room than short presses suggested.
- Verified successful escape path discovery:
  - from (1,7), long `right` reached about (5,7)
  - long `up` reached about (5,3)
  - short `right`, `up`, `right`, `up` reached about (6,1) near the visible staircase at top-right
  - pressing `right` there warped to Player's House 1F (map 0x25)
- Lesson: off-screen or partly off-screen walkable space can exist; visible bottom-edge dead ends are not sufficient evidence of corruption.

## Tooling / harness notes
- `save_checkpoint` may fail with a filesystem 500 error; treat checkpoints as unverified until a save succeeds.
- `list_checkpoints` previously returned none.
- Repeated mechanical sequences worth tooling:
  - intro progression / default-name acceptance
  - dialog mashing with moderate waits
  - systematic tile-probe paths that log coordinate changes
  - simple battle automation for early wild fights
- `probe_path` is very useful for discovering large moves from long holds because it logs the resulting coordinates after each held direction.
- In-game SAVE is distinct from harness checkpoints and appears to work; it may allow `CONTINUE` after an external reset even when harness checkpoints are unavailable.

- From **Route 1 (map 0x0c) at (10,28)**:
  - repeated `up` did nothing, confirming the **north tile by the sign/fence is blocked**
  - moving **right** along the bottom path worked up to about **x=14**
  - trying to go north there led into **grass** and triggered a **wild Rattata Lv3**
- In the wild battle, I used repeated `A` presses to progress through the menus/text and beat the Rattata.
  - Squirtle ended at **16/19 HP**
  - visible result: **“AAAA gained 24 EXP. Points!”**
- I saved

arguments.content: Route 1 clerk + lane notes: at about map 0x0c, around y=24, the apparent obstacle below the player was an NPC clerk on the main route, not impassable geometry. Pressing A while facing him triggered the standard early Route 1 conversation. He gives a free sample item (the usual Potion sample) and says the Poké Mart carries Poké Balls. Nearby topography: around x=5..7, y=24, moving up was blocked by the rock/fence line, so the northbound route is not straight up from that left-side position; interact with NPCs when a sprite seems to block progress.

data.name: update_playbook

arguments.content:
# Strategy playbook

## Early boot / intro
- The harness may begin before the game has fully reached the title screen even if RAM reports Pallet Town. If the screen is blank white, `wait` a few seconds for the boot/logo sequence.
- Once at the title screen, use `start` or `a` to proceed to the main menu / intro.
- RAM location can be stale or meaningless during boot/title; trust the screenshot until actual gameplay begins.
- On the player naming screen, pressing `start` accepted the default name immediately (resulting default shown as AAAA in this ROM/state).
- On the rival naming screen, pressing `start` also accepted the default name; after that, mash `a` through Oak's remaining intro until gameplay resumes in the bedroom.
- After intro finishes, the player may spawn facing the TV/SNES and an `A` press can reopen the inspect textbox immediately. Clear textboxes completely before attempting movement.

## General
- Default d-pad press moves about one tile.
- Long-hold d-pad presses can move many tiles and are essential for discovering larger walkable areas when the visible local probe seems too small.
- A advances dialog/confirms; B cancels; START opens menu.
- If a textbox is open, movement inputs do nothing; clear dialog before trying to walk.
- `B` can also dismiss the SNES/TV textbox; useful sequence from that state: `b, b, down, down` to reach clean standing state at (3,7) facing down.
- When screenshot and RAM disagree during normal gameplay, trust RAM for coordinates/state and screenshot for UI/dialog/world geometry.
- When stuck, trust RAM coordinates to map passable tiles. Probe systematically rather than repeating the same edge press.
- When a sprite is adjacent on the overworld, consider that it may be an NPC interaction target rather than geometry. Press `A` before overfitting to collision assumptions.
- If a map behaves impossibly, do not overfit to short-press geometry: test long holds in each direction before declaring corruption.

## Current route
- We have Squirtle, currently Lv6, and have already completed/lost the first rival battle; story can continue normally.
- Current priority remains reaching Viridian City via Route 1.
- Immediate next goals after the current wild battle: clear battle text, return to overworld, continue probing the true northbound lane from the clerk area instead of the south-east grass trap.
- Squirtle is low HP (currently known as 5/21 at start of turn 30), so avoid unnecessary grass if possible; be ready to use the free sample item if it is in the bag and needed.

## Pallet Town / opening event flow
- Player's House 1F exit: from the downstairs room, the lower corridor leads to the door warp; from about (2,7) on map 0x25, pressing `down` exits to Pallet Town.
- Pallet Town north exit / Oak trigger: the correct opening is on the east side of the north path. At about map 0x00, x=10, y=1, stepping north triggers Oak's interruption and escort to the lab.
- Oak escort scene ends in Oak's Lab (map 0x28) with Oak, rival, and three Poké Balls near the table.

## Oak's Lab expectations
- At the first lab visit, Oak speaks for multiple textboxes before the player can choose a starter. Expect to mash through dialog before movement/selection resumes.
- The starter selection likely requires walking to a Poké Ball and pressing `A`; the rival may intercept certain choices, and Oak may have additional text before/after pickup.
- Save-state/checkpoint would be valuable before the rival fight or before choosing a starter if harness checkpoints become reliable.
- Verified starter choice taken: Squirtle.
- On the nickname screen, pressing `START` immediately declines naming and keeps the default species name.
- First rival battle can be started by pressing `A` while facing the rival in the lab.

## Route 1 notes
- At the south entrance around map 0x0c, (10,28), the north tile by the sign/fence is blocked; repeated `up` there does nothing.
- The bottom path is open to the east at least to about x=14.
- Stepping into grass on the east side can trigger wild encounters; confirmed Wild Rattata Lv3.
- In basic wild battles, repeated `A` is enough to progress when the cursor remains on FIGHT/TACKLE, but watch for menu state changes.
- Additional probe: from around (14,28), moving right reaches at least x=17 on the bottom strip, but `up` from x=14..17 at y=28 still does not advance north. This east edge is a grass/encounter trap, not the route forward.
- Moving left from the south area eventually reaches the clerk section around y=24.
- Around x=5..7, y=24, `up` is blocked by the rock/fence line.
- The sprite in that area is the Route 1 clerk NPC, not blocking geometry; pressing `A` starts the free sample-item conversation about Poké Balls/Poké Mart.
- Route progress lesson: on Route 1, if movement seems blocked near a sprite, test NPC interaction before assuming the lane is sealed.

## Battle heuristics
- Early wild fights can usually be handled with repeated `A` when the default cursor stays on FIGHT then TACKLE.
- However, low HP matters: with Squirtle at low health, avoid extra grass encounters unless necessary for pathing.
- After a battle, there may be lingering result text; clear it fully before attempting movement.

## Player's House 2F notes
- Prior wedge hypothesis was wrong.
- Correct fix: from bottom-left area, long-hold movement revealed a much larger room than short presses suggested.
- Verified successful escape path discovery:
  - from (1,7), long `right` reached about (5,7)
  - long `up` reached about (5,3)
  - short `right`, `up`, `right`, `up` reached about (6,1) near the visible staircase at top-right
  - pressing `right` there warped to Player's House 1F (map 0x25)
- Lesson: off-screen or partly off-screen walkable space can exist; visible bottom-edge dead ends are not sufficient evidence of corruption.

## Tooling / harness notes
- `save_checkpoint` may fail with a filesystem 500 error; treat checkpoints as unverified until a save succeeds.
- `list_checkpoints` previously returned none.
- Repeated mechanical sequences worth tooling:
  - intro progression / default-name acceptance
  - dialog mashing with moderate waits
  - systematic tile-probe paths that log coordinate changes
  - simple battle automation for early wild fights / post-battle text clearing
- `probe_path` is very useful for discovering large moves from long holds because it logs the resulting coordinates after each held direction.
- In-game SAVE is distinct from harness checkpoints and appears to work; it may allow `CONTINUE` after an external reset even when harness checkpoints are unavailable.

arguments.content: Route 1 to Viridian City path verified: from Route 1 around (5,18), go straight north along the west lane to y=14, then head east along the rock barrier to x=14, go north through the trainer-side opening to around (14,12), continue north/right through the upper path, then from around (11,4) go straight north to y=0 and press up again to warp into Viridian City (map 0x01) at about (21,35). Key lesson: the apparent mid-route barrier is bypassed by going east to the trainer/grass opening, then looping north and back west near the top gate line.

data.name: update_playbook

arguments.content:
# Strategy playbook

## Early boot / intro
- The harness may begin before the game has fully reached the title screen even if RAM reports Pallet Town. If the screen is blank white, `wait` a few seconds for the boot/logo sequence.
- Once at the title screen, use `start` or `a` to proceed to the main menu / intro.
- RAM location can be stale or meaningless during boot/title; trust the screenshot until actual gameplay begins.
- On the player naming screen, pressing `start` accepted the default name immediately (resulting default shown as AAAA in this ROM/state).
- On the rival naming screen, pressing `start` also accepted the default name; after that, mash `a` through Oak's remaining intro until gameplay resumes in the bedroom.
- After intro finishes, the player may spawn facing the TV/SNES and an `A` press can reopen the inspect textbox immediately. Clear textboxes completely before attempting movement.

## General
- Default d-pad press moves about one tile.
- Long-hold d-pad presses can move many tiles and are essential for discovering larger walkable areas when the visible local probe seems too small.
- A advances dialog/confirms; B cancels; START opens menu.
- If a textbox is open, movement inputs do nothing; clear dialog before trying to walk.
- `B` can also dismiss the SNES/TV textbox; useful sequence from that state: `b, b, down, down` to reach clean standing state at (3,7) facing down.
- When screenshot and RAM disagree during normal gameplay, trust RAM for coordinates/state and screenshot for UI/dialog/world geometry.
- When stuck, trust RAM coordinates to map passable tiles. Probe systematically rather than repeating the same edge press.
- When a sprite is adjacent on the overworld, consider that it may be an NPC interaction target rather than geometry. Press `A` before overfitting to collision assumptions.
- If a map behaves impossibly, do not overfit to short-press geometry: test long holds in each direction before declaring corruption.

## Current route
- We have Squirtle, currently Lv6.
- Reached Viridian City (map 0x01) from Route 1; current priority is healing / safe progression because Squirtle is low HP.
- If on Route 1 with low HP, avoid grass and prefer RUN in wild battles: menu path from fight screen is `down, right, A`.

## Pallet Town / opening event flow
- Player's House 1F exit: from the downstairs room, the lower corridor leads to the door warp; from about (2,7) on map 0x25, pressing `down` exits to Pallet Town.
- Pallet Town north exit / Oak trigger: the correct opening is on the east side of the north path. At about map 0x00, x=10, y=1, stepping north triggers Oak's interruption and escort to the lab.
- Oak escort scene ends in Oak's Lab (map 0x28) with Oak, rival, and three Poké Balls near the table.

## Oak's Lab expectations
- At the first lab visit, Oak speaks for multiple textboxes before the player can choose a starter. Expect to mash through dialog before movement/selection resumes.
- The starter selection likely requires walking to a Poké Ball and pressing `A`; the rival may intercept certain choices, and Oak may have additional text before/after pickup.
- Save-state/checkpoint would be valuable before the rival fight or before choosing a starter if harness checkpoints become reliable.
- Verified starter choice taken: Squirtle.
- On the nickname screen, pressing `START` immediately declines naming and keeps the default species name.
- First rival battle can be started by pressing `A` while facing the rival in the lab.

## Route 1 notes
- At the south entrance around map 0x0c, (10,28), the north tile by the sign/fence is blocked; repeated `up` there does nothing.
- The bottom path is open to the east at least to about x=14.
- Stepping into grass on the east side can trigger wild encounters; confirmed Wild Rattata Lv3.
- In basic wild battles, repeated `A` is enough to progress when the cursor remains on FIGHT/TACKLE, but watch for menu state changes.
- Additional probe: from around (14,28), moving right reaches at least x=17 on the bottom strip, but `up` from x=14..17 at y=28 still does not advance north. This east edge is a grass/encounter trap, not the route forward.
- Moving left from the south area eventually reaches the clerk section around y=24.
- Around x=5..7, y=24, `up` is blocked by the rock/fence line.
- The sprite in that area is the Route 1 clerk NPC, not blocking geometry; pressing `A` starts the free sample-item conversation about Poké Balls/Poké Mart.
- Verified northbound route from mid-Route 1: from around (5,18), go north to y=14, then east along the horizontal rock barrier to about x=14, go north through the opening near the trainer-side grass, continue north through the upper path, then loop west near the top fence line and go north at about (11,0) to warp into Viridian City.

## Battle heuristics
- Early wild fights can usually be handled with repeated `A` when the default cursor stays on FIGHT then TACKLE.
- However, low HP matters: with Squirtle at low health, avoid extra grass encounters unless necessary for pathing.
- After a battle, there may be lingering result text; clear it fully before attempting movement.
- To run from a wild battle, use `down, right, A` from the main battle menu.

## Player's House 2F notes
- Prior wedge hypothesis was wrong.
- Correct fix: from bottom-left area, long-hold movement revealed a much larger room than short presses suggested.
- Verified successful escape path discovery:
  - from (1,7), long `right` reached about (5,7)
  - long `up` reached about (5,3)
  - short `right`, `up`, `right`, `up` reached about (6,1) near the visible staircase at top-right
  - pressing `right` there warped to Player's House 1F (map 0x25)
- Lesson: off-screen or partly off-screen walkable space can exist; visible bottom-edge dead ends are not sufficient evidence of corruption.

## Tooling / harness notes
- `save_checkpoint` may fail with a filesystem 500 error; treat checkpoints as unverified until a save succeeds.
- `list_checkpoints` previously returned none.
- Repeated mechanical sequences worth tooling:
  - intro progression / default-name acceptance
  - dialog mashing with moderate waits
  - systematic tile-probe paths that log coordinate changes
  - simple battle automation for early wild fights / post-battle text clearing
- `probe_path` is very useful for discovering large moves from long holds because it logs the resulting coordinates after each held direction.
- In-game SAVE is distinct from harness checkpoints and appears to work; it may allow `CONTINUE` after an external reset even when harness checkpoints are unavailable.

data.summary:
Made real progress: we broke the Route 1 “wedge” and reached **Viridian City**.

What I did:
- Probed north from **(5,18)** and confirmed the west lane is open up to **y=14**.
- Followed the horizontal rock barrier east to the opening near the trainer/grass area.
- Moved north through that opening, continued up the upper route, then looped west near the top fence line.
- Final warp:
  - from about **Route 1 (map 0x0c), (11,0)**, pressing **up** entered **Viridian City (map 0x01)** at about **(21,35)**.

What I learned:
- The Route 1 blockage was not a hard dead end.
- The correct path is:
  1.

- Re-probed Viridian City from the south entrance and made real positional progress:
  - moved north from `(21,35)` to around `(21,30)`
  - then west to `(13,30)`
  - then found a usable northbound route on the east side by going right to `(19,28)`, up to `(19,26)`, left to `(15,26)`, and up to `(15,25)`
  - continued north to `(15,18)`, then west to the town sign at about `(16,17)` facing right

- I verified the sign interaction:
  - pressing `A` on the sign opens the Viridian City sign text
  - visible text is the standard:
    - `VIRIDIAN CITY`
    - `The Eternally Green Paradise`
  - I sav

- Important

data.directive: This is a scheduled self-improvement turn. Do NOT press any buttons.

data.name: update_playbook

arguments.content:
# Strategy playbook

## Early boot / intro
- The harness may begin before the game has fully reached the title screen even if RAM reports Pallet Town. If the screen is blank white, `wait` a few seconds for the boot/logo sequence.
- Once at the title screen, use `start` or `a` to proceed to the main menu / intro.
- RAM location can be stale or meaningless during boot/title; trust the screenshot until actual gameplay begins.
- On the player naming screen, pressing `start` accepted the default name immediately (resulting default shown as AAAA in this ROM/state).
- On the rival naming screen, pressing `start` also accepted the default name; after that, mash `a` through Oak's remaining intro until gameplay resumes in the bedroom.
- After intro finishes, the player may spawn facing the TV/SNES and an `A` press can reopen the inspect textbox immediately. Clear textboxes completely before attempting movement.

## General
- Default d-pad press moves about one tile.
- Long-hold d-pad presses can move many tiles and are essential for discovering larger walkable areas when the visible local probe seems too small.
- A advances dialog/confirms; B cancels; START opens menu.
- If a textbox is open, movement inputs do nothing; clear dialog before trying to walk.
- `B` can also dismiss the SNES/TV textbox; useful sequence from that state: `b, b, down, down` to reach clean standing state at (3,7) facing down.
- When screenshot and RAM disagree during normal gameplay, trust RAM for coordinates/state and screenshot for UI/dialog/world geometry.
- When stuck, trust RAM coordinates to map passable tiles. Probe systematically rather than repeating the same edge press.
- When a sprite is adjacent on the overworld, consider that it may be an NPC interaction target rather than geometry. Press `A` before overfitting to collision assumptions.
- When inside buildings, talking to the nearest person is not always the objective; identify the correct service NPC visually before interacting.
- If a map behaves impossibly, do not overfit to short-press geometry: test long holds in each direction before declaring corruption.

## Current route
- We have Squirtle, currently Lv6.
- Immediate priority in Viridian City is healing at the Pokémon Center because Squirtle is at very low HP.
- After healing, next safe story step is the Viridian Mart.
- If on Route 1 with low HP, avoid grass and prefer RUN in wild battles: menu path from fight screen is `down, right, A`.

## Pallet Town / opening event flow
- Player's House 1F exit: from the downstairs room, the lower corridor leads to the door warp; from about (2,7) on map 0x25, pressing `down` exits to Pallet Town.
- Pallet Town north exit / Oak trigger: the correct opening is on the east side of the north path. At about map 0x00, x=10, y=1, stepping north triggers Oak's interruption and escort to the lab.
- Oak escort scene ends in Oak's Lab (map 0x28) with Oak, rival, and three Poké Balls near the table.

## Oak's Lab expectations
- At the first lab visit, Oak speaks for multiple textboxes before the player can choose a starter. Expect to mash through dialog before movement/selection resumes.
- The starter selection likely requires walking to a Poké Ball and pressing `A`; the rival may intercept certain choices, and Oak may have additional text before/after pickup.
- Save-state/checkpoint would be valuable before the rival fight or before choosing a starter if harness checkpoints become reliable.
- Verified starter choice taken: Squirtle.
- On the nickname screen, pressing `START` immediately declines naming and keeps the default species name.
- First rival battle can be started by pressing `A` while facing the rival in the lab.

## Route 1 notes
- At the south entrance around map 0x0c, (10,28), the north tile by the sign/fence is blocked; repeated `up` there does nothing.
- The bottom path is open to the east at least to about x=14.
- Stepping into grass on the east side can trigger wild encounters; confirmed Wild Rattata Lv3.
- In basic wild battles, repeated `A` is enough to progress when the cursor remains on FIGHT/TACKLE, but watch for menu state changes.
- Additional probe: from around (14,28), moving right reaches at least x=17 on the bottom strip, but `up` from x=14..17 at y=28 still does not advance north. This east edge is a grass/encounter trap, not the route forward.
- Moving left from the south area eventually reaches the clerk section around y=24.
- Around x=5..7, y=24, `up` is blocked by the rock/fence line.
- The sprite in that area is the Route 1 clerk NPC, not blocking geometry; pressing `A` starts the free sample-item conversation about Poké Balls/Poké Mart.
- Verified northbound route from mid-Route 1: from around (5,18), go north to y=14, then east along the horizontal rock barrier to about x=14, go north through the opening near the trainer-side grass, continue north through the upper path, then loop west near the top fence line and go north at about (11,0) to warp into Viridian City.

## Viridian City / Pokecenter notes
- From Route 1, entering Viridian City places the player near (21,35) at the south entrance.
- The city sign near about (16,17) reads: `VIRIDIAN CITY - The Eternally Green Paradise`.
- The east-side building labeled `MART` is the Poké Mart.
- There is an awkward horizontal fence/rock barrier around y=28 in the east/central city; many `up` attempts from below it are blocked.
- The usable crossing in that barrier is farther left than it first appears: from around (22,28), move left to about x=19, then north to about (19,25), then right toward the buildings.
- Viridian Pokémon Center entrance path found: from outside around (22,26), pressing `right, up` entered the Pokecenter (map 0x29) at about (3,7).
- Inside the Pokecenter, the standing man in the middle aisle is a normal advice NPC, not Nurse Joy. Do not waste turns re-clearing his long text when low HP healing is the goal.
- Nurse Joy should be at the top counter; after clearing any textbox, route directly to the counter and face the nurse to talk.

## Battle heuristics
- Early wild fights can usually be handled with repeated `A` when the default cursor stays on FIGHT then TACKLE.
- However, low HP matters: with Squirtle at low health, avoid extra grass encounters unless necessary for pathing.
- After a battle, there may be lingering result text; clear it fully before attempting movement.
- To run from a wild battle, use `down, right, A` from the main battle menu.

## Player's House 2F notes
- Prior wedge hypothesis was wrong.
- Correct fix: from bottom-left area, long-hold movement revealed a much larger room than short presses suggested.
- Verified successful escape path discovery:
  - from (1,7), long `right` reached about (5,7)
  - long `up` reached about (5,3)
  - short `right`, `up`, `right`, `up` reached about (6,1) near the visible staircase at top-right
  - pressing `right` there warped to Player's House 1F (map 0x25)
- Lesson: off-screen or partly off-screen walkable space can exist; visible bottom-edge dead ends are not sufficient evidence of corruption.

## Tooling / harness notes
- `save_checkpoint` may fail with a filesystem 500 error; treat checkpoints as unverified until a save succeeds.
- `list_checkpoints` previously returned none.
- Repeated mechanical sequences worth tooling:
  - intro progression / default-name acceptance
  - dialog mashing with moderate waits
  - systematic tile-probe paths that log coordinate changes
  - simple battle automation for early wild fights / post-battle text clearing
- `probe_path` is very useful for discovering large moves from long holds because it logs the resulting coordinates after each held direction.
- In-game SAVE is distinct from harness checkpoints and appears to work; it may allow `CONTINUE` after an external reset even when harness checkpoints are unavailable.

arguments.content:
Viridian Pokecenter notes: entrance path, misleading NPC, and healing priority.

- Viridian Pokémon Center is reachable from the east/central city side after crossing the awkward horizontal fence/rock barrier by going farther left than expected.
- Confirmed approach from city exterior: from around `(22,28)`, move left to about `x=19`, then north to about `(19,25)`, then right toward the building; from outside near `(22,26)`, pressing `right, up` entered **Viridian Pokecenter** `(map 0x29)` at about `(3,7)`.
- Inside the Pokecenter, the standing man in the middle aisle is **not Nurse Joy**. Talking to him gives standard advice text about Pokémon Centers, including lines like:
  - `They don't charge any money either!`
  - `There's a POKéMON CENTER in every town ahead.`
- Current caution: if already in dialog with this NPC, clear the textbox first, then walk to the **top counter** and talk to Nurse Joy for healing.
- At turn 40 state, player is in Viridian Pokecenter at about `(4,4)` facing up, with **Squirtle Lv6 at 5/21 HP**, so healing remains urgent before further route progression.
- After healing, next safe progression target is the **Viridian Mart**.

data.summary:
- Cleared the long stuck Pokecenter dialog by finally using **B** instead of endless A-mashing; that was the genuinely different input needed.
- Re-probed the room with `probe_path` and mapped the top aisle:
  - From the original stuck spot, moving **right, up** reached **(5,3)**.
  - At **(5,3)**, facing **left** and pressing **A** talks to the nearby male NPC, confirming that tile is beside him.
  - Moving to **(3,3)** and facing **right** also talks to the same/adjacent top-aisle NPC.
  - Multiple attempts to go **up** from the top aisle did nothing, so the counter blocks direct north movem

data.summary:
- Cleared the “stuck forever” Pokecenter dialog by using **B**; that finally returned to free movement. So the issue was not a softlock.
- From there I probed the top aisle and confirmed:
  - moving from `(3,3)` to `(5,3)` is possible
  - **up from `(5,3)` is blocked by the counter**
  - the tile/player immediately to the **left** at `(5,3)` is still the same advice NPC; pressing `A` there reopens his text
- I made a mistake by interacting left again and got pulled back into the same repeating advice text:
  - “There’s a POKéMON CENTER in every town ahead.”
  - “They don’t charge any money eit

data.summary:
- Broke out of the 7-turn Pokecenter wedge.
- Key cause found: bulk `A`/dialog-mash while facing the advice NPC can finish his text and immediately re-talk to him, making it look like the dialog never ends.
- I saved this as memory: `viridian_pokecenter_advice_npc_not_working`.

What worked:
- Advanced the advice text with smaller/manual presses until it fully cleared.
- Once free, I moved away from the NPC and re-probed the top aisle.
- Confirmed:
  - from `(5,3)`, `left` is blocked
  - `up` from the aisle is blocked by the counter
  - moving right is open: I reached `(9,3)` on the top aisle

data.summary:
- I made real diagnostic progress in the Viridian Pokecenter top aisle.
- Starting from `(9,3)`, I systematically tested interactions:
  - `(8,3)` facing left and `A` did nothing special.
  - `(7,3)` facing left and `A` opened dialog from the **left-side top NPC**.
  - At `(9,3)`, facing right and pressing `A` also opens dialog — this means there is a **right-side interaction target/NPC** immediately to the right of the player’s aisle position.
- Important nuance confirmed again: bulk dialog mashing can loop because after text closes, another `A` can immediately re-talk to the adjacent NPC.

data.summary:
- Broke out of the repeated Pokecenter dialog again and finally made positional progress.
- Key discovery: from the top aisle, **(9,3) facing up** appears to be the correct tile to talk to Nurse Joy across the counter. data.name: update_playbook

arguments.content:
# Strategy playbook

## Early boot / intro
- The harness may begin before the game has fully reached the title screen even if RAM reports Pallet Town. If the screen is blank white, `wait` a few seconds for the boot/logo sequence.
- Once at the title screen, use `start` or `a` to proceed to the main menu / intro.
- RAM location can be stale or meaningless during boot/title; trust the screenshot until actual gameplay begins.
- On the player naming screen, pressing `start` accepted the default name immediately (resulting default shown as AAAA in this ROM/state).
- On the rival naming screen, pressing `start` also accepted the default name; after that, mash `a` through Oak's remaining intro until gameplay resumes in the bedroom.
- After intro finishes, the player may spawn facing the TV/SNES and an `A` press can reopen the inspect textbox immediately. Clear textboxes completely before attempting movement.

## General
- Default d-pad press moves about one tile.
- Long-hold d-pad presses can move many tiles and are essential for discovering larger walkable areas when the visible local probe seems too small.
- A advances dialog/confirms; B cancels; START opens menu.
- If a textbox is open, movement inputs do nothing; clear dialog before trying to walk.
- `B` can also dismiss the SNES/TV textbox; useful sequence from that state: `b, b, down, down` to reach clean standing state at (3,7) facing down.
- When screenshot and RAM disagree during normal gameplay, trust RAM for coordinates/state and screenshot for UI/dialog/world geometry.
- When stuck, trust RAM coordinates to map passable tiles. Probe systematically rather than repeating the same edge press.
- When a sprite is adjacent on the overworld, consider that it may be an NPC interaction target rather than geometry. Press `A` before overfitting to collision assumptions.
- When inside buildings, talking to the nearest person is not always the objective; identify the correct service NPC visually before interacting.
- If a map behaves impossibly, do not overfit to short-press geometry: test long holds in each direction before declaring corruption.

## Current route
- We have Squirtle, currently Lv6.
- Immediate priority in Viridian City is healing at the Pokémon Center because Squirtle is at very low HP.
- After healing, next safe story step is the Viridian Mart.
- If on Route 1 with low HP, avoid grass and prefer RUN in wild battles: menu path from fight screen is `down, right, A`.

## Pallet Town / opening event flow
- Player's House 1F exit: from the downstairs room, the lower corridor leads to the door warp; from about (2,7) on map 0x25, pressing `down` exits to Pallet Town.
- Pallet Town north exit / Oak trigger: the correct opening is on the east side of the north path. At about map 0x00, x=10, y=1, stepping north triggers Oak's interruption and escort to the lab.
- Oak escort scene ends in Oak's Lab (map 0x28) with Oak, rival, and three Poké Balls near the table.

## Oak's Lab expectations
- At the first lab visit, Oak speaks for multiple textboxes before the player can choose a starter. Expect to mash through dialog before movement/selection resumes.
- The starter selection likely requires walking to a Poké Ball and pressing `A`; the rival may intercept certain choices, and Oak may have additional text before/after pickup.
- Save-state/checkpoint would be valuable before the rival fight or before choosing a starter if harness checkpoints become reliable.
- Verified starter choice taken: Squirtle.
- On the nickname screen, pressing `START` immediately declines naming and keeps the default species name.
- First rival battle can be started by pressing `A` while facing the rival in the lab.

## Route 1 notes
- At the south entrance around map 0x0c, (10,28), the north tile by the sign/fence is blocked; repeated `up` there does nothing.
- The bottom path is open to the east at least to about x=14.
- Stepping into grass on the east side can trigger wild encounters; confirmed Wild Rattata Lv3.
- In basic wild battles, repeated `A` is enough to progress when the cursor remains on FIGHT/TACKLE, but watch for menu state changes.
- Additional probe: from around (14,28), moving right reaches at least x=17 on the bottom strip, but `up` from x=14..17 at y=28 still does not advance north. This east edge is a grass/encounter trap, not the route forward.
- Moving left from the south area eventually reaches the clerk section around y=24.
- Around x=5..7, y=24, `up` is blocked by the rock/fence line.
- The sprite in that area is the Route 1 clerk NPC, not blocking geometry; pressing `A` starts the free sample-item conversation about Poké Balls/Poké Mart.
- Verified northbound route from mid-Route 1: from around (5,18), go north to y=14, then east along the horizontal rock barrier to about x=14, go north through the opening near the trainer-side grass, continue north through the upper path, then loop west near the top fence line and go north at about (11,0) to warp into Viridian City.

## Viridian City / Pokecenter notes
- From Route 1, entering Viridian City places the player near (21,35) at the south entrance.
- The city sign near about (16,17) reads: `VIRIDIAN CITY - The Eternally Green Paradise`.
- The east-side building labeled `MART` is the Poké Mart.
- There is an awkward horizontal fence/rock barrier around y=28 in the east/central city; many `up` attempts from below it are blocked.
- The usable crossing in that barrier is farther left than it first appears: from around (22,28), move left to about x=19, then north to about (19,25), then right toward the buildings.
- Viridian Pokémon Center entrance path found: from outside around (22,26), pressing `right, up` entered the Pokecenter (map 0x29) at about (3,7).
- Inside the Pokecenter, the standing man in the middle aisle is a normal advice NPC, not Nurse Joy. Do not waste turns re-clearing his long text when low HP healing is the goal.
- Nurse Joy is NOT at (11,3) facing up in this room state; that tile gives Cable Club receptionist text: `Welcome to the Cable Club! We're making preparations. Please wait.` Healing must be from a different counter position, likely farther left.
- Bulk dialog mashing near counter NPCs is dangerous because it can immediately re-talk after a textbox closes.

## Battle heuristics
- Early wild fights can usually be handled with repeated `A` when the default cursor stays on FIGHT then TACKLE.
- However, low HP matters: with Squirtle at low health, avoid extra grass encounters unless necessary for pathing.
- After a battle, there may be lingering result text; clear it fully before attempting movement.
- To run from a wild battle, use `down, right, A` from the main battle menu.

## Player's House 2F notes
- Prior wedge hypothesis was wrong.
- Correct fix: from bottom-left area, long-hold movement revealed a much larger room than short presses suggested.
- Verified successful escape path discovery:
  - from (1,7), long `right` reached about (5,7)
  - long `up` reached about (5,3)
  - short `right`, `up`, `right`, `up` reached about (6,1) near the visible staircase at top-right
  - pressing `right` there warped to Player's House 1F (map 0x25)
- Lesson: off-screen or partly off-screen walkable space can exist; visible bottom-edge dead ends are not sufficient evidence of corruption.

## Tooling / harness notes
- `save_checkpoint` may fail with a filesystem 500 error; treat checkpoints as unverified until a save succeeds.
- `list_checkpoints` previously returned none.
- Repeated mechanical sequences worth tooling:
  - intro progression / default-name acceptance
  - dialog mashing with moderate waits
  - systematic tile-probe paths that log coordinate changes
  - simple battle automation for early wild fights / post-battle text clearing
- `probe_path` is very useful for discovering large moves from long holds because it logs the resulting coordinates after each held direction.
- In-game SAVE is distinct from harness checkpoints and appears to work; it may allow `CONTINUE` after an external reset even when harness checkpoints are unavailable.

    - **“Welcome to the Cable Club!”**
    - **“We’re making preparations. Please wait.”**
  - So this tile does **not** heal; it is the **Cable Club receptionist**, not Nurse Joy.

- I also confirmed:
  - The “Please wait.” screen is **not** an automatic timed pause; waiting 240 and then 600 frames did nothing.
  - The textbox can be cleared with repeated **B** presses.
  - After clearing, the player remained at **(11,3)** with **Squi

- Immediate priority in Viridian City is healing at the Pokémon Center because Squirtle is at very low HP.
- After healing, next safe story step is the Viridian Mart.
- If on Route 1 with low HP, avoid grass and prefer RUN in wild battles: menu path from fight screen is `down, right, A`.

## Pallet Town / opening event flow
- Player's House 1F exit: from the downstairs room, the lower corridor leads to the door warp; from about (2,7) on map 0x25, pressing `down` exits to Pallet Town.
- Pallet Town north exit / Oak trigger: the correct opening is on the east side of the north path. At about map 0x00, x=10, y=1, stepping north triggers Oak's interruption and escort to the lab.
- Oak escort scene ends in Oak's Lab (map 0x28) with Oak, rival, and three Poké Balls near the table.

## Oak's Lab expectations
- At the first lab visit, Oak speaks for multiple textboxes before the player can choose a starter. Expect to mash through dialog before movement/selection resumes.
- The starter selection likely requires walking to a Poké Ball and pressing `A`; the rival may intercept certain choices, and Oak may have additional text before/after pickup.
- Save-state/checkpoint would be valuable before the rival fight or before choosing a starter if harness checkpoints become reliable.
- Verified starter choice taken: Squirtle.
- On the nickname screen, pressing `START` immediately declines naming and keeps the default species name.
- First rival battle can be started by pressing `A` while facing the rival in the lab.

## Route 1 notes
- At the south entrance around map 0x0c, (10,28), the north tile by the sign/fence is blocked; repeated `up` there does nothing.
- The bottom path is open to the east at least to about x=14.
- Stepping into grass on the east side can trigger wild encounters; confirmed Wild Rattata Lv3.
- In basic wild battles, repeated `A` is enough to progress when the cursor remains on FIGHT/TACKLE, but watch for menu state changes.
- Additional probe: from around (14,28), moving right reaches at least x=17 on the bottom strip, but `up` from x=14..17 at y=28 still does not advance north. This east edge is a grass/encounter trap, not the route forward.
- Moving left from the south area eventually reaches the clerk section around y=24.
- Around x=5..7, y=24, `up` is blocked by the rock/fence line.
- The sprite in that area is the Route 1 clerk NPC, not blocking geometry; pressing `A` starts the free sample-item conversation about Poké Balls/Poké Mart.
- Verified northbound route from mid-Route 1: from around (5,18), go north to y=14, then east along the horizontal rock barrier to about x=14, go north through the opening near the trainer-side grass, continue north through the upper path, then loop west near the top fence line and go north at about (11,0) to warp into Viridian City.

## Viridian City / Pokecenter notes
- From Route 1, entering Viridian City places the player near (21,35) at the south entrance.
- The city sign near about (16,17) reads: `VIRIDIAN CITY - The Eternally Green Paradise`.
- The east-side building labeled `MART` is the Poké Mart.
- There is an awkward horizontal fence/rock barrier around y=28 in the east/central city; many `up` attempts from below it are blocked.
- The usable crossing in that barrier is farther left than it first appears: from around (22,28), move left to about x=19, then north to about (19,25), then right toward the buildings.
- Viridian Pokémon Center entrance path found: from outside around (22,26), pressing `right, up` entered the Pokecenter (map 0x29) at about (3,7).
- Inside the Pokecenter, the standing man in the middle aisle is a normal advice NPC, not Nurse Joy. Do not waste turns re-clearing his long text when low HP healing is the goal.
- Bulk dialog mashing near counter NPCs is dangerous because it can immediately re-talk after a textbox closes.
- Verified top-aisle interaction map so far:
  - `(7,3)` facing left talks to the left-side top NPC.
  - `(9,3)` facing right talks to the right-side male NPC (`The receptionist told me...` / PC advice).
  - `(9,3)` facing up does nothing useful.
  - `(10,4)` facing up also talks to the right-side male NPC, not the healer.
  - `(11,3)` facing up talks to the Cable Club receptionist: `Welcome to the Cable Club! We're making preparations. Please wait.` This does **not** heal.
- Current best inference: the actual Nurse Joy/healing counter should be left of the Cable Club desk; next play turn should probe leftward from `(11,3)` along the top aisle and test upward interactions one tile at a time with single `A` presses.

## Battle heuristics
- Early wild fights can usually be handled with repeated `A` when the default cursor stays on FIGHT then TACKLE.
- However, low HP matters: with Squirtle at low health, avoid extra grass encounters unless necessary for pathing.
- After a battle, there may be lingering result text; clear it fully before attempting movement.
- To run from a wild battle, use `down, right, A` from the main battle menu.

## Player's House 2F notes
- Prior wedge hypothesis was wrong.
- Correct fix: from bottom-left area, long-hold movement revealed a much larger room than short presses suggested.
- Verified successful escape path discovery:
  - from (1,7), long `right` reached about (5,7)
  - long `up` reached about (5,3)
  - short `right`, `up`, `right`, `up` reached about (6,1) near the visible staircase at top-right
  - pressing `right` there warped to Player's House 1F (map 0x25)
- Lesson: off-screen or partly off-screen walkable space can exist; visible bottom-edge dead ends are not sufficient evidence of corruption.

## Tooling / harness notes
- `save_checkpoint` may fail with a filesystem 500 error; treat checkpoints as unverified until a save succeeds.
- `list_checkpoints` previously returned none.
- Repeated mechanical sequences worth tooling:
  - intro progression / default-name acceptance
  - dialog mashing with moderate waits
  - systematic tile-probe paths that log coordinate changes
  - simple battle automation for early wild fights / post-battle text clearing
  - safe one-step NPC desk scanning in service buildings
- `probe_path` is very useful for discovering large moves from long holds because it logs the resulting coordinates after each held direction.
- In-game SAVE is distinct from harness checkpoints and appears to work; it may allow `CONTINUE` after an external reset even when harness checkpoints are unavailable.

data.name: update_playbook

arguments.content:
# Strategy playbook

## Early boot / intro
- The harness may begin before the game has fully reached the title screen even if RAM reports Pallet Town. If the screen is blank white, `wait` a few seconds for the boot/logo sequence.
- Once at the title screen, use `start` or `a` to proceed to the main menu / intro.
- RAM location can be stale or meaningless during boot/title; trust the screenshot until actual gameplay begins.
- On the player naming screen, pressing `start` accepted the default name immediately (resulting default shown as AAAA in this ROM).
- On the rival naming screen, pressing `start` also accepted the default name; after that, mash `a` through Oak's remaining intro until gameplay resumes in the bedroom.
- After intro finishes, the player may spawn facing the TV/SNES and an `A` press can reopen the inspect textbox immediately. Clear textboxes completely before attempting movement.

## General
- Default d-pad press moves about one tile.
- Long-hold d-pad presses can move many tiles and are essential for discovering larger walkable areas when the visible local probe seems too small.
- A advances dialog/confirms; B cancels; START opens menu.
- If a textbox is open, movement inputs do nothing; clear dialog before trying to walk.
- `B` can also dismiss some overworld textboxes cleanly; when an NPC conversation seems to loop forever, suspect that repeated `A` is finishing the text and immediately re-talking to the NPC.
- When screenshot and RAM disagree during normal gameplay, trust RAM for coordinates/state and screenshot for UI/dialog/world geometry.
- When stuck, trust RAM coordinates to map passable tiles. Probe systematically rather than repeating the same edge press.
- When a sprite is adjacent on the overworld, consider that it may be an NPC interaction target rather than geometry. Press `A` before overfitting to collision assumptions.
- When inside buildings, talking to the nearest person is not always the objective; identify the correct service NPC visually before interacting.
- If a map behaves impossibly, do not overfit to short-press geometry: test long holds in each direction before declaring corruption.
- For risky NPC/counter interaction areas, prefer single `A` presses and then clear text with controlled small batches instead of bulk dialog mashing.

## Current route
- We have Squirtle, currently Lv6.
- Squirtle has now been healed in Viridian City's Pokémon Center.
- Next safe story step is the Viridian Mart parcel event.

## Pallet Town / opening event flow
- Player's House 1F exit: from the downstairs room, the lower corridor leads to the door warp; from about (2,7) on map 0x25, pressing `down` exits to Pallet Town.
- Pallet Town north exit / Oak trigger: the correct opening is on the east side of the north path. At about map 0x00, x=10, y=1, stepping north triggers Oak's interruption and escort to the lab.
- Oak escort scene ends in Oak's Lab (map 0x28) with Oak, rival, and three Poké Balls near the table.

## Oak's Lab expectations
- At the first lab visit, Oak speaks for multiple textboxes before the player can choose a starter. Expect to mash through dialog before movement/selection resumes.
- The starter selection likely requires walking to a Poké Ball and pressing `A`; the rival may intercept certain choices, and Oak may have additional text before/after pickup.
- Save-state/checkpoint would be valuable before the rival fight or before choosing a starter if harness checkpoints become reliable.
- Verified starter choice taken: Squirtle.
- On the nickname screen, pressing `START` immediately declines naming and keeps the default species name.
- First rival battle can be started by pressing `A` while facing the rival in the lab.

## Route 1 notes
- At the south entrance around map 0x0c, (10,28), the north tile by the sign/fence is blocked; repeated `up` there does nothing.
- The bottom path is open to the east at least to about x=14.
- Stepping into grass on the east side can trigger wild encounters; confirmed Wild Rattata Lv3.
- In basic wild battles, repeated `A` is enough to progress when the cursor remains on FIGHT/TACKLE, but watch for menu state changes.
- Additional probe: from around (14,28), moving right reaches at least x=17 on the bottom strip, but `up` from x=14..17 at y=28 still does not advance north. This east edge is a grass/encounter trap, not the route forward.
- Moving left from the south area eventually reaches the clerk section around y=24.
- Around x=5..7, y=24, `up` is blocked by the rock/fence line.
- The sprite in that area is the Route 1 clerk NPC, not blocking geometry; pressing `A` starts the free sample-item conversation about Poké Balls/Poké Mart.
- Verified northbound route from mid-Route 1: from around (5,18), go north to y=14, then east along the horizontal rock barrier to about x=14, go north through the opening near the trainer-side grass, continue north through the upper path, then loop west near the top fence line and go north at about (11,0) to warp into Viridian City.

## Viridian City / Pokecenter notes
- From Route 1, entering Viridian City places the player near (21,35) at the south entrance.
- The city sign near about (16,17) reads: `VIRIDIAN CITY - The Eternally Green Paradise`.
- The east-side building labeled `MART` is the Poké Mart.
- There is an awkward horizontal fence/rock barrier around y=28 in the east/central city; many `up` attempts from below it are blocked.
- The usable crossing in that barrier is farther left than it first appears: from around (22,28), move left to about x=19, then north to about (19,25), then right toward the buildings.
- Viridian Pokémon Center entrance path found: from outside around (22,26), pressing `right, up` entered the Pokecenter (map 0x29) at about (3,7).
- Inside the Pokecenter, the standing man in the middle aisle is a normal advice NPC, not Nurse Joy. Do not waste turns re-clearing his long text when low HP healing is the goal.
- Bulk dialog mashing near counter NPCs is dangerous because it can immediately re-talk after a textbox closes.
- Verified top-aisle interaction map so far:
  - `(7,3)` facing left talks to the left-side top NPC.
  - `(9,3)` facing right talks to the right-side male NPC (`The receptionist told me...` / PC advice).
  - `(9,3)` facing up does nothing useful.
  - `(10,4)` facing up also talks to the right-side male NPC, not the healer.
  - `(11,3)` facing up talks to the Cable Club receptionist: `Welcome to the Cable Club! We're making preparations. Please wait.` This does **not** heal.
  - `(6,3)` facing up also does nothing useful.
  - Actual Nurse Joy / healer tile found: stand at `(3,3)` facing `up` and press `A`. This opens `HEAL / CANCEL`, and choosing `HEAL` restores the party normally.

## Battle heuristics
- Early wild fights can usually be handled with repeated `A` when the default cursor stays on FIGHT then TACKLE.
- However, low HP matters: with Squirtle at low health, avoid extra grass encounters unless necessary for pathing.
- After a battle, there may be lingering result text; clear it fully before attempting movement.
- To run from a wild battle, use `down, right, A` from the main battle menu.

## Player's House 2F notes
- Prior wedge hypothesis was wrong.
- Correct fix: from bottom-left area, long-hold movement revealed a much larger room than short presses suggested.
- Verified successful escape path discovery:
  - from (1,7), long `right` reached about (5,7)
  - long `up` reached about (5,3)
  - short `right`, `up`, `right`, `up` reached about (6,1) near the visible staircase at top-right
  - pressing `right` there warped to Player's House 1F (map 0x25)
- Lesson: off-screen or partly off-screen walkable space can exist; visible bottom-edge dead ends are not sufficient evidence of corruption.

## Tooling / harness notes
- `save_checkpoint` may fail with a filesystem 500 error; treat checkpoints as unverified until a save succeeds.
- `list_checkpoints` previously returned none.
- Repeated mechanical sequences worth tooling:
  - intro progression / default-name acceptance
  - dialog mashing with moderate waits
  - systematic tile-probe paths that log coordinate changes
  - simple battle automation for early wild fights / post-battle text clearing
  - safe one-step NPC desk scanning in service buildings
- `probe_path` is very useful for discovering large moves from long holds because it logs the resulting coordinates after each held direction.
- In-game SAVE is distinct from harness checkpoints and appears to work; it may allow `CONTINUE` after an external reset even when harness checkpoints are unavailable.

  - Actual Nurse Joy / healer tile found: stand at `(3,3)` facing `up` and press `A`. This opens `HEAL / CANCEL`, and choosing `HEAL` restores the party normally.

## Viridian Mart notes
- Entering the Mart can leave the interior visible for a moment before the clerk's automatic parcel dialog appears; a short `wait` can be the right move instead of assuming nothing happened.
- Parcel event flow: after the greeting (`Hey! You came from PALLET TOWN?`), keep advancing text until receiving `OAK's PARCEL`, then clear the final line `Okay! Say hi to PROF.OAK for me!`.
- After the parcel dialog, the player is free inside the Mart around `(2,5)`. To exit, walk down to about `(2,7)` and then use `right, down` to line up with the door warp back to Viridian City.

## Battle heuristics
- Early wild fights can usually be handled with repeated `A` when the default cursor stays on FIGHT then TACKLE.
- However, low HP matters: with Squirtle at low health, avoid extra grass encounters unless necessary for pathing.
- After a battle, there may be lingering result text; clear it fully before attempting movement.
- To run from a wild battle, use `down, right, A` from the main battle menu.

## Player's House 2F notes
- Prior wedge hypothesis was wrong.
- Correct fix: from bottom-left area, long-hold movement revealed a much larger room than short presses suggested.
- Verified successful escape path discovery:
  - from (1,7), long `right` reached about (5,7)
  - long `up` reached about (5,3)
  - short `right`, `up`, `right`, `up` reached about (6,1) near the visible staircase at top-right
  - pressing `right` there warped to Player's House 1F (map 0x25)
- Lesson: off-screen or partly off-screen walkable space can exist; visible bottom-edge dead ends are not sufficient evidence of corruption.

## Tooling / harness notes
- `save_checkpoint` may fail with a filesystem 500 error; treat checkpoints as unverified until a save succeeds.
- `list_checkpoints` previously returned none.
- Repeated mechanical sequences worth tooling:
  - intro progression / default-name acceptance
  - dialog mashing with moderate waits
  - systematic tile-probe paths that log coordinate changes
  - simple battle automation for early wild fights / post-battle text clearing
  - safe one-step NPC desk scanning in service buildings
- `probe_path` is very useful for discovering large moves from long holds because it logs the resulting coordinates after each held direction.
- In-game SAVE is distinct from harness checkpoints and appears to work; it may allow `CONTINUE` after an external reset even when harness checkpoints are unavailable.
