I broke the Route 1 “wedge” by trying a genuinely different path and timing.

What I did:
- Probed the lower-left edge from `(12,31)` instead of repeating blocked downward presses.
- That probe stepped into grass and triggered a wild Pidgey Lv3.
- In battle, I first tried fighting, but with Squirtle at low HP the exchange was bad:
  - Squirtle dropped from `7/23` to `4/23`, then to `1/23`.
- I then used the known run input `down, right, A` and successfully escaped.
- After clearing the “Got away safely!” text, I tested moving straight down from the new position.
- That worked: from `(10,33)` I

data.directive: This is a scheduled self-improvement turn. Do NOT press any buttons.

data.name: update_playbook

arguments.content:
# Strategy playbook

## Early boot / intro
- The harness may begin before the game has fully reached the title screen even if RAM reports Pallet Town. If the screen is blank white, `wait` a few seconds for the boot/logo sequence.
- Once at the title screen, use `start` or `a` to proceed to the main menu / intro.
- RAM location can be stale or meaningless during boot/title; trust the screenshot until actual gameplay begins.
- On the player naming screen, pressing `start` accepted the default name immediately (resulting default shown as AAAA in this ROM).
- On the rival naming screen, pressing `start` also accepted the default name; after that, mash `a` through Oak's remaining intro until gameplay resumes in the bedroom.
- After intro finishes, the player may spawn facing the TV/SNES and an `A` press can reopen the inspect textbox immediately. Clear textboxes completely before attempting movement.

## General
- Default d-pad press moves about one tile.
- Long-hold d-pad presses can move many tiles and are essential for discovering larger walkable areas when the visible local probe seems too small.
- A advances dialog/confirms; B cancels; START opens menu.
- If a textbox is open, movement inputs do nothing; clear dialog before trying to walk.
- `B` can also dismiss some overworld textboxes cleanly; when an NPC conversation seems to loop forever, suspect that repeated `A` is finishing the text and immediately re-talking to the NPC.
- When screenshot and RAM disagree during normal gameplay, trust RAM for coordinates/state and screenshot for UI/dialog/world geometry.
- When stuck, trust RAM coordinates to map passable tiles. Probe systematically rather than repeating the same edge press.
- When a sprite is adjacent on the overworld, consider that it may be an NPC interaction target rather than geometry. Press `A` before overfitting to collision assumptions.
- When inside buildings, talking to the nearest person is not always the objective; identify the correct service NPC visually before interacting.
- If a map behaves impossibly, do not overfit to short-press geometry: test long holds in each direction before declaring corruption.
- For risky NPC/counter interaction areas, prefer single `A` presses and then clear text with controlled small batches instead of bulk dialog mashing.
- If a building entrance seems to leave the screen visually unchanged, try a short `wait`; some scripted shop greetings appear after a delay.

## Current route
- We have Squirtle, currently Lv7.
- Viridian Mart parcel event is complete; next story step is delivering Oak's Parcel in Pallet Town.
- Current high-risk condition: Squirtle can be left at critically low HP after Route 1 travel. If already back in Pallet Town or close to Oak, prioritize parcel delivery before any extra grass/wild steps; heal after the lab scene if needed.

## Pallet Town / opening event flow
- Player's House 1F exit: from the downstairs room, the lower corridor leads to the door warp; from about (2,7) on map 0x25, pressing `down` exits to Pallet Town.
- Pallet Town north exit / Oak trigger: the correct opening is on the east side of the north path. At about map 0x00, x=10, y=1, stepping north triggers Oak's interruption and escort to the lab.
- After the opening Oak escort has already happened once, standing at `(10,1)` at the north path is just a location marker; do not expect the same trigger again.
- Oak's Lab is the large building in the southwest/left side of Pallet Town; when returning with the parcel, go south from the north path and then left toward the lab rather than re-testing the old north-exit trigger.

## Oak's Lab expectations
- At the first lab visit, Oak speaks for multiple textboxes before the player can choose a starter. Expect to mash through dialog before movement/selection resumes.
- The starter selection likely requires walking to a Poké Ball and pressing `A`; the rival may intercept certain choices, and Oak may have additional text before/after pickup.
- Save-state/checkpoint would be valuable before the rival fight or before choosing a starter if harness checkpoints become reliable.
- Verified starter choice taken: Squirtle.
- On the nickname screen, pressing `START` immediately declines naming and keeps the default species name.
- First rival battle can be started by pressing `A` while facing the rival in the lab.
- When returning Oak's Parcel, expect several textboxes and likely Pokédex progression afterward; be ready to clear dialog fully before attempting movement.

## Route 1 notes
- At the south entrance around map 0x0c, (10,28), the north tile by the sign/fence is blocked; repeated `up` there does nothing.
- The bottom path is open to the east at least to about x=14.
- Stepping into grass on the east side can trigger wild encounters; confirmed Wild Rattata Lv3.
- In basic wild battles, repeated `A` is enough to progress when the cursor remains on FIGHT/TACKLE, but watch for menu state changes.
- Additional probe: from around (14,28), moving right reaches at least x=17 on the bottom strip, but `up` from x=14..17 at y=28 still does not advance north. This east edge is a grass/encounter trap, not the route forward.
- Moving left from the south area eventually reaches the clerk section around y=24.
- Around x=5..7, y=24, `up` is blocked by the rock/fence line.
- The sprite in that area is the Route 1 clerk NPC, not blocking geometry; pressing `A` starts the free sample-item conversation about Poké Balls/Poké Mart.
- Verified northbound route from mid-Route 1: from around (5,18), go north to y=14, then east along the horizontal rock barrier to about x=14, go north through the opening near the trainer-side grass, continue north through the upper path, then loop west near the top fence line and go north at about (11,0) to warp into Viridian City.
- Southbound return route matters too: from Viridian City, the usable south gate/opening in the fence line is around x≈20, y≈30. Passing through it warps to Route 1 near `(11,0)`.
- Lower Route 1 can be misleading: from around `(12,31)`, repeated `down` can look like a wedge, but the true south exit is a bit left of there.
- Verified fix for the lower Route 1 south exit: moving left into the lower-left lane and then going down reaches Pallet Town. Do not keep retrying blocked `down` at `(12,31)`.

## Viridian City / Pokecenter notes
- From Route 1, entering Viridian City places the player near (21,35) at the south entrance.
- The city sign near about (16,17) reads: `VIRIDIAN CITY - The Eternally Green Paradise`.
- The east-side building labeled `MART` is the Poké Mart.
- There is an awkward horizontal fence/rock barrier around y=28 in the east/central city; many `up` attempts from below it are blocked.
- The usable crossing in that barrier is farther left than it first appears: from around (22,28), move left to about x=19, then north to about (19,25), then right toward the buildings.
- Viridian Pokémon Center entrance path found: from outside around (22,26), pressing `right, up` entered the Pokecenter (map 0x29) at about (3,7).
- Inside the Pokecenter, the standing man in the middle aisle is a normal advice NPC, not Nurse Joy. Do not waste turns re-clearing his long text when low HP healing is the goal.
- Bulk dialog mashing near counter NPCs is dangerous because it can immediately re-talk after a textbox closes.
- Verified top-aisle interaction map so far:
  - `(7,3)` facing left talks to the left-side top NPC.
  - `(9,3)` facing right talks to the right-side male NPC (`The receptionist told me...` / PC advice).
  - `(9,3)` facing up does nothing useful.
  - `(10,4)` facing up also talks to the right-side male NPC, not the healer.
  - `(11,3)` facing up talks to the Cable Club receptionist: `Welcome to the Cable Club! We're making preparations. Please wait.` This does **not** heal.
  - `(6,3)` facing up also does nothing useful.
  - Actual Nurse Joy / healer tile found: stand at `(3,3)` facing `up` and press `A`. This opens `HEAL / CANCEL`, and choosing `HEAL` restores the party normally.

## Viridian Mart notes
- Entering the Mart can leave the interior visible for a moment before the clerk's automatic parcel dialog appears; a short `wait` can be the right move instead of assuming nothing happened.
- Parcel event flow: after the greeting (`Hey! You came from PALLET TOWN?`), keep advancing text until receiving `OAK's PARCEL`, then clear the final line `Okay! Say hi to PROF.OAK for me!`.
- After the parcel dialog, the player is free inside the Mart around `(2,5)`. To exit, walk down to about `(2,7)` and then use `right, down` to line up with the door warp back to Viridian City.

## Battle heuristics
- Early wild fights can usually be handled with repeated `A` when the default cursor stays on FIGHT then TACKLE.
- However, low HP matters: with Squirtle at low health, avoid extra grass encounters unless necessary for pathing.
- After a battle, there may be lingering result text; clear it fully before attempting movement.
- To run from a wild battle, use `down, right, A` from the main battle menu.
- Important timing lesson: battle-state/tool detection can clear before the visible battle text is gone. Trust the screenshot and keep advancing until the overworld is visibly back.
- If HP is dangerously low, do not stubbornly finish a wild battle just because Tackle is selected by default; fleeing is reliable and should be preferred.

## Player's House 2F notes
- Prior wedge hypothesis was wrong.
- Correct fix: from bottom-left area, long-hold movement revealed a much larger room than short presses suggested.
- Verified successful escape path discovery:
  - from (1,7), long `right` reached about (5,7)
  - long `up` reached about (5,3)
  - short `right`, `up`, `right`, `up` reached about (6,1) near the visible staircase at top-right
  - pressing `right` there warped to Player's House 1F (map 0x25)
- Lesson: off-screen or partly off-screen walkable space can exist; visible bottom-edge dead ends are not sufficient evidence of corruption.

## Tooling / harness notes
- `save_checkpoint` may fail with a filesystem 500 error; treat checkpoints as unverified until a save succeeds.
- `list_checkpoints` previously returned none.
- Repeated mechanical sequences worth tooling:
  - intro progression / default-name acceptance
  - dialog mashing with moderate waits
  - systematic tile-probe paths that log coordinate changes
  - simple battle automation for early wild fights / post-battle text clearing
  - safe one-step NPC desk scanning in service buildings
  - wild-battle flee macro for low-HP travel
- `probe_path` is very useful for discovering large moves from long holds because it logs the resulting coordinates after each held direction.
- In-game SAVE is distinct from harness checkpoints and appears to work; it may allow `CONTINUE` after an external reset even when harness checkpoints are unavailable.

data.summary:
- Reached the correct building and entered **Oak’s Lab** from Pallet Town. Important timing lesson: after the warp, the screen still showed the exterior until a short **wait**; then the lab interior appeared. So building-entry visuals can lag and a brief wait is sometimes required.
- Inside the lab, I probed the room and confirmed:
  - From the entrance area, moving **up/up/left/left/up** reaches the **top-left bookshelf zone**.
  - Pressing **A** there gives the standard bookshelf text: **“Crammed full of POKéMON books!”**
  - Pressing **B** is good for clearing that text; repeated **A** just

arguments.content: Oak's Lab lower-right NPC probe: the NPC near the lower-right side of the lab, reached around `(8,11)` facing up, says `I study POKéMON as PROF.OAK's AIDE.` This is not Professor Oak and does not progress the parcel story. data.result: memory 'oak_lab_aide_position' saved

data.summary:
Cleared a genuinely different Oak's Lab interaction and learned the lower-right NPC is just **Prof. Oak’s aide**, not Oak.

What I did:
- Probed from the entrance area to the lower-right side of the lab.
- Faced the NPC at about `(8,11)` and talked to him.
- Cleared the full dialog safely.

What I learned:
- This NPC says: **“I study POKéMON as PROF.OAK’s AIDE.”**
- So talking here does **not** deliver Oak’s Parcel or advance the story.
- I saved this as memory: `oak_lab_aide_position`.

Current state:
- Still in **Oak’s Lab**
- At about **(8,11)** facing **up**
- **Squirtle is dangerously low

data.summary:
- I stopped repeating the lower-right aide and made a genuinely different Oak’s Lab probe.
- Using RAM-guided movement, I found the actual path to **Professor Oak**:
  - from around `(8,11)`, moving left to the center-left aisle, then up, then across/right to the top lab area reaches Oak near the desk.
- I re-confirmed multiple top bookshelf interactions are just **“Crammed full of POKéMON books!”** and not story progress.
- Important success: at **(4,2)** facing right, pressing `A` talked to **Oak**, and advancing dialog reached:
  - **“AAAA delivered OAK’s PARCEL.”**
- Current state is **mid

data.summary:
Delivered Oak’s Parcel scene fully and got unstuck.

What I did:
- Advanced Oak’s parcel dialog all the way through.
- Accidentally re-talked to the rival when probing movement too early; cleared that long rival/Oak conversation too.
- Confirmed free movement resumed in Oak’s Lab afterward.
- Walked down through the lab and found the exit.
- Successfully left Oak’s Lab to Pallet Town.

What I learned:
- Parcel delivery is definitely complete; free movement returns in the lab after a long Oak/rival dialog chain.
- Important timing/interaction lesson: immediately after story text, movement probe

data.summary:

- Probed around Pallet and found a warp into the right-side house.

- The lower/left woman is not useful for healing.
- Talking to the man in the middle/top triggered a healing-style effect.
- I then used the menu item sequence `START, down, down, A...` and confirmed the result in RAM: **Squirtle went from 1/23 HP to 21/23 HP**.

- On Route 1, I probed the lower entrance area around `(10,32)` down to the familiar sign/fence choke near `(9-14,28)`.

  - The entire fence/rock line just north at `y=28` is blocked at multiple tested x positions: `x=4,5,6,9,10,15`.

What I learned:

data.directive: This is a scheduled self-improvement turn. Do NOT press any buttons.

data.name: update_playbook

arguments.content:
# Strategy playbook

## Early boot / intro
- The harness may begin before the game has fully reached the title screen even if RAM reports Pallet Town. If the screen is blank white, `wait` a few seconds for the boot/logo sequence.
- Once at the title screen, use `start` or `a` to proceed to the main menu / intro.
- RAM location can be stale or meaningless during boot/title; trust the screenshot until actual gameplay begins.
- On the player naming screen, pressing `start` accepted the default name immediately (resulting default shown as AAAA in this ROM).
- On the rival naming screen, pressing `start` also accepted the default name; after that, mash `a` through Oak's remaining intro until gameplay resumes in the bedroom.
- After intro finishes, the player may spawn facing the TV/SNES and an `A` press can reopen the inspect textbox immediately. Clear textboxes completely before attempting movement.

## General
- Default d-pad press moves about one tile.
- Long-hold d-pad presses can move many tiles and are essential for discovering larger walkable areas when the visible local probe seems too small.
- A advances dialog/confirms; B cancels; START opens menu.
- If a textbox is open, movement inputs do nothing; clear dialog before trying to walk.
- `B` can also dismiss some overworld textboxes cleanly; when an NPC conversation seems to loop forever, suspect that repeated `A` is finishing the text and immediately re-talking to the NPC.
- When screenshot and RAM disagree during normal gameplay, trust RAM for coordinates/state and screenshot for UI/dialog/world geometry.
- When stuck, trust RAM coordinates to map passable tiles. Probe systematically rather than repeating the same edge press.
- When a sprite is adjacent on the overworld, consider that it may be an NPC interaction target rather than geometry. Press `A` before overfitting to collision assumptions.
- When inside buildings, talking to the nearest person is not always the objective; identify the correct service NPC visually before interacting.
- If a map behaves impossibly, do not overfit to short-press geometry: test long holds in each direction before declaring corruption.
- For risky NPC/counter interaction areas, prefer single `A` presses and then clear text with controlled small batches instead of bulk dialog mashing.
- If a building entrance seems to leave the screen visually unchanged, try a short `wait`; some scripted shop greetings appear after a delay.
- Right after finishing a long story conversation, avoid immediate blind movement mashing; a stray `A` can re-open conversation with a nearby NPC (notably in Oak's Lab).

## Current route
- We have Squirtle, currently Lv7.
- Oak's Parcel delivery is complete and Pokédex progression already happened.
- Squirtle has been re-healed since the dangerous low-HP return trip; current travel is no longer emergency-low-HP.
- Current overworld objective is to return north through Route 1 to Viridian City and continue early-game progression, most likely testing whether the north exit/tutorial gate state has changed after parcel delivery.

## Pallet Town / opening event flow
- Player's House 1F exit: from the downstairs room, the lower corridor leads to the door warp; from about (2,7) on map 0x25, pressing `down` exits to Pallet Town.
- Pallet Town north exit / Oak trigger: the correct opening is on the east side of the north path. At about map 0x00, x=10, y=1, stepping north triggers Oak's interruption and escort to the lab.
- After the opening Oak escort has already happened once, standing at `(10,1)` at the north path is just a location marker; do not expect the same trigger again.
- Oak's Lab is the large building in the southwest/left side of Pallet Town; when returning with the parcel, go south from the north path and then left toward the lab rather than re-testing the old north-exit trigger.
- The right-side Pallet house can restore Squirtle via NPC interaction; this is a viable local recovery option if returning from Route 1 before reaching a Pokécenter.

## Oak's Lab expectations
- At the first lab visit, Oak speaks for multiple textboxes before the player can choose a starter. Expect to mash through dialog before movement/selection resumes.
- The starter selection likely requires walking to a Poké Ball and pressing `A`; the rival may intercept certain choices, and Oak may have additional text before/after pickup.
- Save-state/checkpoint would be valuable before the rival fight or before choosing a starter if harness checkpoints become reliable.
- Verified starter choice taken: Squirtle.
- On the nickname screen, pressing `START` immediately declines naming and keeps the default species name.
- First rival battle can be started by pressing `A` while facing the rival in the lab.
- When returning Oak's Parcel, expect several textboxes and likely Pokédex progression afterward; be ready to clear dialog fully before attempting movement.
- Verified parcel-delivery talk position: at about `(4,2)` facing right, pressing `A` talks to Oak and advances the delivery scene.
- Lower-right lab NPC around `(8,11)` facing up is only Oak's aide (`I study POKéMON as PROF.OAK's AIDE.`), not story progression.
- Top bookshelves repeatedly give `Crammed full of POKéMON books!`; don't confuse them with useful interactions.

## Route 1 notes
- At the south entrance around map 0x0c, (10,28), the north tile by the sign/fence is blocked; repeated `up` there does nothing.
- The bottom path is open to the east at least to about x=14.
- Stepping into grass on the east side can trigger wild encounters; confirmed Wild Rattata Lv3.
- In basic wild battles, repeated `A` is enough to progress when the cursor remains on FIGHT/TACKLE, but watch for menu state changes.
- Additional probe: from around (14,28), moving right reaches at least x=17 on the bottom strip, but `up` from x=14..17 at y=28 still does not advance north. This east edge is a grass/encounter trap, not the route forward.
- Moving left from the south area eventually reaches the clerk section around y=24.
- Around x=5..7, y=24, `up` is blocked by the rock/fence line.
- The sprite in that area is the Route 1 clerk NPC, not blocking geometry; pressing `A` starts the free sample-item conversation about Poké Balls/Poké Mart.
- Verified northbound route from mid-Route 1: from around `(5,18)`, go north to y=14, then east along the horizontal rock barrier to about x=14, go north through the opening near the trainer-side grass, continue north through the upper path, then loop west near the top fence line and go north at about `(11,0)` to warp into Viridian City.
- Southbound return route matters too: from Viridian City, the usable south gate/opening in the fence line is around x≈20, y≈30. Passing through it warps to Route 1 near `(11,0)`.
- Lower Route 1 can be misleading: from around `(12,31)`, repeated `down` can look like a wedge, but the true south exit is a bit left of there.
- Verified fix for the lower Route 1 south exit: moving left into the lower-left lane and then going down reaches Pallet Town. Do not keep retrying blocked `down` at `(12,31)`.
- Consolidated lower Route 1 choke lesson: the fence/rock line at y≈28 is blocked across many x-values on both the center and left side. Do not keep searching for a hidden north opening from the very bottom strip. To go north, first work upward through the western/middle lane before the known eastward bend.
- Current remembered state from latest probe: standing at about `(4,28)` facing up is beside the left fence/rock edge, but `up` there is still blocked by the choke line.

## Viridian City / Pokecenter notes
- From Route 1, entering Viridian City places the player near (21,35) at the south entrance.
- The city sign near about (16,17) reads: `VIRIDIAN CITY - The Eternally Green Paradise`.
- The east-side building labeled `MART` is the Poké Mart.
- There is an awkward horizontal fence/rock barrier around y=28 in the east/central city; many `up` attempts from below it are blocked.
- The usable crossing in that barrier is farther left than it first appears: from around (22,28), move left to about x=19, then north to about (19,25), then right toward the buildings.
- Viridian Pokémon Center entrance path found: from outside around (22,26), pressing `right, up` entered the Pokecenter (map 0x29) at about (3,7).
- Inside the Pokecenter, the standing man in the middle aisle is a normal advice NPC, not Nurse Joy. Do not waste turns re-clearing his long text when low HP healing is the goal.
- Bulk dialog mashing near counter NPCs is dangerous because it can immediately re-talk after a textbox closes.
- Verified top-aisle interaction map so far:
  - `(7,3)` facing left talks to the left-side top NPC.
  - `(9,3)` facing right talks to the right-side male NPC (`The receptionist told me...` / PC advice).
  - `(9,3)` facing up does nothing useful.
  - `(10,4)` facing up also talks to the right-side male NPC, not the healer.
  - `(11,3)` facing up talks to the Cable Club receptionist: `Welcome to the Cable Club! We're making preparations. Please wait.` This does **not** heal.
  - `(6,3)` facing up also does nothing useful.
  - Actual Nurse Joy / healer tile found: stand at `(3,3)` facing `up` and press `A`. This opens `HEAL / CANCEL`, and choosing `HEAL` restores the party normally.

## Viridian Mart notes
- Entering the Mart can leave the interior visible for a moment before the clerk's automatic parcel dialog appears; a short `wait` can be the right move instead of assuming nothing happened.
- Parcel event flow: after the greeting (`Hey! You came from PALLET TOWN?`), keep advancing text until receiving `OAK's PARCEL`, then clear the final line `Okay! Say hi to PROF.OAK for me!`.
- After the parcel dialog, the player is free inside the Mart around `(2,5)`. To exit, walk down to about `(2,7)` and then use `right, down` to line up with the door warp back to Viridian City.

## Battle heuristics
- Early wild fights can usually be handled with repeated `A` when the default cursor stays on FIGHT then TACKLE.
- However, HP matters: with Squirtle at low health, avoid extra grass encounters unless necessary for pathing.
- After a battle, there may be lingering result text; clear it fully before attempting movement.
- To run from a wild battle, use `down, right, A` from the main battle menu.
- Important timing lesson: battle-state/tool detection can clear before the visible battle text is gone. Trust the screenshot and keep advancing until the overworld is visibly back.
- If HP is dangerously low, do not stubbornly finish a wild battle just because Tackle is selected by default; fleeing is reliable and should be preferred.

## Player's House 2F notes
- Prior wedge hypothesis was wrong.
- Correct fix: from bottom-left area, long-hold movement revealed a much larger room than short presses suggested.
- Verified successful escape path discovery:
  - from (1,7), long `right` reached about (5,7)
  - long `up` reached about (5,3)
  - short `right`, `up`, `right`, `up` reached about (6,1) near the visible staircase at top-right
  - pressing `right` there warped to Player's House 1F (map 0x25)
- Lesson: off-screen or partly off-screen walkable space can exist; visible bottom-edge dead ends are not sufficient evidence of corruption.

## Tooling / harness notes
- `save_checkpoint` may fail with a filesystem 500 error; treat checkpoints as unverified until a save succeeds.
- `list_checkpoints` previously returned none.
- Repeated mechanical sequences worth tooling:
  - intro progression / default-name acceptance
  - dialog mashing with moderate waits
  - systematic tile-probe paths that log coordinate changes
  - simple battle automation for early wild fights / post-battle text clearing
  - safe one-step NPC desk scanning in service buildings
  - wild-battle flee macro for low-HP travel
  - deterministic walk-path execution that stops/logs on battle start or map change
- `probe_path` is very useful for discovering large moves from long holds because it logs the resulting coordinates after each held direction.
- In-game SAVE is distinct from harness checkpoints and appears to work; it may allow `CONTINUE` after an external reset even when harness checkpoints are unavailable.

arguments.name: route1_lower_choke_consolidated
arguments.content: Route 1 lower choke consolidated: the bottom strip near y≈28 is a trap for repeated probing. From multiple tested x-values on both center and left side, the fence/rock line directly north is blocked; there is no hidden north opening from the very bottom edge. Important specifics: `up` is blocked around x=4,5,6,9,10,14,15 at the choke line, and the east side tends to push into grass/wild encounters. The real northbound solution is not to brute-force the bottom strip; instead move upward earlier through the western/middle lane, reaching roughly `(5,18)`, then continue north to y≈14, head east to around x≈14, and go north through the known opening near the grass/trainer side.

arguments.content: Pallet Town right-side house healing note: the house on the east/right side of Pallet Town can function as a local recovery point before reaching Viridian's Pokecenter. On a prior visit after parcel delivery and low-HP travel, interacting with the relevant NPC in that house coincided with Squirtle's HP returning from 1/23 to 21/23. Treat this as a practical backup heal location in Pallet, though the exact NPC/tile should still be re-verified cleanly if used again.

data.name: read_memory
data.result: Route 1 to Viridian City path verified: from Route 1 around (5,18), go straight north along the west lane to y=14, then head east along the rock barrier to x=14, go north through the trainer-side opening to around (14,12), continue north/right through the upper path, then from around (11,4) go straight north to y=0 and press up again to warp into Viridian City (map 0x01) at about (21,35). Key lesson: the apparent mid-route barrier is bypassed by going east to the trainer/grass opening, then looping north and back west near the top gate line.

data.name: read_memory
data.result: Route 1 clerk + lane notes: at about map 0x0c, around y=24, the apparent obstacle below the player was an NPC clerk on the main route, not impassable geometry. Pressing A while facing him triggered the standard early Route 1 conversation. He gives a free sample item (the usual Potion sample) and says the Poké Mart carries Poké Balls. Nearby topography: around x=5..7, y=24, moving up was blocked by the rock/fence line, so the northbound route is not straight up from that left-side position; interact with NPCs when a sprite seems to block progress.

data.summary:
- I finally confirmed the old Route 1 knowledge is still correct: from `(4,28)` I moved `right,right` and then could go north up the west/middle lane to `(6,24)`. So the lower-left area was not a wedge; I had just been one lane too far left.
- From there, I verified:
  - `(5,24)` is the clerk lane and `up` is blocked there.
  - Going east along `y=24` works cleanly to `(14,24)`.
  - From `(14,24)`, `up` to `(14,23)` is possible, but further `up` is blocked.
- I then made a bad exploratory choice: I kept probing around the grass/opening area without enough caution, causing multiple wild Rattata

data.summary:
- Fled two wild **Rattata Lv3** encounters safely from the Route 1 upper-grass choke; Squirtle remains **Lv8, 18/26 HP**.
- Important timing lesson reaffirmed: the battle can sit on blank intro/send-out screens. A good flee sequence here was:
  - advance intro with a couple `A`s until the actual battle menu appears,
  - then `down, right, A`,
  - then a few `A`s to clear the result.
- I made genuinely different path probes around the suspected top passage instead of repeating straight `up`.
- Verified upper Route 1 geometry from this state:
  - From `(13,7)`, `up -> (13,6)`, `left -> (12,6)`,

arguments.content: Viridian City north-fence probe: at y=14, the upper fence line is blocked across a wide span. Verified blocked `up` interactions from about x=24 through x=35; pressing `A` there did nothing useful. The east edge at x=35 is also blocked by rock wall, so the north route opening is not on the far-right side.

- Then I tested Viridian’s north-side geometry rather than wandering blindly:

Key findings: a house entrance exists at about `(21,15)` leading to map `0x2b` (small interior with a bookshelf/table and one NPC); stepping back down exits cleanly. Continuing north-west from there reaches about `(19,12)`, then east across the upper lane reaches the Gym frontage around `(27,10)`. The rocky ledge/fence line south of the Gym blocks moving straight north from below it; probing at `x≈22..27, y=10` showed repeated `up` blocked. So the Gym area is reachable, but the north route entrance was not found in this probe.

data.name: update_playbook

arguments.content:
# Strategy playbook

## Early boot / intro
- The harness may begin before the game has fully reached the title screen even if RAM reports Pallet Town. If the screen is blank white, `wait` a few seconds for the boot/logo sequence.
- Once at the title screen, use `start` or `a` to proceed to the main menu / intro.
- RAM location can be stale or meaningless during boot/title; trust the screenshot until actual gameplay begins.
- On the player naming screen, pressing `start` accepted the default name immediately (resulting default shown as AAAA in this ROM).
- On the rival naming screen, pressing `start` also accepted the default name; after that, mash `a` through Oak's remaining intro until gameplay resumes in the bedroom.
- After intro finishes, the player may spawn facing the TV/SNES and an `A` press can reopen the inspect textbox immediately. Clear textboxes completely before attempting movement.

## General
- Default d-pad press moves about one tile.
- Long-hold d-pad presses can move many tiles and are essential for discovering larger walkable areas when the visible local probe seems too small.
- A advances dialog/confirms; B cancels; START opens menu.
- If a textbox is open, movement inputs do nothing; clear dialog before trying to walk.
- `B` can also dismiss some overworld textboxes cleanly; when an NPC conversation seems to loop forever, suspect that repeated `A` is finishing the text and immediately re-talking to the NPC.
- When screenshot and RAM disagree during normal gameplay, trust RAM for coordinates/state and screenshot for UI/dialog/world geometry.
- When stuck, trust RAM coordinates to map passable tiles. Probe systematically rather than repeating the same edge press.
- When a sprite is adjacent on the overworld, consider that it may be an NPC interaction target rather than geometry. Press `A` before overfitting to collision assumptions.
- When inside buildings, talking to the nearest person is not always the objective; identify the correct service NPC visually before interacting.
- If a map behaves impossibly, do not overfit to short-press geometry: test long holds in each direction before declaring corruption.
- For risky NPC/counter interaction areas, prefer single `A` presses and then clear text with controlled small batches instead of bulk dialog mashing.
- If a building entrance seems to leave the screen visually unchanged, try a short `wait`; some scripted shop greetings appear after a delay.
- Right after finishing a long story conversation, avoid immediate blind movement mashing; a stray `A` can re-open conversation with a nearby NPC (notably in Oak's Lab).
- Blank white/empty-looking screens are not always softlocks. On overworld transitions or certain route renders, first try a short `wait` before assuming failure.

## Current route
- We have Squirtle, currently Lv8.
- Oak's Parcel delivery is complete and Pokédex progression already happened.
- Squirtle has previously been healed in Viridian and in Pallet's right-side house; current HP may still be low enough to avoid unnecessary grass/trainer risk when traveling.
- Current overworld objective is to determine the next legal progression from Viridian City: verify whether the north Route 2 access is blocked, and if so pivot to the next early-game branch such as Route 22 / west exit.

## Pallet Town / opening event flow
- Player's House 1F exit: from the downstairs room, the lower corridor leads to the door warp; from about (2,7) on map 0x25, pressing `down` exits to Pallet Town.
- Pallet Town north exit / Oak trigger: the correct opening is on the east side of the north path. At about map 0x00, x=10, y=1, stepping north triggers Oak's interruption and escort to the lab.
- After the opening Oak escort has already happened once, standing at `(10,1)` at the north path is just a location marker; do not expect the same trigger again.
- Oak's Lab is the large building in the southwest/left side of Pallet Town; when returning with the parcel, go south from the north path and then left toward the lab rather than re-testing the old north-exit trigger.
- The right-side Pallet house can restore Squirtle via NPC interaction; this is a viable local recovery option if returning from Route 1 before reaching a Pokécenter.

## Oak's Lab expectations
- At the first lab visit, Oak speaks for multiple textboxes before the player can choose a starter. Expect to mash through dialog before movement/selection resumes.
- The starter selection likely requires walking to a Poké Ball and pressing `A`; the rival may intercept certain choices, and Oak may have additional text before/after pickup.
- Save-state/checkpoint would be valuable before the rival fight or before choosing a starter if harness checkpoints become reliable.
- Verified starter choice taken: Squirtle.
- On the nickname screen, pressing `START` immediately declines naming and keeps the default species name.
- First rival battle can be started by pressing `A` while facing the rival in the lab.
- When returning Oak's Parcel, expect several textboxes and likely Pokédex progression afterward; be ready to clear dialog fully before attempting movement.
- Verified parcel-delivery talk position: at about `(4,2)` facing right, pressing `A` talks to Oak and advances the delivery scene.
- Lower-right lab NPC around `(8,11)` facing up is only Oak's aide (`I study POKéMON as PROF.OAK's AIDE.`), not story progression.
- Top bookshelves repeatedly give `Crammed full of POKéMON books!`; don't confuse them with useful interactions.

## Route 1 notes
- At the south entrance around map 0x0c, (10,28), the north tile by the sign/fence is blocked; repeated `up` there does nothing.
- The bottom path is open to the east at least to about x=14.
- Stepping into grass on the east side can trigger wild encounters; confirmed Wild Rattata Lv3.
- In basic wild battles, repeated `A` is enough to progress when the cursor remains on FIGHT/TACKLE, but watch for menu state changes.
- Additional probe: from around (14,28), moving right reaches at least x=17 on the bottom strip, but `up` from x=14..17 at y=28 still does not advance north. This east edge is a grass/encounter trap, not the route forward.
- Moving left from the south area eventually reaches the clerk section around y=24.
- Around x=5..7, y=24, `up` is blocked by the rock/fence line.
- The sprite in that area is the Route 1 clerk NPC, not blocking geometry; pressing `A` starts the free sample-item conversation about Poké Balls/Poké Mart.
- Verified northbound route from mid-Route 1: from around `(5,18)`, go north to y=14, then east along the horizontal rock barrier to about x=14, go north through the opening near the trainer-side grass, continue north through the upper path, then loop west near the top fence line and go north at about `(11,0)` to warp into Viridian City.
- Southbound return route matters too: from Viridian City, the usable south gate/opening in the fence line is around x≈20, y≈30. Passing through it warps to Route 1 near `(11,0)`.
- Lower Route 1 can be misleading: from around `(12,31)`, repeated `down` can look like a wedge, but the true south exit is a bit left of there.
- Verified fix for the lower Route 1 south exit: moving left into the lower-left lane and then going down reaches Pallet Town. Do not keep retrying blocked `down` at `(12,31)`.
- Consolidated lower Route 1 choke lesson: the fence/rock line at y≈28 is blocked across many x-values on both the center and left side. Do not keep searching for a hidden north opening from the very bottom strip. To go north, first work upward through the western/middle lane before the known eastward bend.
- Upper Route 1 refinement: the grass pocket around `(10..13, 6..8)` is passable but dangerous and easy to overprobe. If stuck there, bias toward working west and north rather than repeatedly searching the east dead end near `(13,6)`.
- Final top-route finish reconfirmed: from around `(13,4)`, sequence `left,left,up,up,up,up,left,up` reached the Viridian City warp.

## Viridian City / Pokecenter notes
- From Route 1, entering Viridian City places the player near (21,35) at the south entrance.
- The city sign near about (16,17) reads: `VIRIDIAN CITY - The Eternally Green Paradise`.
- The east-side building labeled `MART` is the Poké Mart.
- There is an awkward horizontal fence/rock barrier around y=28 in the east/central city; many `up` attempts from below it are blocked.
- The usable crossing in that barrier is farther left than it first appears: from around (22,28), move left to about x=19, then north to about (19,25), then right toward the buildings.
- Viridian Pokémon Center entrance path found: from outside around (22,26), pressing `right, up` entered the Pokecenter (map 0x29) at about (3,7).
- Inside the Pokecenter, the standing man in the middle aisle is a normal advice NPC, not Nurse Joy. Do not waste turns re-clearing his long text when low HP healing is the goal.
- Bulk dialog mashing near counter NPCs is dangerous because it can immediately re-talk after a textbox closes.
- Verified top-aisle interaction map so far:
  - `(7,3)` facing left talks to the left-side top NPC.
  - `(9,3)` facing right talks to the right-side male NPC (`The receptionist told me...` / PC advice).
  - `(9,3)` facing up does nothing useful.
  - `(10,4)` facing up also talks to the right-side male NPC, not the healer.
  - `(11,3)` facing up talks to the Cable Club receptionist: `Welcome to the Cable Club! We're making preparations. Please wait.` This does **not** heal.
  - `(6,3)` facing up also does nothing useful.
  - Actual Nurse Joy / healer tile found: stand at `(3,3)` facing `up` and press `A`. This opens `HEAL / CANCEL`, and choosing `HEAL` restores the party normally.
- Viridian north-side city geometry consolidated:
  - The north fence line around `y≈14` is blocked across a broad span; repeated `up` from the obvious central/northeast fence tiles is wasted effort.
  - The far-right rock wall near `(35,16)` is also a dead end.
  - To reach the upper city from the eastern half, work **left to around `(20,16)` and then up**, rather than brute-forcing the fence at x≈24..35.
  - The small house/school entrance is around `(21,15)`.
  - The Gym frontage is reachable in the upper city; current known standing area near the Gym was around `(29,10)`.

## Viridian Mart notes
- Entering the Mart can leave the interior visible for a moment before the clerk's automatic parcel dialog appears; a short `wait` can be the right move instead of assuming nothing happened.
- Parcel event flow: after the greeting (`Hey! You came from PALLET TOWN?`), keep advancing text until receiving `OAK's PARCEL`, then clear the final line `Okay! Say hi to PROF.OAK for me!`.
- After the parcel dialog, the player is free inside the Mart around `(2,5)`. To exit, walk down to about `(2,7)` and then use `right, down` to line up with the door warp back to Viridian City.

## Battle heuristics
- Early wild fights can usually be handled with repeated `A` when the default cursor stays on FIGHT then TACKLE.
- However, HP matters: with Squirtle at low health, avoid extra grass encounters unless necessary for pathing.
- After a battle, there may be lingering result text; clear it fully before attempting movement.
- To run from a wild battle, use `down, right, A` from the main battle menu.
- Important timing lesson: battle-state/tool detection can clear before the visible battle text is gone. Trust the screenshot and keep advancing until the overworld is visibly back.
- If HP is dangerously low, do not stubbornly finish a wild battle just because Tackle is selected by default; fleeing is reliable and should be preferred.
- In wild battles with intro/send-out blank screens, first press `A` a couple of times to reach the actual command menu before trying the RUN inputs.

## Player's House 2F notes
- Prior wedge hypothesis was wrong.
- Correct fix: from bottom-left area, long-hold movement revealed a much larger room than short presses suggested.
- Verified successful escape path discovery:
  - from (1,7), long `right` reached about (5,7)
  - long `up` reached about (5,3)
  - short `right`, `up`, `right`, `up` reached about (6,1) near the visible staircase at top-right
  - pressing `right` there warped to Player's House 1F (map 0x25)
- Lesson: off-screen or partly off-screen walkable space can exist; visible bottom-edge dead ends are not sufficient evidence of corruption.

## Tooling / harness notes
- `save_checkpoint` may fail with a filesystem 500 error; treat checkpoints as unverified until a save succeeds.
- `list_checkpoints` previously returned none.
- Repeated mechanical sequences worth tooling:
  - intro progression / default-name acceptance
  - dialog mashing with moderate waits
  - systematic tile-probe paths that log coordinate changes
  - simple battle automation for early wild fights / post-battle text clearing
  - safe one-step NPC desk scanning in service buildings
  - wild-battle flee macro for low-HP travel
  - deterministic walk-path execution that stops/logs on battle start or map change
- `probe_path` is very useful for discovering large moves from long holds because it logs the resulting coordinates after each held direction.
- In-game SAVE is distinct from harness checkpoints and appears to work; it may allow `CONTINUE` after an external reset even when harness checkpoints are unavailable.

arguments.name: viridian_city_upper_layout_consolidated
arguments.content:
Viridian City upper layout consolidated: the obvious north fence line is a trap; access to the upper city comes from the left approach.

Key verified points:
- Entering from Route 1 places the player near the south side of town; central/east upward movement is blocked by a horizontal fence/rock barrier around y≈28.
- The crossing in that lower barrier is farther left than expected: from around `(22,28)`, move left to about `x=19`, then north to around `(19,25)`, then right toward town buildings.
- Near the top of town, the fence line around `y≈14` is blocked across a broad span, including much of the northeast side (`x≈24..35`). Repeated `up` there is wasted.
- The far-right rock wall around `(35,16)` is also a dead end.
- To reach the upper city from the eastern half, move left to around `(20,16)` and then go up.
- The small house/school entrance is around `(21,15)`.
- The Gym frontage is reachable from the upper city; a confirmed standing area in front of the Gym was around `(29,10)`.

Practical lesson:
- In Viridian, when north seems blocked, do not keep probing the obvious fence. Re-route left first, then come up into the upper lane.

data.summary:
- I made genuinely different progress instead of re-testing the Gym front: I walked all the way left across Viridian’s upper city from `(29,10)` to `(17,10)` and found an NPC by the tree wall.
- Interacting while facing left did nothing; facing **up** from directly below her starts dialog.
- Her text is **non-progression advice**, not a gate trigger. It mentions:
  - taking the **winding trail in Viridian Forest** to get to **Pewter City**
  - shopping in **Pewter City**
- I saved this as memory `viridian_northwest_lady_advice`.
- Important local geometry learned:

arguments.content: Viridian City northwest lady/tree-wall probe: at about `(17,10)` the tile(s) immediately left are blocked by the tree wall. Standing below her and trying to go left from `(17,10)` or `(17,12..14)` did not progress west; this spot is not the Route 22 exit. To continue exploring west, first move away from the tree-wall choke and approach from farther south or another lane.

arguments.content: Viridian City west exit found: from the northwest/upper-left side of Viridian, reaching about `(0,14)` and then pressing `left` warps to map `0x21` (decimal 33), which is Route 22. This confirms the actual west progression branch exists and the earlier northwest lady/tree-wall choke was just the wrong lane.

todos.text: Probe Route 22 carefully from the west exit and determine whether the rival battle triggers immediately
Heal Squirtle again before committing to risky grass/trainer probing if a convenient center path is available
In Viridian City, find the actual north exit / Route 2 opening now that the upper city and Gym area are mapped
If north is still blocked, explore the next available early-game branch (Route 22 rival/check or town interactions)
Escape the northwest lady/tree-wall dead end and find Viridian City's actual west exit to Route 22
Verify whether save_checkpoint works before relying on rewind for risky sections
Travel north through Route 1 back to Viridian City
Heal Squirtle immediately after the Oak's Lab parcel scene or sooner if route changes require it
Return to Pallet Town and deliver Oak's Parcel to Professor Oak
After parcel delivery, get the Pokédex / next story progression if not already granted
Heal Squirtle in Viridian City's Pokemon Center
Find the actual Nurse Joy/healing counter position left of the Cable Club desk
After healing, enter Viridian City's Mart and progress the parcel/event safely
Enter Viridian City's Mart / progress safely with low-HP Squirtle
Travel north on Route 1 toward Viridian City
From the clerk area on Route 1, probe the true northbound lane while avoiding unnecessary grass due to low HP
Clear the current wild Pidgey battle/result text on Route 1 and return to the overworld safely
Leave Oak's Lab and continue the early-game route after receiving the starter Pokemon
Win or complete the first rival battle in Oak's Lab
Progress Oak's Lab opening: finish dialog and obtain a starter Pokemon

data.summary:
Summary:
- I escaped the non-progress northwest-lady area by switching to genuinely different pathing instead of re-testing the same blocked left tiles.
- Key Viridian City discoveries by RAM-guided probing:
  - Around `(17,10)`, the immediate left side is blocked by the tree wall; that spot is not the west exit.
  - Moving southeast/southwest through other lanes opened the city’s western half.
  - From roughly `(9,18)`, going left to `x=4`, then up to `y=14`, then left to `x=1`, then down to `y=17`, then left to `x=0`, and finally pressing `left` from about `(0,14)` warped to **map 0x21 / Rou
milestones: Entered Route 22 for the first time

This lower road is not where the rival trigger/path upward happens. From the initial east-side area around `(36,6)`, moving down along the right wall reaches `(35,11)` and then `(35,14..15)`, which connects to this long lower road.

arguments.content:
# Strategy playbook

## Early boot / intro
- The harness may begin before the game has fully reached the title screen even if RAM reports Pallet Town. If the screen is blank white, `wait` a few seconds for the boot/logo sequence.
- Once at the title screen, use `start` or `a` to proceed to the main menu / intro.
- RAM location can be stale or meaningless during boot/title; trust the screenshot until actual gameplay begins.
- On the player naming screen, pressing `start` accepted the default name immediately (resulting default shown as AAAA in this ROM).
- On the rival naming screen, pressing `start` also accepted the default name; after that, mash `a` through Oak's remaining intro until gameplay resumes in the bedroom.
- After intro finishes, the player may spawn facing the TV/SNES and an `A` press can reopen the inspect textbox immediately. Clear textboxes completely before attempting movement.

## General
- Default d-pad press moves about one tile.
- Long-hold d-pad presses can move many tiles and are essential for discovering larger walkable areas when the visible local probe seems too small.
- A advances dialog/confirms; B cancels; START opens menu.
- If a textbox is open, movement inputs do nothing; clear dialog before trying to walk.
- `B` can also dismiss some overworld textboxes cleanly; when an NPC conversation seems to loop forever, suspect that repeated `A` is finishing the text and immediately re-talking to the NPC.
- When screenshot and RAM disagree during normal gameplay, trust RAM for coordinates/state and screenshot for UI/dialog/world geometry.
- When stuck, trust RAM coordinates to map passable tiles. Probe systematically rather than repeating the same edge press.
- When a sprite is adjacent on the overworld, consider that it may be an NPC interaction target rather than geometry. Press `A` before overfitting to collision assumptions.
- When inside buildings, talking to the nearest person is not always the objective; identify the correct service NPC visually before interacting.
- If a map behaves impossibly, do not overfit to short-press geometry: test long holds in each direction before declaring corruption.
- For risky NPC/counter interaction areas, prefer single `A` presses and then clear text with controlled small batches instead of bulk dialog mashing.
- If a building entrance seems to leave the screen visually unchanged, try a short `wait`; some scripted shop greetings appear after a delay.
- Right after finishing a long story conversation, avoid immediate blind movement mashing; a stray `A` can re-open conversation with a nearby NPC (notably in Oak's Lab).
- Blank white/empty-looking screens are not always softlocks. On overworld transitions or certain route renders, first try a short `wait` before assuming failure.

## Current route
- We have Squirtle, currently Lv8.
- Oak's Parcel delivery is complete and Pokédex progression already happened.
- Current objective is to determine the next legal progression from Viridian City: Route 22 rival check and/or Viridian north / Route 2 opening.
- Squirtle is currently low enough that unnecessary grass/trainer probing on Route 22 is risky; if Route 22 requires grass searching, consider healing first.

## Pallet Town / opening event flow
- Player's House 1F exit: from the downstairs room, the lower corridor leads to the door warp; from about (2,7) on map 0x25, pressing `down` exits to Pallet Town.
- Pallet Town north exit / Oak trigger: the correct opening is on the east side of the north path. At about map 0x00, x=10, y=1, stepping north triggers Oak's interruption and escort to the lab.
- After the opening Oak escort has already happened once, standing at `(10,1)` at the north path is just a location marker; do not expect the same trigger again.
- Oak's Lab is the large building in the southwest/left side of Pallet Town; when returning with the parcel, go south from the north path and then left toward the lab rather than re-testing the old north-exit trigger.
- The right-side Pallet house can restore Squirtle via NPC interaction; this is a viable local recovery option if returning from Route 1 before reaching a Pokécenter.

## Oak's Lab expectations
- At the first lab visit, Oak speaks for multiple textboxes before the player can choose a starter. Expect to mash through dialog before movement/selection resumes.
- The starter selection likely requires walking to a Poké Ball and pressing `A`; the rival may intercept certain choices, and Oak may have additional text before/after pickup.
- Save-state/checkpoint would be valuable before the rival fight or before choosing a starter if harness checkpoints become reliable.
- Verified starter choice taken: Squirtle.
- On the nickname screen, pressing `START` immediately declines naming and keeps the default species name.
- First rival battle can be started by pressing `A` while facing the rival in the lab.
- When returning Oak's Parcel, expect several textboxes and likely Pokédex progression afterward; be ready to clear dialog fully before attempting movement.
- Verified parcel-delivery talk position: at about `(4,2)` facing right, pressing `A` talks to Oak and advances the delivery scene.
- Lower-right lab NPC around `(8,11)` facing up is only Oak's aide (`I study POKéMON as PROF.OAK's AIDE.`), not story progression.
- Top bookshelves repeatedly give `Crammed full of POKéMON books!`; don't confuse them with useful interactions.

## Route 1 notes
- At the south entrance around map 0x0c, (10,28), the north tile by the sign/fence is blocked; repeated `up` there does nothing.
- The bottom path is open to the east at least to about x=14.
- Stepping into grass on the east side can trigger wild encounters; confirmed Wild Rattata Lv3.
- In basic wild battles, repeated `A` is enough to progress when the cursor remains on FIGHT/TACKLE, but watch for menu state changes.
- Additional probe: from around (14,28), moving right reaches at least x=17 on the bottom strip, but `up` from x=14..17 at y=28 still does not advance north. This east edge is a grass/encounter trap, not the route forward.
- Moving left from the south area eventually reaches the clerk section around y=24.
- Around x=5..7, y=24, `up` is blocked by the rock/fence line.
- The sprite in that area is the Route 1 clerk NPC, not blocking geometry; pressing `A` starts the free sample-item conversation about Poké Balls/Poké Mart.
- Verified northbound route from mid-Route 1: from around `(5,18)`, go north to y=14, then east along the horizontal rock barrier to about x=14, go north through the opening near the trainer-side grass, continue north through the upper path, then loop west near the top fence line and go north at about `(11,0)` to warp into Viridian City.
- Southbound return route matters too: from Viridian City, the usable south gate/opening in the fence line is around x≈20, y≈30. Passing through it warps to Route 1 near `(11,0)`.
- Lower Route 1 can be misleading: from around `(12,31)`, repeated `down` can look like a wedge, but the true south exit is a bit left of there.
- Verified fix for the lower Route 1 south exit: moving left into the lower-left lane and then going down reaches Pallet Town. Do not keep retrying blocked `down` at `(12,31)`.
- Consolidated lower Route 1 choke lesson: the fence/rock line at y≈28 is blocked across many x-values on both the center and left side. Do not keep searching for a hidden north opening from the very bottom strip. To go north, first work upward through the western/middle lane before the known eastward bend.
- Upper Route 1 refinement: the grass pocket around `(10..13, 6..8)` is passable but dangerous and easy to overprobe. If stuck there, bias toward working west and north rather than repeatedly searching the east dead end near `(13,6)`.
- Final top-route finish reconfirmed: from around `(13,4)`, sequence `left,left,up,up,up,up,left,up` reached the Viridian City warp.

## Viridian City / Pokecenter notes
- From Route 1, entering Viridian City places the player near (21,35) at the south entrance.
- The city sign near about (16,17) reads: `VIRIDIAN CITY - The Eternally Green Paradise`.
- The east-side building labeled `MART` is the Poké Mart.
- There is an awkward horizontal fence/rock barrier around y=28 in the east/central city; many `up` attempts from below it are blocked.
- The usable crossing in that barrier is farther left than it first appears: from around (22,28), move left to about x=19, then north to about (19,25), then right toward the buildings.
- Viridian Pokémon Center entrance path found: from outside around (22,26), pressing `right, up` entered the Pokecenter (map 0x29) at about (3,7).
- Inside the Pokecenter, the standing man in the middle aisle is a normal advice NPC, not Nurse Joy. Do not waste turns re-clearing his long text when low HP healing is the goal.
- Bulk dialog mashing near counter NPCs is dangerous because it can immediately re-talk after a textbox closes.
- Verified top-aisle interaction map so far:
  - `(7,3)` facing left talks to the left-side top NPC.
  - `(9,3)` facing right talks to the right-side male NPC (`The receptionist told me...` / PC advice).
  - `(9,3)` facing up does nothing useful.
  - `(10,4)` facing up also talks to the right-side male NPC, not the healer.
  - `(11,3)` facing up talks to the Cable Club receptionist: `Welcome to the Cable Club! We're making preparations. Please wait.` This does **not** heal.
  - `(6,3)` facing up also does nothing useful.
  - Actual Nurse Joy / healer tile found: stand at `(3,3)` facing `up` and press `A`. This opens `HEAL / CANCEL`, and choosing `HEAL` restores the party normally.
- Viridian north-side city geometry consolidated:
  - The north fence line around `y≈14` is blocked across a broad span; repeated `up` from the obvious central/northeast fence tiles is wasted effort.
  - The far-right rock wall near `(35,16)` is also a dead end.
  - To reach the upper city from the eastern half, work **left to around `(20,16)` and then up**, rather than brute-forcing the fence at x≈24..35.
  - The small house/school entrance is around `(21,15)`.
  - The Gym frontage is reachable in the upper city; current known standing area near the Gym was around `(29,10)`.

## Viridian Mart notes
- Entering the Mart can leave the interior visible for a moment before the clerk's automatic parcel dialog appears; a short `wait` can be the right move instead of assuming nothing happened.
- Parcel event flow: after the greeting (`Hey! You came from PALLET TOWN?`), keep advancing text until receiving `OAK's PARCEL`, then clear the final line `Okay! Say hi to PROF.OAK for me!`.
- After the parcel dialog, the player is free inside the Mart around `(2,5)`. To exit, walk down to about `(2,7)` and then use `right, down` to line up with the door warp back to Viridian City.

## Route 22 notes
- Entering Route 22 from Viridian west exit placed the player around `(39,6)` facing left.
- Immediate west travel works to about `(36,6)`, but farther left is blocked there; `up` is also blocked at that upper-right entry shelf.
- From around `(36,6)`, going down along the right wall reaches `(35,11)` and then the long lower road around y≈14-15.
- On the long lower road, horizontal travel is open across a huge span (tested from about `x=4` to `x=36` at `y=14`).
- The ledge/fence line above that road blocks `up` across many tested x positions (`x=4,6,7,8,12,15,18,20,21,22,29`). Do not keep brute-forcing `up` on the lower road.
- Current hypothesis: the rival trigger / true westward progression on Route 22 is likely **not** on this lower road; it probably requires finding a different ramp/opening from the east-side upper area or using grass/alternate lanes.

## Battle heuristics
- Early wild fights can usually be handled with repeated `A` when the default cursor stays on FIGHT then TACKLE.
- However, HP matters: with Squirtle at low health, avoid extra grass encounters unless necessary for pathing.
- After a battle, there may be lingering result text; clear it fully before attempting movement.
- To run from a wild battle, use `down, right, A` from the main battle menu.
- Important timing lesson: battle-state/tool detection can clear before the visible battle text is gone. Trust the screenshot and keep advancing until the overworld is visibly back.
- If HP is dangerously low, do not stubbornly finish a wild battle just because Tackle is selected by default; fleeing is reliable and should be preferred.
- In wild battles with intro/send-out blank screens, first press `A` a couple of times to reach the actual command menu before trying the RUN inputs.

## Player's House 2F notes
- Prior wedge hypothesis was wrong.
- Correct fix: from bottom-left area, long-hold movement revealed a much larger room than short presses suggested.
- Verified successful escape path discovery:
  - from (1,7), long `right` reached about (5,7)
  - long `up` reached about (5,3)
  - short `right`, `up`, `right`, `up` reached about (6,1) near the visible staircase at top-right
  - pressing `right` there warped to Player's House 1F (map 0x25)
- Lesson: off-screen or partly off-screen walkable space can exist; visible bottom-edge dead ends are not sufficient evidence of corruption.

## Tooling / harness notes
- `save_checkpoint` may fail with a filesystem 500 error; treat checkpoints as unverified until a save succeeds.
- `list_checkpoints` previously returned none.
- Repeated mechanical sequences worth tooling:
  - intro progression / default-name acceptance
  - dialog mashing with moderate waits
  - systematic tile-probe paths that log coordinate changes
  - simple battle automation for early wild fights / post-battle text clearing
  - safe one-step NPC desk scanning in service buildings
  - wild-battle flee macro for low-HP travel
  - deterministic walk-path execution that stops/logs on battle start or map change
- `probe_path` is very useful for discovering large moves from long holds because it logs the resulting coordinates after each held direction.
- In-game SAVE is distinct from harness checkpoints and appears to work; it may allow `CONTINUE` after an external reset even when harness checkpoints are unavailable.

todos.text: Heal Squirtle again before committing to risky Route 22 grass/trainer probing if a convenient center path is available
Probe Route 22 carefully from the west exit and determine whether the rival battle triggers immediately
In Viridian City, find the actual north exit / Route 2 opening now that the upper city and Gym area are mapped
Escape the northwest lady/tree-wall dead end and find Viridian City's actual west exit to Route 22
Verify whether save_checkpoint works before relying on rewind for risky sections
Travel north through Route 1 back to Viridian City
Heal Squirtle immediately after the Oak's Lab parcel scene or sooner if route changes require it
Return to Pallet Town and deliver Oak's Parcel to Professor Oak
After parcel delivery, get the Pokédex / next story progression if not already granted
Heal Squirtle in Viridian City's Pokemon Center
Find the actual Nurse Joy/healing counter position left of the Cable Club desk
After healing, enter Viridian City's Mart and progress the parcel/event safely
Enter Viridian City's Mart / progress safely with low-HP Squirtle
Travel north on Route 1 toward Viridian City
From the clerk area on Route 1, probe the true northbound lane while avoiding unnecessary grass due to low HP
Clear the current wild Pidgey battle/result text on Route 1 and return to the overworld safely
Leave Oak's Lab and continue the early-game route after receiving the starter Pokemon
Win or complete the first rival battle in Oak's Lab
Progress Oak's Lab opening: finish dialog and obtain a starter Pokemon

- I made genuinely different Route 22 progress instead of repeating the same blocked tiles.
- Mapped the route enough to learn an important negative result:
  - Entry from Viridian west exit is around `(39,6)`.
  - West from there reaches about `(36,6)`, but farther left is blocked on that upper-right shelf.
  - Going down from that area reaches a long lower road around `y≈14-15`.
  - That lower road is open horizontally across a very large span, tested from roughly `x=4` to `x=36`.
  - But `up` is blocked all along the tested lower road at many positions (`x=4,6,7,8,12,15,18,20,21,22

arguments.name: route22_lower_road_full_span_and_edges
arguments.content:
Route 22 lower-road consolidation: the broad road at y=14-15 is traversable essentially end-to-end, but it is a dead probe lane for upward progression.

Verified from RAM-guided probing:
- Starting around `(21,14)`, moving right works continuously to at least `(37,14)`.
- At the far east, `right` beyond about `x=37` is blocked by the cliff/wall.
- `up` is blocked from the lower road at many positions including roughly `x=6,8,10,11,12,13,14,15,20,21,26,35,37` on `y=14`, plus `x=34` and nearby on `y=15`.
- At the far west, moving left works to about `(2,14)`, but farther left is blocked by the map edge/wall.
- Near the far west, moving `down` from `(2,14)` reaches `(2,15)`, but further `down` is blocked; this does not open a new lane.
- Near the far east, moving `down` from `(37,14)` reaches `(37,15)`, and moving left along `y=15` is possible, but `up` remains blocked there too.

Conclusion: the visible lower road on Route 22 is not the route to rival/progression. Continuing to brute-force `up` from this road is wasted effort.
