data.name: update_playbook

arguments.content:
# Strategy playbook

## Early boot / intro
- The harness may begin before the game has fully reached the title screen even if RAM reports Pallet Town. If the screen is blank white, `wait` a few seconds for the boot/logo sequence.
- Once at the title screen, use `start` or `a` to proceed to the main menu / intro.
- RAM location can be stale or meaningless during boot/title; trust the screenshot until actual gameplay begins.
- On the player naming screen, pressing `start` accepted the default name immediately (resulting default shown as AAAA in this ROM).
- On the rival naming screen, pressing `start` also accepted the default name; after that, mash `a` through Oak's remaining intro until gameplay resumes in the bedroom.
- After intro finishes, the player may spawn facing the TV/SNES and an `A` press can reopen the inspect textbox immediately. Clear textboxes completely before attempting movement.

## General
- Default d-pad press moves about one tile.
- Long-hold d-pad presses can move many tiles and are essential for discovering larger walkable areas when the visible local probe seems too small.
- A advances dialog/confirms; B cancels; START opens menu.
- If a textbox is open, movement inputs do nothing; clear dialog before trying to walk.
- `B` can also dismiss some overworld textboxes cleanly; when an NPC conversation seems to loop forever, suspect that repeated `A` is finishing the text and immediately re-talking to the NPC.
- When screenshot and RAM disagree during normal gameplay, trust RAM for coordinates/state and screenshot for UI/dialog/world geometry.
- When stuck, trust RAM coordinates to map passable tiles. Probe systematically rather than repeating the same edge press.
- When a sprite is adjacent on the overworld, consider that it may be an NPC interaction target rather than geometry. Press `A` before overfitting to collision assumptions.
- When inside buildings, talking to the nearest person is not always the objective; identify the correct service NPC visually before interacting.
- If a map behaves impossibly, do not overfit to short-press geometry: test long holds in each direction before declaring corruption.
- For risky NPC/counter interaction areas, prefer single `A` presses and then clear text with controlled small batches instead of bulk dialog mashing.
- If a building entrance seems to leave the screen visually unchanged, try a short `wait`; some scripted shop greetings appear after a delay.
- Right after finishing a long story conversation, avoid immediate blind movement mashing; a stray `A` can re-open conversation with a nearby NPC (notably in Oak's Lab).
- Blank white/empty-looking screens are not always softlocks. On overworld transitions or certain route renders, first try a short `wait` before assuming failure.

## Current route
- We have Squirtle, currently Lv8.
- Oak's Parcel delivery is complete and Pokédex progression already happened.
- Current objective has shifted away from brute-forcing Route 22 lower road. Heal if needed, then find Viridian's true north/Route 2 exit and only revisit Route 22 if we discover the correct upper-lane approach.
- Squirtle is low HP enough that unnecessary grass/trainer probing is risky; prefer returning to the Pokécenter before further exploration.

## Pallet Town / opening event flow
- Player's House 1F exit: from the downstairs room, the lower corridor leads to the door warp; from about (2,7) on map 0x25, pressing `down` exits to Pallet Town.
- Pallet Town north exit / Oak trigger: the correct opening is on the east side of the north path. At about map 0x00, x=10, y=1, stepping north triggers Oak's interruption and escort to the lab.
- After the opening Oak escort has already happened once, standing at `(10,1)` at the north path is just a location marker; do not expect the same trigger again.
- Oak's Lab is the large building in the southwest/left side of Pallet Town; when returning with the parcel, go south from the north path and then left toward the lab rather than re-testing the old north-exit trigger.
- The right-side Pallet house can restore Squirtle via NPC interaction; this is a viable local recovery option if returning from Route 1 before reaching a Pokécenter.

## Oak's Lab expectations
- At the first lab visit, Oak speaks for multiple textboxes before the player can choose a starter. Expect to mash through dialog before movement/selection resumes.
- The starter selection likely requires walking to a Poké Ball and pressing `A`; the rival may intercept certain choices, and Oak may have additional text before/after pickup.
- Save-state/checkpoint would be valuable before the rival fight or before choosing a starter if harness checkpoints become reliable.
- Verified starter choice taken: Squirtle.
- On the nickname screen, pressing `START` immediately declines naming and keeps the default species name.
- First rival battle can be started by pressing `A` while facing the rival in the lab.
- When returning Oak's Parcel, expect several textboxes and likely Pokédex progression afterward; be ready to clear dialog fully before attempting movement.
- Verified parcel-delivery talk position: at about `(4,2)` facing right, pressing `A` talks to Oak and advances the delivery scene.
- Lower-right lab NPC around `(8,11)` facing up is only Oak's aide (`I study POKéMON as PROF.OAK's AIDE.`), not story progression.
- Top bookshelves repeatedly give `Crammed full of POKéMON books!`; don't confuse them with useful interactions.

## Route 1 notes
- At the south entrance around map 0x0c, (10,28), the north tile by the sign/fence is blocked; repeated `up` there does nothing.
- The bottom path is open to the east at least to about x=14.
- Stepping into grass on the east side can trigger wild encounters; confirmed Wild Rattata Lv3.
- In basic wild battles, repeated `A` is enough to progress when the cursor remains on FIGHT/TACKLE, but watch for menu state changes.
- Additional probe: from around (14,28), moving right reaches at least x=17 on the bottom strip, but `up` from x=14..17 at y=28 still does not advance north. This east edge is a grass/encounter trap, not the route forward.
- Moving left from the south area eventually reaches the clerk section around y=24.
- Around x=5..7, y=24, `up` is blocked by the rock/fence line.
- The sprite in that area is the Route 1 clerk NPC, not blocking geometry; pressing `A` starts the free sample-item conversation about Poké Balls/Poké Mart.
- Verified northbound route from mid-Route 1: from around `(5,18)`, go north to y=14, then east along the horizontal rock barrier to about x=14, go north through the opening near the trainer-side grass, continue north through the upper path, then loop west near the top fence line and go north at about `(11,0)` to warp into Viridian City.
- Southbound return route matters too: from Viridian City, the usable south gate/opening in the fence line is around x≈20, y≈30. Passing through it warps to Route 1 near `(11,0)`.
- Lower Route 1 can be misleading: from around `(12,31)`, repeated `down` can look like a wedge, but the true south exit is a bit left of there.
- Verified fix for the lower Route 1 south exit: moving left into the lower-left lane and then going down reaches Pallet Town. Do not keep retrying blocked `down` at `(12,31)`.
- Consolidated lower Route 1 choke lesson: the fence/rock line at y≈28 is blocked across many x-values on both the center and left side. Do not keep searching for a hidden north opening from the very bottom strip. To go north, first work upward through the western/middle lane before the known eastward bend.
- Upper Route 1 refinement: the grass pocket around `(10..13, 6..8)` is passable but dangerous and easy to overprobe. If stuck there, bias toward working west and north rather than repeatedly searching the east dead end near `(13,6)`.
- Final top-route finish reconfirmed: from around `(13,4)`, sequence `left,left,up,up,up,up,left,up` reached the Viridian City warp.

## Viridian City / Pokecenter notes
- From Route 1, entering Viridian City places the player near (21,35) at the south entrance.
- The city sign near about (16,17) reads: `VIRIDIAN CITY - The Eternally Green Paradise`.
- The east-side building labeled `MART` is the Poké Mart.
- There is an awkward horizontal fence/rock barrier around y=28 in the east/central city; many `up` attempts from below it are blocked.
- The usable crossing in that barrier is farther left than it first appears: from around (22,28), move left to about x=19, then north to about (19,25), then right toward the buildings.
- Viridian Pokémon Center entrance path found: from outside around (22,26), pressing `right, up` entered the Pokecenter (map 0x29) at about (3,7).
- Inside the Pokecenter, the standing man in the middle aisle is a normal advice NPC, not Nurse Joy. Do not waste turns re-clearing his long text when low HP healing is the goal.
- Bulk dialog mashing near counter NPCs is dangerous because it can immediately re-talk after a textbox closes.
- Verified top-aisle interaction map so far:
  - `(7,3)` facing left talks to the left-side top NPC.
  - `(9,3)` facing right talks to the right-side male NPC (`The receptionist told me...` / PC advice).
  - `(9,3)` facing up does nothing useful.
  - `(10,4)` facing up also talks to the right-side male NPC, not the healer.
  - `(11,3)` facing up talks to the Cable Club receptionist: `Welcome to the Cable Club! We're making preparations. Please wait.` This does **not** heal.
  - `(6,3)` facing up also does nothing useful.
  - Actual Nurse Joy / healer tile found: stand at `(3,3)` facing `up` and press `A`. This opens `HEAL / CANCEL`, and choosing `HEAL` restores the party normally.
- Viridian north-side city geometry consolidated:
  - The north fence line around `y≈14` is blocked across a broad span; repeated `up` from the obvious central/northeast fence tiles is wasted effort.
  - The far-right rock wall near `(35,16)` is also a dead end.
  - To reach the upper city from the eastern half, work **left to around `(20,16)` and then up**, rather than brute-forcing the fence at x≈24..35.
  - The small house/school entrance is around `(21,15)`.
  - The Gym frontage is reachable in the upper city; current known standing area near the Gym was around `(29,10)`.

## Viridian Mart notes
- Entering the Mart can leave the interior visible for a moment before the clerk's automatic parcel dialog appears; a short `wait` can be the right move instead of assuming nothing happened.
- Parcel event flow: after the greeting (`Hey! You came from PALLET TOWN?`), keep advancing text until receiving `OAK's PARCEL`, then clear the final line `Okay! Say hi to PROF.OAK for me!`.
- After the parcel dialog, the player is free inside the Mart around `(2,5)`. To exit, walk down to about `(2,7)` and then use `right, down` to line up with the door warp back to Viridian City.

## Route 22 notes
- Entering Route 22 from Viridian west exit placed the player around `(39,6)` facing left.
- Immediate west travel works to about `(36,6)`, but farther left is blocked there; `up` is also blocked at that upper-right entry shelf.
- From around `(36,6)`, going down along the right wall reaches `(35,11)` and then the long lower road around y≈14-15.
- On the long lower road, horizontal travel is open across a huge span (tested from about `x=2` to `x=37` at `y=14`, plus much of `y=15`).
- The ledge/fence line above that road blocks `up` across many tested x positions (`x=4,6,7,8,10,11,12,13,14,15,18,20,21,22,26,29,35,37`). Do not keep brute-forcing `up` on the lower road.
- The far west of the lower road around `(2,14)` is also a dead end; farther left is blocked, and only a one-tile drop to `(2,15)` is available with no continuation.
- The far east around `(37,14..15)` is likewise a dead end against the cliff wall.
- Current hypothesis strengthened: the rival trigger / true westward progression on Route 22 is **not** on the lower road. It likely requires a different upper approach from near the Viridian-side entrance, or we should deprioritize Route 22 and find Viridian Forest / Route 2 first.

## Battle heuristics
- Early wild fights can usually be handled with repeated `A` when the default cursor stays on FIGHT then TACKLE.
- However, HP matters: with Squirtle at low health, avoid extra grass encounters unless necessary for pathing.
- After a battle, there may be lingering result text; clear it fully before attempting movement.
- To run from a wild battle, use `down, right, A` from the main battle menu.
- Important timing lesson: battle-state/tool detection can clear before the visible battle text is gone. Trust the screenshot and keep advancing until the overworld is visibly back.
- If HP is dangerously low, do not stubbornly finish a wild battle just because Tackle is selected by default; fleeing is reliable and should be preferred.
- In wild battles with intro/send-out blank screens, first press `A` a couple of times to reach the actual command menu before trying the RUN inputs.

## Player's House 2F notes
- Prior wedge hypothesis was wrong.
- Correct fix: from bottom-left area, long-hold movement revealed a much larger room than short presses suggested.
- Verified successful escape path discovery:
  - from (1,7), long `right` reached about (5,7)
  - long `up` reached about (5,3)
  - short `right`, `up`, `right`, `up` reached about (6,1) near the visible staircase at top-right
  - pressing `right` there warped to Player's House 1F (map 0x25)
- Lesson: off-screen or partly off-screen walkable space can exist; visible bottom-edge dead ends are not sufficient evidence of corruption.

## Tooling / harness notes
- `save_checkpoint` may fail with a filesystem 500 error; treat checkpoints as unverified until a save succeeds.
- `list_checkpoints` previously returned none.
- Repeated mechanical sequences worth tooling:
  - intro progression / default-name acceptance
  - dialog mashing with moderate waits
  - systematic tile-probe paths that log coordinate changes
  - simple battle automation for early wild fights / post-battle text clearing
  - safe one-step NPC desk scanning in service buildings
  - wild-battle flee macro for low-HP travel
  - deterministic walk-path execution that stops/logs on battle start or map change
- `probe_path` is very useful for discovering large moves from long holds because it logs the resulting coordinates after each held direction.
- In-game SAVE is distinct from harness checkpoints and appears to work; it may allow `CONTINUE` after an external reset even when harness checkpoints are unavailable.

arguments.content: Route 22 upper approach found: from the dead-end east lower road at about `(37,14)`, alternating back west and probing up revealed a climb on the right side. Confirmed path segment: `(37,14)` -> left to `(33,14)`, then up to `(33,13)` and `(33,12)`, left to `(32,12)`, up to `(32,11)`, left to `(31,11)`, up to `(31,10)`, left to `(30,10)`, up to `(30,9)` and `(30,8)`. This reaches an upper grassy corridor along the cliff; lower road was not the only accessible layer.

arguments.name: route22_upper_grass_corridor_probe
arguments.content: Route 22 upper-right corridor probe: there is a second accessible layer above the lower road, reached from the east side. Confirmed climb from the lower road: from about `(37,14)`, move left to `(33,14)`, then up to `(33,13)` and `(33,12)`, left to `(32,12)`, up to `(32,11)`, left to `(31,11)`, up to `(31,10)`, left to `(30,10)`, then up to `(30,9)` and `(30,8)`. Local geometry at the top: `(30,8)` cannot go left; `(31,8)` and `(32,8)` are reachable to the right; `up` from `(32,8)` is blocked. Grass encounters can knock the player slightly south on return, e.g. to `(33,11)` or `(33,12)`, but the upper corridor remains reachable by redoing `up,up,left,left,left,up,up,right,right` from there. This is genuinely different from the lower-road dead end, but no westward continuation has yet been found from `(30..32,8)`.

data.name: update_todos

todos.text: Return from Route 22 to Viridian City and heal Squirtle at the Pokecenter
In Viridian City, find the actual north exit / Route 2 opening now that the upper city and Gym area are mapped
If Viridian north progression remains unclear, revisit Route 22 from the upper entrance shelf rather than the dead lower road
Escape the northwest lady/tree-wall dead end and find Viridian City's actual west exit to Route 22
Verify whether save_checkpoint works before relying on rewind for risky sections
Travel north through Route 1 back to Viridian City
Heal Squirtle immediately after the Oak's Lab parcel scene or sooner if route changes require it
Return to Pallet Town and deliver Oak's Parcel to Professor Oak
After parcel delivery, get the Pokédex / next story progression if not already granted
Heal Squirtle in Viridian City's Pokemon Center
Find the actual Nurse Joy/healing counter position left of the Cable Club desk
After healing, enter Viridian City's Mart and progress the parcel/event safely
Enter Viridian City's Mart / progress safely with low-HP Squirtle
Travel north on Route 1 toward Viridian City
From the clerk area on Route 1, probe the true northbound lane while avoiding unnecessary grass due to low HP
Clear the current wild Pidgey battle/result text on Route 1 and return to the overworld safely
Leave Oak's Lab and continue the early-game route after receiving the starter Pokemon
Win or complete the first rival battle in Oak's Lab
Progress Oak's Lab opening: finish dialog and obtain a starter Pokemon

data.summary:
I made genuinely different Route 22 progress instead of re-testing the same dead lower road:

- Confirmed the lower road’s far east end at `(37,14)` is a dead end, but from there I found an **upper-right climb**.
- Verified climb path:
  - from `(37,14)` go left to about `(33,14)`
  - then `up` to `(33,13)` and `(33,12)`
  - `left` to `(32,12)`
  - `up` to `(32,11)`
  - `left` to `(31,11)`
  - `up` to `(31,10)`
  - `left` to `(30,10)`
  - `up` to `(30,9)` and `(30,8)`
- This reaches a **different upper grassy corridor** along the cliff, proving Route 22 is not just the useless lower road.

Wha

arguments.name: route22_upper_grass_box_probe
arguments.content:
Route 22 upper-right grass box probe: around the upper corridor reached near `(31..33, 8..11)`, there is a small enclosed grass patch bounded by cliff on the left and a vertical post line on the right. Confirmed local movement:
- From about `(31,8)`, `up -> (31,7)` works but right is blocked there.
- From `(30,9)`, left/up are blocked; only the lower area is open.
- The lower exit from this grass box leads back down to the long lower road around `(32,12)` / `(32,14)`.
- Far-east lower road remains a dead end at about `(37,15)` against the cliff.
- Re-climbing from the east dead end back into the upper area reaches about `(33,9)`, but `up` from `(33,8)` is blocked and repeated right presses are also blocked.
Conclusion: this specific upper-right grass enclosure is not the route forward by itself; it just loops back to the lower road.

- I re-tested the **far east lower road** and confirmed again it dead-ends at about **(37,15)**.
- I then re-climbed into the **upper-right Route 22 grass area** and mapped it more carefully:

  - You can move around inside it and drop back to the lower road.
  - But the apparent top/right continuations are blocked.
  - Reaching about **(33,8)-(33,9)** still does **not*

arguments.name: route22_enclosed_grass_box_and_lower_return
arguments.content: Route 22 upper-right enclosed grass box + safe return: from the far east lower road at about `(37,14)`, the climb `left x4, up,up, left, up, left, up, left, up,up` reaches the upper-right grass area around `(30..33, 8..12)`. Local probe confirms it is enclosed: e.g. from `(33,9)`, right is blocked; from `(33,13)`, right is blocked; from `(31,8)`, repeated right is blocked. The area connects back down to the lower road via the left/down side, and a safe exit from the box is `left, down x6` from around `(32,9)` to return to the lower road near `(31,15)`. No westward/story progression found there; it appears to be a small dead-end grass patch.

data.name: update_playbook

arguments.content:
# Strategy playbook

## Early boot / intro
- The harness may begin before the game has fully reached the title screen even if RAM reports Pallet Town. If the screen is blank white, `wait` a few seconds for the boot/logo sequence.
- Once at the title screen, use `start` or `a` to proceed to the main menu / intro.
- RAM location can be stale or meaningless during boot/title; trust the screenshot until actual gameplay begins.
- On the player naming screen, pressing `start` accepted the default name immediately (resulting default shown as AAAA in this ROM).
- On the rival naming screen, pressing `start` also accepted the default name; after that, mash `a` through Oak's remaining intro until gameplay resumes in the bedroom.
- After intro finishes, the player may spawn facing the TV/SNES and an `A` press can reopen the inspect textbox immediately. Clear textboxes completely before attempting movement.

## General
- Default d-pad press moves about one tile.
- Long-hold d-pad presses can move many tiles and are essential for discovering larger walkable areas when the visible local probe seems too small.
- A advances dialog/confirms; B cancels; START opens menu.
- If a textbox is open, movement inputs do nothing; clear dialog before trying to walk.
- `B` can also dismiss some overworld textboxes cleanly; when an NPC conversation seems to loop forever, suspect that repeated `A` is finishing the text and immediately re-talking to the NPC.
- When screenshot and RAM disagree during normal gameplay, trust RAM for coordinates/state and screenshot for UI/dialog/world geometry.
- When stuck, trust RAM coordinates to map passable tiles. Probe systematically rather than repeating the same edge press.
- When a sprite is adjacent on the overworld, consider that it may be an NPC interaction target rather than geometry. Press `A` before overfitting to collision assumptions.
- When inside buildings, talking to the nearest person is not always the objective; identify the correct service NPC visually before interacting.
- If a map behaves impossibly, do not overfit to short-press geometry: test long holds in each direction before declaring corruption.
- For risky NPC/counter interaction areas, prefer single `A` presses and then clear text with controlled small batches instead of bulk dialog mashing.
- If a building entrance seems to leave the screen visually unchanged, try a short `wait`; some scripted shop greetings appear after a delay.
- Right after finishing a long story conversation, avoid immediate blind movement mashing; a stray `A` can re-open conversation with a nearby NPC (notably in Oak's Lab).
- Blank white/empty-looking screens are not always softlocks. On overworld transitions or certain route renders, first try a short `wait` before assuming failure.

## Current route
- We have Squirtle, currently Lv8.
- Oak's Parcel delivery is complete and Pokédex progression already happened.
- Current objective has shifted away from brute-forcing Route 22 lower road. Heal if needed, then find Viridian's true north/Route 2 exit and only revisit Route 22 if we discover the correct upper-lane approach.
- Squirtle is low HP enough that unnecessary grass/trainer probing is risky; prefer returning to the Pokécenter before further exploration.
- Route 22 current verdict: the far-east upper grass climb is a small enclosed dead-end patch, not story progression.

## Pallet Town / opening event flow
- Player's House 1F exit: from the downstairs room, the lower corridor leads to the door warp; from about (2,7) on map 0x25, pressing `down` exits to Pallet Town.
- Pallet Town north exit / Oak trigger: the correct opening is on the east side of the north path. At about map 0x00, x=10, y=1, stepping north triggers Oak's interruption and escort to the lab.
- After the opening Oak escort has already happened once, standing at `(10,1)` at the north path is just a location marker; do not expect the same trigger again.
- Oak's Lab is the large building in the southwest/left side of Pallet Town; when returning with the parcel, go south from the north path and then left toward the lab rather than re-testing the old north-exit trigger.
- The right-side Pallet house can restore Squirtle via NPC interaction; this is a viable local recovery option if returning from Route 1 before reaching a Pokécenter.

## Oak's Lab expectations
- At the first lab visit, Oak speaks for multiple textboxes before the player can choose a starter. Expect to mash through dialog before movement/selection resumes.
- The starter selection likely requires walking to a Poké Ball and pressing `A`; the rival may intercept certain choices, and Oak may have additional text before/after pickup.
- Save-state/checkpoint would be valuable before the rival fight or before choosing a starter if harness checkpoints become reliable.
- Verified starter choice taken: Squirtle.
- On the nickname screen, pressing `START` immediately declines naming and keeps the default species name.
- First rival battle can be started by pressing `A` while facing the rival in the lab.
- When returning Oak's Parcel, expect several textboxes and likely Pokédex progression afterward; be ready to clear dialog fully before attempting movement.
- Verified parcel-delivery talk position: at about `(4,2)` facing right, pressing `A` talks to Oak and advances the delivery scene.
- Lower-right lab NPC around `(8,11)` facing up is only Oak's aide (`I study POKéMON as PROF.OAK's AIDE.`), not story progression.
- Top bookshelves repeatedly give `Crammed full of POKéMON books!`; don't confuse them with useful interactions.

## Route 1 notes
- At the south entrance around map 0x0c, (10,28), the north tile by the sign/fence is blocked; repeated `up` there does nothing.
- The bottom path is open to the east at least to about x=14.
- Stepping into grass on the east side can trigger wild encounters; confirmed Wild Rattata Lv3.
- In basic wild battles, repeated `A` is enough to progress when the cursor remains on FIGHT/TACKLE, but watch for menu state changes.
- Additional probe: from around (14,28), moving right reaches at least x=17 on the bottom strip, but `up` from x=14..17 at y=28 still does not advance north. This east edge is a grass/encounter trap, not the route forward.
- Moving left from the south area eventually reaches the clerk section around y=24.
- Around x=5..7, y=24, `up` is blocked by the rock/fence line.
- The sprite in that area is the Route 1 clerk NPC, not blocking geometry; pressing `A` starts the free sample-item conversation about Poké Balls/Poké Mart.
- Verified northbound route from mid-Route 1: from around `(5,18)`, go north to y=14, then east along the horizontal rock barrier to about x=14, go north through the opening near the trainer-side grass, continue north through the upper path, then loop west near the top fence line and go north at about `(11,0)` to warp into Viridian City.
- Southbound return route matters too: from Viridian City, the usable south gate/opening in the fence line is around x≈20, y≈30. Passing through it warps to Route 1 near `(11,0)`.
- Lower Route 1 can be misleading: from around `(12,31)`, repeated `down` can look like a wedge, but the true south exit is a bit left of there.
- Verified fix for the lower Route 1 south exit: moving left into the lower-left lane and then going down reaches Pallet Town. Do not keep retrying blocked `down` at `(12,31)`.
- Consolidated lower Route 1 choke lesson: the fence/rock line at y≈28 is blocked across many x-values on both the center and left side. Do not keep searching for a hidden north opening from the very bottom strip. To go north, first work upward through the western/middle lane before the known eastward bend.
- Upper Route 1 refinement: the grass pocket around `(10..13, 6..8)` is passable but dangerous and easy to overprobe. If stuck there, bias toward working west and north rather than repeatedly searching the east dead end near `(13,6)`.
- Final top-route finish reconfirmed: from around `(13,4)`, sequence `left,left,up,up,up,up,left,up` reached the Viridian City warp.

## Viridian City / Pokecenter notes
- From Route 1, entering Viridian City places the player near (21,35) at the south entrance.
- The city sign near about (16,17) reads: `VIRIDIAN CITY - The Eternally Green Paradise`.
- The east-side building labeled `MART` is the Poké Mart.
- There is an awkward horizontal fence/rock barrier around y=28 in the east/central city; many `up` attempts from below it are blocked.
- The usable crossing in that barrier is farther left than it first appears: from around (22,28), move left to about x=19, then north to about (19,25), then right toward the buildings.
- Viridian Pokémon Center entrance path found: from outside around (22,26), pressing `right, up` entered the Pokecenter (map 0x29) at about (3,7).
- Inside the Pokecenter, the standing man in the middle aisle is a normal advice NPC, not Nurse Joy. Do not waste turns re-clearing his long text when low HP healing is the goal.
- Bulk dialog mashing near counter NPCs is dangerous because it can immediately re-talk after a textbox closes.
- Verified top-aisle interaction map so far:
  - `(7,3)` facing left talks to the left-side top NPC.
  - `(9,3)` facing right talks to the right-side male NPC (`The receptionist told me...` / PC advice).
  - `(9,3)` facing up does nothing useful.
  - `(10,4)` facing up also talks to the right-side male NPC, not the healer.
  - `(11,3)` facing up talks to the Cable Club receptionist: `Welcome to the Cable Club! We're making preparations. Please wait.` This does **not** heal.
  - `(6,3)` facing up also does nothing useful.
  - Actual Nurse Joy / healer tile found: stand at `(3,3)` facing `up` and press `A`. This opens `HEAL / CANCEL`, and choosing `HEAL` restores the party normally.
- Viridian north-side city geometry consolidated:
  - The north fence line around `y≈14` is blocked across a broad span; repeated `up` from the obvious central/northeast fence tiles is wasted effort.
  - The far-right rock wall near `(35,16)` is also a dead end.
  - To reach the upper city from the eastern half, work **left to around `(20,16)` and then up**, rather than brute-forcing the fence at x≈24..35.
  - The small house/school entrance is around `(21,15)`.
  - The Gym frontage is reachable in the upper city; current known standing area near the Gym was around `(29,10)`.

## Viridian Mart notes
- Entering the Mart can leave the interior visible for a moment before the clerk's automatic parcel dialog appears; a short `wait` can be the right move instead of assuming nothing happened.
- Parcel event flow: after the greeting (`Hey! You came from PALLET TOWN?`), keep advancing text until receiving `OAK's PARCEL`, then clear the final line `Okay! Say hi to PROF.OAK for me!`.
- After the parcel dialog, the player is free inside the Mart around `(2,5)`. To exit, walk down to about `(2,7)` and then use `right, down` to line up with the door warp back to Viridian City.

## Route 22 notes
- Entering Route 22 from Viridian west exit placed the player around `(39,6)` facing left.
- Immediate west travel works to about `(36,6)`, but farther left is blocked there; `up` is also blocked at that upper-right entry shelf.
- From around `(36,6)`, going down along the right wall reaches `(35,11)` and then the long lower road around y≈14-15.
- On the long lower road, horizontal travel is open across a huge span (tested from about `x=2` to `x=37` at `y=14`, plus much of `y=15`).
- The ledge/fence line above that road blocks `up` across many tested x positions (`x=4,6,7,8,10,11,12,13,14,15,18,20,21,22,26,29,35,37`). Do not keep brute-forcing `up` on the lower road.
- The far west of the lower road around `(2,14)` is also a dead end; farther left is blocked, and only a one-tile drop to `(2,15)` is available with no continuation.
- The far east around `(37,14..15)` is likewise a dead end against the cliff wall.
- Upper-right climb confirmed from the far east lower road: from about `(37,14)`, go `left x4, up,up, left, up, left, up, left, up,up` to reach around `(30,8)`.
- That upper-right area is only a small enclosed grass box around roughly `(30..33, 8..12)`. Its apparent right/top continuations are blocked, and it only drops back to the lower road. It is not current progression.
- Current hypothesis strengthened: the rival trigger / true westward progression on Route 22 is **not** on the lower road or the enclosed far-east grass box. It likely requires a different upper approach from near the Viridian-side entrance, or we should deprioritize Route 22 and find Viridian Forest / Route 2 first.

## Battle heuristics
- Early wild fights can usually be handled with repeated `A` when the default cursor stays on FIGHT then TACKLE.
- However, HP matters: with Squirtle at low health, avoid extra grass encounters unless necessary for pathing.
- After a battle, there may be lingering result text; clear it fully before attempting movement.
- To run from a wild battle, use `down, right, A` from the main battle menu.
- Important timing lesson: battle-state/tool detection can clear before the visible battle text is gone. Trust the screenshot and keep advancing until the overworld is visibly back.
- If HP is dangerously low, do not stubbornly finish a wild battle just because Tackle is selected by default; fleeing is reliable and should be preferred.
- In wild battles with intro/send-out blank screens, first press `A` a couple of times to reach the actual command menu before trying the RUN inputs.

## Player's House 2F notes
- Prior wedge hypothesis was wrong.
- Correct fix: from bottom-left area, long-hold movement revealed a much larger room than short presses suggested.
- Verified successful escape path discovery:
  - from (1,7), long `right` reached about (5,7)
  - long `up` reached about (5,3)
  - short `right`, `up`, `right`, `up` reached about (6,1) near the visible staircase at top-right
  - pressing `right` there warped to Player's House 1F (map 0x25)
- Lesson: off-screen or partly off-screen walkable space can exist; visible bottom-edge dead ends are not sufficient evidence of corruption.

## Tooling / harness notes
- `save_checkpoint` may fail with a filesystem 500 error; treat checkpoints as unverified until a save succeeds.
- `list_checkpoints` previously returned none.
- Repeated mechanical sequences worth tooling:
  - intro progression / default-name acceptance
  - dialog mashing with moderate waits
  - systematic tile-probe paths that log coordinate changes
  - simple battle automation for early wild fights / post-battle text clearing
  - safe one-step NPC desk scanning in service buildings
  - wild-battle flee macro for low-HP travel
  - deterministic walk-path execution that stops/logs on battle start or map change
- `probe_path` is very useful for discovering large moves from long holds because it logs the resulting coordinates after each held direction.
- In-game SAVE is distinct from harness checkpoints and appears to work; it may allow `CONTINUE` after an external reset even when harness checkpoints are unavailable.

arguments.name: route22_upper_strip_and_grass_entry
arguments.content: Route 22 upper-right approach refinement: from the east lower road, the climb can continue to a narrow upper strip near `(33,10..12)` beside a vertical line of tree stumps. From `(34,14)`, `left -> 33,14`, then `up -> 33,13`, `up -> 33,12`, `up -> 33,11`, `up -> 33,10` works. At `(33,10)` the apparent moves left/up/right were blocked in the probe, and stepping around this area triggered a wild Nidoran♂. After fleeing, the player was left around `(34,12)` on the clear tile just east of the stump line. This strengthens that the east-side upper Route 22 area is grass/encounter-heavy and not obvious story progression.

data.name: update_playbook

arguments.content:
# Strategy playbook

## Early boot / intro
- The harness may begin before the game has fully reached the title screen even if RAM reports Pallet Town. If the screen is blank white, `wait` a few seconds for the boot/logo sequence.
- Once at the title screen, use `start` or `a` to proceed to the main menu / intro.
- RAM location can be stale or meaningless during boot/title; trust the screenshot until actual gameplay begins.
- On the player naming screen, pressing `start` accepted the default name immediately (resulting default shown as AAAA in this ROM).
- On the rival naming screen, pressing `start` also accepted the default name; after that, mash `a` through Oak's remaining intro until gameplay resumes in the bedroom.
- After intro finishes, the player may spawn facing the TV/SNES and an `A` press can reopen the inspect textbox immediately. Clear textboxes completely before attempting movement.

## General
- Default d-pad press moves about one tile.
- Long-hold d-pad presses can move many tiles and are essential for discovering larger walkable areas when the visible local probe seems too small.
- A advances dialog/confirms; B cancels; START opens menu.
- If a textbox is open, movement inputs do nothing; clear dialog before trying to walk.
- `B` can also dismiss some overworld textboxes cleanly; when an NPC conversation seems to loop forever, suspect that repeated `A` is finishing the text and immediately re-talking to the NPC.
- When screenshot and RAM disagree during normal gameplay, trust RAM for coordinates/state and screenshot for UI/dialog/world geometry.
- When stuck, trust RAM coordinates to map passable tiles. Probe systematically rather than repeating the same edge press.
- When a sprite is adjacent on the overworld, consider that it may be an NPC interaction target rather than geometry. Press `A` before overfitting to collision assumptions.
- When inside buildings, talking to the nearest person is not always the objective; identify the correct service NPC visually before interacting.
- If a map behaves impossibly, do not overfit to short-press geometry: test long holds in each direction before declaring corruption.
- For risky NPC/counter interaction areas, prefer single `A` presses and then clear text with controlled small batches instead of bulk dialog mashing.
- If a building entrance seems to leave the screen visually unchanged, try a short `wait`; some scripted shop greetings appear after a delay.
- Right after finishing a long story conversation, avoid immediate blind movement mashing; a stray `A` can re-open conversation with a nearby NPC (notably in Oak's Lab).
- Blank white/empty-looking screens are not always softlocks. On overworld transitions or certain route renders, first try a short `wait` before assuming failure.

## Current route
- We have Squirtle, currently Lv8.
- Oak's Parcel delivery is complete and Pokédex progression already happened.
- Current priority: return from Route 22 and heal if HP is low, then solve Viridian's true north/Route 2 exit.
- Route 22 has not yielded clear progression yet; treat it as secondary until Viridian north is exhausted.

## Pallet Town / opening event flow
- Player's House 1F exit: from the downstairs room, the lower corridor leads to the door warp; from about (2,7) on map 0x25, pressing `down` exits to Pallet Town.
- Pallet Town north exit / Oak trigger: the correct opening is on the east side of the north path. At about map 0x00, x=10, y=1, stepping north triggers Oak's interruption and escort to the lab.
- After the opening Oak escort has already happened once, standing at `(10,1)` at the north path is just a location marker; do not expect the same trigger again.
- Oak's Lab is the large building in the southwest/left side of Pallet Town; when returning with the parcel, go south from the north path and then left toward the lab rather than re-testing the old north-exit trigger.
- The right-side Pallet house can restore Squirtle via NPC interaction; this is a viable local recovery option if returning from Route 1 before reaching a Pokécenter.

## Oak's Lab expectations
- At the first lab visit, Oak speaks for multiple textboxes before the player can choose a starter. Expect to mash through dialog before movement/selection resumes.
- The starter selection likely requires walking to a Poké Ball and pressing `A`; the rival may intercept certain choices, and Oak may have additional text before/after pickup.
- Save-state/checkpoint would be valuable before the rival fight or before choosing a starter if harness checkpoints become reliable.
- Verified starter choice taken: Squirtle.
- On the nickname screen, pressing `START` immediately declines naming and keeps the default species name.
- First rival battle can be started by pressing `A` while facing the rival in the lab.
- When returning Oak's Parcel, expect several textboxes and likely Pokédex progression afterward; be ready to clear dialog fully before attempting movement.
- Verified parcel-delivery talk position: at about `(4,2)` facing right, pressing `A` talks to Oak and advances the delivery scene.
- Lower-right lab NPC around `(8,11)` facing up is only Oak's aide (`I study POKéMON as PROF.OAK's AIDE.`), not story progression.
- Top bookshelves repeatedly give `Crammed full of POKéMON books!`; don't confuse them with useful interactions.

## Route 1 notes
- At the south entrance around map 0x0c, (10,28), the north tile by the sign/fence is blocked; repeated `up` there does nothing.
- The bottom path is open to the east at least to about x=14.
- Stepping into grass on the east side can trigger wild encounters; confirmed Wild Rattata Lv3.
- In basic wild battles, repeated `A` is enough to progress when the cursor remains on FIGHT/TACKLE, but watch for menu state changes.
- Additional probe: from around (14,28), moving right reaches at least x=17 on the bottom strip, but `up` from x=14..17 at y=28 still does not advance north. This east edge is a grass/encounter trap, not the route forward.
- Moving left from the south area eventually reaches the clerk section around y=24.
- Around x=5..7, y=24, `up` is blocked by the rock/fence line.
- The sprite in that area is the Route 1 clerk NPC, not blocking geometry; pressing `A` starts the free sample-item conversation about Poké Balls/Poké Mart.
- Verified northbound route from mid-Route 1: from around `(5,18)`, go north to y=14, then east along the horizontal rock barrier to about x=14, go north through the opening near the trainer-side grass, continue north through the upper path, then loop west near the top fence line and go north at about `(11,0)` to warp into Viridian City.
- Southbound return route matters too: from Viridian City, the usable south gate/opening in the fence line is around x≈20, y≈30. Passing through it warps to Route 1 near `(11,0)`.
- Lower Route 1 can be misleading: from around `(12,31)`, repeated `down` can look like a wedge, but the true south exit is a bit left of there.
- Verified fix for the lower Route 1 south exit: moving left into the lower-left lane and then going down reaches Pallet Town. Do not keep retrying blocked `down` at `(12,31)`.
- Consolidated lower Route 1 choke lesson: the fence/rock line at y≈28 is blocked across many x-values on both the center and left side. Do not keep searching for a hidden north opening from the very bottom strip. To go north, first work upward through the western/middle lane before the known eastward bend.
- Upper Route 1 refinement: the grass pocket around `(10..13, 6..8)` is passable but dangerous and easy to overprobe. If stuck there, bias toward working west and north rather than repeatedly searching the east dead end near `(13,6)`.
- Final top-route finish reconfirmed: from around `(13,4)`, sequence `left,left,up,up,up,up,left,up` reached the Viridian City warp.

## Viridian City / Pokecenter notes
- From Route 1, entering Viridian City places the player near (21,35) at the south entrance.
- The city sign near about (16,17) reads: `VIRIDIAN CITY - The Eternally Green Paradise`.
- The east-side building labeled `MART` is the Poké Mart.
- There is an awkward horizontal fence/rock barrier around y=28 in the east/central city; many `up` attempts from below it are blocked.
- The usable crossing in that barrier is farther left than it first appears: from around (22,28), move left to about x=19, then north to about (19,25), then right toward the buildings.
- Viridian Pokémon Center entrance path found: from outside around (22,26), pressing `right, up` entered the Pokecenter (map 0x29) at about (3,7).
- Inside the Pokecenter, the standing man in the middle aisle is a normal advice NPC, not Nurse Joy. Do not waste turns re-clearing his long text when low HP healing is the goal.
- Bulk dialog mashing near counter NPCs is dangerous because it can immediately re-talk after a textbox closes.
- Verified top-aisle interaction map so far:
  - `(7,3)` facing left talks to the left-side top NPC.
  - `(9,3)` facing right talks to the right-side male NPC (`The receptionist told me...` / PC advice).
  - `(9,3)` facing up does nothing useful.
  - `(10,4)` facing up also talks to the right-side male NPC, not the healer.
  - `(11,3)` facing up talks to the Cable Club receptionist: `Welcome to the Cable Club! We're making preparations. Please wait.` This does **not** heal.
  - `(6,3)` facing up also does nothing useful.
  - Actual Nurse Joy / healer tile found: stand at `(3,3)` facing `up` and press `A`. This opens `HEAL / CANCEL`, and choosing `HEAL` restores the party normally.
- Viridian north-side city geometry consolidated:
  - The north fence line around `y≈14` is blocked across a broad span; repeated `up` from the obvious central/northeast fence tiles is wasted effort.
  - The far-right rock wall near `(35,16)` is also a dead end.
  - To reach the upper city from the eastern half, work **left to around `(20,16)` and then up**, rather than brute-forcing the fence at x≈24..35.
  - The small house/school entrance is around `(21,15)`.
  - The Gym frontage is reachable in the upper city; current known standing area near the Gym was around `(29,10)`.

## Viridian Mart notes
- Entering the Mart can leave the interior visible for a moment before the clerk's automatic parcel dialog appears; a short `wait` can be the right move instead of assuming nothing happened.
- Parcel event flow: after the greeting (`Hey! You came from PALLET TOWN?`), keep advancing text until receiving `OAK's PARCEL`, then clear the final line `Okay! Say hi to PROF.OAK for me!`.
- After the parcel dialog, the player is free inside the Mart around `(2,5)`. To exit, walk down to about `(2,7)` and then use `right, down` to line up with the door warp back to Viridian City.

## Route 22 notes
- Entering Route 22 from Viridian west exit placed the player around `(39,6)` facing left.
- Immediate west travel works to about `(36,6)`, but farther left is blocked there; `up` is also blocked at that upper-right entry shelf.
- From around `(36,6)`, going down along the right wall reaches `(35,11)` and then the long lower road around y≈14-15.
- On the long lower road, horizontal travel is open across a huge span (tested from about `x=2` to `x=37` at `y=14`, plus much of `y=15`).
- The ledge/fence line above that road blocks `up` across many tested x positions (`x=4,6,7,8,10,11,12,13,14,15,18,20,21,22,26,29,35,37`). Do not keep brute-forcing `up` on the lower road.
- The far west of the lower road around `(2,14)` is also a dead end; farther left is blocked, and only a one-tile drop to `(2,15)` is available with no continuation.
- The far east around `(37,14..15)` is likewise a dead end against the cliff wall.
- Upper-right climb confirmed from the far east lower road: from about `(37,14)`, go `left x4, up,up, left, up, left, up, left, up,up` to reach around `(30,8)`.
- That upper-right area is only a small enclosed grass box around roughly `(30..33, 8..12)`. Its apparent right/top continuations are blocked, and it only drops back to the lower road. It is not current progression.
- Additional refinement: there is also a narrow upper strip by the east-side stump line near `(33,10..12)`, but probing there still led only to encounter-prone grass and blocked edges, not an exit.
- Current hypothesis strengthened: the rival trigger / true westward progression on Route 22 is **not** on the lower road or the enclosed far-east grass box. It likely requires a different upper approach from near the Viridian-side entrance, or we should deprioritize Route 22 and find Viridian Forest / Route 2 first.

## Battle heuristics
- Early wild fights can usually be handled with repeated `A` when the default cursor stays on FIGHT then TACKLE.
- However, HP matters: with Squirtle at low health, avoid extra grass encounters unless necessary for pathing.
- After a battle, there may be lingering result text; clear it fully before attempting movement.
- To run from a wild battle, use `down, right, A` from the main battle menu.
- Important timing lesson: battle-state/tool detection can clear before the visible battle text is gone. Trust the screenshot and keep advancing until the overworld is visibly back.
- If HP is dangerously low, do not stubbornly finish a wild battle just because Tackle is selected by default; fleeing is reliable and should be preferred.
- In wild battles with intro/send-out blank screens, first press `A` a couple of times to reach the actual command menu before trying the RUN inputs.

## Player's House 2F notes
- Prior wedge hypothesis was wrong.
- Correct fix: from bottom-left area, long-hold movement revealed a much larger room than short presses suggested.
- Verified successful escape path discovery:
  - from (1,7), long `right` reached about (5,7)
  - long `up` reached about (5,3)
  - short `right`, `up`, `right`, `up` reached about (6,1) near the visible staircase at top-right
  - pressing `right` there warped to Player's House 1F (map 0x25)
- Lesson: off-screen or partly off-screen walkable space can exist; visible bottom-edge dead ends are not sufficient evidence of corruption.

## Tooling / harness notes
- `save_checkpoint` may fail with a filesystem 500 error; treat checkpoints as unverified until a save succeeds.
- `list_checkpoints` previously returned none.
- Repeated mechanical sequences worth tooling:
  - intro progression / default-name acceptance
  - dialog mashing with moderate waits
  - systematic tile-probe paths that log coordinate changes
  - simple battle automation for early wild fights / post-battle text clearing
  - safe one-step NPC desk scanning in service buildings
  - wild-battle flee macro for low-HP travel
  - deterministic walk-path execution that stops/logs on battle start or map change
- `probe_path` is very useful for discovering large moves from long holds because it logs the resulting coordinates after each held direction.
- In-game SAVE is distinct from harness checkpoints and appears to work; it may allow `CONTINUE` after an external reset even when harness checkpoints are unavailable.

data.result: playbook updated (15065 chars); it will be in your prompt from the next turn on

data.summary:
- I refined Route 22 knowledge and confirmed the east-side upper approach still isn’t yielding progression.
- New durable note saved: `route22_upper_strip_and_grass_entry`
  - There’s a narrow upper strip near `(33,10..12)` by the stump line.
  - It still leads into encounter-prone grass / blocked edges, not an exit.
  - A wild Nidoran♂ appeared there; I fled successfully.
- Current post-flee state from the tool log:
  - Route 22 at about `(34,12)`
  - Squirtle Lv8 at `12/26 HP`
  - not in battle
- I updated the playbook and todos to reflect the stronger conclusion:
  - Route 22 is deprioritiz

data.name: update_playbook

arguments.content:
# Strategy playbook

## Early boot / intro
- The harness may begin before the game has fully reached the title screen even if RAM reports Pallet Town. If the screen is blank white, `wait` a few seconds for the boot/logo sequence.
- Once at the title screen, use `start` or `a` to proceed to the main menu / intro.
- RAM location can be stale or meaningless during boot/title; trust the screenshot until actual gameplay begins.
- On the player naming screen, pressing `start` accepted the default name immediately (resulting default shown as AAAA in this ROM).
- On the rival naming screen, pressing `start` also accepted the default name; after that, mash `a` through Oak's remaining intro until gameplay resumes in the bedroom.
- After intro finishes, the player may spawn facing the TV/SNES and an `A` press can reopen the inspect textbox immediately. Clear textboxes completely before attempting movement.

## General
- Default d-pad press moves about one tile.
- Long-hold d-pad presses can move many tiles and are essential for discovering larger walkable areas when the visible local probe seems too small.
- A advances dialog/confirms; B cancels; START opens menu.
- If a textbox is open, movement inputs do nothing; clear dialog before trying to walk.
- `B` can also dismiss some overworld textboxes cleanly; when an NPC conversation seems to loop forever, suspect that repeated `A` is finishing the text and immediately re-talking to the NPC.
- When screenshot and RAM disagree during normal gameplay, trust RAM for coordinates/state and screenshot for UI/dialog/world geometry.
- When stuck, trust RAM coordinates to map passable tiles. Probe systematically rather than repeating the same edge press.
- When a sprite is adjacent on the overworld, consider that it may be an NPC interaction target rather than geometry. Press `A` before overfitting to collision assumptions.
- When inside buildings, talking to the nearest person is not always the objective; identify the correct service NPC visually before interacting.
- If a map behaves impossibly, do not overfit to short-press geometry: test long holds in each direction before declaring corruption.
- For risky NPC/counter interaction areas, prefer single `A` presses and then clear text with controlled small batches instead of bulk dialog mashing.
- If a building entrance seems to leave the screen visually unchanged, try a short `wait`; some scripted shop greetings appear after a delay.
- Right after finishing a long story conversation, avoid immediate blind movement mashing; a stray `A` can re-open conversation with a nearby NPC (notably in Oak's Lab).
- Blank white/empty-looking screens are not always softlocks. On overworld transitions or certain route renders, first try a short `wait` before assuming failure.
- Blank black/empty-looking screens during encounter-prone outdoor probing can also just be battle transition/render lag; check battle flag/screenshot before assuming a softlock.

## Current route
- We have Squirtle, currently Lv8.
- Oak's Parcel delivery is complete and Pokédex progression already happened.
- Route 22 has not yielded progression; current priority is to get back to Viridian City safely, heal, and then find the true north exit / Route 2 / Viridian Forest path.
- Current live danger state at the end of turn 90 setup: Route 22 wild battle vs Rattata with Squirtle at 9/26 HP and visible `used TACKLE!` text. Next action turn should prioritize safely clearing/fleeing battle text, then returning to Viridian rather than further Route 22 exploration.

## Pallet Town / opening event flow
- Player's House 1F exit: from the downstairs room, the lower corridor leads to the door warp; from about (2,7) on map 0x25, pressing `down` exits to Pallet Town.
- Pallet Town north exit / Oak trigger: the correct opening is on the east side of the north path. At about map 0x00, x=10, y=1, stepping north triggers Oak's interruption and escort to the lab.
- After the opening Oak escort has already happened once, standing at `(10,1)` at the north path is just a location marker; do not expect the same trigger again.
- Oak's Lab is the large building in the southwest/left side of Pallet Town; when returning with the parcel, go south from the north path and then left toward the lab rather than re-testing the old north-exit trigger.
- The right-side Pallet house can restore Squirtle via NPC interaction; this is a viable local recovery option if returning from Route 1 before reaching a Pokécenter.

## Oak's Lab expectations
- At the first lab visit, Oak speaks for multiple textboxes before the player can choose a starter. Expect to mash through dialog before movement/selection resumes.
- The starter selection likely requires walking to a Poké Ball and pressing `A`; the rival may intercept certain choices, and Oak may have additional text before/after pickup.
- Save-state/checkpoint would be valuable before the rival fight or before choosing a starter if harness checkpoints become reliable.
- Verified starter choice taken: Squirtle.
- On the nickname screen, pressing `START` immediately declines naming and keeps the default species name.
- First rival battle can be started by pressing `A` while facing the rival in the lab.
- When returning Oak's Parcel, expect several textboxes and likely Pokédex progression afterward; be ready to clear dialog fully before attempting movement.
- Verified parcel-delivery talk position: at about `(4,2)` facing right, pressing `A` talks to Oak and advances the delivery scene.
- Lower-right lab NPC around `(8,11)` facing up is only Oak's aide (`I study POKéMON as PROF.OAK's AIDE.`), not story progression.
- Top bookshelves repeatedly give `Crammed full of POKéMON books!`; don't confuse them with useful interactions.

## Route 1 notes
- At the south entrance around map 0x0c, (10,28), the north tile by the sign/fence is blocked; repeated `up` there does nothing.
- The bottom path is open to the east at least to about x=14.
- Stepping into grass on the east side can trigger wild encounters; confirmed Wild Rattata Lv3.
- In basic wild battles, repeated `A` is enough to progress when the cursor remains on FIGHT/TACKLE, but watch for menu state changes.
- Additional probe: from around (14,28), moving right reaches at least x=17 on the bottom strip, but `up` from x=14..17 at y=28 still does not advance north. This east edge is a grass/encounter trap, not the route forward.
- Moving left from the south area eventually reaches the clerk section around y=24.
- Around x=5..7, y=24, `up` is blocked by the rock/fence line.
- The sprite in that area is the Route 1 clerk NPC, not blocking geometry; pressing `A` starts the free sample-item conversation about Poké Balls/Poké Mart.
- Verified northbound route from mid-Route 1: from around `(5,18)`, go north to y=14, then east along the horizontal rock barrier to about x=14, go north through the opening near the trainer-side grass, continue north through the upper path, then loop west near the top fence line and go north at about `(11,0)` to warp into Viridian City.
- Southbound return route matters too: from Viridian City, the usable south gate/opening in the fence line is around x≈20, y≈30. Passing through it warps to Route 1 near `(11,0)`.
- Lower Route 1 can be misleading: from around `(12,31)`, repeated `down` can look like a wedge, but the true south exit is a bit left of there.
- Verified fix for the lower Route 1 south exit: moving left into the lower-left lane and then going down reaches Pallet Town. Do not keep retrying blocked `down` at `(12,31)`.
- Consolidated lower Route 1 choke lesson: the fence/rock line at y≈28 is blocked across many x-values on both the center and left side. Do not keep searching for a hidden north opening from the very bottom strip. To go north, first work upward through the western/middle lane before the known eastward bend.
- Upper Route 1 refinement: the grass pocket around `(10..13, 6..8)` is passable but dangerous and easy to overprobe. If stuck there, bias toward working west and north rather than repeatedly searching the east dead end near `(13,6)`.
- Final top-route finish reconfirmed: from around `(13,4)`, sequence `left,left,up,up,up,up,left,up` reached the Viridian City warp.

## Viridian City / Pokecenter notes
- From Route 1, entering Viridian City places the player near (21,35) at the south entrance.
- The city sign near about (16,17) reads: `VIRIDIAN CITY - The Eternally Green Paradise`.
- The east-side building labeled `MART` is the Poké Mart.
- There is an awkward horizontal fence/rock barrier around y=28 in the east/central city; many `up` attempts from below it are blocked.
- The usable crossing in that barrier is farther left than it first appears: from around (22,28), move left to about x=19, then north to about (19,25), then right toward the buildings.
- Viridian Pokémon Center entrance path found: from outside around (22,26), pressing `right, up` entered the Pokecenter (map 0x29) at about (3,7).
- Inside the Pokecenter, the standing man in the middle aisle is a normal advice NPC, not Nurse Joy. Do not waste turns re-clearing his long text when low HP healing is the goal.
- Bulk dialog mashing near counter NPCs is dangerous because it can immediately re-talk after a textbox closes.
- Verified top-aisle interaction map so far:
  - `(7,3)` facing left talks to the left-side top NPC.
  - `(9,3)` facing right talks to the right-side male NPC (`The receptionist told me...` / PC advice).
  - `(9,3)` facing up does nothing useful.
  - `(10,4)` facing up also talks to the right-side male NPC, not the healer.
  - `(11,3)` facing up talks to the Cable Club receptionist: `Welcome to the Cable Club! We're making preparations. Please wait.` This does **not** heal.
  - `(6,3)` facing up also does nothing useful.
  - Actual Nurse Joy / healer tile found: stand at `(3,3)` facing `up` and press `A`. This opens `HEAL / CANCEL`, and choosing `HEAL` restores the party normally.
- Viridian north-side city geometry consolidated:
  - The north fence line around `y≈14` is blocked across a broad span; repeated `up` from the obvious central/northeast fence tiles is wasted effort.
  - The far-right rock wall near `(35,16)` is also a dead end.
  - To reach the upper city from the eastern half, work **left to around `(20,16)` and then up**, rather than brute-forcing the fence at x≈24..35.
  - The small house/school entrance is around `(21,15)`.
  - The Gym frontage is reachable in the upper city; current known standing area near the Gym was around `(29,10)`.
- The northwest woman in Viridian is only hint text: she mentions the winding trail in Viridian Forest leading to Pewter. She is not an event trigger.
- West exit to Route 22 is around the far-left side: from the city’s western lanes, getting to about `(0,14)` and pressing `left` warps to Route 22.
- Strong current hypothesis for main progression: Viridian Forest / Route 2 is accessed from the upper city or north side, not through Route 22.

## Viridian Mart notes
- Entering the Mart can leave the interior visible for a moment before the clerk's automatic parcel dialog appears; a short `wait` can be the right move instead of assuming nothing happened.
- Parcel event flow: after the greeting (`Hey! You came from PALLET TOWN?`), keep advancing text until receiving `OAK's PARCEL`, then clear the final line `Okay! Say hi to PROF.OAK for me!`.
- After the parcel dialog, the player is free inside the Mart around `(2,5)`. To exit, walk down to about `(2,7)` and then use `right, down` to line up with the door warp back to Viridian City.

## Route 22 notes
- Entering Route 22 from Viridian west exit placed the player around `(39,6)` facing left.
- Immediate west travel works to about `(36,6)`, but farther left is blocked there; `up` is also blocked at that upper-right entry shelf.
- From around `(36,6)`, going down along the right wall reaches `(35,11)` and then the long lower road around y≈14-15.
- On the long lower road, horizontal travel is open across a huge span (tested from about `x=2` to `x=37` at `y=14`, plus much of `y=15`).
- The ledge/fence line above that road blocks `up` across many tested x positions (`x=4,6,7,8,10,11,12,13,14,15,18,20,21,22,26,29,35,37`). Do not keep brute-forcing `up` on the lower road.
- The far west of the lower road around `(2,14)` is also a dead end; farther left is blocked, and only a one-tile drop to `(2,15)` is available with no continuation.
- The far east around `(37,14..15)` is likewise a dead end against the cliff wall.
- Upper-right climb confirmed from the far east lower road: from about `(37,14)`, go `left x4, up,up, left, up, left, up, left, up,up` to reach around `(30,8)`.
- That upper-right area is only a small enclosed grass box around roughly `(30..33, 8..12)`. Its apparent right/top continuations are blocked, and it only drops back to the lower road. It is not current progression.
- Additional refinement: there is also a narrow upper strip by the east-side stump line near `(33,10..12)`, but probing there still led only to encounter-prone grass and blocked edges, not an exit.
- Strong consolidation: everything verified so far on Route 22 points to a non-progressing optional area at this stage. Use it only if deliberately revisiting the rival trigger from a new upper-entry approach; otherwise deprioritize it in favor of Viridian north / Route 2.
- Because Route 22 grass repeatedly triggers wilds while giving little information, avoid exploratory grass steps there when HP is low.

## Battle heuristics
- Early wild fights can usually be handled with repeated `A` when the default cursor stays on FIGHT then TACKLE.
- However, HP matters: with Squirtle at low health, avoid extra grass encounters unless necessary for pathing.
- After a battle, there may be lingering result text; clear it fully before attempting movement.
- To run from a wild battle, use `down, right, A` from the main battle menu.
- Important timing lesson: battle-state/tool detection can clear before the visible battle text is gone. Trust the screenshot and keep advancing until the overworld is visibly back.
- If HP is dangerously low, do not stubbornly finish a wild battle just because Tackle is selected by default; fleeing is reliable and should be preferred.
- In wild battles with intro/send-out blank screens, first press `A` a couple of times to reach the actual command menu before trying the RUN inputs.
- If the screen currently shows move-result text like `used TACKLE!`, the next required input is usually to clear text with `A`; RUN is not selectable again until the textboxes resolve back to a command menu.

## Player's House 2F notes
- Prior wedge hypothesis was wrong.
- Correct fix: from bottom-left area, long-hold movement revealed a much larger room than short presses suggested.
- Verified successful escape path discovery:
  - from (1,7), long `right` reached about (5,7)
  - long `up` reached about (5,3)
  - short `right`, `up`, `right`, `up` reached about (6,1) near the visible staircase at top-right
  - pressing `right` there warped to Player's House 1F (map 0x25)
- Lesson: off-screen or partly off-screen walkable space can exist; visible bottom-edge dead ends are not sufficient evidence of corruption.

## Tooling / harness notes
- `save_checkpoint` may fail with a filesystem 500 error; treat checkpoints as unverified until a save succeeds.
- `list_checkpoints` previously returned none.
- Repeated mechanical sequences worth tooling:
  - intro progression / default-name acceptance
  - dialog mashing with moderate waits
  - systematic tile-probe paths that log coordinate changes
  - simple battle automation for early wild fights / post-battle text clearing
  - safe one-step NPC desk scanning in service buildings
  - wild-battle flee macro for low-HP travel
  - deterministic walk-path execution that stops/logs on battle start or map change
- `probe_path` is very useful for discovering large moves from long holds because it logs the resulting coordinates after each held direction.
- In-game SAVE is distinct from harness checkpoints and appears to work; it may allow `CONTINUE` after an external reset even when harness checkpoints are unavailable.

arguments.content:
# Strategy playbook

## Early boot / intro
- If the screen is blank white at boot, `wait` a few seconds for logo/title.
- At title/menu, `start` or `a` proceeds.
- RAM location can be stale during boot/title; trust screenshot until gameplay begins.
- On player and rival naming screens, pressing `start` accepts the default name.
- After intro, if spawned facing TV/SNES, an `A` press can reopen the inspect textbox; clear text before moving.

## General
- Default d-pad press moves about one tile.
- Long-hold d-pad presses can reveal larger walkable areas than short probes suggest.
- A advances/confirms; B cancels; START opens menu.
- If a textbox is open, movement does nothing; clear text first.
- `B` can dismiss some overworld textboxes cleanly; repeated `A` can accidentally re-talk to NPCs.
- Trust RAM for coordinates/state and screenshot for UI/world geometry.
- Probe systematically with RAM coordinates instead of retrying the same blocked tile.
- Adjacent sprites may be NPCs, not geometry; try `A` before assuming collision.
- In buildings, identify the correct service NPC before mashing.
- If a map seems impossible, test long holds before assuming corruption.
- For risky counters/NPCs, prefer single `A` presses plus controlled `B` clears.
- Blank white or black screens during transitions/outdoor probing are not always softlocks; often wait or check battle flag first.

## Current route
- We have Squirtle Lv8.
- Oak's Parcel delivery and Pokédex progression are complete.
- Route 22 has not yielded progression; priority is to return to Viridian, heal, then find Route 2 / Viridian Forest.
- Current live danger state: Route 22 wild battle vs Rattata with Squirtle at 9/26 HP and visible `used TACKLE!` text. Next action turn should first clear/flee battle safely, then head back to Viridian instead of exploring more Route 22 grass.

## Pallet Town / Oak sequence
- Player's House 1F exit: from downstairs, the lower corridor leads to the door warp; around (2,7) on map 0x25, pressing `down` exits.
- Pallet north exit trigger: the opening is on the east side of the north path; around map 0x00, x=10, y=1, stepping north triggers Oak.
- After that event already happened, do not expect the same trigger again.
- Oak's Lab is the southwest/left large building in Pallet.
- The right-side Pallet house can heal Squirtle via NPC interaction.

## Oak's Lab
- Early lab scene requires multiple textboxes before choosing a starter.
- Verified starter: Squirtle.
- On nickname screen, `START` declines naming.
- First rival battle can be started by pressing `A` while facing the rival.
- Oak parcel return: talking to Oak around `(4,2)` facing right advances the delivery scene.
- Lower-right lab NPC around `(8,11)` is only Oak's aide.
- Top bookshelves just say `Crammed full of POKéMON books!`.

## Route 1
- South entrance around map 0x0c, (10,28): the north tile by sign/fence is blocked.
- East-side bottom strip reaches at least x=17 but `up` there is still blocked; this is a grass/encounter trap.
- Moving left from the south area reaches the clerk around y=24; the clerk is an NPC, not geometry.
- Verified northbound route: from about `(5,18)`, go north to y=14, east along the rock barrier to about x=14, north through the opening, continue north, then loop west near the top fence and go north at about `(11,0)` to warp into Viridian.
- Viridian south return uses the fence opening around x≈20, y≈30.
- Lower Route 1 south exit fix: if blocked around `(12,31)`, move left into the lower-left lane, then go down.
- Upper grass pocket around `(10..13, 6..8)` is dangerous and easy to overprobe; bias west/north over the east dead end.
- Confirmed top finish: from around `(13,4)`, `left,left,up,up,up,up,left,up` reaches Viridian City.

## Viridian City / Pokecenter
- Entering from Route 1 places the player near (21,35).
- City sign near (16,17): `VIRIDIAN CITY - The Eternally Green Paradise`.
- East-side building labeled `MART` is the Poké Mart.
- There is a fence/rock barrier around y=28; many central/east `up` attempts from below are blocked.
- Usable crossing: from around (22,28), move left to about x=19, then north to about (19,25), then right.
- Pokecenter entrance: from outside around (22,26), `right, up` enters.
- The middle-aisle man is only an advice NPC.
- Nurse Joy position: stand at `(3,3)` facing `up` and press `A`; choose `HEAL`.
- Upper-city geometry:
  - The north fence line around y≈14 is blocked across a broad span.
  - Far-right rock wall near `(35,16)` is a dead end.
  - To reach upper city from the east half, work left to around `(20,16)` and then up.
  - Small house/school entrance is around `(21,15)`.
  - Gym frontage is reachable in the upper city.
- Northwest woman is only hint text about Viridian Forest/Pewter, not a trigger.
- West exit to Route 22 is at the far-left side; from about `(0,14)`, pressing `left` warps out.
- Strong current hypothesis: real progression is north/Route 2/Viridian Forest, not Route 22.

## Viridian Mart
- On entry, interior may show briefly before clerk parcel dialog; a short `wait` can be correct.
- Parcel flow: advance greeting, receive `OAK's PARCEL`, then clear final goodbye line.
- After parcel dialog, free position is around `(2,5)`; to exit, walk down to about `(2,7)` then use `right, down` to align with the door.

## Route 22
- Entering from Viridian west exit places the player around `(39,6)` facing left.
- Immediate west travel works to about `(36,6)`, but farther left is blocked there; `up` is also blocked on that entry shelf.
- Going down from there reaches a long lower road around y≈14-15.
- That lower road is open almost end-to-end (`x≈2..37`) but `up` is blocked across many tested positions; both far west and far east ends are dead ends.
- Upper-right climb from the far east lower road: from about `(37,14)`, go `left x4, up,up, left, up, left, up, left, up,up` to reach around `(30,8)`.
- That upper-right area is only a small enclosed grass box around roughly `(30..33, 8..12)` plus a narrow upper strip near `(33,10..12)`; apparent exits are blocked and it only drops back to the lower road.
- Consolidation: everything verified so far on Route 22 points to a non-progressing optional area at this stage. Revisit only if testing a genuinely new upper-entry approach; otherwise deprioritize it.
- Because Route 22 grass repeatedly triggers wilds while giving little information, avoid exploratory grass steps there when HP is low.

## Battle heuristics
- Early wild fights can usually be handled with repeated `A` when the cursor stays on FIGHT then TACKLE.
- With low HP, avoid unnecessary battles; fleeing is preferred.
- After a battle, visible text can linger after battle-state tools think it cleared; keep advancing until overworld is visibly back.
- To run from a wild battle, use `down, right, A` from the main battle menu.
- In wild battles with blank intro/send-out screens, press `A` a couple times to reach the command menu before trying RUN.
- If the screen shows move-result text like `used TACKLE!`, the next useful input is usually `A` to clear text; RUN is not selectable until the text resolves back to the command menu.

## Player's House 2F lesson
- Prior wedge hypothesis was wrong.
- Long-hold movement revealed a much larger room than short presses suggested; visible room edges are not always true movement bounds.

## Tooling / harness notes
- `save_checkpoint` may fail with a filesystem 500 error; do not rely on it until a save succeeds.
- Repeated mechanical sequences worth tooling: dialog mashing, systematic coordinate probes, early battle mash, safe service-desk scans, fleeing wild battles, and walk-path execution that stops on battle/map change.
- `probe_path` is excellent for discovering the effect of long holds because it logs coordinates after each step.
- In-game SAVE is distinct from harness checkpoints and appears usable as a fallback.

arguments.content:
Route 22 consolidated: current verified explored space is optional/non-progressing at this stage.

- Entry from Viridian west exit is around `(39,6)`.
- Immediate west movement only reaches about `(36,6)`; farther left and `up` on that shelf are blocked.
- Going down reaches a long lower road around `y≈14-15`.
- That lower road is traversable almost end-to-end (`x≈2..37`) but repeatedly dead-ends on both sides and `up` is blocked across many tested positions.
- A climb exists only on the far east side: from about `(37,14)`, go `left x4, up,up, left, up, left, up, left, up,up` to reach around `(30,8)`.
- That upper-right area is just a small enclosed grass box around `(30..33, 8..12)` plus a narrow strip near `(33,10..12)`. Its top/right continuations are blocked and it only drops back to the lower road.
- Many probes here triggered wilds (Spearow, Nidoran♂, Rattata) while giving little navigation information.
- Operational conclusion: deprioritize Route 22 until Viridian north / Route 2 / Viridian Forest is exhausted. If revisiting, do it only for a genuinely new approach near the upper entry shelf, not the lower road or east grass box.

arguments.name: route22_upper_right_to_viridian_upper_left_warp
arguments.content: Route 22 upper-right discovery: from the far-east upper area, climbing to about `(39,9)` and pressing `right` warps to map `0x01` (Viridian City) at about `(0,17)`. This is a hidden/overlooked reconnect from Route 22 back into Viridian's upper-left side, not forward progression.
