data.name: update_playbook

arguments.content:
# Strategy playbook

## Early boot / intro
- If the screen is blank white at boot, `wait` a few seconds for logo/title.
- At title/menu, `start` or `a` proceeds.
- RAM location can be stale during boot/title; trust screenshot until gameplay begins.
- On player and rival naming screens, pressing `start` accepts the default name.
- After intro, if spawned facing TV/SNES, an `A` press can reopen the inspect textbox; clear text before moving.

## General
- Default d-pad press moves about one tile.
- Long-hold d-pad presses can reveal larger walkable areas than short probes suggest.
- A advances/confirms; B cancels; START opens menu.
- If a textbox is open, movement does nothing; clear text first.
- `B` can dismiss some overworld textboxes cleanly; repeated `A` can accidentally re-talk to NPCs.
- Trust RAM for coordinates/state and screenshot for UI/world geometry.
- Probe systematically with RAM coordinates instead of retrying the same blocked tile.
- Adjacent sprites may be NPCs, not geometry; try `A` before assuming collision.
- In buildings, identify the correct service NPC before mashing.
- If a map seems impossible, test long holds before assuming corruption.
- For risky counters/NPCs, prefer single `A` presses plus controlled `B` clears.
- Blank white or black screens during transitions/outdoor probing are not always softlocks; often wait or check battle flag first.

## Current route
- We have Squirtle Lv8.
- Oak's Parcel delivery and Pokédex progression are complete.
- Route 22 has not yielded forward progression; priority is to return to Viridian, heal, then find Route 2 / Viridian Forest.
- New note: the far-east upper Route 22 area has a return warp into Viridian's upper-left side, but this is only a city reconnect, not forward progress.

## Pallet Town / Oak sequence
- Player's House 1F exit: from downstairs, the lower corridor leads to the door warp; around (2,7) on map 0x25, pressing `down` exits.
- Pallet north exit trigger: the opening is on the east side of the north path; around map 0x00, x=10, y=1, stepping north triggers Oak.
- After that event already happened, do not expect the same trigger again.
- Oak's Lab is the southwest/left large building in Pallet.
- The right-side Pallet house can heal Squirtle via NPC interaction.

## Oak's Lab
- Early lab scene requires multiple textboxes before choosing a starter.
- Verified starter: Squirtle.
- On nickname screen, `START` declines naming.
- First rival battle can be started by pressing `A` while facing the rival.
- Oak parcel return: talking to Oak around `(4,2)` facing right advances the delivery scene.
- Lower-right lab NPC around `(8,11)` is only Oak's aide.
- Top bookshelves just say `Crammed full of POKéMON books!`.

## Route 1
- South entrance around map 0x0c, (10,28): the north tile by sign/fence is blocked.
- East-side bottom strip reaches at least x=17 but `up` there is still blocked; this is a grass/encounter trap.
- Moving left from the south area reaches the clerk around y=24; the clerk is an NPC, not geometry.
- Verified northbound route: from about `(5,18)`, go north to y=14, east along the rock barrier to about x=14, north through the opening, continue north, then loop west near the top fence and go north at about `(11,0)` to warp into Viridian.
- Viridian south return uses the fence opening around x≈20, y≈30.
- Lower Route 1 south exit fix: if blocked around `(12,31)`, move left into the lower-left lane, then go down.
- Upper grass pocket around `(10..13, 6..8)` is dangerous and easy to overprobe; bias west/north over the east dead end.
- Confirmed top finish: from around `(13,4)`, `left,left,up,up,up,up,left,up` reaches Viridian City.

## Viridian City / Pokecenter
- Entering from Route 1 places the player near (21,35).
- City sign near (16,17): `VIRIDIAN CITY - The Eternally Green Paradise`.
- East-side building labeled `MART` is the Poké Mart.
- There is a fence/rock barrier around y=28; many central/east `up` attempts from below are blocked.
- Usable crossing: from around (22,28), move left to about x=19, then north to about (19,25), then right.
- Pokecenter entrance: from outside around (22,26), `right, up` enters.
- The middle-aisle man is only an advice NPC.
- Nurse Joy position: stand at `(3,3)` facing `up` and press `A`; choose `HEAL`.
- Upper-city geometry:
  - The north fence line around y≈14 is blocked across a broad span.
  - Far-right rock wall near `(35,16)` is a dead end.
  - To reach upper city from the east half, work left to around `(20,16)` and then up.
  - Small house/school entrance is around `(21,15)`.
  - Gym frontage is reachable in the upper city.
- Northwest woman is only hint text about Viridian Forest/Pewter, not a trigger.
- West exit to Route 22 is at the far-left side; from about `(0,14)`, pressing `left` warps out.
- Hidden reconnect: from Route 22's far-east upper area, reaching about `(39,9)` and pressing `right` warps back into Viridian upper-left around `(0,17)`.
- Strong current hypothesis: real progression is north/Route 2/Viridian Forest, not Route 22.

## Viridian Mart
- On entry, interior may show briefly before clerk parcel dialog; a short `wait` can be correct.
- Parcel flow: advance greeting, receive `OAK's PARCEL`, then clear final goodbye line.
- After parcel dialog, free position is around `(2,5)`; to exit, walk down to about `(2,7)` then use `right, down` to align with the door.

## Route 22
- Entering from Viridian west exit places the player around `(39,6)` facing left.
- Immediate west travel works to about `(36,6)`, but farther left is blocked there; `up` is also blocked on that entry shelf.
- Going down from there reaches a long lower road around y≈14-15.
- That lower road is open almost end-to-end (`x≈2..37`) but `up` is blocked across many tested positions; both far west and far east ends are dead ends.
- Upper-right climb from the far east lower road: from about `(37,14)`, go `left x4, up,up, left, up, left, up, left, up,up` to reach around `(30,8)`.
- That upper-right area is only a small enclosed grass box around roughly `(30..33, 8..12)` plus a narrow upper strip near `(33,10..12)`; apparent exits are blocked and it only drops back to the lower road.
- New reconnect: a different far-east upper path reaches about `(39,9)`; pressing `right` there warps back into Viridian City upper-left. Treat this as a return connection, not new progression.
- Consolidation: everything verified so far on Route 22 points to a non-progressing optional area at this stage. Revisit only if testing a genuinely new upper-entry approach; otherwise deprioritize it.
- Because Route 22 grass repeatedly triggers wilds while giving little information, avoid exploratory grass steps there when HP is low.

## Battle heuristics
- Early wild fights can usually be handled with repeated `A` when the cursor stays on FIGHT then TACKLE.
- With low HP, avoid unnecessary battles; fleeing is preferred.
- After a battle, visible text can linger after battle-state tools think it cleared; keep advancing until overworld is visibly back.
- To run from a wild battle, use `down, right, A` from the main battle menu.
- In wild battles with blank intro/send-out screens, press `A` a couple times to reach the command menu before trying RUN.
- If the screen shows move-result text like `used TACKLE!`, the next useful input is usually `A` to clear text; RUN is not selectable until the text resolves back to the command menu.

## Player's House 2F lesson
- Prior wedge hypothesis was wrong.
- Long-hold movement revealed a much larger room than short presses suggested; visible room edges are not always true movement bounds.

## Tooling / harness notes
- `save_checkpoint` may fail with a filesystem 500 error; do not rely on it until a save succeeds.
- Repeated mechanical sequences worth tooling: dialog mashing, systematic coordinate probes, early battle mash, safe service-desk scans, fleeing wild battles, and walk-path execution that stops on battle/map change.
- `probe_path` is excellent for discovering the effect of long holds because it logs coordinates after each step.
- In-game SAVE is distinct from harness checkpoints and appears usable as a fallback.

data.name: update_todos

todos.text: Heal Squirtle at the Viridian Pokecenter immediately from this upper-left Viridian reconnect
In Viridian City, find the actual north exit / Route 2 / Viridian Forest opening
If Viridian north progression remains unclear, revisit Route 22 only from a genuinely new upper-entry approach near the west/entry shelf
Deprioritize the Route 22 lower road and enclosed east grass box; they appear non-progressing
Escape the northwest lady/tree-wall dead end and find Viridian City's actual west exit to Route 22
Verify whether save_checkpoint works before relying on rewind for risky sections
Travel north through Route 1 back to Viridian City
Heal Squirtle immediately after the Oak's Lab parcel scene or sooner if route changes require it
Return to Pallet Town and deliver Oak's Parcel to Professor Oak
After parcel delivery, get the Pokédex / next story progression if not already granted
Heal Squirtle in Viridian City's Pokemon Center
Find the actual Nurse Joy/healing counter position left of the Cable Club desk
After healing, enter Viridian City's Mart and progress the parcel/event safely
Enter Viridian City's Mart / progress safely with low-HP Squirtle
Travel north on Route 1 toward Viridian City
From the clerk area on Route 1, probe the true northbound lane while avoiding unnecessary grass due to low HP
Clear the current wild Pidgey battle/result text on Route 1 and return to the overworld safely
Leave Oak's Lab and continue the early-game route after receiving the starter Pokemon
Win or complete the first rival battle in Oak's Lab
Progress Oak's Lab opening: finish dialog and obtain a starter Pokemon

arguments.content: Viridian Pokecenter heal dialog resolution: after healing, the textbox flow can look stuck and even reopen the `HEAL / CANCEL` choice over the final line `POKEMON back to perfect health!`. Working escape sequence from Nurse Joy tile `(3,3)` facing up: press `B` to clear the stray choice, then if a blank textbox remains, another input such as `B`/movement can advance to `We hope to see you again!`; from that final line, `B` plus a movement input like `down` successfully closes the dialog and steps away to free movement at about `(3,4)`.

data.name: update_playbook

arguments.content:
# Strategy playbook

## Early boot / intro
- If the screen is blank white at boot, `wait` a few seconds for logo/title.
- At title/menu, `start` or `a` proceeds.
- RAM location can be stale during boot/title; trust screenshot until gameplay begins.
- On player and rival naming screens, pressing `start` accepts the default name.
- After intro, if spawned facing TV/SNES, an `A` press can reopen the inspect textbox; clear text before moving.

## General
- Default d-pad press moves about one tile.
- Long-hold d-pad presses can reveal larger walkable areas than short probes suggest.
- A advances/confirms; B cancels; START opens menu.
- If a textbox is open, movement does nothing; clear text first.
- `B` can dismiss some overworld textboxes cleanly; repeated `A` can accidentally re-talk to NPCs.
- Trust RAM for coordinates/state and screenshot for UI/world geometry.
- Probe systematically with RAM coordinates instead of retrying the same blocked tile.
- Adjacent sprites may be NPCs, not geometry; try `A` before assuming collision.
- In buildings, identify the correct service NPC before mashing.
- If a map seems impossible, test long holds before assuming corruption.
- For risky counters/NPCs, prefer single `A` presses plus controlled `B` clears.
- Blank white or black screens during transitions/outdoor probing are not always softlocks; often wait or check battle flag first.

## Current route
- We have Squirtle Lv8.
- Oak's Parcel delivery and Pokédex progression are complete.
- Route 22 has not yielded forward progression; priority is to return to Viridian, heal, then find Route 2 / Viridian Forest.
- New note: the far-east upper Route 22 area has a return warp into Viridian's upper-left side, but this is only a city reconnect, not forward progress.
- Squirtle is healed; next priority is Viridian north progression.

## Pallet Town / Oak sequence
- Player's House 1F exit: from downstairs, the lower corridor leads to the door warp; around (2,7) on map 0x25, pressing `down` exits.
- Pallet north exit trigger: the opening is on the east side of the north path; around map 0x00, x=10, y=1, stepping north triggers Oak.
- After that event already happened, do not expect the same trigger again.
- Oak's Lab is the southwest/left large building in Pallet.
- The right-side Pallet house can heal Squirtle via NPC interaction.

## Oak's Lab
- Early lab scene requires multiple textboxes before choosing a starter.
- Verified starter: Squirtle.
- On nickname screen, `START` declines naming.
- First rival battle can be started by pressing `A` while facing the rival.
- Oak parcel return: talking to Oak around `(4,2)` facing right advances the delivery scene.
- Lower-right lab NPC around `(8,11)` is only Oak's aide.
- Top bookshelves just say `Crammed full of POKéMON books!`.

## Route 1
- South entrance around map 0x0c, (10,28): the north tile by sign/fence is blocked.
- East-side bottom strip reaches at least x=17 but `up` there is still blocked; this is a grass/encounter trap.
- Moving left from the south area reaches the clerk around y=24; the clerk is an NPC, not geometry.
- Verified northbound route: from about `(5,18)`, go north to y=14, east along the rock barrier to about x=14, north through the opening, continue north, then loop west near the top fence and go north at about `(11,0)` to warp into Viridian.
- Viridian south return uses the fence opening around x≈20, y≈30.
- Lower Route 1 south exit fix: if blocked around `(12,31)`, move left into the lower-left lane, then go down.
- Upper grass pocket around `(10..13, 6..8)` is dangerous and easy to overprobe; bias west/north over the east dead end.
- Confirmed top finish: from around `(13,4)`, `left,left,up,up,up,up,left,up` reaches Viridian City.

## Viridian City / Pokecenter
- Entering from Route 1 places the player near (21,35).
- City sign near (16,17): `VIRIDIAN CITY - The Eternally Green Paradise`.
- East-side building labeled `MART` is the Poké Mart.
- There is a fence/rock barrier around y=28; many central/east `up` attempts from below are blocked.
- Usable crossing: from around (22,28), move left to about x=19, then north to about (19,25), then right.
- Pokecenter entrance: from outside around (22,26), `right, up` enters.
- The middle-aisle man is only an advice NPC.
- Nurse Joy position: stand at `(3,3)` facing `up` and press `A`; choose `HEAL`.
- After healing, dialog can appear sticky: if a stray `HEAL / CANCEL` choice reopens or a blank textbox remains, use `B` to clear it; from `We hope to see you again!`, `B` plus `down` successfully closes the dialog and steps away.
- Upper-city geometry:
  - The north fence line around y≈14 is blocked across a broad span.
  - Far-right rock wall near `(35,16)` is a dead end.
  - To reach upper city from the east half, work left to around `(20,16)` and then up.
  - Small house/school entrance is around `(21,15)`.
  - Gym frontage is reachable in the upper city.
- Northwest woman is only hint text about Viridian Forest/Pewter, not a trigger.
- West exit to Route 22 is at the far-left side; from about `(0,14)`, pressing `left` warps out.
- Hidden reconnect: from Route 22's far-east upper area, reaching about `(39,9)` and pressing `right` warps back into Viridian upper-left around `(0,17)`.
- Strong current hypothesis: real progression is north/Route 2/Viridian Forest, not Route 22.

## Viridian Mart
- On entry, interior may show briefly before clerk parcel dialog; a short `wait` can be correct.
- Parcel flow: advance greeting, receive `OAK's PARCEL`, then clear final goodbye line.
- After parcel dialog, free position is around `(2,5)`; to exit, walk down to about `(2,7)` then use `right, down` to align with the door.

## Route 22
- Entering from Viridian west exit places the player around `(39,6)` facing left.
- Immediate west travel works to about `(36,6)`, but farther left is blocked there; `up` is also blocked on that entry shelf.
- Going down from there reaches a long lower road around y≈14-15.
- That lower road is open almost end-to-end (`x≈2..37`) but `up` is blocked across many tested positions; both far west and far east ends are dead ends.
- Upper-right climb from the far east lower road: from about `(37,14)`, go `left x4, up,up, left, up, left, up, left, up,up` to reach around `(30,8)`.
- That upper-right area is only a small enclosed grass box around roughly `(30..33, 8..12)` plus a narrow upper strip near `(33,10..12)`; apparent exits are blocked and it only drops back to the lower road.
- New reconnect: a different far-east upper path reaches about `(39,9)`; pressing `right` there warps back into Viridian City upper-left. Treat this as a return connection, not new progression.
- Consolidation: everything verified so far on Route 22 points to a non-progressing optional area at this stage. Revisit only if testing a genuinely new upper-entry approach; otherwise deprioritize it.
- Because Route 22 grass repeatedly triggers wilds while giving little information, avoid exploratory grass steps there when HP is low.

## Battle heuristics
- Early wild fights can usually be handled with repeated `A` when the cursor stays on FIGHT then TACKLE.
- With low HP, avoid unnecessary battles; fleeing is preferred.
- After a battle, visible text can linger after battle-state tools think it cleared; keep advancing until overworld is visibly back.
- To run from a wild battle, use `down, right, A` from the main battle menu.
- In wild battles with blank intro/send-out screens, press `A` a couple times to reach the command menu before trying RUN.
- If the screen shows move-result text like `used TACKLE!`, the next useful input is usually `A` to clear text; RUN is not selectable until the text resolves back to the command menu.

## Player's House 2F lesson
- Prior wedge hypothesis was wrong.
- Long-hold movement revealed a much larger room than short presses suggested; visible room edges are not always true movement bounds.

## Tooling / harness notes
- `save_checkpoint` may fail with a filesystem 500 error; do not rely on it until a save succeeds.
- Repeated mechanical sequences worth tooling: dialog mashing, systematic coordinate probes, early battle mash, safe service-desk scans, fleeing wild battles, and walk-path execution that stops on battle/map change.
- `probe_path` is excellent for discovering the effect of long holds because it logs coordinates after each step.
- In-game SAVE is distinct from harness checkpoints and appears usable as a fallback.

Escape the northwest lady/tree-wall dead end and find Viridian City's actual west exit to Route 22
Verify whether save_checkpoint works before relying on rewind for risky sections
Travel north through Route 1 back to Viridian City

Return to Pallet Town and deliver Oak's Parcel to Professor Oak

Win or complete the first rival battle in Oak's Lab
Progress Oak's Lab opening: finish dialog and obtain a starter Pokemon

What worked:
- `A` advanced the lingering heal text to `POKEMON back to perfect health!`
- A stray `HEAL / CANCEL` menu reopened over that line
- `B` cleared the stray menu
- Another `B`/movement advanced the remaining farewell text
- From `We hope to see you again!`, `B + down` closed the dialog and moved us away

Current state:
- Viridian Pokecenter
- Position `(3,4)`, facing down
- Squirtle Lv8 at full HP `26/26`
- Free movement restored

I also saved this behavior as memory `viridian_pokecenter_heal_dialog_resolution`, updated the playbook, and

data.summary:
Made real progress: I got out of the Pokecenter, found the actual north path in Viridian, and reached Route 2.

What I did
- Exited Viridian Pokecenter by walking straight down from `(3,4)` to the door.
- Tried a new approach instead of re-testing the same north fence from the Pokecenter side:
  - moved through the upper city
  - accidentally entered Viridian School while probing the opening near `(21,16)`
  - exited School to Viridian upper area at `(21,15)`
- Continued probing west/north lanes and found the real north corridor:
  - from around `(19,10)`, went up to `(19,8)`
  - then west/nor
milestones: Entered Route 2 for the first time

data.summary:
- Reached and confirmed real progress on **Route 2**. From the entry at `(7,71)`, I walked north up the main path, found a fence gate/opening area, and kept probing upward.
- Useful Route 2 geometry learned this turn:
  - The central path from `x=7` is open north at least up to about `y=57`.
  - From `(7,57)`, going left reaches a west-side lane; at `(2,56)` the path continues.
  - Taking the west lane north works cleanly up to about `(3,48)`.

data.summary:
- Cleared two Route 2 wild battles that were causing confusing screen/state desync:
  - First Pidgey battle at `(7,48)` ended, but battle transition/artifacts made the overworld look corrupted while RAM still showed `battle: wild`.
  - A new Rattata battle had started during/after movement in grass at `(7,50)`; I eventually finished that too.
- Important lesson confirmed again: when the screen shows battle result text like `A used TACKLE!` or `Enemy RATTATA ...`, the right action is usually just more `A` to clear text. RAM/screen can lag through transitions.

arguments.name: route2_top_grass_right_dead_end
arguments.content: Route 2 top grass dead-end note: at about `(2..5,48)` there is a top boundary/ledge line blocking further north. From `(2,48)`, moving right only reaches about `(5,48)` before blocking again; this upper grass strip is encounter-prone and does not obviously progress east. Avoid re-probing the top grass edge; back out and search a different lane.

data.summary:
- I broke the Route 2 stall by doing something genuinely different: I stopped re-testing the same top edge and instead mapped a wider loop around this grass patch.
- I hit two wild Pidgey battles while probing:
  - first one was successfully escaped/cleared
  - second one got stuck because the flee tool overshot timing, but I manually selected **RUN** with `down, right, A` and cleared the text with `A`
- I saved a durable negative result as memory:
  - `route2_top_grass_right_dead_end`
  - At the top-west grass edge around `(2..5,48)`, north is blocked and east only reaches about `(5,48)` befo

data.name: update_playbook

arguments.content:
# Strategy playbook

## Early boot / intro
- If the screen is blank white at boot, `wait` a few seconds for logo/title.
- At title/menu, `start` or `a` proceeds.
- RAM location can be stale during boot/title; trust screenshot until gameplay begins.
- On player and rival naming screens, pressing `start` accepts the default name.
- After intro, if spawned facing TV/SNES, an `A` press can reopen the inspect textbox; clear text before moving.

## General
- Default d-pad press moves about one tile.
- Long-hold d-pad presses can reveal larger walkable areas than short probes suggest.
- A advances/confirms; B cancels; START opens menu.
- If a textbox is open, movement does nothing; clear text first.
- `B` can dismiss some overworld textboxes cleanly; repeated `A` can accidentally re-talk to NPCs.
- Trust RAM for coordinates/state and screenshot for UI/world geometry.
- Probe systematically with RAM coordinates instead of retrying the same blocked tile.
- Adjacent sprites may be NPCs, not geometry; try `A` before assuming collision.
- In buildings, identify the correct service NPC before mashing.
- If a map seems impossible, test long holds before assuming corruption.
- For risky counters/NPCs, prefer single `A` presses plus controlled `B` clears.
- Blank white or black screens during transitions/outdoor probing are not always softlocks; often wait or check battle flag first.
- In battle, if text like `Critical hit!`, `used TACKLE!`, or enemy move text is visible, the right move is usually still just more `A` until command menu or overworld returns.

## Current route
- We now have Squirtle Lv9.
- Oak's Parcel delivery and Pokédex progression are complete.
- Real progression continues north from Viridian onto Route 2 and toward Viridian Forest/Pewter, not Route 22.
- Current priority on Route 2: stop looping in the same top grass patch and find a genuinely different lane/entrance to continue north.

## Pallet Town / Oak sequence
- Player's House 1F exit: from downstairs, the lower corridor leads to the door warp; around (2,7) on map 0x25, pressing `down` exits.
- Pallet north exit trigger: the opening is on the east side of the north path; around map 0x00, x=10, y=1, stepping north triggers Oak.
- After that event already happened, do not expect the same trigger again.
- Oak's Lab is the southwest/left large building in Pallet.
- The right-side Pallet house can heal Squirtle via NPC interaction.

## Oak's Lab
- Early lab scene requires multiple textboxes before choosing a starter.
- Verified starter: Squirtle.
- On nickname screen, `START` declines naming.
- First rival battle can be started by pressing `A` while facing the rival.
- Oak parcel return: talking to Oak around `(4,2)` facing right advances the delivery scene.
- Lower-right lab NPC around `(8,11)` is only Oak's aide.
- Top bookshelves just say `Crammed full of POKéMON books!`.

## Route 1
- South entrance around map 0x0c, (10,28): the north tile by sign/fence is blocked.
- East-side bottom strip reaches at least x=17 but `up` there is still blocked; this is a grass/encounter trap.
- Moving left from the south area reaches the clerk around y=24; the clerk is an NPC, not geometry.
- Verified northbound route: from about `(5,18)`, go north to y=14, east along the rock barrier to about x=14, north through the opening, continue north, then loop west near the top fence and go north at about `(11,0)` to warp into Viridian.
- Viridian south return uses the fence opening around x≈20, y≈30.
- Lower Route 1 south exit fix: if blocked around `(12,31)`, move left into the lower-left lane, then go down.
- Upper grass pocket around `(10..13, 6..8)` is dangerous and easy to overprobe; bias west/north over the east dead end.
- Confirmed top finish: from around `(13,4)`, `left,left,up,up,up,up,left,up` reaches Viridian City.

## Viridian City / Pokecenter
- Entering from Route 1 places the player near (21,35).
- City sign near (16,17): `VIRIDIAN CITY - The Eternally Green Paradise`.
- East-side building labeled `MART` is the Poké Mart.
- There is a fence/rock barrier around y=28; many central/east `up` attempts from below are blocked.
- Usable crossing: from around (22,28), move left to about x=19, then north to about (19,25), then right.
- Pokecenter entrance: from outside around (22,26), `right, up` enters.
- The middle-aisle man is only an advice NPC.
- Nurse Joy position: stand at `(3,3)` facing `up` and press `A`; choose `HEAL`.
- After healing, dialog can appear sticky: if a stray `HEAL / CANCEL` choice reopens or a blank textbox remains, use `B` to clear it; from `We hope to see you again!`, `B` plus `down` successfully closes the dialog and steps away.
- Upper-city geometry:
  - The north fence line around y≈14 is blocked across a broad span.
  - Far-right rock wall near `(35,16)` is a dead end.
  - To reach upper city from the east half, work left to around `(20,16)` and then up.
  - Small house/school entrance is around `(21,15)`.
  - Gym frontage is reachable in the upper city.
- Northwest woman is only hint text about Viridian Forest/Pewter, not a trigger.
- West exit to Route 22 is at the far-left side; from about `(0,14)`, pressing `left` warps out.
- Hidden reconnect: from Route 22's far-east upper area, reaching about `(39,9)` and pressing `right` warps back into Viridian upper-left around `(0,17)`.
- Strong current hypothesis: real progression is north/Route 2/Viridian Forest, not Route 22.

## Viridian Mart
- On entry, interior may show briefly before clerk parcel dialog; a short `wait` can be correct.
- Parcel flow: advance greeting, receive `OAK's PARCEL`, then clear final goodbye line.
- After parcel dialog, free position is around `(2,5)`; to exit, walk down to about `(2,7)` then use `right, down` to align with the door.

## Route 22
- Entering from Viridian west exit places the player around `(39,6)` facing left.
- Immediate west travel works to about `(36,6)`, but farther left is blocked there; `up` is also blocked on that entry shelf.
- Going down from there reaches a long lower road around y≈14-15.
- That lower road is open almost end-to-end (`x≈2..37`) but `up` is blocked across many tested positions; both far west and far east ends are dead ends.
- Upper-right climb from the far east lower road: from about `(37,14)`, go `left x4, up,up, left, up, left, up, left, up,up` to reach around `(30,8)`.
- That upper-right area is only a small enclosed grass box around roughly `(30..33, 8..12)` plus a narrow upper strip near `(33,10..12)`; apparent exits are blocked and it only drops back to the lower road.
- New reconnect: a different far-east upper path reaches about `(39,9)`; pressing `right` there warps back into Viridian City upper-left. Treat this as a return connection, not new progression.
- Consolidation: everything verified so far on Route 22 points to a non-progressing optional area at this stage. Revisit only if testing a genuinely new upper-entry approach; otherwise deprioritize it.
- Because Route 22 grass repeatedly triggers wilds while giving little information, avoid exploratory grass steps there when HP is low.

## Route 2
- Entered Route 2 from Viridian north.
- Central path from about `(7,71)` is open north at least to about `y=57`.
- From `(7,57)`, going left reaches a west-side lane; taking that west lane north works to about `(3,48)`.
- Top-west area has a horizontal barrier/top edge: from `(3,48)`, right works to about `(7,48)`, but farther right is blocked; north is blocked there too.
- New note: at about `(8,48)` the tile(s) south are blocked, and wild encounters in this grass patch can trigger with black-screen/partial-overworld transition artifacts before the battle fully renders.
- Avoid grinding the same top grass patch; back out and look for a different Route 2 lane or building/gate approach.

## Battle heuristics
- Early wild fights can usually be handled with repeated `A` when the cursor stays on FIGHT then TACKLE.
- With low HP, avoid unnecessary battles; fleeing is preferred.
- After a battle, visible text can linger after battle-state tools think it cleared; keep advancing until overworld is visibly back.
- To run from a wild battle, use `down, right, A` from the main battle menu.
- In wild battles with blank intro/send-out screens, press `A` a couple times to reach the command menu before trying RUN.
- If the screen shows move-result text like `used TACKLE!`, the next useful input is usually `A` to clear text; RUN is not selectable until the text resolves back to the command menu.

## Player's House 2F lesson
- Prior wedge hypothesis was wrong.
- Long-hold movement revealed a much larger room than short presses suggested; visible room edges are not always true movement bounds.

## Tooling / harness notes
- `save_checkpoint` may fail with a filesystem 500 error; do not rely on it until a save succeeds.
- Repeated mechanical sequences worth tooling: dialog mashing, systematic coordinate probes, early battle mash, safe service-desk scans, fleeing wild battles, and walk-path execution that stops on battle/map change.
- `probe_path` is excellent for discovering the effect of long holds because it logs coordinates after each step.
- In-game SAVE is distinct from harness checkpoints and appears usable as a fallback.

- Lower Route 1 south exit fix: if blocked around `(12,31)`, move left into the lower-left lane, then go down.
- Upper grass pocket around `(10..13, 6..8)` is dangerous and easy to overprobe; bias west/north over the east dead end.
- Confirmed top finish: from around `(13,4)`, `left,left,up,up,up,up,left,up` reaches Viridian City.

## Viridian City / Pokecenter
- Entering from Route 1 places the player near (21,35).
- City sign near (16,17): `VIRIDIAN CITY - The Eternally Green Paradise`.
- East-side building labeled `MART` is the Poké Mart.
- There is a fence/rock barrier around y=28; many central/east `up` attempts from below are blocked.

- Entered Route 2 from Viridian north.
- Central path from about `(7,71)` is open north at least to about `y=57`.

- New note: at about `(8,48)` the tile(s) south are blocked, and wild encounters in this grass patch can trigger with black-screen/partial-overworld transition artifacts before the battle fully renders.
- Current battle-resolution state on turn 100: a fight ended with Squirtle leveling to 9 at `(8,48)` while battle flag still reads wild. Level-up text like `grew to level 9!` is still battle text; clear it with `A` until the command/menu or overworld visibly returns.

## Battle heuristics
- Early wild fights can usually be handled with repeated `A` when the cursor stays on FIGHT then TACKLE.
- With low HP, avoid unnecessary battles; fleeing is preferred.
- After a battle, visible text can linger after battle-state tools think it cleared; keep advancing until overworld is visibly back.

- In wild battles with blank intro/send-out screens, press `A` a couple times to reach the command menu before trying RUN.
- If the screen shows move-result text like `used TACKLE!`, `Enemy ... fainted!`, `gained EXP. Points!`, or `grew to level ...`, the next useful input is usually still just `A` until the battle flow finishes.
- Do not try RUN or movement while experience/level-up text is still showing; those inputs are wasted until the text resolves.

## Player's House 2F lesson

## Tooling / harness notes

arguments.name: route2_consolidated_early_probe
arguments.content:
Route 2 early probe consolidation: real progression is north from Viridian, but the first explored west/top grass area is partly a trap.

Verified layout so far:
- Entry from Viridian north puts the player around `(7,71)` on map `0x0d`.
- The central path is open north at least to about `(7,57)`.
- From `(7,57)`, going left reaches a west-side lane.
- Taking that west lane north works to about `(3,48)`.
- At the top-west edge, `north` is blocked. Moving right from `(3,48)` reaches about `(7,48)`, but farther right is blocked.
- Around `(8,48)`, moving `down` is also blocked, so this top patch is not a clean through-route.

Encounter / transition notes:
- Grass encounters here can trigger with misleading partial-overworld or black-screen transition artifacts before the battle fully renders.
- Do not interpret those artifacts as a softlock; check battle flag and advance with `A` if battle text is present.
- On turn 100, Squirtle leveled to 9 here; `grew to level 9!` was still battle text even though it looked like the fight was basically over.

Practical takeaway:
- Stop looping this same top grass patch.
- On return to play, clear current battle text, then retreat or re-approach from a lower/surer Route 2 position and inspect for a different lane, gatehouse, or Viridian Forest entrance.

- Cleared the stuck Route 2 level-up/post-battle text successfully with manual `A` presses; overworld is restored at **Route 2 (8,48)**.
- I verified again that this top grass patch is a trap:

  - from `(8,48)`, many attempted moves are blocked
- I then tried a genuinely different long path, but it immediately triggered a **wild Weedle** instead of escaping the area.
- Battle outcome:
  - Squirtle was hit by **Poison Sting**
  - Squirtle is now **poisoned (PSN)** and at **11/28 HP**

arguments.content: Route 2 mid-west tree maze trap: from the earlier top-grass area, probing south eventually reached around `(5..11, 51..52)` beside a dense tree block. arguments.name: route2_poison_whiteout_to_pokecenter
arguments.content: Route 2 whiteout/auto-heal note: after Squirtle fainted from overworld poison on Route 2 and the player remained standing at `(7,59)` with `0/28 HP`, walking south from Route 2 warped back into Viridian City. Re-entering the Viridian Pokecenter immediately afterward caused the party state to show Squirtle restored to full HP `28/28`, consistent with a whiteout/auto-heal return. Also observed money drop from `$3000` to `$1500`, confirming blackout money loss.

data.result: memory 'route2_poison_whiteout_to_pokecenter' saved

data.summary:
I found the recovery path and confirmed what happened:

- Squirtle had blacked out from poison on Route 2.
- Going south from Route 2 returned us to Viridian City.
- Re-entering the Pokecenter resulted in Squirtle being fully restored.
- This was a true blackout/whiteout:
  - HP restored to 28/28
  - money dropped from $3000 to $1500

Current state:
- Viridian Pokecenter
- at (3,7), facing up
- Squirtle Lv9, 28/28 HP
- $1500

I also:
- saved memory: `route2_poison_whiteout_to_pokecenter`
- updated todos to replace the outdated “heal immediately” task with confirming/documenting this whiteout b

data.summary:
I did a genuinely different Route 2 probe focused on the lower/southern area instead of re-looping the top grass trap.

What I did:
- Systematically probed the visible fence/tree choke around `(6..11,62)`.
- Confirmed the apparent fence “gaps” near the central vertical tree line are fake from below: multiple `up` attempts from both sides were blocked.
- Saved this as memory: `route2_south_fence_gaps_blocked`.
- Backtracked and explored farther south-west instead of retrying the same choke.
- Found a broader lower-left area with:
  - a sign around the `(4..6,66)` area
  - a bottom rock boundary

- Returned to the lower-left sign area and tested interaction.

- The sign at about `(5,66)` is just the Route sign, not progression.

data.summary:
I finally made genuinely different Route 2 probes instead of re-testing the sign textbox.

What I did:
- Cleared the Route 2 sign with `B`; movement had been blocked only because the textbox was still open.
- Probed the lower-right/southern area around the sign and confirmed free movement there:
  - moved from `(5,66)` to at least `(11,67)`
  - north along the right side works up to about `(11,63)`
  - from there, going right is blocked
- Returned to the known choke at `(7,57)` and then took a genuinely different west-edge route:
  - `left x5, up x5, left, up x2, right x3, up, left x2, up`
  -

data.summary:
- Cleared the stuck **Pidgey Lv3** battle manually after the auto-mash tool kept desyncing on move selection.
- Important battle detail learned: Squirtle now knows **Tail Whip** and **Bubble**; in the move menu the cursor was on **Tail Whip**, so blind A-mashing can waste turns. I moved **up** to **Tackle** to attack.
- After finishing Pidgey, I resumed probing from **Route 2 (6,48)**. A new path test moved to **(4,49)** before triggering a **wild Rattata Lv4**.
- Tried `flee_wild_battle`, but it failed again in this state and left the battle ongoing.

data.directive: This is a scheduled self-improvement turn. Do NOT press any buttons.

data.name: update_playbook

arguments.content:
# Strategy playbook

## Early boot / intro
- If the screen is blank white at boot, `wait` a few seconds for logo/title.
- At title/menu, `start` or `a` proceeds.
- RAM location can be stale during boot/title; trust screenshot until gameplay begins.
- On player and rival naming screens, pressing `start` accepts the default name.
- After intro, if spawned facing TV/SNES, an `A` press can reopen the inspect textbox; clear text before moving.

## General
- Default d-pad press moves about one tile.
- Long-hold d-pad presses can reveal larger walkable areas than short probes suggest.
- A advances/confirms; B cancels; START opens menu.
- If a textbox is open, movement does nothing; clear text first.
- `B` can dismiss some overworld textboxes cleanly; repeated `A` can accidentally re-talk to NPCs.
- Trust RAM for coordinates/state and screenshot for UI/world geometry.
- Probe systematically with RAM coordinates instead of retrying the same blocked tile.
- Adjacent sprites may be NPCs, not geometry; try `A` before assuming collision.
- In buildings, identify the correct service NPC before mashing.
- If a map seems impossible, test long holds before assuming corruption.
- For risky counters/NPCs, prefer single `A` presses plus controlled `B` clears.
- Blank white or black screens during transitions/outdoor probing are not always softlocks; often wait or check battle flag first.
- When a battle just ended or is mid-transition, do not trust the partially rendered screen as overworld access. Check battle flag first.

## Current route
- We now have Squirtle Lv9.
- Oak's Parcel delivery and Pokédex progression are complete.
- Real progression continues north from Viridian onto Route 2 and toward Viridian Forest/Pewter, not Route 22.
- Current Route 2 focus: from the upper Route 2 area around y=48, stop looping the west/top grass trap and instead look for the east-side lane, gatehouse, or forest entrance.
- Current known safe-ish resume state from recent probing is upper Route 2 around `(10,48)` with low but noncritical HP; avoid unnecessary grass if a structure/path is visible.

## Pallet Town / Oak sequence
- Player's House 1F exit: from downstairs, the lower corridor leads to the door warp; around (2,7) on map 0x25, pressing `down` exits.
- Pallet north exit trigger: the opening is on the east side of the north path; around map 0x00, x=10, y=1, stepping north triggers Oak.
- After that event already happened, do not expect the same trigger again.
- Oak's Lab is the southwest/left large building in Pallet.
- The right-side Pallet house can heal Squirtle via NPC interaction.

## Oak's Lab
- Early lab scene requires multiple textboxes before choosing a starter.
- Verified starter: Squirtle.
- On nickname screen, `START` declines naming.
- First rival battle can be started by pressing `A` while facing the rival.
- Oak parcel return: talking to Oak around `(4,2)` facing right advances the delivery scene.
- Lower-right lab NPC around `(8,11)` is only Oak's aide.
- Top bookshelves just say `Crammed full of POKéMON books!`.

## Route 1
- South entrance around map 0x0c, (10,28): the north tile by sign/fence is blocked.
- East-side bottom strip reaches at least x=17 but `up` there is still blocked; this is a grass/encounter trap.
- Moving left from the south area reaches the clerk around y=24; the clerk is an NPC, not geometry.
- Verified northbound route: from about `(5,18)`, go north to y=14, east along the rock barrier to about x=14, north through the opening, continue north, then loop west near the top fence and go north at about `(11,0)` to warp into Viridian.
- Viridian south return uses the fence opening around x≈20, y≈30.
- Lower Route 1 south exit fix: if blocked around `(12,31)`, move left into the lower-left lane, then go down.
- Upper grass pocket around `(10..13, 6..8)` is dangerous and easy to overprobe; bias west/north over the east dead end.
- Confirmed top finish: from around `(13,4)`, `left,left,up,up,up,up,left,up` reaches Viridian City.

## Viridian City / Pokecenter
- Entering from Route 1 places the player near (21,35).
- City sign near (16,17): `VIRIDIAN CITY - The Eternally Green Paradise`.
- East-side building labeled `MART` is the Poké Mart.
- There is a fence/rock barrier around y=28; many central/east `up` attempts from below are blocked.
- Usable crossing: from around (22,28), move left to about x=19, then north to about (19,25), then right.
- Pokecenter entrance: from outside around (22,26), `right, up` enters.
- The middle-aisle man is only an advice NPC.
- Nurse Joy position: stand at `(3,3)` facing `up` and press `A`; choose `HEAL`.
- After healing, dialog can appear sticky: if a stray `HEAL / CANCEL` choice reopens or a blank textbox remains, use `B` to clear it; from `We hope to see you again!`, `B` plus `down` successfully closes the dialog and steps away.
- Upper-city geometry:
  - The north fence line around y≈14 is blocked across a broad span.
  - Far-right rock wall near `(35,16)` is a dead end.
  - To reach upper city from the east half, work left to around `(20,16)` and then up.
  - Small house/school entrance is around `(21,15)`.
  - Gym frontage is reachable in the upper city.
- Northwest woman is only hint text about Viridian Forest/Pewter, not a trigger.
- West exit to Route 22 is at the far-left side; from about `(0,14)`, pressing `left` warps out.
- Hidden reconnect: from Route 22's far-east upper area, reaching about `(39,9)` and pressing `right` warps back into Viridian upper-left around `(0,17)`.
- Strong current hypothesis: real progression is north/Route 2/Viridian Forest, not Route 22.

## Viridian Mart
- On entry, interior may show briefly before clerk parcel dialog; a short `wait` can be correct.
- Parcel flow: advance greeting, receive `OAK's PARCEL`, then clear final goodbye line.
- After parcel dialog, free position is around `(2,5)`; to exit, walk down to about `(2,7)` then use `right, down` to align with the door.

## Route 22
- Entering from Viridian west exit places the player around `(39,6)` facing left.
- Immediate west travel works to about `(36,6)`, but farther left is blocked there; `up` is also blocked on that entry shelf.
- Going down from there reaches a long lower road around y≈14-15.
- That lower road is open almost end-to-end (`x≈2..37`) but `up` is blocked across many tested positions; both far west and far east ends are dead ends.
- Upper-right climb from the far east lower road: from about `(37,14)`, go `left x4, up,up, left, up, left, up, left, up,up` to reach around `(30,8)`.
- That upper-right area is only a small enclosed grass box around roughly `(30..33, 8..12)` plus a narrow upper strip near `(33,10..12)`; apparent exits are blocked and it only drops back to the lower road.
- New reconnect: a different far-east upper path reaches about `(39,9)`; pressing `right` there warps back into Viridian City upper-left. Treat this as a return connection, not new progression.
- Consolidation: everything verified so far on Route 22 points to a non-progressing optional area at this stage. Revisit only if testing a genuinely new upper-entry approach; otherwise deprioritize it.
- Because Route 22 grass repeatedly triggers wilds while giving little information, avoid exploratory grass steps there when HP is low.

## Route 2
- Entered Route 2 from Viridian north.
- Central path from about `(7,71)` is open north at least to about `y=57`.
- From `(7,57)`, going left reaches a west-side lane; taking that west lane north works to about `(3,48)`.
- Top-west area has a horizontal barrier/top edge: from `(3,48)`, right works to about `(7,48)`, but farther right is blocked; north is blocked there too.
- New note: at about `(8,48)` the tile(s) south are blocked, and wild encounters in this grass patch can trigger with black-screen/partial-overworld transition artifacts before the battle fully renders.
- Current battle-resolution state on turn 100: a fight ended with Squirtle leveling to 9 at `(8,48)` while battle flag still reads wild. Level-up text like `grew to level 9!` is still battle text; clear it with `A` until the command/menu or overworld visibly returns.
- Avoid grinding the same top grass patch; back out and look for a different Route 2 lane or building/gate approach from a lower, safer position.
- South fence-gap lesson: the apparent openings around `(6..11,62)` are fake from below; repeated `up` attempts there are blocked.
- Lower-left sign around `(5,66)` only reads `ROUTE 2 / VIRIDIAN CITY - PEWTER CITY`.
- Midwest tree-maze trap: the area around roughly `(5..11,51..52)` reconnects awkwardly to the top row, triggers many wilds, and gives little new information.
- Recent reconnection: from the trap area, moving to the safer west lane around `(2,57)` and then reprobeing north can reconnect to the upper row near `(2,48)`.
- New tactical note from the live screen/state: current position `(10,48)` is farther east than the old `(7..8,48)` trap points; prioritize mapping this east-side upper lane before falling back south.

## Battle heuristics
- Early wild fights can sometimes be handled with repeated `A`, but blind mash is no longer safe once Squirtle's move list matters.
- Squirtle now knows **Tail Whip** and **Bubble**. If the move menu is open with the cursor on Tail Whip, blind `A` wastes turns.
- In simple wild fights, deliberately choose a damaging move; if unsure, `Tackle` is a safe manual fallback.
- With low HP, avoid unnecessary battles; fleeing is preferred.
- After a battle, visible text can linger after battle-state tools think it cleared; keep advancing until overworld is visibly back.
- To run from a wild battle, use the battle menu deliberately; do not assume the cursor stayed where a previous tool left it.
- In wild battles with blank intro/send-out screens, press `A` a couple times to reach the command menu before trying RUN.
- If the screen shows move-result text like `used TACKLE!`, `Enemy ... fainted!`, `gained EXP. Points!`, or `grew to level ...`, the next useful input is usually still just `A` until the battle flow finishes.
- Do not try RUN or movement while experience/level-up text is still showing; those inputs are wasted until the text resolves.
- If accidental input opens the Pokémon submenu during battle, `B` backs out cleanly to the main battle menu.

## Player's House 2F lesson
- Prior wedge hypothesis was wrong.
- Long-hold movement revealed a much larger room than short presses suggested; visible room edges are not always true movement bounds.

## Tooling / harness notes
- `save_checkpoint` may fail with a filesystem 500 error; do not rely on it until a save succeeds.
- Repeated mechanical sequences worth tooling: dialog mashing, systematic coordinate probes, early battle mash, safe service-desk scans, fleeing wild battles, walk-path execution that stops on battle/map change, and clearing battle/result text until the battle flag drops.
- `probe_path` is excellent for discovering the effect of long holds because it logs coordinates after each step.
- Existing `clear_battle_text` is broken (`ctx.frame is not a function`); replace it with a simpler version that only loops on `press` and state checks.
- In-game SAVE is distinct from harness checkpoints and appears usable as a fallback.

arguments.content:
Route 2 upper-area consolidation: the repeatedly tested west/top grass pocket is a trap, but the latest state reached a more eastern top position.

Verified / consolidated from turns 101-109:
- The old stall area around `(7..8,48)` is bad for progress:
  - from `(8,48)`, south can be blocked
  - loops like `down,down,right,right,up,up,left,left` can return to the same tile
  - this area repeatedly triggers wilds with misleading black/partial-overworld transitions
- The mid-west tree/grass maze around roughly `(5..11,51..52)` is also poor for probing: encounter-prone and reconnects awkwardly.
- A safer reconnect exists from around `(2,57)` back north toward the upper row near `(2,48)`.
- After more probing, the current live state by turn 110 is **Route 2 `(10,48)` facing down**, which is farther east than the earlier documented `(7..8,48)` trap tiles.

Implication for future play:
- Next Route 2 progress attempt should prioritize mapping the **east side of the upper row / visible lane or structure access** from around `(10,48)` before retreating into the old west trap or lower fake fence gaps.
- Avoid blind A-mash in battles while exploring this area, because Squirtle's move menu can land on Tail Whip/Bubble instead of a damaging move.

Keep Squirtle alive while probing Route 2; flee unnecessary wilds and return to heal before another poison/low-HP whiteout
Confirm and document the apparent auto-heal/whiteout return from Route 2 to Viridian Pokecenter
Deprioritize the Route 22 lower road and enclosed east grass box; they appear non-progressing
Escape the northwest lady/tree-wall dead end and find Viridian City's actual west exit to Route 22
Verify whether save_checkpoint works before relying on rewind for risky sections
Travel north through Route 1 back to Viridian City

Enter Viridian City's Mart / progress safely with low-HP Squirtle

From the clerk area on Route 1, probe the true northbound lane while avoiding unnecessary grass due to low HP

data.name: update_playbook

arguments.content:
# Strategy playbook

## Early boot / intro
- If the screen is blank white at boot, `wait` a few seconds for logo/title.
- At title/menu, `start` or `a` proceeds.
- RAM location can be stale during boot/title; trust screenshot until gameplay begins.
- On player and rival naming screens, pressing `start` accepts the default name.
- After intro, if spawned facing TV/SNES, an `A` press can reopen the inspect textbox; clear text before moving.

## General
- Default d-pad press moves about one tile.
- Long-hold d-pad presses can reveal larger walkable areas than short probes suggest.
- A advances/confirms; B cancels; START opens menu.
- If a textbox is open, movement does nothing; clear text first.
- `B` can dismiss some overworld textboxes cleanly; repeated `A` can accidentally re-talk to NPCs.
- Trust RAM for coordinates/state and screenshot for UI/world geometry.
- Probe systematically with RAM coordinates instead of retrying the same blocked tile.
- Adjacent sprites may be NPCs, not geometry; try `A` before assuming collision.
- In buildings, identify the correct service NPC before mashing.
- If a map seems impossible, test long holds before assuming corruption.
- For risky counters/NPCs, prefer single `A` presses plus controlled `B` clears.
- Blank white or black screens during transitions/outdoor probing are not always softlocks; often wait or check battle flag first.
- When a battle just ended or is mid-transition, do not trust the partially rendered screen as overworld access. Check battle flag first.
- On route signs, `A` often leaves a textbox that blocks all movement; clear with `B` before probing further.

## Current route
- We now have Squirtle Lv9.
- Oak's Parcel delivery and Pokédex progression are complete.
- Real progression continues north from Viridian onto Route 2 and toward Viridian Forest/Pewter, not Route 22.
- New progress: from Route 2 upper area around `(7,44)`, `left,left,left,left,up` enters the gatehouse; going north through it reaches the Route 2 / Viridian Forest south-side outdoor area (map 0x33).
- Current focus is to find the actual Viridian Forest entrance from that north-side outdoor area while avoiding unnecessary grass and low-HP battles.

## Pallet Town / Oak sequence
- Player's House 1F exit: from downstairs, the lower corridor leads to the door warp; around (2,7) on map 0x25, pressing `down` exits.
- Pallet north exit trigger: the opening is on the east side of the north path; around map 0x00, x=10, y=1, stepping north triggers Oak.
- After that event already happened, do not expect the same trigger again.
- Oak's Lab is the southwest/left large building in Pallet.
- The right-side Pallet house can heal Squirtle via NPC interaction.

## Oak's Lab
- Early lab scene requires multiple textboxes before choosing a starter.
- Verified starter: Squirtle.
- On nickname screen, `START` declines naming.
- First rival battle can be started by pressing `A` while facing the rival.
- Oak parcel return: talking to Oak around `(4,2)` facing right advances the delivery scene.
- Lower-right lab NPC around `(8,11)` is only Oak's aide.
- Top bookshelves just say `Crammed full of POKéMON books!`.

## Route 1
- South entrance around map 0x0c, (10,28): the north tile by sign/fence is blocked.
- East-side bottom strip reaches at least x=17 but `up` there is still blocked; this is a grass/encounter trap.
- Moving left from the south area reaches the clerk around y=24; the clerk is an NPC, not geometry.
- Verified northbound route: from about `(5,18)`, go north to y=14, east along the rock barrier to about x=14, north through the opening, continue north, then loop west near the top fence and go north at about `(11,0)` to warp into Viridian.
- Viridian south return uses the fence opening around x≈20, y≈30.
- Lower Route 1 south exit fix: if blocked around `(12,31)`, move left into the lower-left lane, then go down.
- Upper grass pocket around `(10..13, 6..8)` is dangerous and easy to overprobe; bias west/north over the east dead end.
- Confirmed top finish: from around `(13,4)`, `left,left,up,up,up,up,left,up` reaches Viridian City.

## Viridian City / Pokecenter
- Entering from Route 1 places the player near (21,35).
- City sign near (16,17): `VIRIDIAN CITY - The Eternally Green Paradise`.
- East-side building labeled `MART` is the Poké Mart.
- There is a fence/rock barrier around y=28; many central/east `up` attempts from below are blocked.
- Usable crossing: from around (22,28), move left to about x=19, then north to about (19,25), then right.
- Pokecenter entrance: from outside around (22,26), `right, up` enters.
- The middle-aisle man is only an advice NPC.
- Nurse Joy position: stand at `(3,3)` facing `up` and press `A`; choose `HEAL`.
- After healing, dialog can appear sticky: if a stray `HEAL / CANCEL` choice reopens or a blank textbox remains, use `B` to clear it; from `We hope to see you again!`, `B` plus `down` successfully closes the dialog and steps away.
- Upper-city geometry:
  - The north fence line around y≈14 is blocked across a broad span.
  - Far-right rock wall near `(35,16)` is a dead end.
  - To reach upper city from the east half, work left to around `(20,16)` and then up.
  - Small house/school entrance is around `(21,15)`.
  - Gym frontage is reachable in the upper city.
- Northwest woman is only hint text about Viridian Forest/Pewter, not a trigger.
- West exit to Route 22 is at the far-left side; from about `(0,14)`, pressing `left` warps out.
- Hidden reconnect: from Route 22's far-east upper area, reaching about `(39,9)` and pressing `right` warps back into Viridian upper-left around `(0,17)`.
- Strong current hypothesis: real progression is north/Route 2/Viridian Forest, not Route 22.

## Viridian Mart
- On entry, interior may show briefly before clerk parcel dialog; a short `wait` can be correct.
- Parcel flow: advance greeting, receive `OAK's PARCEL`, then clear final goodbye line.
- After parcel dialog, free position is around `(2,5)`; to exit, walk down to about `(2,7)` then use `right, down` to align with the door.

## Route 22
- Entering from Viridian west exit places the player around `(39,6)` facing left.
- Immediate west travel works to about `(36,6)`, but farther left is blocked there; `up` is also blocked on that entry shelf.
- Going down from there reaches a long lower road around y≈14-15.
- That lower road is open almost end-to-end (`x≈2..37`) but `up` is blocked across many tested positions; both far west and far east ends are dead ends.
- Upper-right climb from the far east lower road: from about `(37,14)`, go `left x4, up,up, left, up, left, up, left, up,up` to reach around `(30,8)`.
- That upper-right area is only a small enclosed grass box around roughly `(30..33, 8..12)` plus a narrow upper strip near `(33,10..12)`; apparent exits are blocked and it only drops back to the lower road.
- New reconnect: a different far-east upper path reaches about `(39,9)`; pressing `right` there warps back into Viridian City upper-left. Treat this as a return connection, not new progression.
- Consolidation: everything verified so far on Route 22 points to a non-progressing optional area at this stage. Revisit only if testing a genuinely new upper-entry approach; otherwise deprioritize it.
- Because Route 22 grass repeatedly triggers wilds while giving little information, avoid exploratory grass steps there when HP is low.

## Route 2
- Entered Route 2 from Viridian north.
- Central path from about `(7,71)` is open north at least to about `y=57`.
- From `(7,57)`, going left reaches a west-side lane; taking that west lane north works to about `(3,48)`.
- Top-west area has a horizontal barrier/top edge: from `(3,48)`, right works to about `(7,48)`, but farther right is blocked; north is blocked there too.
- New note: at about `(8,48)` the tile(s) south are blocked, and wild encounters in this grass patch can trigger with black-screen/partial-overworld transition artifacts before the battle fully renders.
- Current battle-resolution state on turn 100: a fight ended with Squirtle leveling to 9 at `(8,48)` while battle flag still reads wild. Level-up text like `grew to level 9!` is still battle text; clear it with `A` until the command/menu or overworld visibly returns.
- Avoid grinding the same top grass patch; back out and look for a different Route 2 lane or building/gate approach from a lower, safer position.
- South fence-gap lesson: the apparent openings around `(6..11,62)` are fake from below; repeated `up` attempts there are blocked.
- Lower-left sign around `(5,66)` only reads `ROUTE 2 / VIRIDIAN CITY - PEWTER CITY`.
- Midwest tree-maze trap: the area around roughly `(5..11,51..52)` reconnects awkwardly to the top row, triggers many wilds, and gives little new information.
- Recent reconnection: from the trap area, moving to the safer west lane around `(2,57)` and then reprobeing north can reconnect to the upper row near `(2,48)`.
- New tactical note from the live screen/state: current position `(10,48)` is farther east than the old `(7..8,48)` trap points; prioritize mapping this east-side upper lane before falling back south.
- Real progress discovery: from the upper area around `(7,44)`, `left,left,left,left,up` enters the gatehouse to Viridian Forest.
- North-side outdoor sign near the forest approach says: `For poison, use ANTIDOTE! Get it at POKEMON MARTs!`

## Battle heuristics
- Early wild fights can sometimes be handled with repeated `A`, but blind mash is no longer safe once Squirtle's move list matters.
- Squirtle now knows **Tail Whip** and **Bubble**. If the move menu is open with the cursor on Tail Whip, blind `A` wastes turns.
- In simple wild fights, deliberately choose a damaging move; if unsure, `Tackle` is a safe manual fallback.
- With low HP, avoid unnecessary battles; fleeing is preferred.
- After a battle, visible text can linger after battle-state tools think it cleared; keep advancing until overworld is visibly back.
- To run from a wild battle, use the battle menu deliberately; do not assume the cursor stayed where a previous tool left it.
- In wild battles with blank intro/send-out screens, press `A` a couple times to reach the command menu before trying RUN.
- If the screen shows move-result text like `used TACKLE!`, `Enemy ... fainted!`, `gained EXP. Points!`, or `grew to level ...`, the next useful input is usually still just `A` until the battle flow finishes.
- Do not try RUN or movement while experience/level-up text is still showing; those inputs are wasted until the text resolves.
- If accidental input opens the Pokémon submenu during battle, `B` backs out cleanly to the main battle menu.

## Player's House 2F lesson
- Prior wedge hypothesis was wrong.
- Long-hold movement revealed a much larger room than short presses suggested; visible room edges are not always true movement bounds.

## Tooling / harness notes
- `save_checkpoint` may fail with a filesystem 500 error; do not rely on it until a save succeeds.
- Repeated mechanical sequences worth tooling: dialog mashing, systematic coordinate probes, early battle mash, safe service-desk scans, fleeing wild battles, walk-path execution that stops on battle/map change, and clearing battle/result text until the battle flag drops.
- `probe_path` is excellent for discovering the effect of long holds because it logs coordinates after each step.
- Existing `clear_battle_text` is broken (`ctx.frame is not a function`); replace it with a simpler version that only loops on `press` and state checks.
- In-game SAVE is distinct from harness checkpoints and appears usable as a fallback.

arguments.name: route2_gate_north_outdoor_layout_partial
arguments.content: Route 2 Gate north-side outdoor area partial map: from start around `(6,30)`, west reaches `(2,30)` then south only to `(2,31)`; east lane reaches `(8,30)` and can go down to at least `(8,35)`. A broader lower path exists: from `(6,34)` going down reaches `(6,40)`, then east reaches at least `(14,40)`, and south to `(14,43)`. Near `(14,43)` there is an NPC under/left of a tree and a sign to the southeast. Talking to the NPC from `(15,43)` facing right gives: `They're out for POKEMON fights!`

data.name: update_todos

todos.text: From the south Route 2 gate, continue through the Route 2 / Viridian Forest gatehouse toward Viridian Forest entrance or next north exit
Keep Squirtle alive while probing Route 2; flee unnecessary wilds and return to heal before another poison/low-HP whiteout
If the forest/gate progression stalls, document the gate interior layout and find the correct exit/entrance alignment
Confirm whether the north-side outdoor Route 2 area leads into Viridian Forest directly, another gate/building, or a dead-end optional NPC/sign pocket
Confirm and document the apparent auto-heal/whiteout return from Route 2 to Viridian Pokecenter
Deprioritize the Route 22 lower road and enclosed east grass box; they appear non-progressing
Escape the northwest lady/tree-wall dead end and find Viridian City's actual west exit to Route 22
Verify whether save_checkpoint works before relying on rewind for risky sections
Travel north through Route 1 back to Viridian City
Heal Squirtle immediately after the Oak's Lab parcel scene or sooner if route changes require it
Return to Pallet Town and deliver Oak's Parcel to Professor Oak
After parcel delivery, get the Pokédex / next story progression if not already granted
Heal Squirtle in Viridian City's Pokemon Center
Find the actual Nurse Joy/healing counter position left of the Cable Club desk
After healing, enter Viridian City's Mart and progress the parcel/event safely
Enter Viridian City's Mart / progress safely with low-HP Squirtle
Travel north on Route 1 toward Viridian City
From the clerk area on Route 1, probe the true northbound lane while avoiding unnecessary grass due to low HP
Clear the current wild Pidgey battle/result text on Route 1 and return to the overworld safely
Leave Oak's Lab and continue the early-game route after receiving the starter Pokemon

data.summary:
- Mapped the **north-side Route 2 outdoor pocket** much more effectively instead of re-testing the blocked top grass:
  - Start area `(6,30)` is hemmed in above.
  - West reaches `(2,30)` but only down to `(2,31)`.
  - East lane reaches `(8,30)` and can go down.
  - A real lower route exists: from around `(6,34)` I got down to `(6,40)`, then east to at least `(14,40)`, and south to `(14,43)`.

- Found an **NPC + sign area** near `(14,43)`:
  - Talking to the NPC from `(15,43)` facing right gives:
    - **“They’re out for POKEMON fights!”**
  - This confirms that lower-right area is not the ent

data.summary:
- I finally made real progress out of the apparent “stuck” gatehouse state by re-reading the room instead of insisting the top-right door was the only exit.
- In `MAP_0x32` (the building), I mapped the top row:
  - left from `(9,1)` all the way to `(1,1)` is open
  - trying `up` from x=1..4 stayed blocked until **x=5**
  - from **`(5,1)` pressing `up` exits north** to **map 0x33 Route 2 Gate (Viridian Forest south)** at about `(17,47)`
- This means the correct gatehouse exit alignment is **not** the visible top-right doorway I was re-testing; the usable warp is along the upper row nearer the m

data.summary:
Cleared an accidental battle-menu misstate: we were in the “Choose a POKéMON” submenu during a wild Weedle fight, so `B` backed out cleanly, then `down, A` successfully selected RUN. That confirms again that manual flee from the main battle menu is reliable.

After escaping, I made genuinely different Route 2 / forest-approach probes instead of re-testing the same blocked tile:
- From `(4,40)` I moved through the lower-right open lane to about `(8,43)`.
- Probe mapping showed:
  - open corridor from `(4,43)` east through `(12,43)`
  - south blocked at `x=8` and also blocked at `x=10`

data.summary:
- Cleared the visually stuck Pikachu battle flow by manually advancing text; the safe-text tool falsely thought battle was over because RAM/state was desynced from the screen.
- Important new timing lesson: after `used TACKLE!`, the screen can sit on the text for a while; a short `wait` revealed the actual battle menu cleanly.
- From that menu, I selected `RUN` with `down, right, A`. The resulting screen now says `Go...`, which is likely the run-success text or the next battle text line; I had to stop due to tool budget before confirming the battle fully cleared.

data.result: Route 2 Gate north-side outdoor area partial map: from start around `(6,30)`, west reaches `(2,30)` then south only to `(2,31)`; east lane reaches `(8,30)` and can go down to at least `(8,35)`.   - Moving left across the row works to at least **(10,43)**

data.summary:
- Escaped the live **wild Kakuna** cleanly:
  - `B` backed out of the bad submenu (`CANCEL` in move/Pokémon substate).
  - `right, A` selected **RUN**.
  - Then `A, A` cleared `Got away safely!`
  - A short `wait` was required for the white battle transition to finish.
- Then I made **genuinely different Route 2 / Viridian Forest south-side probes** instead of re-testing the same lower row:
  - From `(14,40)`, moving **up is blocked** at `x=10` when coming from the left lane.
  - New lane found: from `(15,39)` I could go **right to `(17,39)`**, then **straight north** to at least **`(17,34)`**
