data.name: update_playbook

arguments.content:
# Strategy playbook

## Early boot / intro
- If the screen is blank white at boot, `wait` a few seconds for logo/title.
- At title/menu, `start` or `a` proceeds.
- RAM location can be stale during boot/title; trust screenshot until gameplay begins.
- On player and rival naming screens, pressing `start` accepts the default name.
- After intro, if spawned facing TV/SNES, an `A` press can reopen the inspect textbox; clear text before moving.

## General
- Default d-pad press moves about one tile.
- Long-hold d-pad presses can reveal larger walkable areas than short probes suggest.
- A advances/confirms; B cancels; START opens menu.
- If a textbox is open, movement does nothing; clear text first.
- `B` can dismiss some overworld textboxes cleanly; repeated `A` can accidentally re-talk to NPCs.
- Trust RAM for coordinates/state and screenshot for UI/world geometry.
- Probe systematically with RAM coordinates instead of retrying the same blocked tile.
- Adjacent sprites may be NPCs, not geometry; try `A` before assuming collision.
- In buildings, identify the correct service NPC before mashing.
- If a map seems impossible, test long holds before assuming corruption.
- For risky counters/NPCs, prefer single `A` presses plus controlled `B` clears.
- Blank white or black screens during transitions/outdoor probing are not always softlocks; often wait or check battle flag first.
- When a battle just ended or is mid-transition, do not trust the partially rendered screen as overworld access. Check battle flag first.
- On route signs, `A` often leaves a textbox that blocks all movement; clear with `B` before probing further.

## Current route
- We now have Squirtle Lv9.
- Oak's Parcel delivery and Pokédex progression are complete.
- Real progression continues north from Viridian onto Route 2 and toward Viridian Forest/Pewter, not Route 22.
- From Route 2 upper area around `(7,44)`, `left,left,left,left,up` enters the gatehouse; going north through it reaches the Route 2 / Viridian Forest south-side outdoor area (map 0x33).
- Current live area is the north-side outdoor pocket south of Viridian Forest. Progress has **not** been the sign/NPC pocket near `(14,43)`; useful probes came from the farther-right lanes.
- Best current lead: the right-side lane around `x≈17..18` is the promising northbound route. Reprobe there before falling back to older dead rows.
- Current live state at start of turn 120: map `0x33`, `(15,44)`, facing left, Squirtle `13/28 HP`.

## Pallet Town / Oak sequence
- Player's House 1F exit: from downstairs, the lower corridor leads to the door warp; around (2,7) on map 0x25, pressing `down` exits.
- Pallet north exit trigger: the opening is on the east side of the north path; around map 0x00, x=10, y=1, stepping north triggers Oak.
- After that event already happened, do not expect the same trigger again.
- Oak's Lab is the southwest/left large building in Pallet.
- The right-side Pallet house can heal Squirtle via NPC interaction.

## Oak's Lab
- Early lab scene requires multiple textboxes before choosing a starter.
- Verified starter: Squirtle.
- On nickname screen, `START` declines naming.
- First rival battle can be started by pressing `A` while facing the rival.
- Oak parcel return: talking to Oak around `(4,2)` facing right advances the delivery scene.
- Lower-right lab NPC around `(8,11)` is only Oak's aide.
- Top bookshelves just say `Crammed full of POKéMON books!`.

## Route 1
- South entrance around map 0x0c, (10,28): the north tile by sign/fence is blocked.
- East-side bottom strip reaches at least x=17 but `up` there is still blocked; this is a grass/encounter trap.
- Moving left from the south area reaches the clerk around y=24; the clerk is an NPC, not geometry.
- Verified northbound route: from about `(5,18)`, go north to y=14, east along the rock barrier to about x=14, north through the opening, continue north, then loop west near the top fence and go north at about `(11,0)` to warp into Viridian.
- Viridian south return uses the fence opening around x≈20, y≈30.
- Lower Route 1 south exit fix: if blocked around `(12,31)`, move left into the lower-left lane, then go down.
- Upper grass pocket around `(10..13, 6..8)` is dangerous and easy to overprobe; bias west/north over the east dead end.
- Confirmed top finish: from around `(13,4)`, `left,left,up,up,up,up,left,up` reaches Viridian City.

## Viridian City / Pokecenter
- Entering from Route 1 places the player near (21,35).
- City sign near (16,17): `VIRIDIAN CITY - The Eternally Green Paradise`.
- East-side building labeled `MART` is the Poké Mart.
- There is a fence/rock barrier around y=28; many central/east `up` attempts from below are blocked.
- Usable crossing: from around (22,28), move left to about x=19, then north to about (19,25), then right.
- Pokecenter entrance: from outside around (22,26), `right, up` enters.
- The middle-aisle man is only an advice NPC.
- Nurse Joy position: stand at `(3,3)` facing `up` and press `A`; choose `HEAL`.
- After healing, dialog can appear sticky: if a stray `HEAL / CANCEL` choice reopens or a blank textbox remains, use `B` to clear it; from `We hope to see you again!`, `B` plus `down` successfully closes the dialog and steps away.
- Upper-city geometry:
  - The north fence line around y≈14 is blocked across a broad span.
  - Far-right rock wall near `(35,16)` is a dead end.
  - To reach upper city from the east half, work left to around `(20,16)` and then up.
  - Small house/school entrance is around `(21,15)`.
  - Gym frontage is reachable in the upper city.
- Northwest woman is only hint text about Viridian Forest/Pewter, not a trigger.
- West exit to Route 22 is at the far-left side; from about `(0,14)`, pressing `left` warps out.
- Hidden reconnect: from Route 22's far-east upper area, reaching about `(39,9)` and pressing `right` warps back into Viridian upper-left around `(0,17)`.
- Strong current hypothesis: real progression is north/Route 2/Viridian Forest, not Route 22.

## Viridian Mart
- On entry, interior may show briefly before clerk parcel dialog; a short `wait` can be correct.
- Parcel flow: advance greeting, receive `OAK's PARCEL`, then clear final goodbye line.
- After parcel dialog, free position is around `(2,5)`; to exit, walk down to about `(2,7)` then use `right, down` to align with the door.

## Route 22
- Entering from Viridian west exit places the player around `(39,6)` facing left.
- Immediate west travel works to about `(36,6)`, but farther left is blocked there; `up` is also blocked on that entry shelf.
- Going down from there reaches a long lower road around y≈14-15.
- That lower road is open almost end-to-end (`x≈2..37`) but `up` is blocked across many tested positions; both far west and far east ends are dead ends.
- Upper-right climb from the far east lower road: from about `(37,14)`, go `left x4, up,up, left, up, left, up, left, up,up` to reach around `(30,8)`.
- That upper-right area is only a small enclosed grass box around roughly `(30..33, 8..12)` plus a narrow upper strip near `(33,10..12)`; apparent exits are blocked and it only drops back to the lower road.
- New reconnect: a different far-east upper path reaches about `(39,9)`; pressing `right` there warps back into Viridian City upper-left. Treat this as a return connection, not new progression.
- Consolidation: everything verified so far on Route 22 points to a non-progressing optional area at this stage. Revisit only if testing a genuinely new upper-entry approach; otherwise deprioritize it.
- Because Route 22 grass repeatedly triggers wilds while giving little information, avoid exploratory grass steps there when HP is low.

## Route 2
- Entered Route 2 from Viridian north.
- Central path from about `(7,71)` is open north at least to about `y=57`.
- From `(7,57)`, going left reaches a west-side lane; taking that west lane north works to about `(3,48)`.
- Top-west area has a horizontal barrier/top edge: from `(3,48)`, right works to about `(7,48)`, but farther right is blocked; north is blocked there too.
- New note: at about `(8,48)` the tile(s) south are blocked, and wild encounters in this grass patch can trigger with black-screen/partial-overworld transition artifacts before the battle fully renders.
- Current battle-resolution state on turn 100: a fight ended with Squirtle leveling to 9 at `(8,48)` while battle flag still reads wild. Level-up text like `grew to level 9!` is still battle text; clear it with `A` until the command/menu or overworld visibly returns.
- Avoid grinding the same top grass patch; back out and look for a different Route 2 lane or building/gate approach from a lower, safer position.
- South fence-gap lesson: the apparent openings around `(6..11,62)` are fake from below; repeated `up` attempts there are blocked.
- Lower-left sign around `(5,66)` only reads `ROUTE 2 / VIRIDIAN CITY - PEWTER CITY`.
- Midwest tree-maze trap: the area around roughly `(5..11,51..52)` reconnects awkwardly to the top row, triggers many wilds, and gives little new information.
- Recent reconnection: from the trap area, moving to the safer west lane around `(2,57)` and then reprobeing north can reconnect to the upper row near `(2,48)`.
- New tactical note from the live screen/state: current position `(10,48)` is farther east than the old `(7..8,48)` trap points; prioritize mapping this east-side upper lane before falling back south.
- Real progress discovery: from the upper area around `(7,44)`, `left,left,left,left,up` enters the gatehouse to Viridian Forest.
- North-side outdoor sign near the forest approach says: `For poison, use ANTIDOTE! Get it at POKEMON MARTs!`
- Gatehouse exit alignment lesson: inside `MAP_0x32`, the correct north exit is along the top row near `x=5`, not the misleading top-right doorway. From around `(5,1)`, pressing `up` exits to the north-side outdoor area at about `(17,47)` on map `0x33`.
- North-side outdoor pocket partial map so far:
  - Start area near `(6,30)` is blocked above.
  - West reaches about `(2,30)` but only slightly downward there.
  - A lower route exists via the east/lower lanes, reaching at least `(14,43)` where there is an NPC/sign pocket.
  - NPC near `(14,43)` only says `They're out for POKEMON fights!`
  - South is blocked at `(14,43)`; do not keep hammering that tile.
  - Better progress came from the farther-right lane: from about `(15,39)`, moving right to `(17,39)` and then north reached at least `(17,34)`.
  - Most recent lead from turn 119: the lane around `x≈18` remains open and is the best candidate for finding the actual Viridian Forest entrance.

## Battle heuristics
- Early wild fights can sometimes be handled with repeated `A`, but blind mash is no longer safe once Squirtle's move list matters.
- Squirtle now knows **Tail Whip** and **Bubble**. If the move menu is open with the cursor on Tail Whip, blind `A` wastes turns.
- In simple wild fights, deliberately choose a damaging move; if unsure, `Tackle` is a safe manual fallback.
- With low HP, avoid unnecessary battles; fleeing is preferred.
- After a battle, visible text can linger after battle-state tools think it cleared; keep advancing until overworld is visibly back.
- To run from a wild battle, use the battle menu deliberately; do not assume the cursor stayed where a previous tool left it.
- In wild battles with blank intro/send-out screens, press `A` a couple times to reach the command menu before trying RUN.
- If the screen shows move-result text like `used TACKLE!`, `Enemy ... fainted!`, `gained EXP. Points!`, or `grew to level ...`, the next useful input is usually still just `A` until the battle flow finishes.
- Do not try RUN or movement while experience/level-up text is still showing; those inputs are wasted until the text resolves.
- If accidental input opens the Pokémon submenu during battle, `B` backs out cleanly to the main battle menu.
- Reliable manual flee pattern from messy wild-battle substates:
  - If stuck in move or Pokémon submenu, press `B` until back at the 4-option main battle menu.
  - Common RUN selections that have worked in this run: `down, A` and `right, A` depending on current cursor position; if needed, `down, right, A` from FIGHT also works.
  - After `Got away safely!`, one more `A` is usually needed, then a short `wait` for the white transition back to overworld.
- After `used TACKLE!` or similar, a short `wait` can reveal the actual next menu/text more cleanly than more inputs.

## Player's House 2F lesson
- Prior wedge hypothesis was wrong.
- Long-hold movement revealed a much larger room than short presses suggested; visible room edges are not always true movement bounds.

## Tooling / harness notes
- `save_checkpoint` may fail with a filesystem 500 error; do not rely on it until a save succeeds.
- Repeated mechanical sequences worth tooling: dialog mashing, systematic coordinate probes, early battle mash, safe service-desk scans, fleeing wild battles, walk-path execution that stops on battle/map change, and clearing battle/result text until the battle flag drops.
- `probe_path` is excellent for discovering the effect of long holds because it logs coordinates after each step.
- Old `clear_battle_text` is broken (`ctx.frame is not a function`); use/keep the safer state-based replacement instead.
- In-game SAVE is distinct from harness checkpoints and appears usable as a fallback.

arguments.content:
Route 2 Gate north-side outdoor progress: the south-of-Viridian-Forest outdoor pocket on map 0x33 is larger than the first blocked sign row suggests, but the lower-right NPC/sign pocket is not the entrance.

Verified points:
- The advice sign near about `(16,33)` says `For poison, use ANTIDOTE! Get it at POKEMON MARTs!`
- The upper-left/start row near `(6,30)` is blocked above; west reaches about `(2,30)` but only a little downward there.
- A lower path exists by going farther right/down instead of insisting on the top row.
- The lower-right area reaches at least `(14,43)` and contains:
  - an NPC whose line is `They're out for POKEMON fights!`
  - a sign nearby
- South is blocked at `(14,43)`; that spot is not progression.
- Better lead: from about `(15,39)`, moving right to `(17,39)` and then north reached at least `(17,34)`.
- Turn 119 consolidation: the farther-right lane around `x≈18` remained open and looked like the best current candidate for the real Viridian Forest entrance.

Related gatehouse lesson:
- Inside `MAP_0x32`, the correct north exit is not the misleading top-right doorway. Walking to about `(5,1)` on the top row and pressing `up` exits to map `0x33` around `(17,47)`.

data.summary:
Turn 123 summary:

What I did
- Escaped the live wild Weedle battle manually after the recover tool left us in a half-cleared state:
  - waited to reach the real battle menu
  - used `down, right, A` to select RUN
  - advanced `Got away safely!` with extra `A`
  - waited through the white transition back to overworld
- From Route 2 north-side outdoor area, I tried genuinely different exploration on the far east side instead of reusing the old x≈17–18 lane.
- New movement findings from the east side:
  - Starting at `(25,41)`, `right,right` reached `(27,41)`.
  - North is blocked at `(27,41)`.

  - `A` advanced to **Enemy CATERPIE’s SPEED...**

  - Squirtle took damage from **11/30 to 9/30**

data.directive: This is a scheduled self-improvement turn. Do NOT press any buttons.

data.name: update_playbook

arguments.content:
# Strategy playbook

## Early boot / intro
- If the screen is blank white at boot, `wait` a few seconds for logo/title.
- At title/menu, `start` or `a` proceeds.
- RAM location can be stale during boot/title; trust screenshot until gameplay begins.
- On player and rival naming screens, pressing `start` accepts the default name.
- After intro, if spawned facing TV/SNES, an `A` press can reopen the inspect textbox; clear text before moving.

## General
- Default d-pad press moves about one tile.
- Long-hold d-pad presses can reveal larger walkable areas than short probes suggest.
- A advances/confirms; B cancels; START opens menu.
- If a textbox is open, movement does nothing; clear text first.
- `B` can dismiss some overworld textboxes cleanly; repeated `A` can accidentally re-talk to NPCs.
- Trust RAM for coordinates/state and screenshot for UI/world geometry.
- Probe systematically with RAM coordinates instead of retrying the same blocked tile.
- Adjacent sprites may be NPCs, not geometry; try `A` before assuming collision.
- In buildings, identify the correct service NPC before mashing.
- If a map seems impossible, test long holds before assuming corruption.
- For risky counters/NPCs, prefer single `A` presses plus controlled `B` clears.
- Blank white or black screens during transitions/outdoor probing are not always softlocks; often wait or check battle flag first.
- When a battle just ended or is mid-transition, do not trust the partially rendered screen as overworld access. Check battle flag first.
- On route signs, `A` often leaves a textbox that blocks all movement; clear with `B` before probing further.

## Current route
- We now have Squirtle Lv10.
- Oak's Parcel delivery and Pokédex progression are complete.
- Real progression continues north from Viridian onto Route 2 and toward Viridian Forest/Pewter, not Route 22.
- From Route 2 upper area around `(7,44)`, `left,left,left,left,up` enters the gatehouse; going north through it reaches the Route 2 / Viridian Forest south-side outdoor area (map 0x33).
- Current live state at start of turn 130 is a trainer battle on map `0x33` at `(26,33)`, Squirtle `9/30 HP`, versus Bug Catcher's Caterpie Lv6.
- The overworld trainer at about `(26,33)` in the north-side outdoor pocket is hostile; talking to him starts a Bug Catcher battle.

## Pallet Town / Oak sequence
- Player's House 1F exit: from downstairs, the lower corridor leads to the door warp; around (2,7) on map 0x25, pressing `down` exits.
- Pallet north exit trigger: the opening is on the east side of the north path; around map 0x00, x=10, y=1, stepping north triggers Oak.
- After that event already happened, do not expect the same trigger again.
- Oak's Lab is the southwest/left large building in Pallet.
- The right-side Pallet house can heal Squirtle via NPC interaction.

## Oak's Lab
- Early lab scene requires multiple textboxes before choosing a starter.
- Verified starter: Squirtle.
- On nickname screen, `START` declines naming.
- First rival battle can be started by pressing `A` while facing the rival.
- Oak parcel return: talking to Oak around `(4,2)` facing right advances the delivery scene.
- Lower-right lab NPC around `(8,11)` is only Oak's aide.
- Top bookshelves just say `Crammed full of POKéMON books!`.

## Route 1
- South entrance around map 0x0c, (10,28): the north tile by sign/fence is blocked.
- East-side bottom strip reaches at least x=17 but `up` there is still blocked; this is a grass/encounter trap.
- Moving left from the south area reaches the clerk around y=24; the clerk is an NPC, not geometry.
- Verified northbound route: from about `(5,18)`, go north to y=14, east along the rock barrier to about x=14, north through the opening, continue north, then loop west near the top fence and go north at about `(11,0)` to warp into Viridian.
- Viridian south return uses the fence opening around x≈20, y≈30.
- Lower Route 1 south exit fix: if blocked around `(12,31)`, move left into the lower-left lane, then go down.
- Upper grass pocket around `(10..13, 6..8)` is dangerous and easy to overprobe; bias west/north over the east dead end.
- Confirmed top finish: from around `(13,4)`, `left,left,up,up,up,up,left,up` reaches Viridian City.

## Viridian City / Pokecenter
- Entering from Route 1 places the player near (21,35).
- City sign near (16,17): `VIRIDIAN CITY - The Eternally Green Paradise`.
- East-side building labeled `MART` is the Poké Mart.
- There is a fence/rock barrier around y=28; many central/east `up` attempts from below are blocked.
- Usable crossing: from around (22,28), move left to about x=19, then north to about (19,25), then right.
- Pokecenter entrance: from outside around (22,26), `right, up` enters.
- The middle-aisle man is only an advice NPC.
- Nurse Joy position: stand at `(3,3)` facing `up` and press `A`; choose `HEAL`.
- After healing, dialog can appear sticky: if a stray `HEAL / CANCEL` choice reopens or a blank textbox remains, use `B` to clear it; from `We hope to see you again!`, `B` plus `down` successfully closes the dialog and steps away.
- Upper-city geometry:
  - The north fence line around y≈14 is blocked across a broad span.
  - Far-right rock wall near `(35,16)` is a dead end.
  - To reach upper city from the east half, work left to around `(20,16)` and then up.
  - Small house/school entrance is around `(21,15)`.
  - Gym frontage is reachable in the upper city.
- Northwest woman is only hint text about Viridian Forest/Pewter, not a trigger.
- West exit to Route 22 is at the far-left side; from about `(0,14)`, pressing `left` warps out.
- Hidden reconnect: from Route 22's far-east upper area, reaching about `(39,9)` and pressing `right` warps back into Viridian upper-left around `(0,17)`.
- Strong current hypothesis: real progression is north/Route 2/Viridian Forest, not Route 22.

## Viridian Mart
- On entry, interior may show briefly before clerk parcel dialog; a short `wait` can be correct.
- Parcel flow: advance greeting, receive `OAK's PARCEL`, then clear final goodbye line.
- After parcel dialog, free position is around `(2,5)`; to exit, walk down to about `(2,7)` then use `right, down` to align with the door.

## Route 22
- Entering from Viridian west exit places the player around `(39,6)` facing left.
- Immediate west travel works to about `(36,6)`, but farther left is blocked there; `up` is also blocked on that entry shelf.
- Going down from there reaches a long lower road around y≈14-15.
- That lower road is open almost end-to-end (`x≈2..37`) but `up` is blocked across many tested positions; both far west and far east ends are dead ends.
- Upper-right climb from the far east lower road: from about `(37,14)`, go `left x4, up,up, left, up, left, up, left, up,up` to reach around `(30,8)`.
- That upper-right area is only a small enclosed grass box around roughly `(30..33, 8..12)` plus a narrow upper strip near `(33,10..12)`; apparent exits are blocked and it only drops back to the lower road.
- New reconnect: a different far-east upper path reaches about `(39,9)`; pressing `right` there warps back into Viridian City upper-left. Treat this as a return connection, not new progression.
- Consolidation: everything verified so far on Route 22 points to a non-progressing optional area at this stage. Revisit only if testing a genuinely new upper-entry approach; otherwise deprioritize it.
- Because Route 22 grass repeatedly triggers wilds while giving little information, avoid exploratory grass steps there when HP is low.

## Route 2
- Entered Route 2 from Viridian north.
- Central path from about `(7,71)` is open north at least to about `y=57`.
- From `(7,57)`, going left reaches a west-side lane; taking that west lane north works to about `(3,48)`.
- Top-west area has a horizontal barrier/top edge: from `(3,48)`, right works to about `(7,48)`, but farther right is blocked; north is blocked there too.
- New note: at about `(8,48)` the tile(s) south are blocked, and wild encounters in this grass patch can trigger with black-screen/partial-overworld transition artifacts before the battle fully renders.
- Current battle-resolution state on turn 100: a fight ended with Squirtle leveling to 9 at `(8,48)` while battle flag still reads wild. Level-up text like `grew to level 9!` is still battle text; clear it with `A` until the command/menu or overworld visibly returns.
- Avoid grinding the same top grass patch; back out and look for a different Route 2 lane or building/gate approach from a lower, safer position.
- South fence-gap lesson: the apparent openings around `(6..11,62)` are fake from below; repeated `up` attempts there are blocked.
- Lower-left sign around `(5,66)` only reads `ROUTE 2 / VIRIDIAN CITY - PEWTER CITY`.
- Midwest tree-maze trap: the area around roughly `(5..11,51..52)` reconnects awkwardly to the top row, triggers many wilds, and gives little new information.
- Recent reconnection: from the trap area, moving to the safer west lane around `(2,57)` and then reprobeing north can reconnect to the upper row near `(2,48)`.
- New tactical note from the live screen/state: current position `(10,48)` is farther east than the old `(7..8,48)` trap points; prioritize mapping this east-side upper lane before falling back south.
- Real progress discovery: from the upper area around `(7,44)`, `left,left,left,left,up` enters the gatehouse to Viridian Forest.
- North-side outdoor sign near the forest approach says: `For poison, use ANTIDOTE! Get it at POKEMON MARTs!`
- Gatehouse exit alignment lesson: inside `MAP_0x32`, the correct north exit is along the top row near `x=5`, not the misleading top-right doorway. From around `(5,1)`, pressing `up` exits to the north-side outdoor area at about `(17,47)` on map `0x33`.
- North-side outdoor pocket partial map so far:
  - Start area near `(6,30)` is blocked above.
  - West reaches about `(2,30)` but only slightly downward there.
  - A lower route exists via the east/lower lanes, reaching at least `(14,43)` where there is an NPC/sign pocket.
  - NPC near `(14,43)` only says `They're out for POKEMON fights!`
  - South is blocked at `(14,43)`; do not keep hammering that tile.
  - Better progress came from the farther-right lane: from about `(15,39)`, moving right to `(17,39)` and then north reached at least `(17,34)`.
  - East side opens farther than first thought: reachable tiles include roughly `(19..23, 40..43)`.
  - North is blocked at least at `(22,40)`, `(23,40)`, and `(27,41)`.
  - The trainer/NPC at about `(26,33)` is a Bug Catcher who challenges immediately.
  - After this trainer is defeated, continue probing east/north here for the actual forest entrance.

## Battle heuristics
- Early wild fights can sometimes be handled with repeated `A`, but blind mash is no longer safe once Squirtle's move list matters.
- Squirtle now knows **Tackle**, **Tail Whip**, and **Bubble**. Visible move order is **TACKLE / TAIL WHIP / BUBBLE**.
- In battle, if the move menu cursor is sitting on **TAIL WHIP**, then **down + A** selects **BUBBLE** and plain **A** wastes a turn on Tail Whip.
- In simple wild fights, deliberately choose a damaging move; if unsure, `Tackle` is a safe manual fallback.
- With low HP, avoid unnecessary battles; fleeing is preferred.
- After a battle, visible text can linger after battle-state tools think it cleared; keep advancing until overworld is visibly back.
- To run from a wild battle, use the battle menu deliberately; do not assume the cursor stayed where a previous tool left it.
- In wild battles with blank intro/send-out screens, press `A` a couple times to reach the command menu before trying RUN.
- If the screen shows move-result text like `used TACKLE!`, `Enemy ... fainted!`, `gained EXP. Points!`, or `grew to level ...`, the next useful input is usually still just `A` until the battle flow finishes.
- Do not try RUN or movement while experience/level-up text is still showing; those inputs are wasted until the text resolves.
- If accidental input opens the Pokémon submenu during battle, `B` backs out cleanly to the main battle menu.
- Reliable manual flee pattern from messy wild-battle substates:
  - If stuck in move or Pokémon submenu, press `B` until back at the 4-option main battle menu.
  - Common RUN selections that have worked in this run: `down, A` and `right, A` depending on current cursor position; if needed, `down, right, A` from FIGHT also works.
  - After `Got away safely!`, one more `A` is usually needed, then a short `wait` for the white transition back to overworld.
- After `used TACKLE!` or similar, a short `wait` can reveal the actual next menu/text more cleanly than more inputs.
- New repeated lesson from the Bug Catcher fight: **Bubble** text/animation can lag for a long time. Best pattern is usually **wait first, then single `A` presses with long settle** rather than mashing.
- Enemy move text may chain strangely across boxes (for example `attack missed! But, it failed!` after Weedle). Treat it as normal battle text and advance one box at a time.
- Trainer battles on the route approach should be handled manually, not with wild-battle mash/flee tools.

## Player's House 2F lesson
- Prior wedge hypothesis was wrong.
- Long-hold movement revealed a much larger room than short presses suggested; visible room edges are not always true movement bounds.

## Tooling / harness notes
- `save_checkpoint` may fail with a filesystem 500 error; do not rely on it until a save succeeds.
- Repeated mechanical sequences worth tooling: dialog mashing, systematic coordinate probes, early battle mash, safe service-desk scans, fleeing wild battles, walk-path execution that stops on battle/map change, and clearing battle/result text until the battle flag drops.
- `probe_path` is excellent for discovering the effect of long holds because it logs coordinates after each step.
- Old `clear_battle_text` is broken (`ctx.frame is not a function`); use/keep the safer state-based replacement instead.
- In-game SAVE is distinct from harness checkpoints and appears usable as a fallback.

arguments.content:
# Strategy playbook

## Early boot / intro
- If the screen is blank white at boot, `wait` a few seconds for logo/title.
- At title/menu, `start` or `a` proceeds.
- RAM location can be stale during boot/title; trust screenshot until gameplay begins.
- On player and rival naming screens, pressing `start` accepts the default name.
- After intro, if spawned facing TV/SNES, an `A` press can reopen the inspect textbox; clear text before moving.

## General
- Default d-pad press moves about one tile.
- Long-hold d-pad presses can reveal larger walkable areas than short probes suggest.
- A advances/confirms; B cancels; START opens menu.
- If a textbox is open, movement does nothing; clear text first.
- `B` can dismiss some overworld textboxes cleanly; repeated `A` can accidentally re-talk to NPCs.
- Trust RAM for coordinates/state and screenshot for UI/world geometry.
- Probe systematically with RAM coordinates instead of retrying the same blocked tile.
- Adjacent sprites may be NPCs, not geometry; try `A` before assuming collision.
- In buildings, identify the correct service NPC before mashing.
- If a map seems impossible, test long holds before assuming corruption.
- For risky counters/NPCs, prefer single `A` presses plus controlled `B` clears.
- Blank white or black screens during transitions/outdoor probing are not always softlocks; often wait or check battle flag first.
- When a battle just ended or is mid-transition, do not trust the partially rendered screen as overworld access. Check battle flag first.
- On route signs, `A` often leaves a textbox that blocks all movement; clear with `B` before probing further.

## Current route
- We now have Squirtle Lv10.
- Oak's Parcel delivery and Pokédex progression are complete.
- Real progression continues north from Viridian onto Route 2 and toward Viridian Forest/Pewter, not Route 22.
- From Route 2 upper area around `(7,44)`, `left,left,left,left,up` enters the gatehouse; going north through it reaches the Route 2 / Viridian Forest south-side outdoor area (map 0x33).
- New upper-pocket progress: a northbound lane around `x≈25` continues far upward from the Bug Catcher area and reaches the upper Route 2 pocket near `(25,20)`.
- Current live state at start of turn 140 is a trainer battle on map `0x33` at `(26,19)`, Squirtle `7/30 HP`, versus upper-pocket Bug Catcher's Kakuna Lv7, with visible text `Enemy KAKUNA's DEFENSE ...` after Harden.

## Pallet Town / Oak sequence
- Player's House 1F exit: from downstairs, the lower corridor leads to the door warp; around (2,7) on map 0x25, pressing `down` exits.
- Pallet north exit trigger: the opening is on the east side of the north path; around map 0x00, x=10, y=1, stepping north triggers Oak.
- After that event already happened, do not expect the same trigger again.
- Oak's Lab is the southwest/left large building in Pallet.
- The right-side Pallet house can heal Squirtle via NPC interaction.

## Oak's Lab
- Early lab scene requires multiple textboxes before choosing a starter.
- Verified starter: Squirtle.
- On nickname screen, `START` declines naming.
- First rival battle can be started by pressing `A` while facing the rival.
- Oak parcel return: talking to Oak around `(4,2)` facing right advances the delivery scene.
- Lower-right lab NPC around `(8,11)` is only Oak's aide.
- Top bookshelves just say `Crammed full of POKéMON books!`.

## Route 1
- South entrance around map 0x0c, (10,28): the north tile by sign/fence is blocked.
- East-side bottom strip reaches at least x=17 but `up` there is still blocked; this is a grass/encounter trap.
- Moving left from the south area reaches the clerk around y=24; the clerk is an NPC, not geometry.
- Verified northbound route: from about `(5,18)`, go north to y=14, east along the rock barrier to about x=14, north through the opening, continue north, then loop west near the top fence and go north at about `(11,0)` to warp into Viridian.
- Viridian south return uses the fence opening around x≈20, y≈30.
- Lower Route 1 south exit fix: if blocked around `(12,31)`, move left into the lower-left lane, then go down.
- Upper grass pocket around `(10..13, 6..8)` is dangerous and easy to overprobe; bias west/north over the east dead end.
- Confirmed top finish: from around `(13,4)`, `left,left,up,up,up,up,left,up` reaches Viridian City.

## Viridian City / Pokecenter
- Entering from Route 1 places the player near (21,35).
- City sign near (16,17): `VIRIDIAN CITY - The Eternally Green Paradise`.
- East-side building labeled `MART` is the Poké Mart.
- There is a fence/rock barrier around y=28; many central/east `up` attempts from below are blocked.
- Usable crossing: from around (22,28), move left to about x=19, then north to about (19,25), then right.
- Pokecenter entrance: from outside around (22,26), `right, up` enters.
- The middle-aisle man is only an advice NPC.
- Nurse Joy position: stand at `(3,3)` facing `up` and press `A`; choose `HEAL`.
- After healing, dialog can appear sticky: if a stray `HEAL / CANCEL` choice reopens or a blank textbox remains, use `B` to clear it; from `We hope to see you again!`, `B` plus `down` successfully closes the dialog and steps away.
- Upper-city geometry:
  - The north fence line around y≈14 is blocked across a broad span.
  - Far-right rock wall near `(35,16)` is a dead end.
  - To reach upper city from the east half, work left to around `(20,16)` and then up.
  - Small house/school entrance is around `(21,15)`.
  - Gym frontage is reachable in the upper city.
- Northwest woman is only hint text about Viridian Forest/Pewter, not a trigger.
- West exit to Route 22 is at the far-left side; from about `(0,14)`, pressing `left` warps out.
- Hidden reconnect: from Route 22's far-east upper area, reaching about `(39,9)` and pressing `right` warps back into Viridian upper-left around `(0,17)`.
- Strong current hypothesis: real progression is north/Route 2/Viridian Forest, not Route 22.

## Viridian Mart
- On entry, interior may show briefly before clerk parcel dialog; a short `wait` can be correct.
- Parcel flow: advance greeting, receive `OAK's PARCEL`, then clear final goodbye line.
- After parcel dialog, free position is around `(2,5)`; to exit, walk down to about `(2,7)` then use `right, down` to align with the door.

## Route 22
- Entering from Viridian west exit places the player around `(39,6)` facing left.
- Immediate west travel works to about `(36,6)`, but farther left is blocked there; `up` is also blocked on that entry shelf.
- Going down from there reaches a long lower road around y≈14-15.
- That lower road is open almost end-to-end (`x≈2..37`) but `up` is blocked across many tested positions; both far west and far east ends are dead ends.
- Upper-right climb from the far east lower road: from about `(37,14)`, go `left x4, up,up, left, up, left, up, left, up,up` to reach around `(30,8)`.
- That upper-right area is only a small enclosed grass box around roughly `(30..33, 8..12)` plus a narrow upper strip near `(33,10..12)`; apparent exits are blocked and it only drops back to the lower road.
- New reconnect: a different far-east upper path reaches about `(39,9)`; pressing `right` there warps back into Viridian City upper-left. Treat this as a return connection, not new progression.
- Consolidation: everything verified so far on Route 22 points to a non-progressing optional area at this stage. Revisit only if testing a genuinely new upper-entry approach; otherwise deprioritize it.
- Because Route 22 grass repeatedly triggers wilds while giving little information, avoid exploratory grass steps there when HP is low.

## Route 2
- Entered Route 2 from Viridian north.
- Central path from about `(7,71)` is open north at least to about `y=57`.
- From `(7,57)`, going left reaches a west-side lane; taking that west lane north works to about `(3,48)`.
- Top-west area has a horizontal barrier/top edge: from `(3,48)`, right works to about `(7,48)`, but farther right is blocked; north is blocked there too.
- New note: at about `(8,48)` the tile(s) south are blocked, and wild encounters in this grass patch can trigger with black-screen/partial-overworld transition artifacts before the battle fully renders.
- Current battle-resolution state on turn 100: a fight ended with Squirtle leveling to 9 at `(8,48)` while battle flag still reads wild. Level-up text like `grew to level 9!` is still battle text; clear it with `A` until the command/menu or overworld visibly returns.
- Avoid grinding the same top grass patch; back out and look for a different Route 2 lane or building/gate approach from a lower, safer position.
- South fence-gap lesson: the apparent openings around `(6..11,62)` are fake from below; repeated `up` attempts there are blocked.
- Lower-left sign around `(5,66)` only reads `ROUTE 2 / VIRIDIAN CITY - PEWTER CITY`.
- Midwest tree-maze trap: the area around roughly `(5..11,51..52)` reconnects awkwardly to the top row, triggers many wilds, and gives little new information.
- Recent reconnection: from the trap area, moving to the safer west lane around `(2,57)` and then reprobeing north can reconnect to the upper row near `(2,48)`.
- New tactical note from the live screen/state: current position `(10,48)` is farther east than the old `(7..8,48)` trap points; prioritize mapping this east-side upper lane before falling back south.
- Real progress discovery: from the upper area around `(7,44)`, `left,left,left,left,up` enters the gatehouse to Viridian Forest.
- North-side outdoor sign near the forest approach says: `For poison, use ANTIDOTE! Get it at POKEMON MARTs!`
- Gatehouse exit alignment lesson: inside `MAP_0x32`, the correct north exit is along the top row near `x=5`, not the misleading top-right doorway. From around `(5,1)`, pressing `up` exits to the north-side outdoor area at about `(17,47)` on map `0x33`.
- North-side outdoor pocket partial map so far:
  - Start area near `(6,30)` is blocked above.
  - West reaches about `(2,30)` but only slightly downward there.
  - A lower route exists via the east/lower lanes, reaching at least `(14,43)` where there is an NPC/sign pocket.
  - NPC near `(14,43)` only says `They're out for POKEMON fights!`
  - South is blocked at `(14,43)`; do not keep hammering that tile.
  - Better progress came from the farther-right lane: from about `(15,39)`, moving right to `(17,39)` and then north reached at least `(17,34)`.
  - East side opens farther than first thought: reachable tiles include roughly `(19..23, 40..43)`.
  - North is blocked at least at `(22,40)`, `(23,40)`, and `(27,41)`.
  - The trainer/NPC at about `(26,33)` is a Bug Catcher who challenges immediately.
  - From around `(25,34)`, going north repeatedly reaches at least `(25,20)`; this is a real upper lane, not a dead end.
  - The upper pocket contains another Bug Catcher on the right side who also challenges, plus a sign near the upper center.
  - After the upper Bug Catcher is defeated, probe for the actual forest entrance around the upper-center/right side instead of dropping back to the lower trap lanes.

## Battle heuristics
- Early wild fights can sometimes be handled with repeated `A`, but blind mash is no longer safe once Squirtle's move list matters.
- Squirtle now knows **Tackle**, **Tail Whip**, and **Bubble**. Visible move order is **TACKLE / TAIL WHIP / BUBBLE**.
- In battle, if the move menu cursor is sitting on **TAIL WHIP**, then **down + A** selects **BUBBLE** and plain **A** wastes a turn on Tail Whip.
- In simple wild fights, deliberately choose a damaging move; if unsure, `Tackle` is a safe manual fallback.
- With low HP, avoid unnecessary battles; fleeing is preferred.
- After a battle, visible text can linger after battle-state tools think it cleared; keep advancing until overworld is visibly back.
- To run from a wild battle, use the battle menu deliberately; do not assume the cursor stayed where a previous tool left it.
- In wild battles with blank intro/send-out screens, press `A` a couple times to reach the command menu before trying RUN.
- If the screen shows move-result text like `used TACKLE!`, `Enemy ... fainted!`, `gained EXP. Points!`, or `grew to level ...`, the next useful input is usually still just `A` until the battle flow finishes.
- Do not try RUN or movement while experience/level-up text is still showing; those inputs are wasted until the text resolves.
- If accidental input opens the Pokémon submenu during battle, `B` backs out cleanly to the main battle menu.
- Reliable manual flee pattern from messy wild-battle substates:
  - If stuck in move or Pokémon submenu, press `B` until back at the 4-option main battle menu.
  - Common RUN selections that have worked in this run: `down, A` and `right, A` depending on current cursor position; if needed, `down, right, A` from FIGHT also works.
  - After `Got away safely!`, one more `A` is usually needed, then a short `wait` for the white transition back to overworld.
- After `used TACKLE!` or similar, a short `wait` can reveal the actual next menu/text more cleanly than more inputs.
- New repeated lesson from Route 2 trainer fights: **Bubble** text/animation can lag for a long time. Best pattern is usually **wait first, then single `A` presses with long settle** rather than mashing.
- Enemy move text may chain strangely across boxes (for example `attack missed! But, it failed!` after Weedle). Treat it as normal battle text and advance one box at a time.
- Trainer battles on the route approach should be handled manually, not with wild-battle mash/flee tools.
- Upper Route 2 Bug Catcher battle specifics:
  - Weedle used repeated **String Shot**; the animation/text regularly looked stuck until a wait.
  - Kakuna uses **Harden**; expect the text pair `Enemy KAKUNA used HARDEN!` then `Enemy KAKUNA's DEFENSE rose!`.
  - Because Kakuna is not threatening much immediate damage, the main risk is wasting turns with Tail Whip or mashing into animation lag, not being KO'd instantly.

## Player's House 2F lesson
- Prior wedge hypothesis was wrong.
- Long-hold movement revealed a much larger room than short presses suggested; visible room edges are not always true movement bounds.

## Tooling / harness notes
- `save_checkpoint` may fail with a filesystem 500 error; do not rely on it until a save succeeds.
- Repeated mechanical sequences worth tooling: dialog mashing, systematic coordinate probes, early battle mash, safe service-desk scans, fleeing wild battles, walk-path execution that stops on battle/map change, and clearing battle/result text until the battle flag drops.
- `probe_path` is excellent for discovering the effect of long holds because it logs coordinates after each step.
- Old `clear_battle_text` is broken (`ctx.frame is not a function`); use/keep the safer state-based replacement instead.
- In-game SAVE is distinct from harness checkpoints and appears usable as a fallback.

arguments.content: Route 2 north-side outdoor pocket top-row probe: from the upper pocket, the entire top walkable row around y=1 is traversable from at least x=16 to x=32, but pressing up anywhere tested there does NOT enter Viridian Forest. Right edge stops at x=32. From the far-right side, a vertical lane descends to at least `(32,6)`, then west is blocked by the tree/stump barrier until higher rows. A lower traverse from `(31,8)` goes left through `(30..23,8)`; up is blocked at `(23,8)` and `(21,8)`. Current live safe state after the probe is outside on map 0x33 at `(21,8)` facing up, standing in grass with Squirtle low HP (5/32), so avoid further grass probing unless necessary.

data.summary:
Turn 147 summary:

What I did
- Responded to the “10 turns stuck” warning by trying genuinely different probing in the upper-right portion of the Route 2 north-side outdoor pocket instead of retesting the top row.
- Mapped several new walkable tiles and chokepoints:
  - From `(21,8)`, rightward movement works through `(22,8)`, `(23,8)`, and farther to `(25,8)`.
  - A southward lane exists around the middle-right area:
    - `(25,8) -> (25,9) -> (25,10)` works.
  - From there, moving east/up reaches a broader open area around `(26,9)`, `(26,10)`, `(26,11)`, and farther right/up to `(31,6)` and

arguments.content:
# Strategy playbook

## Early boot / intro
- If the screen is blank white at boot, `wait` a few seconds for logo/title.
- At title/menu, `start` or `a` proceeds.
- RAM location can be stale during boot/title; trust screenshot until gameplay begins.
- On player and rival naming screens, pressing `start` accepts the default name.
- After intro, if spawned facing TV/SNES, an `A` press can reopen the inspect textbox; clear text before moving.

## General
- Default d-pad press moves about one tile.
- Long-hold d-pad presses can reveal larger walkable areas than short probes suggest.
- A advances/confirms; B cancels; START opens menu.
- If a textbox is open, movement does nothing; clear text first.
- `B` can dismiss some overworld textboxes cleanly; repeated `A` can accidentally re-talk to NPCs.
- Trust RAM for coordinates/state and screenshot for UI/world geometry.
- Probe systematically with RAM coordinates instead of retrying the same blocked tile.
- Adjacent sprites may be NPCs, not geometry; try `A` before assuming collision.
- In buildings, identify the correct service NPC before mashing.
- If a map seems impossible, test long holds before assuming corruption.
- For risky counters/NPCs, prefer single `A` presses plus controlled `B` clears.
- Blank white or black screens during transitions/outdoor probing are not always softlocks; often wait or check battle flag first.
- When a battle just ended or is mid-transition, do not trust the partially rendered screen as overworld access. Check battle flag first.
- On route signs, `A` often leaves a textbox that blocks all movement; clear with `B` before probing further.

## Current route
- We now have Squirtle Lv11.
- Oak's Parcel delivery and Pokédex progression are complete.
- Real progression continues north from Viridian onto Route 2 and toward Viridian Forest/Pewter, not Route 22.
- From Route 2 upper area around `(7,44)`, `left,left,left,left,up` enters the gatehouse; going north through it reaches the Route 2 / Viridian Forest south-side outdoor area (map 0x33).
- New upper-pocket progress: a northbound lane around `x≈25` continues far upward from the Bug Catcher area and reaches the upper Route 2 pocket near `(25,20)`.
- Upper Route 2 outdoor pocket has been explored heavily; current best next step after healing is to resume from a safe state and look for the actual forest entrance/path without blind grass probing.

## Pallet Town / Oak sequence
- Player's House 1F exit: from downstairs, the lower corridor leads to the door warp; around (2,7) on map 0x25, pressing `down` exits.
- Pallet north exit trigger: the opening is on the east side of the north path; around map 0x00, x=10, y=1, stepping north triggers Oak.
- After that event already happened, do not expect the same trigger again.
- Oak's Lab is the southwest/left large building in Pallet.
- The right-side Pallet house can heal Squirtle via NPC interaction.

## Oak's Lab
- Early lab scene requires multiple textboxes before choosing a starter.
- Verified starter: Squirtle.
- On nickname screen, `START` declines naming.
- First rival battle can be started by pressing `A` while facing the rival.
- Oak parcel return: talking to Oak around `(4,2)` facing right advances the delivery scene.
- Lower-right lab NPC around `(8,11)` is only Oak's aide.
- Top bookshelves just say `Crammed full of POKéMON books!`.

## Route 1
- South entrance around map 0x0c, (10,28): the north tile by sign/fence is blocked.
- East-side bottom strip reaches at least x=17 but `up` there is still blocked; this is a grass/encounter trap.
- Moving left from the south area reaches the clerk around y=24; the clerk is an NPC, not geometry.
- Verified northbound route: from about `(5,18)`, go north to y=14, east along the rock barrier to about x=14, north through the opening, continue north, then loop west near the top fence and go north at about `(11,0)` to warp into Viridian.
- Viridian south return uses the fence opening around x≈20, y≈30.
- Lower Route 1 south exit fix: if blocked around `(12,31)`, move left into the lower-left lane, then go down.
- Upper grass pocket around `(10..13, 6..8)` is dangerous and easy to overprobe; bias west/north over the east dead end.
- Confirmed top finish: from around `(13,4)`, `left,left,up,up,up,up,left,up` reaches Viridian City.

## Viridian City / Pokecenter
- Entering from Route 1 places the player near (21,35).
- City sign near (16,17): `VIRIDIAN CITY - The Eternally Green Paradise`.
- East-side building labeled `MART` is the Poké Mart.
- There is a fence/rock barrier around y=28; many central/east `up` attempts from below are blocked.
- Usable crossing: from around (22,28), move left to about x=19, then north to about (19,25), then right.
- Pokecenter frontage lesson: from outside near `(22,26)`, repeated `up` can fail; moving farther right to about `(24,26)` and then using `left, up` enters the Viridian Pokecenter.
- Pokecenter entrance: from outside around (22,26), `right, up` can work when aligned; if not, try the farther-right frontage alignment above.
- The middle-aisle man is only an advice NPC.
- Nurse Joy position: stand at `(3,3)` facing `up` and press `A`; choose `HEAL`.
- After healing, dialog can appear sticky: if a stray `HEAL / CANCEL` choice reopens or a blank textbox remains, use `B` to clear it; from `We hope to see you again!`, `B` plus `down` successfully closes the dialog and steps away.
- Healing-dialog reminder: once the greeting text starts (`OK. We'll need your...`), the next steps should just be advancing the standard heal flow, not movement.
- Upper-city geometry:
  - The north fence line around y≈14 is blocked across a broad span.
  - Far-right rock wall near `(35,16)` is a dead end.
  - To reach upper city from the east half, work left to around `(20,16)` and then up.
  - Small house/school entrance is around `(21,15)`.
  - Gym frontage is reachable in the upper city.
- Northwest woman is only hint text about Viridian Forest/Pewter, not a trigger.
- West exit to Route 22 is at the far-left side; from about `(0,14)`, pressing `left` warps out.
- Hidden reconnect: from Route 22's far-east upper area, reaching about `(39,9)` and pressing `right` warps back into Viridian upper-left around `(0,17)`.
- Strong current hypothesis: real progression is north/Route 2/Viridian Forest, not Route 22.

## Viridian Mart
- On entry, interior may show briefly before clerk parcel dialog; a short `wait` can be correct.
- Parcel flow: advance greeting, receive `OAK's PARCEL`, then clear final goodbye line.
- After parcel dialog, free position is around `(2,5)`; to exit, walk down to about `(2,7)` then use `right, down` to align with the door.

## Route 22
- Entering from Viridian west exit places the player around `(39,6)` facing left.
- Immediate west travel works to about `(36,6)`, but farther left is blocked there; `up` is also blocked on that entry shelf.
- Going down from there reaches a long lower road around y≈14-15.
- That lower road is open almost end-to-end (`x≈2..37`) but `up` is blocked across many tested positions; both far west and far east ends are dead ends.
- Upper-right climb from the far east lower road: from about `(37,14)`, go `left x4, up,up, left, up, left, up, left, up,up` to reach around `(30,8)`.
- That upper-right area is only a small enclosed grass box around roughly `(30..33, 8..12)` plus a narrow upper strip near `(33,10..12)`; apparent exits are blocked and it only drops back to the lower road.
- New reconnect: a different far-east upper path reaches about `(39,9)`; pressing `right` there warps back into Viridian City upper-left. Treat this as a return connection, not new progression.
- Consolidation: everything verified so far on Route 22 points to a non-progressing optional area at this stage. Revisit only if testing a genuinely new upper-entry approach; otherwise deprioritize it.
- Because Route 22 grass repeatedly triggers wilds while giving little information, avoid exploratory grass steps there when HP is low.

## Route 2
- Entered Route 2 from Viridian north.
- Central path from about `(7,71)` is open north at least to about `y=57`.
- From `(7,57)`, going left reaches a west-side lane; taking that west lane north works to about `(3,48)`.
- Top-west area has a horizontal barrier/top edge: from `(3,48)`, right works to about `(7,48)`, but farther right is blocked; north is blocked there too.
- New note: at about `(8,48)` the tile(s) south are blocked, and wild encounters in this grass patch can trigger with black-screen/partial-overworld transition artifacts before the battle fully renders.
- Current battle-resolution state on turn 100: a fight ended with Squirtle leveling to 9 at `(8,48)` while battle flag still reads wild. Level-up text like `grew to level 9!` is still battle text; clear it with `A` until the command/menu or overworld visibly returns.
- Avoid grinding the same top grass patch; back out and look for a different Route 2 lane or building/gate approach from a lower, safer position.
- South fence-gap lesson: the apparent openings around `(6..11,62)` are fake from below; repeated `up` attempts there are blocked.
- Lower-left sign around `(5,66)` only reads `ROUTE 2 / VIRIDIAN CITY - PEWTER CITY`.
- Midwest tree-maze trap: the area around roughly `(5..11,51..52)` reconnects awkwardly to the top row, triggers many wilds, and gives little new information.
- Recent reconnection: from the trap area, moving to the safer west lane around `(2,57)` and then reprobeing north can reconnect to the upper row near `(2,48)`.
- New tactical note from the live screen/state: current position `(10,48)` is farther east than the old `(7..8,48)` trap points; prioritize mapping this east-side upper lane before falling back south.
- Real progress discovery: from the upper area around `(7,44)`, `left,left,left,left,up` enters the gatehouse to Viridian Forest.
- North-side outdoor sign near the forest approach says: `For poison, use ANTIDOTE! Get it at POKEMON MARTs!`
- Gatehouse exit alignment lesson: inside `MAP_0x32`, the correct north exit is along the top row near `x=5`, not the misleading top-right doorway. From around `(5,1)`, pressing `up` exits to the north-side outdoor area at about `(17,47)` on map `0x33`.
- North-side outdoor pocket partial map so far:
  - Start area near `(6,30)` is blocked above.
  - West reaches about `(2,30)` but only slightly downward there.
  - A lower route exists via the east/lower lanes, reaching at least `(14,43)` where there is an NPC/sign pocket.
  - NPC near `(14,43)` only says `They're out for POKEMON fights!`
  - South is blocked at `(14,43)`; do not keep hammering that tile.
  - Better progress came from the farther-right lane: from about `(15,39)`, moving right to `(17,39)` and then north reached at least `(17,34)`.
  - East side opens farther than first thought: reachable tiles include roughly `(19..23, 40..43)`.
  - North is blocked at least at `(22,40)`, `(23,40)`, and `(27,41)`.
  - The trainer/NPC at about `(26,33)` is a Bug Catcher who challenges immediately.
  - From around `(25,34)`, going north repeatedly reaches at least `(25,20)`; this is a real upper lane, not a dead end.
  - The upper pocket contains another Bug Catcher on the right side who also challenges, plus a sign near the upper center.
  - The entire top walkable outside row around `y=1` from roughly `x=16..32` is a dead top boundary; `up` does not enter the forest there.
  - Far-right boundary caps around `x=32`, with a downward lane on that side.
  - After the upper Bug Catcher is defeated, probe for the actual forest entrance around the upper-center/right side or via a side/downward choke, not by re-testing the top row.
- Safe retreat lesson from low HP: if stuck in the upper pocket with 5 HP, retreat to Viridian rather than taking more grass steps; the Pokecenter is reachable without further Route 2 battles once back in town.

## Battle heuristics
- Early wild fights can sometimes be handled with repeated `A`, but blind mash is no longer safe once Squirtle's move list matters.
- Squirtle now knows **Tackle**, **Tail Whip**, and **Bubble**. Visible move order is **TACKLE / TAIL WHIP / BUBBLE**.
- In battle, if the move menu cursor is sitting on **TAIL WHIP**, then **down + A** selects **BUBBLE** and plain **A** wastes a turn on Tail Whip.
- In simple wild fights, deliberately choose a damaging move; if unsure, `Tackle` is a safe manual fallback.
- With low HP, avoid unnecessary battles; fleeing is preferred.
- After a battle, visible text can linger after battle-state tools think it cleared; keep advancing until overworld is visibly back.
- To run from a wild battle, use the battle menu deliberately; do not assume the cursor stayed where a previous tool left it.
- In wild battles with blank intro/send-out screens, press `A` a couple times to reach the command menu before trying RUN.
- If the screen shows move-result text like `used TACKLE!`, `Enemy ... fainted!`, `gained EXP. Points!`, or `grew to level ...`, the next useful input is usually still just `A` until the battle flow finishes.
- Do not try RUN or movement while experience/level-up text is still showing; those inputs are wasted until the text resolves.
- If accidental input opens the Pokémon submenu during battle, `B` backs out cleanly to the main battle menu.
- Reliable manual flee pattern from messy wild-battle substates:
  - If stuck in move or Pokémon submenu, press `B` until back at the 4-option main battle menu.
  - Common RUN selections that have worked in this run: `down, A` and `right, A` depending on current cursor position; if needed, `down, right, A` from FIGHT also works.
  - After `Got away safely!`, one more `A` is usually needed, then a short `wait` for the white transition back to overworld.
- After `used TACKLE!` or similar, a short `wait` can reveal the actual next menu/text more cleanly than more inputs.
- New repeated lesson from Route 2 trainer fights: **Bubble** text/animation can lag for a long time. Best pattern is usually **wait first, then single `A` presses with long settle** rather than mashing.
- Enemy move text may chain strangely across boxes (for example `attack missed! But, it failed!` after Weedle). Treat it as normal battle text and advance one box at a time.
- Trainer battles on the route approach should be handled manually, not with wild-battle mash/flee tools.
- Upper Route 2 Bug Catcher battle specifics:
  - Weedle used repeated **String Shot**; the animation/text regularly looked stuck until a wait.
  - Kakuna uses **Harden**; expect the text pair `Enemy KAKUNA used HARDEN!` then `Enemy KAKUNA's DEFENSE rose!`.
  - Because Kakuna is not threatening much immediate damage, the main risk is wasting turns with Tail Whip or mashing into animation lag, not being KO'd instantly.

## Player's House 2F lesson
- Prior wedge hypothesis was wrong.
- Long-hold movement revealed a much larger room than short presses suggested; visible room edges are not always true movement bounds.

## Tooling / harness notes
- `save_checkpoint` may fail with a filesystem 500 error; do not rely on it until a save succeeds.
- Repeated mechanical sequences worth tooling: dialog mashing, systematic coordinate probes, early battle mash, safe service-desk scans, fleeing wild battles, walk-path execution that stops on battle/map change, and clearing battle/result text until the battle flag drops.
- `probe_path` is excellent for discovering the effect of long holds because it logs coordinates after each step.
- Old `clear_battle_text` is broken (`ctx.frame is not a function`); use/keep the safer state-based replacement instead.
- In-game SAVE is distinct from harness checkpoints and appears usable as a fallback.

data.name: update_playbook

arguments.content:
# Strategy playbook

## Early boot / intro
- If the screen is blank white at boot, `wait` a few seconds for logo/title.
- At title/menu, `start` or `a` proceeds.
- RAM location can be stale during boot/title; trust screenshot until gameplay begins.
- On player and rival naming screens, pressing `start` accepts the default name.
- After intro, if spawned facing TV/SNES, an `A` press can reopen the inspect textbox; clear text before moving.

## General
- Default d-pad press moves about one tile.
- Long-hold d-pad presses can reveal larger walkable areas than short probes suggest.
- A advances/confirms; B cancels; START opens menu.
- If a textbox is open, movement does nothing; clear text first.
- `B` can dismiss some overworld textboxes cleanly; repeated `A` can accidentally re-talk to NPCs.
- Trust RAM for coordinates/state and screenshot for UI/world geometry.
- Probe systematically with RAM coordinates instead of retrying the same blocked tile.
- Adjacent sprites may be NPCs, not geometry; try `A` before assuming collision.
- In buildings, identify the correct service NPC before mashing.
- If a map seems impossible, test long holds before assuming corruption.
- For risky counters/NPCs, prefer single `A` presses plus controlled `B` clears.
- Blank white or black screens during transitions/outdoor probing are not always softlocks; often wait or check battle flag first.
- When a battle just ended or is mid-transition, do not trust the partially rendered screen as overworld access. Check battle flag first.
- On route signs, `A` often leaves a textbox that blocks all movement; clear with `B` before probing further.
- Pokecenter door transitions can leave a fully black screen briefly after RAM already reports a map change; wait ~60-90 frames instead of immediately pressing opposite movement and re-entering.

## Current route
- We now have Squirtle Lv11.
- Oak's Parcel delivery and Pokédex progression are complete.
- Real progression continues north from Viridian onto Route 2 and toward Viridian Forest/Pewter, not Route 22.
- From Route 2 upper area around `(7,44)`, `left,left,left,left,up` enters the gatehouse; going north through it reaches the Route 2 / Viridian Forest south-side outdoor area (map 0x33).
- New upper-pocket progress: a northbound lane around `x≈25` continues far upward from the Bug Catcher area and reaches the upper Route 2 pocket near `(25,20)`.
- Upper Route 2 outdoor pocket has been explored heavily; current best next step after healing is to resume from a safe state and look for the actual forest entrance/path without blind grass probing.

## Pallet Town / Oak sequence
- Player's House 1F exit: from downstairs, the lower corridor leads to the door warp; around (2,7) on map 0x25, pressing `down` exits.
- Pallet north exit trigger: the opening is on the east side of the north path; around map 0x00, x=10, y=1, stepping north triggers Oak.
- After that event already happened, do not expect the same trigger again.
- Oak's Lab is the southwest/left large building in Pallet.
- The right-side Pallet house can heal Squirtle via NPC interaction.

## Oak's Lab
- Early lab scene requires multiple textboxes before choosing a starter.
- Verified starter: Squirtle.
- On nickname screen, `START` declines naming.
- First rival battle can be started by pressing `A` while facing the rival.
- Oak parcel return: talking to Oak around `(4,2)` facing right advances the delivery scene.
- Lower-right lab NPC around `(8,11)` is only Oak's aide.
- Top bookshelves just say `Crammed full of POKéMON books!`.

## Route 1
- South entrance around map 0x0c, (10,28): the north tile by sign/fence is blocked.
- East-side bottom strip reaches at least x=17 but `up` there is still blocked; this is a grass/encounter trap.
- Moving left from the south area reaches the clerk around y=24; the clerk is an NPC, not geometry.
- Verified northbound route: from about `(5,18)`, go north to y=14, east along the rock barrier to about x=14, north through the opening, continue north, then loop west near the top fence and go north at about `(11,0)` to warp into Viridian.
- Viridian south return uses the fence opening around x≈20, y≈30.
- Lower Route 1 south exit fix: if blocked around `(12,31)`, move left into the lower-left lane, then go down.
- Upper grass pocket around `(10..13, 6..8)` is dangerous and easy to overprobe; bias west/north over the east dead end.
- Confirmed top finish: from around `(13,4)`, `left,left,up,up,up,up,left,up` reaches Viridian City.

## Viridian City / Pokecenter
- Entering from Route 1 places the player near (21,35).
- City sign near (16,17): `VIRIDIAN CITY - The Eternally Green Paradise`.
- East-side building labeled `MART` is the Poké Mart.
- There is a fence/rock barrier around y=28; many central/east `up` attempts from below are blocked.
- Usable crossing: from around (22,28), move left to about x=19, then north to about (19,25), then right.
- Pokecenter frontage lesson: from outside near `(22,26)`, repeated `up` can fail; moving farther right to about `(24,26)` and then using `left, up` enters the Viridian Pokecenter.
- Pokecenter entrance: from outside around (22,26), `right, up` can work when aligned; if not, try the farther-right frontage alignment above.
- The middle-aisle man is only an advice NPC.
- Nurse Joy position: stand at `(3,3)` facing `up` and press `A`; choose `HEAL`.
- After healing, dialog can appear sticky: if a stray `HEAL / CANCEL` choice reopens or a blank textbox remains, use `B` to clear it; from `We hope to see you again!`, `B` plus `down` successfully closes the dialog and steps away.
- Healing-dialog reminder: once the greeting text starts (`OK. We'll need your...`), the next steps should just be advancing the standard heal flow, not movement.
- Upper-city geometry:
  - The north fence line around y≈14 is blocked across a broad span.
  - Far-right rock wall near `(35,16)` is a dead end.
  - To reach upper city from the east half, work left to around `(20,16)` and then up.
  - Small house/school entrance is around `(21,15)`.
  - Gym frontage is reachable in the upper city.
- Northwest woman is only hint text about Viridian Forest/Pewter, not a trigger.
- West exit to Route 22 is at the far-left side; from about `(0,14)`, pressing `left` warps out.
- Hidden reconnect: from Route 22's far-east upper area, reaching about `(39,9)` and pressing `right` warps back into Viridian upper-left around `(0,17)`.
- Strong current hypothesis: real progression is north/Route 2/Viridian Forest, not Route 22.

## Viridian Mart
- On entry, interior may show briefly before clerk parcel dialog; a short `wait` can be correct.
- Parcel flow: advance greeting, receive `OAK's PARCEL`, then clear final goodbye line.
- After parcel dialog, free position is around `(2,5)`; to exit, walk down to about `(2,7)` then use `right, down` to align with the door.

## Route 22
- Entering from Viridian west exit places the player around `(39,6)` facing left.
- Immediate west travel works to about `(36,6)`, but farther left is blocked there; `up` is also blocked on that entry shelf.
- Going down from there reaches a long lower road around y≈14-15.
- That lower road is open almost end-to-end (`x≈2..37`) but `up` is blocked across many tested positions; both far west and far east ends are dead ends.
- Upper-right climb from the far east lower road: from about `(37,14)`, go `left x4, up,up, left, up, left, up, left, up,up` to reach around `(30,8)`.
- That upper-right area is only a small enclosed grass box around roughly `(30..33, 8..12)` plus a narrow upper strip near `(33,10..12)`; apparent exits are blocked and it only drops back to the lower road.
- New reconnect: a different far-east upper path reaches about `(39,9)`; pressing `right` there warps back into Viridian City upper-left. Treat this as a return connection, not new progression.
- Consolidation: everything verified so far on Route 22 points to a non-progressing optional area at this stage. Revisit only if testing a genuinely new upper-entry approach; otherwise deprioritize it.
- Because Route 22 grass repeatedly triggers wilds while giving little information, avoid exploratory grass steps there when HP is low.

## Route 2
- Entered Route 2 from Viridian north.
- Central path from about `(7,71)` is open north at least to about `y=57`.
- From `(7,57)`, going left reaches a west-side lane; taking that west lane north works to about `(3,48)`.
- Top-west area has a horizontal barrier/top edge: from `(3,48)`, right works to about `(7,48)`, but farther right is blocked; north is blocked there too.
- New note: at about `(8,48)` the tile(s) south are blocked, and wild encounters in this grass patch can trigger with black-screen/partial-overworld transition artifacts before the battle fully renders.
- Current battle-resolution state on turn 100: a fight ended with Squirtle leveling to 9 at `(8,48)` while battle flag still reads wild. Level-up text like `grew to level 9!` is still battle text; clear it with `A` until the command/menu or overworld visibly returns.
- Avoid grinding the same top grass patch; back out and look for a different Route 2 lane or building/gate approach from a lower, safer position.
- South fence-gap lesson: the apparent openings around `(6..11,62)` are fake from below; repeated `up` attempts there are blocked.
- Lower-left sign around `(5,66)` only reads `ROUTE 2 / VIRIDIAN CITY - PEWTER CITY`.
- Midwest tree-maze trap: the area around roughly `(5..11,51..52)` reconnects awkwardly to the top row, triggers many wilds, and gives little new information.
- Recent reconnection: from the trap area, moving to the safer west lane around `(2,57)` and then reprobeing north can reconnect to the upper row near `(2,48)`.
- New tactical note from the live screen/state: current position `(10,48)` is farther east than the old `(7..8,48)` trap points; prioritize mapping this east-side upper lane before falling back south.
- Real progress discovery: from the upper area around `(7,44)`, `left,left,left,left,up` enters the gatehouse to Viridian Forest.
- North-side outdoor sign near the forest approach says: `For poison, use ANTIDOTE! Get it at POKEMON MARTs!`
- Gatehouse exit alignment lesson: inside `MAP_0x32`, the correct north exit is along the top row near `x=5`, not the misleading top-right doorway. From around `(5,1)`, pressing `up` exits to the north-side outdoor area at about `(17,47)` on map `0x33`.
- North-side outdoor pocket partial map so far:
  - Start area near `(6,30)` is blocked above.
  - West reaches about `(2,30)` but only slightly downward there.
  - A lower route exists via the east/lower lanes, reaching at least `(14,43)` where there is an NPC/sign pocket.
  - NPC near `(14,43)` only says `They're out for POKEMON fights!`
  - South is blocked at `(14,43)`; do not keep hammering that tile.
  - Better progress came from the farther-right lane: from about `(15,39)`, moving right to `(17,39)` and then north reached at least `(17,34)`.
  - East side opens farther than first thought: reachable tiles include roughly `(19..23, 40..43)`.
  - North is blocked at least at `(22,40)`, `(23,40)`, and `(27,41)`.
  - The trainer/NPC at about `(26,33)` is a Bug Catcher who challenges immediately.
  - From around `(25,34)`, going north repeatedly reaches at least `(25,20)`; this is a real upper lane, not a dead end.
  - The upper pocket contains another Bug Catcher on the right side who also challenges, plus a sign near the upper center.
  - The entire top walkable outside row around `y=1` from roughly `x=16..32` is a dead top boundary; `up` does not enter the forest there.
  - Far-right boundary caps around `x=32`, with a downward lane on that side.
  - After the upper Bug Catcher is defeated, probe for the actual forest entrance around the upper-center/right side or via a side/downward choke, not by re-testing the top row.
- Safe retreat lesson from low HP: if stuck in the upper pocket with 5 HP, retreat to Viridian rather than taking more grass steps; the Pokecenter is reachable without further Route 2 battles once back in town.

## Battle heuristics
- Early wild fights can sometimes be handled with repeated `A`, but blind mash is no longer safe once Squirtle's move list matters.
- Squirtle now knows **Tackle**, **Tail Whip**, and **Bubble**. Visible move order is **TACKLE / TAIL WHIP / BUBBLE**.
- In battle, if the move menu cursor is sitting on **TAIL WHIP**, then **down + A** selects **BUBBLE** and plain **A** wastes a turn on Tail Whip.
- In simple wild fights, deliberately choose a damaging move; if unsure, `Tackle` is a safe manual fallback.
- With low HP, avoid unnecessary battles; fleeing is preferred.
- After a battle, visible text can linger after battle-state tools think it cleared; keep advancing until overworld is visibly back.
- To run from a wild battle, use the battle menu deliberately; do not assume the cursor stayed where a previous tool left it.
- In wild battles with blank intro/send-out screens, press `A` a couple times to reach the command menu before trying RUN.
- If the screen shows move-result text like `used TACKLE!`, `Enemy ... fainted!`, `gained EXP. Points!`, or `grew to level ...`, the next useful input is usually still just `A` until the battle flow finishes.
- Do not try RUN or movement while experience/level-up text is still showing; those inputs are wasted until the text resolves.
- If accidental input opens the Pokémon submenu during battle, `B` backs out cleanly to the main battle menu.
- Reliable manual flee pattern from messy wild-battle substates:
  - If stuck in move or Pokémon submenu, press `B` until back at the 4-option main battle menu.
  - Common RUN selections that have worked in this run: `down, A` and `right, A` depending on current cursor position; if needed, `down, right, A` from FIGHT also works.
  - After `Got away safely!`, one more `A` is usually needed, then a short `wait` for the white transition back to overworld.
- After `used TACKLE!` or similar, a short `wait` can reveal the actual next menu/text more cleanly than more inputs.
- New repeated lesson from Route 2 trainer fights: **Bubble** text/animation can lag for a long time. Best pattern is usually **wait first, then single `A` presses with long settle** rather than mashing.
- Enemy move text may chain strangely across boxes (for example `attack missed! But, it failed!` after Weedle). Treat it as normal battle text and advance one box at a time.
- Trainer battles on the route approach should be handled manually, not with wild-battle mash/flee tools.
- Upper Route 2 Bug Catcher battle specifics:
  - Weedle used repeated **String Shot**; the animation/text regularly looked stuck until a wait.
  - Kakuna uses **Harden**; expect the text pair `Enemy KAKUNA used HARDEN!` then `Enemy KAKUNA's DEFENSE rose!`.
  - Because Kakuna is not threatening much immediate damage, the main risk is wasting turns with Tail Whip or mashing into animation lag, not being KO'd instantly.

## Player's House 2F lesson
- Prior wedge hypothesis was wrong.
- Long-hold movement revealed a much larger room than short presses suggested; visible room edges are not always true movement bounds.

## Tooling / harness notes
- `save_checkpoint` may fail with a filesystem 500 error; do not rely on it until a save succeeds.
- Repeated mechanical sequences worth tooling: dialog mashing, systematic coordinate probes, early battle mash, safe service-desk scans, fleeing wild battles, walk-path execution that stops on battle/map change, and clearing battle/result text until the battle flag drops.
- `probe_path` is excellent for discovering the effect of long holds because it logs coordinates after each step.
- Old `clear_battle_text` is broken (`ctx.frame is not a function`); use/keep the safer state-based replacement instead.
- In-game SAVE is distinct from harness checkpoints and appears usable as a fallback.

Turn 152 summary:

What I did
- Left the Viridian Pokecenter successfully.
- Learned that Pokecenter door transitions can show a fully black screen briefly even after RAM already says the map changed; waiting resolves it cleanly.
- Accidentally re-entered the Pokecenter once by pressing `up` too soon from directly in front of the door, then exited again correctly.
- Crossed from the Pokecenter side of Viridian into the upper city using the known route:
  - from `(23,26)` went left to about `(19,26)`
  - then north to about `(19,15)`
- Probed the northwest and northeast upper-city fence lines i
