data.name: update_playbook

arguments.content:
# Strategy playbook

## Early boot / intro
- If the screen is blank white at boot, `wait` a few seconds for logo/title.
- At title/menu, `start` or `a` proceeds.
- RAM location can be stale during boot/title; trust screenshot until gameplay begins.
- On player and rival naming screens, pressing `start` accepts the default name.
- After intro, if spawned facing TV/SNES, an `A` press can reopen the inspect textbox; clear text before moving.

## General
- Default d-pad press moves about one tile.
- Long-hold d-pad presses can reveal larger walkable areas than short probes suggest.
- A advances/confirms; B cancels; START opens menu.
- If a textbox is open, movement does nothing; clear text first.
- `B` can dismiss some overworld textboxes cleanly; repeated `A` can accidentally re-talk to NPCs.
- Trust RAM for coordinates/state and screenshot for UI/world geometry.
- Probe systematically with RAM coordinates instead of retrying the same blocked tile.
- Adjacent sprites may be NPCs, not geometry; try `A` before assuming collision.
- In buildings, identify the correct service NPC before mashing.
- If a map seems impossible, test long holds before assuming corruption.
- For risky counters/NPCs, prefer single `A` presses plus controlled `B` clears.
- Blank white or black screens during transitions/outdoor probing are not always softlocks; often wait or check battle flag first.
- When a battle just ended or is mid-transition, do not trust the partially rendered screen as overworld access. Check battle flag first.
- On route signs, `A` often leaves a textbox that blocks all movement; clear with `B` before probing further.
- Pokecenter door transitions can leave a fully black screen briefly after RAM already reports a map change; wait ~60-90 frames instead of immediately pressing opposite movement and re-entering.
- If a doorway seems to fail, test the actual door column with one lateral adjustment before repeating vertical presses.

## Current route
- We now have Squirtle Lv11.
- Oak's Parcel delivery and Pokédex progression are complete.
- Real progression continues north from Viridian onto Route 2 and toward Viridian Forest/Pewter, not Route 22.
- From Route 2 upper area around `(7,44)`, `left,left,left,left,up` enters the gatehouse; going north through it reaches the Route 2 / Viridian Forest south-side outdoor area (map 0x33).
- New upper-pocket progress: a northbound lane around `x≈25` continues far upward from the Bug Catcher area and reaches the upper Route 2 pocket near `(25,20)`.
- Upper Route 2 outdoor pocket has been explored heavily; current best next step after healing is to resume from a safe state and look for the actual forest entrance/path without blind grass probing.

## Pallet Town / Oak sequence
- Player's House 1F exit: from downstairs, the lower corridor leads to the door warp; around (2,7) on map 0x25, pressing `down` exits.
- Pallet north exit trigger: the opening is on the east side of the north path; around map 0x00, x=10, y=1, stepping north triggers Oak.
- After that event already happened, do not expect the same trigger again.
- Oak's Lab is the southwest/left large building in Pallet.
- The right-side Pallet house can heal Squirtle via NPC interaction.

## Oak's Lab
- Early lab scene requires multiple textboxes before choosing a starter.
- Verified starter: Squirtle.
- On nickname screen, `START` declines naming.
- First rival battle can be started by pressing `A` while facing the rival.
- Oak parcel return: talking to Oak around `(4,2)` facing right advances the delivery scene.
- Lower-right lab NPC around `(8,11)` is only Oak's aide.
- Top bookshelves just say `Crammed full of POKéMON books!`.

## Route 1
- South entrance around map 0x0c, (10,28): the north tile by sign/fence is blocked.
- East-side bottom strip reaches at least x=17 but `up` there is still blocked; this is a grass/encounter trap.
- Moving left from the south area reaches the clerk around y=24; the clerk is an NPC, not geometry.
- Verified northbound route: from about `(5,18)`, go north to y=14, east along the rock barrier to about x=14, north through the opening, continue north, then loop west near the top fence and go north at about `(11,0)` to warp into Viridian.
- Viridian south return uses the fence opening around x≈20, y≈30.
- Lower Route 1 south exit fix: if blocked around `(12,31)`, move left into the lower-left lane, then go down.
- Upper grass pocket around `(10..13, 6..8)` is dangerous and easy to overprobe; bias west/north over the east dead end.
- Confirmed top finish: from around `(13,4)`, `left,left,up,up,up,up,left,up` reaches Viridian City.

## Viridian City / Pokecenter
- Entering from Route 1 places the player near (21,35).
- City sign near (16,17): `VIRIDIAN CITY - The Eternally Green Paradise`.
- East-side building labeled `MART` is the Poké Mart.
- There is a fence/rock barrier around y=28; many central/east `up` attempts from below are blocked.
- Usable crossing: from around (22,28), move left to about x=19, then north to about (19,25), then right.
- Pokecenter frontage lesson: from outside near `(22,26)`, repeated `up` can fail; moving farther right to about `(24,26)` and then using `left, up` enters the Viridian Pokecenter.
- Pokecenter entrance: from outside around (22,26), `right, up` can work when aligned; if not, try the farther-right frontage alignment above.
- The middle-aisle man is only an advice NPC.
- Nurse Joy position: stand at `(3,3)` facing `up` and press `A`; choose `HEAL`.
- After healing, dialog can appear sticky: if a stray `HEAL / CANCEL` choice reopens or a blank textbox remains, use `B` to clear it; from `We hope to see you again!`, `B` plus `down` successfully closes the dialog and steps away.
- Healing-dialog reminder: once the greeting text starts (`OK. We'll need your...`), the next steps should just be advancing the standard heal flow, not movement.
- Upper-city geometry:
  - The north fence line around y≈14 is blocked across a broad span.
  - Far-right rock wall near `(35,16)` is a dead end.
  - To reach upper city from the east half, work left to around `(20,16)` and then up.
  - Small house/school entrance is around `(21,15)`.
  - Gym frontage is reachable in the upper city.
- Northwest woman is only hint text about Viridian Forest/Pewter, not a trigger.
- West exit to Route 22 is at the far-left side; from about `(0,14)`, pressing `left` warps out.
- Hidden reconnect: from Route 22's far-east upper area, reaching about `(39,9)` and pressing `right` warps back into Viridian upper-left around `(0,17)`.
- Strong current hypothesis: real progression is north/Route 2/Viridian Forest, not Route 22.
- New upper-left house note: the building entered from around `(21,9)` is just `Viridian House` (map `0x2c`), non-progressing. Exit back out from around `(2,7)` with `down`; avoid re-entering it while routing to Route 2.

## Viridian Mart
- On entry, interior may show briefly before clerk parcel dialog; a short `wait` can be correct.
- Parcel flow: advance greeting, receive `OAK's PARCEL`, then clear final goodbye line.
- After parcel dialog, free position is around `(2,5)`; to exit, walk down to about `(2,7)` then use `right, down` to align with the door.

## Route 22
- Entering from Viridian west exit places the player around `(39,6)` facing left.
- Immediate west travel works to about `(36,6)`, but farther left is blocked there; `up` is also blocked on that entry shelf.
- Going down from there reaches a long lower road around y≈14-15.
- That lower road is open almost end-to-end (`x≈2..37`) but `up` is blocked across many tested positions; both far west and far east ends are dead ends.
- Upper-right climb from the far east lower road: from about `(37,14)`, go `left x4, up,up, left, up, left, up, left, up,up` to reach around `(30,8)`.
- That upper-right area is only a small enclosed grass box around roughly `(30..33, 8..12)` plus a narrow upper strip near `(33,10..12)`; apparent exits are blocked and it only drops back to the lower road.
- New reconnect: a different far-east upper path reaches about `(39,9)`; pressing `right` there warps back into Viridian City upper-left. Treat this as a return connection, not new progression.
- Consolidation: everything verified so far on Route 22 points to a non-progressing optional area at this stage. Revisit only if testing a genuinely new upper-entry approach; otherwise deprioritize it.
- Because Route 22 grass repeatedly triggers wilds while giving little information, avoid exploratory grass steps there when HP is low.

## Route 2
- Entered Route 2 from Viridian north.
- Central path from about `(7,71)` is open north at least to about `y=57`.
- From `(7,57)`, going left reaches a west-side lane; taking that west lane north works to about `(3,48)`.
- Top-west area has a horizontal barrier/top edge: from `(3,48)`, right works to about `(7,48)`, but farther right is blocked; north is blocked there too.
- New note: at about `(8,48)` the tile(s) south are blocked, and wild encounters in this grass patch can trigger with black-screen/partial-overworld transition artifacts before the battle fully renders.
- Current battle-resolution state on turn 100: a fight ended with Squirtle leveling to 9 at `(8,48)` while battle flag still reads wild. Level-up text like `grew to level 9!` is still battle text; clear it with `A` until the command/menu or overworld visibly returns.
- Avoid grinding the same top grass patch; back out and look for a different Route 2 lane or building/gate approach from a lower, safer position.
- South fence-gap lesson: the apparent openings around `(6..11,62)` are fake from below; repeated `up` attempts there are blocked.
- Lower-left sign around `(5,66)` only reads `ROUTE 2 / VIRIDIAN CITY - PEWTER CITY`.
- Midwest tree-maze trap: the area around roughly `(5..11,51..52)` reconnects awkwardly to the top row, triggers many wilds, and gives little new information.
- Recent reconnection: from the trap area, moving to the safer west lane around `(2,57)` and then reprobeing north can reconnect to the upper row near `(2,48)`.
- New tactical note from the live screen/state: current position `(10,48)` is farther east than the old `(7..8,48)` trap points; prioritize mapping this east-side upper lane before falling back south.
- Real progress discovery: from the upper area around `(7,44)`, `left,left,left,left,up` enters the gatehouse to Viridian Forest.
- North-side outdoor sign near the forest approach says: `For poison, use ANTIDOTE! Get it at POKEMON MARTs!`
- Gatehouse exit alignment lesson: inside `MAP_0x32`, the correct north exit is along the top row near `x=5`, not the misleading top-right doorway. From around `(5,1)`, pressing `up` exits to the north-side outdoor area at about `(17,47)` on map `0x33`.
- North-side outdoor pocket partial map so far:
  - Start area near `(6,30)` is blocked above.
  - West reaches about `(2,30)` but only slightly downward there.
  - A lower route exists via the east/lower lanes, reaching at least `(14,43)` where there is an NPC/sign pocket.
  - NPC near `(14,43)` only says `They're out for POKEMON fights!`
  - South is blocked at `(14,43)`; do not keep hammering that tile.
  - Better progress came from the farther-right lane: from about `(15,39)`, moving right to `(17,39)` and then north reached at least `(17,34)`.
  - East side opens farther than first thought: reachable tiles include roughly `(19..23, 40..43)`.
  - North is blocked at least at `(22,40)`, `(23,40)`, and `(27,41)`.
  - The trainer/NPC at about `(26,33)` is a Bug Catcher who challenges immediately.
  - From around `(25,34)`, going north repeatedly reaches at least `(25,20)`; this is a real upper lane, not a dead end.
  - The upper pocket contains another Bug Catcher on the right side who also challenges, plus a sign near the upper center.
  - The entire top walkable outside row around `y=1` from roughly `x=16..32` is a dead top boundary; `up` does not enter the forest there.
  - Far-right boundary caps around `x=32`, with a downward lane on that side.
  - After the upper Bug Catcher is defeated, probe for the actual forest entrance around the upper-center/right side or via a side/downward choke, not by re-testing the top row.
- Safe retreat lesson from low HP: if stuck in the upper pocket with 5 HP, retreat to Viridian rather than taking more grass steps; the Pokecenter is reachable without further Route 2 battles once back in town.

## Battle heuristics
- Early wild fights can sometimes be handled with repeated `A`, but blind mash is no longer safe once Squirtle's move list matters.
- Squirtle now knows **Tackle**, **Tail Whip**, and **Bubble**. Visible move order is **TACKLE / TAIL WHIP / BUBBLE**.
- In battle, if the move menu cursor is sitting on **TAIL WHIP**, then **down + A** selects **BUBBLE** and plain **A** wastes a turn on Tail Whip.
- In simple wild fights, deliberately choose a damaging move; if unsure, `Tackle` is a safe manual fallback.
- With low HP, avoid unnecessary battles; fleeing is preferred.
- After a battle, visible text can linger after battle-state tools think it cleared; keep advancing until overworld is visibly back.
- To run from a wild battle, use the battle menu deliberately; do not assume the cursor stayed where a previous tool left it.
- In wild battles with blank intro/send-out screens, press `A` a couple times to reach the command menu before trying RUN.
- If the screen shows move-result text like `used TACKLE!`, `Enemy ... fainted!`, `gained EXP. Points!`, or `grew to level ...`, the next useful input is usually still just `A` until the battle flow finishes.
- Do not try RUN or movement while experience/level-up text is still showing; those inputs are wasted until the text resolves.
- If accidental input opens the Pokémon submenu during battle, `B` backs out cleanly to the main battle menu.
- Reliable manual flee pattern from messy wild-battle substates:
  - If stuck in move or Pokémon submenu, press `B` until back at the 4-option main battle menu.
  - Common RUN selections that have worked in this run: `down, A` and `right, A` depending on current cursor position; if needed, `down, right, A` from FIGHT also works.
  - After `Got away safely!`, one more `A` is usually needed, then a short `wait` for the white transition back to overworld.
- After `used TACKLE!` or similar, a short `wait` can reveal the actual next menu/text more cleanly than more inputs.
- New repeated lesson from Route 2 trainer fights: **Bubble** text/animation can lag for a long time. Best pattern is usually **wait first, then single `A` presses with long settle** rather than mashing.
- Enemy move text may chain strangely across boxes (for example `attack missed! But, it failed!` after Weedle). Treat it as normal battle text and advance one box at a time.
- Trainer battles on the route approach should be handled manually, not with wild-battle mash/flee tools.
- Upper Route 2 Bug Catcher battle specifics:
  - Weedle used repeated **String Shot**; the animation/text regularly looked stuck until a wait.
  - Kakuna uses **Harden**; expect the text pair `Enemy KAKUNA used HARDEN!` then `Enemy KAKUNA's DEFENSE rose!`.
  - Because Kakuna is not threatening much immediate damage, the main risk is wasting turns with Tail Whip or mashing into animation lag, not being KO'd instantly.

## Player's House 2F lesson
- Prior wedge hypothesis was wrong.
- Long-hold movement revealed a much larger room than short presses suggested; visible room edges are not always true movement bounds.

## Tooling / harness notes
- `save_checkpoint` may fail with a filesystem 500 error; do not rely on it until a save succeeds.
- Repeated mechanical sequences worth tooling: dialog mashing, systematic coordinate probes, early battle mash, safe service-desk scans, fleeing wild battles, walk-path execution that stops on battle/map change, and clearing battle/result text until the battle flag drops.
- `probe_path` is excellent for discovering the effect of long holds because it logs coordinates after each step.
- Old `clear_battle_text` is broken (`ctx.frame is not a function`); use/keep the safer state-based replacement instead.
- In-game SAVE is distinct from harness checkpoints and appears usable as a fallback.

data.summary:
Turn 153 summary:

What I did
- Responded to the “stuck” warning by trying genuinely different exploration instead of repeating the Route 2 probes.
- Mapped the upper-left Viridian area around the Gym and found a new enterable building:
  - from Gym frontage, moving left to about `(20,10)` showed north was blocked there
  - moving farther left/down/up reached a doorway around Viridian `(21,9)`
  - pressing `up` there entered **Viridian House** (`map 0x2c`)
- Probed the house interior and confirmed it is a normal NPC house, not progression:
  - interior walkable area includes roughly `(2..4, 5.
milestones: Entered Viridian House for the first time

- Exited the non-progress house in Viridian after a couple messy re-entry/transition tests.
- Re-probed the upper-left/north side of Viridian City instead of staying stuck around the house frontage.
- Found a clean route from the house area back to the real north exit:

  - then north through `(18,8) -> (19,6) -> (18,5) -> (18,4) -> (18,2) -> (17,1) -> (18,0)`
  - pressing `up` from **Viridian `(18,0)`** successfully warps to **Route 2** at about `(8,71)`

  - advanced past `Enemy KAKUNA's DEFENSE rose!`
  - then `A, down, right, A` reached RUN

arguments.content:
# Strategy playbook

## Early boot / intro
- If the screen is blank white at boot, `wait` a few seconds for logo/title.
- At title/menu, `start` or `a` proceeds.
- RAM location can be stale during boot/title; trust screenshot until gameplay begins.
- On player and rival naming screens, pressing `start` accepts the default name.
- After intro, if spawned facing TV/SNES, an `A` press can reopen the inspect textbox; clear text before moving.

## General
- Default d-pad press moves about one tile.
- Long-hold d-pad presses can reveal larger walkable areas than short probes suggest.
- A advances/confirms; B cancels; START opens menu.
- If a textbox is open, movement does nothing; clear text first.
- `B` can dismiss some overworld textboxes cleanly; repeated `A` can accidentally re-talk to NPCs.
- Trust RAM for coordinates/state and screenshot for UI/world geometry.
- Probe systematically with RAM coordinates instead of retrying the same blocked tile.
- Adjacent sprites may be NPCs, not geometry; try `A` before assuming collision.
- In buildings, identify the correct service NPC before mashing.
- If a map seems impossible, test long holds before assuming corruption.
- For risky counters/NPCs, prefer single `A` presses plus controlled `B` clears.
- Blank white or black screens during transitions/outdoor probing are not always softlocks; often wait or check battle flag first.
- When a battle just ended or is mid-transition, do not trust the partially rendered screen as overworld access. Check battle flag first.
- On route signs, `A` often leaves a textbox that blocks all movement; clear with `B` before probing further.
- Pokecenter door transitions can leave a fully black screen briefly after RAM already reports a map change; wait ~60-90 frames instead of immediately pressing opposite movement and re-entering.
- Outdoor-route NPCs near signs/trees can look like scenery from the side; if facing a suspicious sprite/object and `A` opens text, record the tile and message before moving on.

## Current route
- We now have Squirtle Lv11.
- Oak's Parcel delivery and Pokédex progression are complete.
- Real progression continues north from Viridian onto Route 2 and toward Viridian Forest/Pewter, not Route 22.
- From Route 2 upper area around `(7,44)`, `left,left,left,left,up` enters the gatehouse; going north through it reaches the Route 2 / Viridian Forest south-side outdoor area (map 0x33).
- New upper-pocket progress: a northbound lane around `x≈25` continues far upward from the Bug Catcher area and reaches the upper Route 2 pocket near `(25,20)`.
- Upper Route 2 outdoor pocket has been explored heavily; current best next step after healing is to resume from a safe state and look for the actual forest entrance/path without blind grass probing.
- Current live focus area is the east side of the north-side outdoor pocket around `(28,40)`, where a left-facing interaction opened NPC text; follow up by finishing that NPC conversation and mapping nearby side/downward chokes.

## Pallet Town / Oak sequence
- Player's House 1F exit: from downstairs, the lower corridor leads to the door warp; around (2,7) on map 0x25, pressing `down` exits.
- Pallet north exit trigger: the opening is on the east side of the north path; around map 0x00, x=10, y=1, stepping north triggers Oak.
- After that event already happened, do not expect the same trigger again.
- Oak's Lab is the southwest/left large building in Pallet.
- The right-side Pallet house can heal Squirtle via NPC interaction.

## Oak's Lab
- Early lab scene requires multiple textboxes before choosing a starter.
- Verified starter: Squirtle.
- On nickname screen, `START` declines naming.
- First rival battle can be started by pressing `A` while facing the rival.
- Oak parcel return: talking to Oak around `(4,2)` facing right advances the delivery scene.
- Lower-right lab NPC around `(8,11)` is only Oak's aide.
- Top bookshelves just say `Crammed full of POKéMON books!`.

## Route 1
- South entrance around map 0x0c, (10,28): the north tile by sign/fence is blocked.
- East-side bottom strip reaches at least x=17 but `up` there is still blocked; this is a grass/encounter trap.
- Moving left from the south area reaches the clerk around y=24; the clerk is an NPC, not geometry.
- Verified northbound route: from about `(5,18)`, go north to y=14, east along the rock barrier to about x=14, north through the opening, continue north, then loop west near the top fence and go north at about `(11,0)` to warp into Viridian.
- Viridian south return uses the fence opening around x≈20, y≈30.
- Lower Route 1 south exit fix: if blocked around `(12,31)`, move left into the lower-left lane, then go down.
- Upper grass pocket around `(10..13, 6..8)` is dangerous and easy to overprobe; bias west/north over the east dead end.
- Confirmed top finish: from around `(13,4)`, `left,left,up,up,up,up,left,up` reaches Viridian City.

## Viridian City / Pokecenter
- Entering from Route 1 places the player near (21,35).
- City sign near (16,17): `VIRIDIAN CITY - The Eternally Green Paradise`.
- East-side building labeled `MART` is the Poké Mart.
- There is a fence/rock barrier around y=28; many central/east `up` attempts from below are blocked.
- Usable crossing: from around (22,28), move left to about x=19, then north to about (19,25), then right.
- Pokecenter frontage lesson: from outside near `(22,26)`, repeated `up` can fail; moving farther right to about `(24,26)` and then using `left, up` enters the Viridian Pokecenter.
- Pokecenter entrance: from outside around (22,26), `right, up` can work when aligned; if not, try the farther-right frontage alignment above.
- The middle-aisle man is only an advice NPC.
- Nurse Joy position: stand at `(3,3)` facing `up` and press `A`; choose `HEAL`.
- After healing, dialog can appear sticky: if a stray `HEAL / CANCEL` choice reopens or a blank textbox remains, use `B` to clear it; from `We hope to see you again!`, `B` plus `down` successfully closes the dialog and steps away.
- Healing-dialog reminder: once the greeting text starts (`OK. We'll need your...`), the next steps should just be advancing the standard heal flow, not movement.
- Upper-city geometry:
  - The north fence line around y≈14 is blocked across a broad span.
  - Far-right rock wall near `(35,16)` is a dead end.
  - To reach upper city from the east half, work left to around `(20,16)` and then up.
  - Small house/school entrance is around `(21,15)`.
  - Gym frontage is reachable in the upper city.
- Northwest woman is only hint text about Viridian Forest/Pewter, not a trigger.
- West exit to Route 22 is at the far-left side; from about `(0,14)`, pressing `left` warps out.
- Hidden reconnect: from Route 22's far-east upper area, reaching about `(39,9)` and pressing `right` warps back into Viridian upper-left around `(0,17)`.
- Strong current hypothesis: real progression is north/Route 2/Viridian Forest, not Route 22.

## Viridian Mart
- On entry, interior may show briefly before clerk parcel dialog; a short `wait` can be correct.
- Parcel flow: advance greeting, receive `OAK's PARCEL`, then clear final goodbye line.
- After parcel dialog, free position is around `(2,5)`; to exit, walk down to about `(2,7)` then use `right, down` to align with the door.

## Route 22
- Entering from Viridian west exit places the player around `(39,6)` facing left.
- Immediate west travel works to about `(36,6)`, but farther left is blocked there; `up` is also blocked on that entry shelf.
- Going down from there reaches a long lower road around y≈14-15.
- That lower road is open almost end-to-end (`x≈2..37`) but `up` is blocked across many tested positions; both far west and far east ends are dead ends.
- Upper-right climb from the far east lower road: from about `(37,14)`, go `left x4, up,up, left, up, left, up, left, up,up` to reach around `(30,8)`.
- That upper-right area is only a small enclosed grass box around roughly `(30..33, 8..12)` plus a narrow upper strip near `(33,10..12)`; apparent exits are blocked and it only drops back to the lower road.
- New reconnect: a different far-east upper path reaches about `(39,9)`; pressing `right` there warps back into Viridian City upper-left. Treat this as a return connection, not new progression.
- Consolidation: everything verified so far on Route 22 points to a non-progressing optional area at this stage. Revisit only if testing a genuinely new upper-entry approach; otherwise deprioritize it.
- Because Route 22 grass repeatedly triggers wilds while giving little information, avoid exploratory grass steps there when HP is low.

## Route 2
- Entered Route 2 from Viridian north.
- Central path from about `(7,71)` is open north at least to about `y=57`.
- From `(7,57)`, going left reaches a west-side lane; taking that west lane north works to about `(3,48)`.
- Top-west area has a horizontal barrier/top edge: from `(3,48)`, right works to about `(7,48)`, but farther right is blocked; north is blocked there too.
- New note: at about `(8,48)` the tile(s) south are blocked, and wild encounters in this grass patch can trigger with black-screen/partial-overworld transition artifacts before the battle fully renders.
- Current battle-resolution state on turn 100: a fight ended with Squirtle leveling to 9 at `(8,48)` while battle flag still reads wild. Level-up text like `grew to level 9!` is still battle text; clear it with `A` until the command/menu or overworld visibly returns.
- Avoid grinding the same top grass patch; back out and look for a different Route 2 lane or building/gate approach from a lower, safer position.
- South fence-gap lesson: the apparent openings around `(6..11,62)` are fake from below; repeated `up` attempts there are blocked.
- Lower-left sign around `(5,66)` only reads `ROUTE 2 / VIRIDIAN CITY - PEWTER CITY`.
- Midwest tree-maze trap: the area around roughly `(5..11,51..52)` reconnects awkwardly to the top row, triggers many wilds, and gives little new information.
- Recent reconnection: from the trap area, moving to the safer west lane around `(2,57)` and then reprobeing north can reconnect to the upper row near `(2,48)`.
- New tactical note from the live screen/state: current position `(10,48)` is farther east than the old `(7..8,48)` trap points; prioritize mapping this east-side upper lane before falling back south.
- Real progress discovery: from the upper area around `(7,44)`, `left,left,left,left,up` enters the gatehouse to Viridian Forest.
- North-side outdoor sign near the forest approach says: `For poison, use ANTIDOTE! Get it at POKEMON MARTs!`
- Gatehouse exit alignment lesson: inside `MAP_0x32`, the correct north exit is along the top row near `x=5`, not the misleading top-right doorway. From around `(5,1)`, pressing `up` exits to the north-side outdoor area at about `(17,47)` on map `0x33`.
- North-side outdoor pocket partial map so far:
  - Start area near `(6,30)` is blocked above.
  - West reaches about `(2,30)` but only slightly downward there.
  - A lower route exists via the east/lower lanes, reaching at least `(14,43)` where there is an NPC/sign pocket.
  - NPC near `(14,43)` only says `They're out for POKEMON fights!`
  - South is blocked at `(14,43)`; do not keep hammering that tile.
  - Better progress came from the farther-right lane: from about `(15,39)`, moving right to `(17,39)` and then north reached at least `(17,34)`.
  - East side opens farther than first thought: reachable tiles include roughly `(19..23, 40..43)`.
  - North is blocked at least at `(22,40)`, `(23,40)`, and `(27,41)`.
  - The trainer/NPC at about `(26,33)` is a Bug Catcher who challenges immediately.
  - From around `(25,34)`, going north repeatedly reaches at least `(25,20)`; this is a real upper lane, not a dead end.
  - The upper pocket contains another Bug Catcher on the right side who also challenges, plus a sign near the upper center.
  - The entire top walkable outside row around `y=1` from roughly `x=16..32` is a dead top boundary; `up` does not enter the forest there.
  - Far-right boundary caps around `x=32`, with a downward lane on that side.
  - After the upper Bug Catcher is defeated, probe for the actual forest entrance around the upper-center/right side or via a side/downward choke, not by re-testing the top row.
  - New east-side interaction: at about `(28,40)` facing left, the adjacent sprite/object is an interactable NPC, not scenery; visible text includes `You should carry extras!` and likely continues with practical item advice.
- Safe retreat lesson from low HP: if stuck in the upper pocket with 5 HP, retreat to Viridian rather than taking more grass steps; the Pokecenter is reachable without further Route 2 battles once back in town.

## Battle heuristics
- Early wild fights can sometimes be handled with repeated `A`, but blind mash is no longer safe once Squirtle's move list matters.
- Squirtle now knows **Tackle**, **Tail Whip**, and **Bubble**. Visible move order is **TACKLE / TAIL WHIP / BUBBLE**.
- In battle, if the move menu cursor is sitting on **TAIL WHIP**, then **down + A** selects **BUBBLE** and plain **A** wastes a turn on Tail Whip.
- In simple wild fights, deliberately choose a damaging move; if unsure, `Tackle` is a safe manual fallback.
- With low HP, avoid unnecessary battles; fleeing is preferred.
- After a battle, visible text can linger after battle-state tools think it cleared; keep advancing until overworld is visibly back.
- To run from a wild battle, use the battle menu deliberately; do not assume the cursor stayed where a previous tool left it.
- In wild battles with blank intro/send-out screens, press `A` a couple times to reach the command menu before trying RUN.
- If the screen shows move-result text like `used TACKLE!`, `Enemy ... fainted!`, `gained EXP. Points!`, or `grew to level ...`, the next useful input is usually still just `A` until the battle flow finishes.
- Do not try RUN or movement while experience/level-up text is still showing; those inputs are wasted until the text resolves.
- If accidental input opens the Pokémon submenu during battle, `B` backs out cleanly to the main battle menu.
- Reliable manual flee pattern from messy wild-battle substates:
  - If stuck in move or Pokémon submenu, press `B` until back at the 4-option main battle menu.
  - Common RUN selections that have worked in this run: `down, A` and `right, A` depending on current cursor position; if needed, `down, right, A` from FIGHT also works.
  - After `Got away safely!`, one more `A` is usually needed, then a short `wait` for the white transition back to overworld.
- After `used TACKLE!` or similar, a short `wait` can reveal the actual next menu/text more cleanly than more inputs.
- New repeated lesson from Route 2 trainer fights: **Bubble** text/animation can lag for a long time. Best pattern is usually **wait first, then single `A` presses with long settle** rather than mashing.
- Enemy move text may chain strangely across boxes (for example `attack missed! But, it failed!` after Weedle). Treat it as normal battle text and advance one box at a time.
- Trainer battles on the route approach should be handled manually, not with wild-battle mash/flee tools.
- Upper Route 2 Bug Catcher battle specifics:
  - Weedle used repeated **String Shot**; the animation/text regularly looked stuck until a wait.
  - Kakuna uses **Harden**; expect the text pair `Enemy KAKUNA used HARDEN!` then `Enemy KAKUNA's DEFENSE rose!`.
  - Because Kakuna is not threatening much immediate damage, the main risk is wasting turns with Tail Whip or mashing into animation lag, not being KO'd instantly.

## Player's House 2F lesson
- Prior wedge hypothesis was wrong.
- Long-hold movement revealed a much larger room than short presses suggested; visible room edges are not always true movement bounds.

## Tooling / harness notes
- `save_checkpoint` may fail with a filesystem 500 error; do not rely on it until a save succeeds.
- Repeated mechanical sequences worth tooling: dialog mashing, systematic coordinate probes, early battle mash, safe service-desk scans, fleeing wild battles, walk-path execution that stops on battle/map change, and clearing battle/result text until the battle flag drops.
- `probe_path` is excellent for discovering the effect of long holds because it logs coordinates after each step.
- Old `clear_battle_text` is broken (`ctx.frame is not a function`); use/keep the safer state-based replacement instead.
- In-game SAVE is distinct from harness checkpoints and appears usable as a fallback.

## Current route
- We have Squirtle Lv11.
- Oak's Parcel delivery and Pokédex progression are complete.

- From Route 2 upper area around `(7,44)`, `left,left,left,left,up` enters the gatehouse; the north exit leads to the outdoor forest-approach pocket (map `0x33`).
- Current live focus: the east side of that north-side outdoor pocket around `(28,40)`, where a left-facing interaction opened NPC text; finish that conversation and map nearby side/downward chokes for the real forest entrance.

## Pallet / Lab quick notes
- Player's House 1F exit: around `(2,7)` on map `0x25`, pressing `down` exits.
- Pallet north exit trigger after game start is on the east side of the north path around map `0x00`, `x=10,y=1`.
- Oak's Lab is the southwest/left large building in Pallet.
- Right-side Pallet house can heal Squirtle via NPC interaction.

## Route 1

- East-side bottom strip reaches at least `x=17` but `up` there is blocked; grass trap.
- Clerk on the road is an NPC, not geometry.
- Verified northbound route: from about `(5,18)`, go north to `y=14`, east along the rock barrier to about `x=14`, north through the opening, continue north, then loop west near the top fence and go north at about `(11,0)` to warp into Viridian.

- Pokecenter frontage lesson: from outside near `(22,26)`, repeated `up` can fail; moving farther right to about `(24,26)` and then using `left, up` enters.

- After healing, if dialog gets sticky, use `B`; from `We hope to see you again!`, `B` plus `down` closes and steps away.
- Upper city: the broad north fence line near `y≈14` is blocked; to reach upper city from the east half, work left to around `(20,16)` and then up.
- Small house/school entrance is around `(21,15)`.
- Northwest woman is only hint text about Viridian Forest/Pewter.
- West exit to Route 22 is at the far-left side; from about `(0,14)`, pressing `left` warps out.
- Route 22 far-east upper area can warp back into Viridian upper-left around `(0,17)` by pressing `right` near `(39,9)`.

## Viridian Mart
- On entry, interior may show briefly before clerk parcel dialog; a short `wait` can be correct.
- Parcel flow: advance greeting, receive `OAK's PARCEL`, then clear final goodbye line.
- After parcel dialog, free position is around `(2,5)`; to exit, walk down to about `(2,7)` then use `right, down` to align with the door.

## Route 22
- Everything verified so far points to optional/non-progressing space.
- Lower road is traversable broadly but `up` is blocked across many tested positions; far-east upper grass also dead-ends.
- Grass there causes many low-value encounters; avoid exploratory grass steps when HP is low.

## Route 2
- Central lower path from about `(7,71)` is open north to about `y=57`.
- From `(7,57)`, going left reaches a west lane; taking that north works to about `(3,48)`.
- Top-west area has a horizontal barrier/top edge: from `(3,48)`, right works to about `(7,48)`, but north is blocked there too.
- Avoid grinding the same top grass patch; it triggers wilds and reveals little.
- The apparent fence openings around `(x=3..11,y=62)` are fake from below; `up` is blocked there.
- Lower-left sign around `(5,66)` reads `ROUTE 2 / VIRIDIAN CITY - PEWTER CITY`.
- Real progress discovery: from the upper area around `(7,44)`, `left,left,left,left,up` enters the gatehouse.
- Gatehouse north exit alignment: inside `MAP_0x32`, the correct north exit is along the top row near `x=5`; pressing `up` there exits to map `0x33` at about `(17,47)`.
- Forest-approach outdoor pocket (`0x33`) notes:
  - Start area near `(6,30)` is blocked above.
  - West reaches about `(2,30)` but only slightly downward there.
  - A lower route exists via the east/lower lanes, reaching at least `(14,43)` where an NPC says `They're out for POKEMON fights!`
  - South is blocked at `(14,43)`; stop hammering that tile.
  - Better progress came from the farther-right lane: from about `(15,39)`, moving right to `(17,39)` and then north reached at least `(17,34)`.
  - East side opens farther than first thought: reachable tiles include roughly `(19..23,40..43)` and farther east to around `(29,41)`.
  - North is blocked at least at `(22,40)`, `(23,40)`, and `(27,41)`.
  - The trainer at about `(26,33)` is a Bug Catcher who challenges immediately.
  - From around `(25,34)`, going north repeatedly reaches at least `(25,20)`; this is a real upper lane.
  - The upper pocket contains another Bug Catcher on the right side plus a sign near the upper center.
  - The entire top outside row around `y=1` from roughly `x=16..32` is a dead boundary; `up` does not enter the forest there.
  - Far-right boundary caps around `x=32`, with a downward lane on that side.
  - New east-side interaction: at about `(28,40)` facing left, the adjacent sprite/object is an interactable NPC, not scenery; visible text includes `You should carry extras!`
- If stuck in the upper pocket with very low HP, retreat to Viridian rather than taking more grass steps; the Pokecenter is reachable without more Route 2 battles once back in town.

## Battle heuristics
- Early wild fights can sometimes be handled with repeated `A`, but blind mash is unsafe once move choice matters.
- Squirtle knows **Tackle**, **Tail Whip**, and **Bubble**. Visible order: **TACKLE / TAIL WHIP / BUBBLE**.
- If the move cursor is on **TAIL WHIP**, then `down + A` selects **BUBBLE**; plain `A` wastes a turn.
- In simple wild fights, deliberately choose a damaging move; `Tackle` is the safe manual fallback.
- With low HP, avoid unnecessary battles; fleeing is preferred.
- After a battle, visible text can linger after battle-state tools think it cleared; keep advancing until overworld is visibly back.
- If stuck in move or Pokémon submenu, `B` backs out cleanly to the main battle menu.
- Reliable flee pattern from messy wild-battle substates: use `B` until at the 4-option menu, then try `down, A` or `right, A`; if needed, `down, right, A` from FIGHT also works.
- After `Got away safely!`, one more `A` is usually needed, then a short `wait` for the white transition back to overworld.
- After `used TACKLE!` or Bubble text, a short `wait` often reveals the actual next state more cleanly than extra inputs.
- Route 2 trainer fights: Bubble animation/text can lag a long time; prefer `wait` first, then single `A` presses with longer settle instead of mashing.
- Kakuna often uses **Harden**; the main risk is wasting turns with Tail Whip or mashing through lag, not sudden KO.

## Player's House 2F lesson
- Prior wedge hypothesis was wrong.
- Long-hold movement revealed a much larger room than short presses suggested; visible room edges are not always true movement bounds.

## Tooling / harness notes
- `save_checkpoint` may fail with a filesystem 500 error; do not rely on it until one succeeds.
- `probe_path` is excellent for discovering the effect of long holds because it logs coordinates after each step.
- Old `clear_battle_text` is broken (`ctx.frame is not a function`); use the safer state-based replacement instead.
- In-game SAVE is distinct from harness checkpoints and appears usable as a fallback.

data.name: update_playbook

arguments.content:
# Strategy playbook

## Early boot / intro
- If boot is blank white, `wait` a few seconds for logo/title.
- At title/menu, `start` or `a` proceeds.
- RAM location can be stale during boot/title; trust screenshot until gameplay begins.
- On player/rival naming screens, `start` accepts the default name.
- After intro, if spawned facing TV/SNES, `A` can reopen inspect text; clear it before moving.

## General
- Default d-pad press moves about one tile.
- Long-hold d-pad presses can reveal larger walkable areas than short probes suggest.
- A advances/confirms; B cancels; START opens menu.
- If a textbox is open, movement does nothing; clear text first.
- `B` often dismisses overworld textboxes more cleanly than `A`.
- Trust RAM for coordinates/state and screenshot for UI/world geometry.
- Probe systematically with RAM coordinates instead of retrying the same blocked tile.
- Adjacent sprites may be NPCs, not geometry; try `A` before assuming collision.
- Blank white/black screens during transitions often just need `wait`; check battle flag before panicking.
- When a battle just ended or is mid-transition, do not trust the partially rendered screen as overworld access.
- On route signs, `A` often leaves a textbox that blocks movement; clear with `B` before probing further.
- Pokecenter door transitions can leave a fully black screen briefly after RAM already reports a map change; wait ~60-90 frames.
- Outdoor-route NPCs near signs/trees can look like scenery; if `A` opens text, record tile + message.

## Current route
- We have Squirtle Lv11.
- Oak's Parcel delivery and Pokédex progression are complete.
- Real progression is north from Viridian onto Route 2 / Viridian Forest, not Route 22.
- From Route 2 upper area around `(7,44)`, `left,left,left,left,up` enters the gatehouse; the north exit leads to the outdoor forest-approach pocket (map `0x33`).
- Current live focus: finish mapping the Route 2 north-side outdoor pocket and find the actual Viridian Forest entrance without retesting dead upper boundaries.

## Pallet / Lab quick notes
- Player's House 1F exit: around `(2,7)` on map `0x25`, pressing `down` exits.
- Pallet north exit trigger after game start is on the east side of the north path around map `0x00`, `x=10,y=1`.
- Oak's Lab is the southwest/left large building in Pallet.
- Right-side Pallet house can heal Squirtle via NPC interaction.
- Verified starter: Squirtle.
- On nickname screen, `START` declines naming.
- Oak parcel return: talking to Oak around `(4,2)` facing right advances the delivery scene.
- Lower-right lab NPC around `(8,11)` is only Oak's aide.

## Route 1
- South entrance around map `0x0c`, `(10,28)`: the north tile by sign/fence is blocked.
- East-side bottom strip reaches at least `x=17` but `up` there is blocked; grass trap.
- Clerk on the road is an NPC, not geometry.
- Verified northbound route: from about `(5,18)`, go north to `y=14`, east along the rock barrier to about `x=14`, north through the opening, continue north, then loop west near the top fence and go north at about `(11,0)` to warp into Viridian.
- Viridian south return uses the fence opening around `x≈20,y≈30`.
- Upper grass pocket around `(10..13,6..8)` is dangerous and easy to overprobe; bias west/north over the east dead end.

## Viridian City / Pokecenter
- City sign near `(16,17)`: `VIRIDIAN CITY - The Eternally Green Paradise`.
- East-side building labeled `MART` is the Poké Mart.
- There is a fence/rock barrier around `y=28`; many central/east `up` attempts from below are blocked.
- Usable crossing: from around `(22,28)`, move left to about `x=19`, then north to about `(19,25)`, then right.
- Pokecenter frontage lesson: from outside near `(22,26)`, repeated `up` can fail; moving farther right to about `(24,26)` and then using `left, up` enters.
- Nurse Joy position: stand at `(3,3)` facing up and press `A`; choose `HEAL`.
- After healing, if dialog gets sticky, use `B`; from `We hope to see you again!`, `B` plus `down` closes and steps away.
- Upper city: the broad north fence line near `y≈14` is blocked; to reach upper city from the east half, work left to around `(20,16)` and then up.
- Small house/school entrance is around `(21,15)`.
- Northwest woman is only hint text about Viridian Forest/Pewter.
- West exit to Route 22 is at the far-left side; from about `(0,14)`, pressing `left` warps out.
- Route 22 far-east upper area can warp back into Viridian upper-left around `(0,17)` by pressing `right` near `(39,9)`.

## Viridian Mart
- On entry, interior may show briefly before clerk parcel dialog; a short `wait` can be correct.
- Parcel flow: advance greeting, receive `OAK's PARCEL`, then clear final goodbye line.
- After parcel dialog, free position is around `(2,5)`; to exit, walk down to about `(2,7)` then use `right, down` to align with the door.

## Route 22
- Everything verified so far points to optional/non-progressing space.
- Lower road is traversable broadly but `up` is blocked across many tested positions; far-east upper grass also dead-ends.
- Grass there causes many low-value encounters; avoid exploratory grass steps when HP is low.

## Route 2
- Central lower path from about `(7,71)` is open north to about `y=57`.
- From `(7,57)`, going left reaches a west lane; taking that north works to about `(3,48)`.
- Top-west area has a horizontal barrier/top edge: from `(3,48)`, right works to about `(7,48)`, but north is blocked there too.
- Avoid grinding the same top grass patch; it triggers wilds and reveals little.
- The apparent fence openings around `(x=3..11,y=62)` are fake from below; `up` is blocked there.
- Lower-left sign around `(5,66)` reads `ROUTE 2 / VIRIDIAN CITY - PEWTER CITY`.
- Real progress discovery: from the upper area around `(7,44)`, `left,left,left,left,up` enters the gatehouse.
- Gatehouse north exit alignment: inside `MAP_0x32`, the correct north exit is along the top row near `x=5`; pressing `up` there exits to map `0x33` at about `(17,47)`.
- Forest-approach outdoor pocket (`0x33`) notes:
  - Start area near `(6,30)` is blocked above.
  - West reaches about `(2,30)` but only slightly downward there.
  - A lower route exists via the east/lower lanes, reaching at least `(14,43)` where an NPC says `They're out for POKEMON fights!`
  - South is blocked at `(14,43)`; stop hammering that tile.
  - Better progress came from the farther-right lane: from about `(15,39)`, moving right to `(17,39)` and then north reached at least `(17,34)`.
  - East side opens farther than first thought: reachable tiles include roughly `(19..23,40..43)` and farther east to around `(29,41)`.
  - North is blocked at least at `(22,40)`, `(23,40)`, and `(27,41)`.
  - The trainer at about `(26,33)` is a Bug Catcher who challenges immediately.
  - From around `(25,34)`, going north repeatedly reaches at least `(25,20)`; this is a real upper lane.
  - The upper pocket contains another Bug Catcher on the right side plus a sign near the upper center.
  - The entire top outside row around `y=1` from roughly `x=16..32` is a dead boundary; `up` does not enter the forest there.
  - Far-right boundary caps around `x=32`, with a downward lane on that side.
  - New east-side interaction: at about `(28,40)` facing left, the adjacent sprite/object is an interactable NPC, not scenery; visible text includes `You should carry extras!`
  - Turn 162: from the mid-right/upper route, a clear lane reaches the upper clearing around `(25,8)`, but `up` is blocked there and the upper-left continuation to about `(17,8)` is also a dead corridor. A small south dip exists to `(17,10)`, but left from `(16,10)` is blocked. Do not keep treating the upper-left edge as the forest entrance.
- If stuck in the upper pocket with very low HP, retreat to Viridian rather than taking more grass steps; the Pokecenter is reachable without more Route 2 battles once back in town.

## Battle heuristics
- Early wild fights can sometimes be handled with repeated `A`, but blind mash is unsafe once move choice matters.
- Squirtle knows **Tackle**, **Tail Whip**, and **Bubble**. Visible order: **TACKLE / TAIL WHIP / BUBBLE**.
- If the move cursor is on **TAIL WHIP**, then `down + A` selects **BUBBLE**; plain `A` wastes a turn.
- In simple wild fights, deliberately choose a damaging move; `Tackle` is the safe manual fallback.
- With low HP, avoid unnecessary battles; fleeing is preferred.
- After a battle, visible text can linger after battle-state tools think it cleared; keep advancing until overworld is visibly back.
- If stuck in move or Pokémon submenu, `B` backs out cleanly to the main battle menu.
- Reliable flee pattern from messy wild-battle substates: use `B` until at the 4-option menu, then try `down, A` or `right, A`; if needed, `down, right, A` from FIGHT also works.
- After `Got away safely!`, one more `A` is usually needed, then a short `wait` for the white transition back to overworld.
- After `used TACKLE!` or Bubble text, a short `wait` often reveals the actual next state more cleanly than extra inputs.
- Route 2 trainer fights: Bubble animation/text can lag a long time; prefer `wait` first, then single `A` presses with longer settle instead of mashing.
- Kakuna often uses **Harden**; the main risk is wasting turns with Tail Whip or mashing through lag, not sudden KO.

## Player's House 2F lesson
- Prior wedge hypothesis was wrong.
- Long-hold movement revealed a much larger room than short presses suggested; visible room edges are not always true movement bounds.

## Tooling / harness notes
- `save_checkpoint` may fail with a filesystem 500 error; do not rely on it until one succeeds.
- `probe_path` is excellent for discovering the effect of long holds because it logs coordinates after each step.
- Old `clear_battle_text` is broken (`ctx.frame is not a function`); use the safer state-based replacement instead.
- In-game SAVE is distinct from harness checkpoints and appears usable as a fallback.

Confirm and document the actual Viridian Forest entrance tile/path from the Route 2 north-side outdoor pocket
If forest approach still stalls, fully map the north-side outdoor pocket and document blocked rows/lanes so we stop retesting dead tiles
Probe from the newly verified upper clearing around (25,8) / left corridor around (17,8) toward any unexplored south or center exits instead of retesting blocked north edges
If a battle starts from a bad submenu state, use B to recover to the main battle menu before selecting RUN
From the Route 2 north-side outdoor pocket, finish the east-side NPC conversation at about (28,40) and record the full advice text
Use the verified lower Route 2 note: y=62 is blocked across x=3..11 from below; stop re-testing these fake gaps
Use the newly verified west-lane route from (3,62) to reach the upper Route 2 row safely, then head for the known gatehouse path instead of probing the blocked top barrier

data.summary:
Turn 162 summary:

What I did
- Cleared the open sign textbox with `B`. The full warning was:
  - `TRAINER TIPS`
  - `No stealing of POKEMON from other trainers!`
  - `Catch only wild POKEMON!`
  - `stay away from grassy areas!`
- Then I deliberately tried genuinely different exploration instead of retesting the same east-side NPC area.
- Mapped a long new northbound route in the Route 2 north-side outdoor pocket:
  - from `(23,40)` I moved through the lower-right lane to `(26,43)`
  - then up and around to `(27,36)`
  - then continued north via the upper lane all the way to about `(25,21)`

data.summary:
Turn 165 summary:

What I did
- Resolved the awkward wild-battle intro instead of assuming a wedge:
  - waited through the delayed encounter text/animation until the battle UI fully appeared
  - manually selected **RUN** with `A, down, right, A`
  - cleared `Got away safely!` with one more `A`
  - waited through the full-white transition back to overworld
- Confirmed the battle/transition lesson again:
  - RAM can say `battle: none` while the screen is still white; a short wait restores the overworld cleanly
- Used two genuine probe sequences from the current far-right Route 2 north-side pocke

What I did
- Took the directive seriously and tried genuinely different probing from the far-right area instead of re-testing the dead north edge.

- Interacted with the sign at `(26,18)` facing up and advanced the text. It is another `TRAINER TIPS` sign; the visible ending line was:

- Confirmed a useful new corridor structure:
  - from `(28,19)`, `up` to `(28,18)` works but a second `up` is blocked
  - left works through `(27,18) -> (26,18) -> (25,18)`
  - from there, north works through `(25,17) -> (25,16)`
  - east from `(26,16)` is blocked at least at `(27,16)` and farther
  - north from that column works up to `(25,12)`, then later to `(25,11) -> (25,10) -> (25,9)`
  - from `(24,9)`, `up` is blocked at `(24,8)`
- Reached the previously suspected

data.name: update_playbook

arguments.content:
# Strategy playbook

## Early boot / intro
- If boot is blank white, `wait` a few seconds for logo/title.
- At title/menu, `start` or `a` proceeds.
- RAM location can be stale during boot/title; trust screenshot until gameplay begins.
- On player/rival naming screens, `start` accepts the default name.
- After intro, if spawned facing TV/SNES, `A` can reopen inspect text; clear it before moving.

## General
- Default d-pad press moves about one tile.
- Long-hold d-pad presses can reveal larger walkable areas than short probes suggest.
- A advances/confirms; B cancels; START opens menu.
- If a textbox is open, movement does nothing; clear text first.
- `B` often dismisses overworld textboxes more cleanly than `A`.
- Trust RAM for coordinates/state and screenshot for UI/world geometry.
- Probe systematically with RAM coordinates instead of retrying the same blocked tile.
- Adjacent sprites may be NPCs, not geometry; try `A` before assuming collision.
- Blank white/black screens during transitions often just need `wait`; check battle flag before panicking.
- When a battle just ended or is mid-transition, do not trust the partially rendered screen as overworld access.
- On route signs, `A` often leaves a textbox that blocks movement; clear with `B` before probing further.
- Pokecenter door transitions can leave a fully black screen briefly after RAM already reports a map change; wait ~60-90 frames.
- Outdoor-route NPCs near signs/trees can look like scenery; if `A` opens text, record tile + message.

## Current route
- We have Squirtle Lv11.
- Oak's Parcel delivery and Pokédex progression are complete.
- Real progression is north from Viridian onto Route 2 / Viridian Forest, not Route 22.
- From Route 2 upper area around `(7,44)`, `left,left,left,left,up` enters the gatehouse; the north exit leads to the outdoor forest-approach pocket (map `0x33`).
- Current live focus: finish mapping the Route 2 north-side outdoor pocket and find the actual Viridian Forest entrance without retesting dead upper boundaries.

## Pallet / Lab quick notes
- Player's House 1F exit: around `(2,7)` on map `0x25`, pressing `down` exits.
- Pallet north exit trigger after game start is on the east side of the north path around map `0x00`, `x=10,y=1`.
- Oak's Lab is the southwest/left large building in Pallet.
- Right-side Pallet house can heal Squirtle via NPC interaction.
- Verified starter: Squirtle.
- On nickname screen, `START` declines naming.
- Oak parcel return: talking to Oak around `(4,2)` facing right advances the delivery scene.
- Lower-right lab NPC around `(8,11)` is only Oak's aide.

## Route 1
- South entrance around map `0x0c`, `(10,28)`: the north tile by sign/fence is blocked.
- East-side bottom strip reaches at least `x=17` but `up` there is blocked; grass trap.
- Clerk on the road is an NPC, not geometry.
- Verified northbound route: from about `(5,18)`, go north to `y=14`, east along the rock barrier to about `x=14`, north through the opening, continue north, then loop west near the top fence and go north at about `(11,0)` to warp into Viridian.
- Viridian south return uses the fence opening around `x≈20,y≈30`.
- Upper grass pocket around `(10..13,6..8)` is dangerous and easy to overprobe; bias west/north over the east dead end.

## Viridian City / Pokecenter
- City sign near `(16,17)`: `VIRIDIAN CITY - The Eternally Green Paradise`.
- East-side building labeled `MART` is the Poké Mart.
- There is a fence/rock barrier around `y=28`; many central/east `up` attempts from below are blocked.
- Usable crossing: from around `(22,28)`, move left to about `x=19`, then north to about `(19,25)`, then right.
- Pokecenter frontage lesson: from outside near `(22,26)`, repeated `up` can fail; moving farther right to about `(24,26)` and then using `left, up` enters.
- Nurse Joy position: stand at `(3,3)` facing up and press `A`; choose `HEAL`.
- After healing, if dialog gets sticky, use `B`; from `We hope to see you again!`, `B` plus `down` closes and steps away.
- Upper city: the broad north fence line near `y≈14` is blocked; to reach upper city from the east half, work left to around `(20,16)` and then up.
- Small house/school entrance is around `(21,15)`.
- Northwest woman is only hint text about Viridian Forest/Pewter.
- West exit to Route 22 is at the far-left side; from about `(0,14)`, pressing `left` warps out.
- Route 22 far-east upper area can warp back into Viridian upper-left around `(0,17)` by pressing `right` near `(39,9)`.

## Viridian Mart
- On entry, interior may show briefly before clerk parcel dialog; a short `wait` can be correct.
- Parcel flow: advance greeting, receive `OAK's PARCEL`, then clear final goodbye line.
- After parcel dialog, free position is around `(2,5)`; to exit, walk down to about `(2,7)` then use `right, down` to align with the door.

## Route 22
- Everything verified so far points to optional/non-progressing space.
- Lower road is traversable broadly but `up` is blocked across many tested positions; far-east upper grass also dead-ends.
- Grass there causes many low-value encounters; avoid exploratory grass steps when HP is low.

## Route 2
- Central lower path from about `(7,71)` is open north to about `y=57`.
- From `(7,57)`, going left reaches a west lane; taking that north works to about `(3,48)`.
- Top-west area has a horizontal barrier/top edge: from `(3,48)`, right works to about `(7,48)`, but north is blocked there too.
- Avoid grinding the same top grass patch; it triggers wilds and reveals little.
- The apparent fence openings around `(x=3..11,y=62)` are fake from below; `up` is blocked there.
- Lower-left sign around `(5,66)` reads `ROUTE 2 / VIRIDIAN CITY - PEWTER CITY`.
- Real progress discovery: from the upper area around `(7,44)`, `left,left,left,left,up` enters the gatehouse.
- Gatehouse north exit alignment: inside `MAP_0x32`, the correct north exit is along the top row near `x=5`; pressing `up` there exits to map `0x33` at about `(17,47)`.
- Forest-approach outdoor pocket (`0x33`) notes:
  - Start area near `(6,30)` is blocked above.
  - West reaches about `(2,30)` but only slightly downward there.
  - A lower route exists via the east/lower lanes, reaching at least `(14,43)` where an NPC says `They're out for POKEMON fights!`
  - South is blocked at `(14,43)`; stop hammering that tile.
  - Better progress came from the farther-right lane: from about `(15,39)`, moving right to `(17,39)` and then north reached at least `(17,34)`.
  - East side opens farther than first thought: reachable tiles include roughly `(19..23,40..43)` and farther east to around `(29,41)`.
  - North is blocked at least at `(22,40)`, `(23,40)`, and `(27,41)`.
  - The trainer at about `(26,33)` is a Bug Catcher who challenges immediately.
  - From around `(25,34)`, going north repeatedly reaches at least `(25,20)`; this is a real upper lane.
  - The upper pocket contains another Bug Catcher on the right side plus a sign near the upper center.
  - The entire top outside row around `y=1` from roughly `x=16..32` is a dead boundary; `up` does not enter the forest there.
  - Far-right boundary caps around `x=32`, with a downward lane on that side.
  - New east-side interaction: at about `(28,40)` facing left, the adjacent sprite/object is an interactable NPC, not scenery; visible text includes `You should carry extras!`
  - Turn 162: from the mid-right/upper route, a clear lane reaches the upper clearing around `(25,8)`, but `up` is blocked there and the upper-left continuation to about `(17,8)` is also a dead corridor. A small south dip exists to `(17,10)`, but left from `(16,10)` is blocked. Do not keep treating the upper-left edge as the forest entrance.
  - Turn 169: a different upper-left route reaches another top-row clearing. Verified `(11,7) -> (11,3)` and top-row movement through about `x=6..13` at `y=1`, but `up` is still blocked across that row. The visible upper-left sign/tree cluster is misleading: `A` from `(6,1)`, `(6,2)`, and `(6,4)` facing left produced no text. Treat this as another cul-de-sac; next probe should target lower-left/center routes from this upper area rather than the top edge.
- If stuck in the upper pocket with very low HP, retreat to Viridian rather than taking more grass steps; the Pokecenter is reachable without more Route 2 battles once back in town.

## Battle heuristics
- Early wild fights can sometimes be handled with repeated `A`, but blind mash is unsafe once move choice matters.
- Squirtle knows **Tackle**, **Tail Whip**, and **Bubble**. Visible order: **TACKLE / TAIL WHIP / BUBBLE**.
- If the move cursor is on **TAIL WHIP**, then `down + A` selects **BUBBLE**; plain `A` wastes a turn.
- In simple wild fights, deliberately choose a damaging move; `Tackle` is the safe manual fallback.
- With low HP, avoid unnecessary battles; fleeing is preferred.
- After a battle, visible text can linger after battle-state tools think it cleared; keep advancing until overworld is visibly back.
- If stuck in move or Pokémon submenu, `B` backs out cleanly to the main battle menu.
- Reliable flee pattern from messy wild-battle substates: use `B` until at the 4-option menu, then try `down, A` or `right, A`; if needed, `down, right, A` from FIGHT also works.
- After `Got away safely!`, one more `A` is usually needed, then a short `wait` for the white transition back to overworld.
- After `used TACKLE!` or Bubble text, a short `wait` often reveals the actual next state more cleanly than extra inputs.
- Route 2 trainer fights: Bubble animation/text can lag a long time; prefer `wait` first, then single `A` presses with longer settle instead of mashing.
- Kakuna often uses **Harden**; the main risk is wasting turns with Tail Whip or mashing through lag, not sudden KO.

## Player's House 2F lesson
- Prior wedge hypothesis was wrong.
- Long-hold movement revealed a much larger room than short presses suggested; visible room edges are not always true movement bounds.

## Tooling / harness notes
- `save_checkpoint` may fail with a filesystem 500 error; do not rely on it until one succeeds.
- `probe_path` is excellent for discovering the effect of long holds because it logs coordinates after each step.
- Old `clear_battle_text` is broken (`ctx.frame is not a function`); use the safer state-based replacement instead.
- In-game SAVE is distinct from harness checkpoints and appears usable as a fallback.

What I did

- Started from `(12,18)` and mapped a new central/upper route:
  - found left blockage at `(11,16)`
  - climbed via the central path through `(13,14)`, `(13,11)`, `(13,10)`, `(11,9)`
  - then continued north to `(11,7)`, `(11,5)`, `(11,3)`
- Reached a new upper clearing and confirmed a whole additional top-row segment:
  - traversed across the top row through about `x=6..13` at `y=1`
- Deliberately tested t

- The apparent fence openings around `(x=3..11,y=62)` are fake from below; `up` is blocked there.
- Lower-left sign around `(5,66)` reads `ROUTE 2 / VIRIDIAN CITY - PEWTER CITY`.
- Real progress discovery: from the upper area around `(7,44)`, `left,left,left,left,up` enters the gatehouse.
- Gatehouse north exit alignment: inside `MAP_0x32`, the correct north exit is along the top row near `x=5`; pressing `up` there exits to map `0x33` at about `(17,47)`.
- Forest-approach outdoor pocket (`0x33`) notes:
  - Start area near `(6,30)` is blocked above.
  - West reaches about `(2,30)` but only slightly downward there.
  - A lower route exists via the east/lower lanes, reaching at least `(14,43)` where an NPC says `They're out for POKEMON fights!`
  - South is blocked at `(14,43)`; stop hammering that tile.
  - Better progress came from the farther-right lane: from about `(15,39)`, moving right to `(17,39)` and then north reached at least `(17,34)`.
  - East side opens farther than first thought: reachable tiles include roughly `(19..23,40..43)` and farther east to around `(29,41)`.
  - North is blocked at least at `(22,40)`, `(23,40)`, and `(27,41)`.
  - The trainer at about `(26,33)` is a Bug Catcher who challenges immediately.
  - From around `(25,34)`, going north repeatedly reaches at least `(25,20)`; this is a real upper lane.
  - The upper pocket contains another Bug Catcher on the right side plus a sign near the upper center.
  - The entire top outside row around `y=1` from roughly `x=6..32` is a dead boundary; `up` does not enter the forest there.
  - Far-right boundary caps around `x=32`, with a downward lane on that side.
  - At about `(28,40)` facing left, the adjacent sprite/object is an interactable NPC; text includes `You should carry extras!`
  - From the mid-right/upper route, a clear lane reaches the upper clearing around `(25,8)`, but `up` is blocked there and the upper-left continuation to about `(17,8)` is also a dead corridor. A small south dip exists to `(17,10)`, but left from `(16,10)` is blocked. Do not keep treating the upper-left edge as the forest entrance.
  - A different upper-left route reaches another top-row clearing. Verified `(11,7) -> (11,3)` and top-row movement through about `x=6..13` at `y=1`, but `up` is still blocked across that row. The visible upper-left sign/tree cluster is misleading.
  - New synthesis: the unexplored value is now in **descending from the upper-left/top-row clearings back into center-left lanes** and checking for a hidden west/center opening, not in testing more `up` presses.

## Battle heuristics
- Early wild fights can sometimes be handled with repeated `A`, but blind mash is unsafe once move choice matters.
- Squirtle knows **Tackle**, **Tail Whip**, and **Bubble**. Visible order: **TACKLE / TAIL WHIP / BUBBLE**.
- If the move cursor is on **TAIL WHIP**, then `down + A` selects **BUBBLE**; plain `A` wastes a turn.
- In simple wild fights, deliberately choose a damaging move; `Tackle` is the safe manual fallback.
- With low HP, avoid unnecessary battles; fleeing is preferred.
- After a battle, visible text can linger after battle-state tools think it cleared; keep advancing until overworld is visibly back.
- If stuck in move or Pokémon submenu, `B` backs out cleanly to the main battle menu.
- Reliable flee pattern from messy wild-battle substates: use `B` until at the 4-option menu, then try `down, A` or `right, A`; if needed, `down, right, A` from FIGHT also works.
- After `Got away safely!`, one more `A` is usually needed, then a short `wait` for the white transition back to overworld.
- After `used TACKLE!` or Bubble text, a short `wait` often reveals the actual next state more cleanly than extra inputs.
- Route 2 trainer fights: Bubble animation/text can lag a long time; prefer `wait` first, then single `A` presses with longer settle instead of mashing.
- Kakuna often uses **Harden**; the main risk is wasting turns with Tail Whip or mashing through lag, not sudden KO.

## Player's House 2F lesson
- Prior wedge hypothesis was wrong.
- Long-hold movement revealed a much larger room than short presses suggested; visible room edges are not always true movement bounds.

## Tooling / harness notes
- `save_checkpoint` may fail with a filesystem 500 error; do not rely on it until one succeeds.
- `probe_path` is excellent for discovering the effect of long holds because it logs coordinates after each step.
- `walk_path_until_event` is better than raw batched movement when following a known route through grass or near warps because it stops on battle/map change.
- Old `clear_battle_text` is broken (`ctx.frame is not a function`); use the safer state-based replacement instead.
- In-game SAVE is distinct from harness checkpoints and appears usable as a fallback.

arguments.content:
Route 2 north-side outdoor pocket (`map 0x33`) consolidated through turn 170: the true challenge is layout discovery, not a missed obvious north gate.

Key verified structure:
- Enter from the south gatehouse north exit at about `(17,47)`.
- Early west/start area near `(6,30)` is mostly boxed in above; west reaches roughly `(2,30)`.
- Meaningful progress comes from the east/lower lanes, not the apparent lower fence gaps on main Route 2 and not the first top row you see.
- A lower/east route reaches at least `(14,43)` where an NPC says `They're out for POKEMON fights!`
- Mid-right route opens to `(19..23,40..43)` and farther east to around `(29,41)`.
- At about `(28,40)` facing left, an NPC gives the hint `You should carry extras!`
- The nearby sign text is:
  - `TRAINER TIPS`
  - `No stealing of POKEMON from other trainers!`
  - `Catch only wild POKEMON!`
  - `stay away from grassy areas!`
- A trainer at about `(26,33)` is a Bug Catcher who auto-engages.
- From about `(25,34)`, a real upper lane climbs north through the map to around `(25,20)` and then into the upper pocket.
- Another upper sign near `(26,18)` is `TRAINER TIPS` ending with `...your POKEDEX evaluated!`

Most important negative result:
- The entire top outside row around `y=1` is a dead boundary across all tested segments, including roughly `x=6..32`.
- This includes:
  - upper-center/right clearing near `(25,8)`
  - upper-left route reaching `(11,3)`
  - top-row walking across about `x=6..13` at `y=1`
- Pressing `up` there does **not** enter Viridian Forest. The visible fence/tree/sign clusters along that top edge are misleading.

Other boundary notes:
- North is also blocked at least at `(22,40)`, `(23,40)`, `(27,41)` on the mid-right band.
- Far-right boundary caps around `x=32`, though a descending lane exists on that side.
- Around the upper-left/center, `(16,10)` left is blocked; this area is not an immediate hidden entrance either.

Battle/interaction notes tied to this area:
- Wild encounters frequently trigger in grass during probing; fleeing is usually the right choice unless a trainer battle is forced.
- Post-battle white screen is normal; a short `wait` restores the overworld.

Actionable next-step synthesis:
- If resuming from the upper-left/top-row area (turn 170 starts around `(8,1)` facing up), do **not** press `up` again.
- The best unexplored value is to move back down from the top row into center-left lanes and test for a hidden continuation or west/center opening, rather than re-checking the top boundary.
