data.name: update_playbook

arguments.content:
# Strategy playbook

## Early boot / intro
- If boot is blank white, `wait` a few seconds for logo/title.
- At title/menu, `start` or `a` proceeds.
- RAM location can be stale during boot/title; trust screenshot until gameplay begins.
- On player/rival naming screens, `start` accepts the default name.
- After intro, if spawned facing TV/SNES, `A` can reopen inspect text; clear it before moving.

## General
- Default d-pad press moves about one tile.
- Long-hold d-pad presses can reveal larger walkable areas than short probes suggest.
- A advances/confirms; B cancels; START opens menu.
- If a textbox is open, movement does nothing; clear text first.
- `B` often dismisses overworld textboxes more cleanly than `A`.
- Trust RAM for coordinates/state and screenshot for UI/world geometry.
- Probe systematically with RAM coordinates instead of retrying the same blocked tile.
- Adjacent sprites may be NPCs, not geometry; try `A` before assuming collision.
- Blank white/black screens during transitions often just need `wait`; check battle flag before panicking.
- When a battle just ended or is mid-transition, do not trust the partially rendered screen as overworld access.
- On route signs, `A` often leaves a textbox that blocks movement; clear with `B` before probing further.
- Pokecenter door transitions can leave a fully black screen briefly after RAM already reports a map change; wait ~60-90 frames.
- Outdoor-route NPCs near signs/trees can look like scenery; if `A` opens text, record tile + message.
- After trainer battles, the opponent's post-battle line can remain open on the overworld even though `battle: none`; clear that text before trying to move.
- If movement seems totally dead but RAM/facing stay fixed, re-read the screen for sign/NPC text before assuming a navigation wedge.

## Current route
- We have Squirtle Lv12.
- Oak's Parcel delivery and Pokédex progression are complete.
- Real progression is north from Viridian onto Route 2 / Viridian Forest, not Route 22.
- From Viridian City upper-left, the real north exit is **not** the sign at `(19,2)`. The verified city-to-Route-2 warp is from about `(17,0)` pressing `up`, which lands on Route 2 around `(7,71)`.
- From Route 2 upper area around `(7,44)`, `left,left,left,left,up` enters the gatehouse; the north exit leads to the outdoor forest-approach pocket (map `0x33`).
- Turn 185 anti-stall check: from Viridian upper-left sign area, a fully different probe succeeded by leaving the sign corridor, moving down to `(19,14)`, then using the known upper-city lane rightward. The small house/school in upper Viridian is enterable from about `(21,16)` by stepping `up`; it is accessible but non-progressing.
- Turn 186 east-side anti-stall check: from the small house area, moving right reaches the Mart/rock-wall side. The long north fence row is still blocked from the east (`(27,14)` blocked upward), and the rock-wall lane beside the Mart is just ordinary city space, not the Route 2 entrance.

## Pallet / Lab quick notes
- Player's House 1F exit: around `(2,7)` on map `0x25`, pressing `down` exits.
- Pallet north exit trigger after game start is on the east side of the north path around map `0x00`, `x=10,y=1`.
- Oak's Lab is the southwest/left large building in Pallet.
- Right-side Pallet house can heal Squirtle via NPC interaction.
- Verified starter: Squirtle.
- On nickname screen, `START` declines naming.
- Oak parcel return: talking to Oak around `(4,2)` facing right advances the delivery scene.
- Lower-right lab NPC around `(8,11)` is only Oak's aide.

## Route 1
- South entrance around map `0x0c`, `(10,28)`: the north tile by sign/fence is blocked.
- East-side bottom strip reaches at least `x=17` but `up` there is blocked; grass trap.
- Clerk on the road is an NPC, not geometry.
- Verified northbound route: from about `(5,18)`, go north to `y=14`, east along the rock barrier to about `x=14`, north through the opening, continue north, then loop west near the top fence and go north at about `(11,0)` to warp into Viridian.
- Viridian south return uses the fence opening around `x≈20,y≈30`.
- Upper grass pocket around `(10..13,6..8)` is dangerous and easy to overprobe; bias west/north over the east dead end.

## Viridian City / Pokecenter
- City sign near `(16,17)`: `VIRIDIAN CITY - The Eternally Green Paradise`.
- East-side building labeled `MART` is the Poké Mart.
- There is a fence/rock barrier around `y=28`; many central/east `up` attempts from below are blocked.
- Usable crossing: from around `(22,28)`, move left to about `x=19`, then north to about `(19,25)`, then right.
- Pokecenter frontage lesson: from outside near `(22,26)`, repeated `up` can fail; moving farther right to about `(24,26)` and then using `left, up` enters.
- Nurse Joy position: stand at `(3,3)` facing up and press `A`; choose `HEAL`.
- After healing, if dialog gets sticky, use `B`; from `We hope to see you again!`, `B` plus `down` closes and steps away.
- Upper city: the broad north fence line near `y≈14` is blocked; to reach upper city from the east half, work left to around `(20,16)` and then up.
- Small house/school entrance is around `(21,15)`/`(21,16)`; from outside in front of the door, stepping `up` enters interior map `0x2b`.
- Northwest woman is only hint text about Viridian Forest/Pewter.
- West exit to Route 22 is at the far-left side; from about `(0,14)`, pressing `left` warps out.
- Route 22 far-east upper area can warp back into Viridian upper-left around `(0,17)` by pressing `right` near `(39,9)`.
- Upper-left `TRAINER TIPS` sign around `(19,2)` is multi-page text, not a trap; `B` can page/close it. After clearing, movement from `(19,2)` down to `(19,6)` and left to `(17,6)` is valid, but further left at `(17,6)` is blocked and repeated right from `(19,6)` is also blocked.
- Exact north exit discovery: from the upper-left corridor, go to about `(17,0)` and press `up` to warp to Route 2 at about `(7,71)`. This is the reliable return route; do not keep trying to force progress at the sign on `(19,2)`.
- East-side Mart / rock-wall probe: from the school area, rightward travel reaches at least `(34,15..23)` beside the rock wall. `(27,14) -> up` is blocked by the same long north fence. Being left of the Mart door around `(28,20)` is normal city frontage, not progression.

## Viridian Mart
- On entry, interior may show briefly before clerk parcel dialog; a short `wait` can be correct.
- Parcel flow: advance greeting, receive `OAK's PARCEL`, then clear final goodbye line.
- After parcel dialog, free position is around `(2,5)`; to exit, walk down to about `(2,7)` then use `right, down` to align with the door.

## Route 22
- Everything verified so far points to optional/non-progressing space.
- Lower road is traversable broadly but `up` is blocked across many tested positions; far-east upper grass also dead-ends.
- Grass there causes many low-value encounters; avoid exploratory grass steps when HP is low.

## Route 2
- Central lower path from about `(7,71)` is open north to about `y=57`.
- From `(7,57)`, going left reaches a west lane; taking that north works to about `(3,48)`.
- Top-west area has a horizontal barrier/top edge: from `(3,48)`, right works to about `(7,48)`, but north is blocked there too.
- Avoid grinding the same top grass patch; it triggers wilds and reveals little.
- The apparent fence openings around `(x=3..11,y=62)` are fake from below; `up` is blocked there.
- Lower-left sign around `(5,66)` reads `ROUTE 2 / VIRIDIAN CITY - PEWTER CITY`.
- Real progress discovery: from the upper area around `(7,44)`, `left,left,left,left,up` enters the gatehouse.
- Gatehouse north exit alignment: inside `MAP_0x32`, the correct north exit is along the top row near `x=5`; pressing `up` there exits to map `0x33` at about `(17,47)`.
- New turn-189 caution: the whole tested upper row around `y=48` is still blocked from at least `x=0` through `x=4` (and previously at `x=7`). So standing on the top visible row is not enough by itself; the gatehouse entrance must be reached from a different lower/right alignment, not by hammering `up` from `(0..7,48)`.
- Forest-approach outdoor pocket (`0x33`) notes:
  - Start area near `(6,30)` is blocked above.
  - West reaches about `(2,30)` but only slightly downward there.
  - A lower route exists via the east/lower lanes, reaching at least `(14,43)` where an NPC says `They're out for POKEMON fights!`
  - South is blocked at `(14,43)`; stop hammering that tile.
  - Better progress came from the farther-right lane: from about `(15,39)`, moving right to `(17,39)` and then north reached at least `(17,34)`.
  - East side opens farther than first thought: reachable tiles include roughly `(19..23,40..43)` and farther east to around `(29,41)`.
  - North is blocked at least at `(22,40)`, `(23,40)`, and `(27,41)`.
  - The trainer at about `(26,33)` is a Bug Catcher who challenges immediately.
  - From around `(25,34)`, going north repeatedly reaches at least `(25,20)`; this is a real upper lane.
  - The upper pocket contains another Bug Catcher on the right side plus a sign near the upper center.
  - The entire top outside row around `y=1` from roughly `x=6..32` is a dead boundary; `up` does not enter the forest there.
  - Far-right boundary caps around `x=32`, with a downward lane on that side.
  - At about `(28,40)` facing left, the adjacent sprite/object is an interactable NPC; text includes `You should carry extras!`
  - From the mid-right/upper route, a clear lane reaches the upper clearing around `(25,8)`, but `up` is blocked there and the upper-left continuation to about `(17,8)` is also a dead corridor. A small south dip exists to `(17,10)`, but left from `(16,10)` is blocked. Do not keep treating the upper-left edge as the forest entrance.
  - A different upper-left route reaches another top-row clearing. Verified `(11,7) -> (11,3)` and top-row movement through about `x=6..13` at `y=1`, but `up` is still blocked across that row. The visible upper-left sign/tree cluster is misleading.
  - New synthesis: the unexplored value is now in descending from the upper-left/top-row clearings back into center-left lanes and checking for a hidden west/center opening, not in testing more `up` presses.
  - New center-left findings from turns 171-181:
    - From the upper-left top-row area, a valid descent runs roughly through `x=8` down to at least `y=21`.
    - At `(6,13)` and `(6,16)`, further `left` is blocked; at `(8,15)` and `(8,23)`, `right` is blocked.
    - Around `y=21`, left works to `(6,21)` but further left is blocked there.
    - Lower-left continuation exists through about `(6,23)` to `(3,23)`.
    - Around the trainer area, `(2,20) -> up` reaches `(2,19)`, but a second `up` is blocked; `(2,19) -> left` reaches `(1,19)` while `(2,19) -> right` is blocked.
    - The apparent object at `(2,19)` facing up is a Bug Catcher trainer, not scenery. After defeat, his text begins `I'm looking for the ...` and can be cleared with repeated `B`.
    - Important new connection: the center-left trainer corridor is not isolated. From the lower-left clearing around `(1..5,23)`, east/north routes reconnect back into the previously mapped upper-left lanes, including a climb through roughly `(7,23) -> (7,13) -> (6,11) -> (8,10) -> (6,10)`.

## Battle heuristics
- Early wild fights can sometimes be handled with repeated `A`, but blind mash is unsafe once move choice matters.
- Squirtle knows **Tackle**, **Tail Whip**, and **Bubble**. Visible order: **TACKLE / TAIL WHIP / BUBBLE**.
- If the move cursor is on **TAIL WHIP**, then `down + A` selects **BUBBLE**; plain `A` wastes a turn.
- In simple wild fights, deliberately choose a damaging move; `Tackle` is the safe manual fallback.
- With low HP, avoid unnecessary battles; fleeing is preferred.
- After a battle, visible text can linger after battle-state tools think it cleared; keep advancing until overworld is visibly back.
- If stuck in move or Pokémon submenu, `B` backs out cleanly to the main battle menu.
- Reliable flee pattern from messy wild-battle substates: use `B` until at the 4-option menu, then try `down, A` or `right, A`; if needed, `down, right, A` from FIGHT also works.
- After `Got away safely!`, one more `A` is usually needed, then a short `wait` for the white transition back to overworld.
- After `used TACKLE!` or Bubble text, a short `wait` often reveals the actual next state more cleanly than extra inputs.
- Route 2 trainer fights: Bubble animation/text can lag a long time; prefer `wait` first, then single `A` presses with longer settle instead of mashing.
- Kakuna often uses **Harden**; the main risk is wasting turns with Tail Whip or mashing through lag, not sudden KO.
- Strong recent lesson for early trainer fights with Squirtle: when text seems frozen, use a cadence of long waits (~60-120 frames) plus single `A` presses. This cleanly resolved Weedle/Kakuna text, poison ticks, level-up, and faint text better than mashing.

## Player's House 2F lesson
- Prior wedge hypothesis was wrong.
- Long-hold movement revealed a much larger room than short presses suggested; visible room edges are not always true movement bounds.

## Tooling / harness notes
- `save_checkpoint` may fail with a filesystem 500 error; do not rely on it until one succeeds.
- `probe_path` is excellent for discovering the effect of long holds because it logs coordinates after each step.
- `walk_path_until_event` is better than raw batched movement when following a known route through grass or near warps because it stops on battle/map change.
- Old `clear_battle_text` is broken (`ctx.frame is not a function`); use the safer state-based replacement instead.
- In-game SAVE is distinct from harness checkpoints and appears usable as a fallback.

- Re-read the gatehouse memories and tested the nearby upper row again from a different angle:

- Real progress discovery: from the upper area around `(7,44)`, `left,left,left,left,up` enters the gatehouse.
- Gatehouse north exit alignment: inside `MAP_0x32`, the correct north exit is along the top row near `x=5`; pressing `up` there exits to map `0x33` at about `(17,47)`.
- New turn-189 caution: the whole tested upper row around `y=48` is still blocked from at least `x=0` through `x=7` (reconfirmed west side at `(3,48)` and previously east side at `(7,48)`). So standing on the top visible row is not enough by itself; the gatehouse entrance must be reached from the known lower/right alignment around `(7,44)`, not by hammering `up` from `(0..7,48)`.
- Practical transit rule: if you emerge onto Route 2 from Viridian at `(7,71)`, the reliable return path toward forest progress is north to `y≈57`, west to `x≈3`, north to `y≈44`, then `left,left,left,left,up` into the gatehouse.
- Forest-approach outdoor pocket (`0x33`) notes:
  - Start area near `(6,30)` is blocked above.
  - West reaches about `(2,30)` but only slightly downward there.
  - A lower route exists via the east/lower lanes, reaching at least `(14,43)` where an NPC says `They're out for POKEMON fights!`
  - South is blocked at `(14,43)`; stop hammering that tile.
  - Better progress came from the farther-right lane: from about `(15,39)`, moving right to `(17,39)` and then north reached at least `(17,34)`.
  - East side opens farther than first thought: reachable tiles include roughly `(19..23,40..43)` and farther east to around `(29,41)`.
  - North is blocked at least at `(22,40)`, `(23,40)`, and `(27,41)`.
  - The trainer at about `(26,33)` is a Bug Catcher who challenges immediately.
  - From around `(25,34)`, going north repeatedly reaches at least `(25,20)`; this is a real upper lane.
  - The upper pocket contains another Bug Catcher on the right side plus a sign near the upper center.
  - The entire top outside row around `y=1` from roughly `x=6..32` is a dead boundary; `up` does not enter the forest there.
  - Far-right boundary caps around `x=32`, with a downward lane on that side.
  - At about `(28,40)` facing left, the adjacent sprite/object is an interactable NPC; text includes `You should carry extras!`
  - From the mid-right/upper route, a clear lane reaches the upper clearing around `(25,8)`, but `up` is blocked there and the upper-left continuation to about `(17,8)` is also a dead corridor. A small south dip exists to `(17,10)`, but left from `(16,10)` is blocked. Do not keep treating the upper-left edge as the forest entrance.
  - A different upper-left route reaches another top-row clearing. Verified `(11,7) -> (11,3)` and top-row movement through about `x=6..13` at `y=1`, but `up` is still blocked across that row. The visible upper-left sign/tree cluster is misleading.
  - New synthesis: the unexplored value is now in descending from the upper-left/top-row clearings back into center-left lanes and checking for a hidden west/center opening, not in testing more `up` presses.
  - New center-left findings from turns 171-181:
    - From the upper-left top-row area, a valid descent runs roughly through `x=8` down to at least `y=21`.
    - At `(6,13)` and `(6,16)`, further `left` is blocked; at `(8,15)` and `(8,23)`, `right` is blocked.
    - Around `y=21`, left works to `(6,21)` but further left is blocked there.
    - Lower-left continuation exists through about `(6,23)` to `(3,23)`.
    - Around the trainer area, `(2,20) -> up` reaches `(2,19)`, but a second `up` is blocked; `(2,19) -> left` reaches `(1,19)` while `(2,19) -> right` is blocked.
    - The apparent object at `(2,19)` facing up is a Bug Catcher trainer, not scenery. After defeat, his text begins `I'm looking for the ...` and can be cleared with repeated `B`.
    - Important new connection: the center-left trainer corridor is not isolated. From the lower-left clearing around `(1..5,23)`, east/north routes reconnect back into the previously mapped upper-left lanes, including a climb through roughly `(7,23) -> (7,13) -> (6,11) -> (8,10) -> (6,10)`.

## Battle heuristics
- Early wild fights can sometimes be handled with repeated `A`, but blind mash is unsafe once move choice matters.
- Squirtle knows **Tackle**, **Tail Whip**, and **Bubble**. Visible order: **TACKLE / TAIL WHIP / BUBBLE**.
- If the move cursor is on **TAIL WHIP**, then `down + A` selects **BUBBLE**; plain `A` wastes a turn.
- In simple wild fights, deliberately choose a damaging move; `Tackle` is the safe manual fallback.
- With low HP, avoid unnecessary battles; fleeing is preferred.
- After a battle, visible text can linger after battle-state tools think it cleared; keep advancing until overworld is visibly back.
- If stuck in move or Pokémon submenu, `B` backs out cleanly to the main battle menu.
- Reliable flee pattern from messy wild-battle substates: use `B` until at the 4-option menu, then try `down, A` or `right, A`; if needed, `down, right, A` from FIGHT also works.
- After `Got away safely!`, one more `A` is usually needed, then a short `wait` for the white transition back to overworld.
- After `used TACKLE!` or Bubble text, a short `wait` often reveals the actual next state more cleanly than extra inputs.
- Route 2 trainer fights: Bubble animation/text can lag a long time; prefer `wait` first, then single `A` presses with longer settle instead of mashing.
- Kakuna often uses **Harden**; the main risk is wasting turns with Tail Whip or mashing through lag, not sudden KO.
- Strong recent lesson for early trainer fights with Squirtle: when text seems frozen, use a cadence of long waits (~60-120 frames) plus single `A` presses. This cleanly resolved Weedle/Kakuna text, poison ticks, level-up, and faint text better than mashing.

## Player's House 2F lesson
- Prior wedge hypothesis was wrong.
- Long-hold movement revealed a much larger room than short presses suggested; visible room edges are not always true movement bounds.

## Tooling / harness notes
- `save_checkpoint` may fail with a filesystem 500 error; do not rely on it until one succeeds.
- `probe_path` is excellent for discovering the effect of long holds because it logs coordinates after each step.
- `walk_path_until_event` is better than raw batched movement when following a known route through grass or near warps because it stops on battle/map change.
- Old `clear_battle_text` is broken (`ctx.frame is not a function`); use the safer state-based replacement instead.
- In-game SAVE is distinct from harness checkpoints and appears usable as a fallback.
- Current safe checkpoint pair: `turn183_pokecenter_reset` for Viridian recovery and `turn189_route2_3_48` for Route 2 upper-area reprobes.

The tempting upper visible row around `y=48` is a repeat trap: north is blocked not only at the previously tested `(7,48)` side but also from the west/middle span around `(0..7,48)`, including a fresh reconfirm at `(3,48)` on turn 189. arguments.content: Route 2 y=48 trap vs remembered gatehouse y=44 mismatch (turn 197): from a clean reset at `(7,71)`, following the known return path reaches `(3,48)` exactly, but repeated `up` there is blocked. Probing the whole visible top row again showed `(4,48)`, `(5,48)`, `(7,48)`, and `(9,48)` all have blocked `up` as well. The old memory saying the south gatehouse entrance is around `(3,44)` is likely still compatible, but it means the entrance is NOT reached by simply standing on the y=48 top row and pressing up; some different lower/right alignment or descent/re-approach is required to get to y=44. A wild Pidgey can trigger while probing near `(4,48)`; safe resolution was slow waits plus Tackle, ending back on overworld with Squirtle at 32/34 HP.

data.name: update_playbook

arguments.content:
# Strategy playbook

## Early boot / intro
- If boot is blank white, `wait` a few seconds for logo/title.
- At title/menu, `start` or `a` proceeds.
- RAM location can be stale during boot/title; trust screenshot until gameplay begins.
- On player/rival naming screens, `start` accepts the default name.
- After intro, if spawned facing TV/SNES, `A` can reopen inspect text; clear it before moving.

## General
- Default d-pad press moves about one tile.
- Long-hold d-pad presses can reveal larger walkable areas than short probes suggest.
- A advances/confirms; B cancels; START opens menu.
- If a textbox is open, movement does nothing; clear text first.
- `B` often dismisses overworld textboxes more cleanly than `A`.
- Trust RAM for coordinates/state and screenshot for UI/world geometry.
- Probe systematically with RAM coordinates instead of retrying the same blocked tile.
- Adjacent sprites may be NPCs, not geometry; try `A` before assuming collision.
- Blank white/black screens during transitions often just need `wait`; check battle flag before panicking.
- When a battle just ended or is mid-transition, do not trust the partially rendered screen as overworld access.
- On route signs, `A` often leaves a textbox that blocks movement; clear with `B` before probing further.
- Pokecenter door transitions can leave a fully black screen briefly after RAM already reports a map change; wait ~60-90 frames.
- Outdoor-route NPCs near signs/trees can look like scenery; if `A` opens text, record tile + message.
- After trainer battles, the opponent's post-battle line can remain open on the overworld even though `battle: none`; clear that text before trying to move.
- If movement seems totally dead but RAM/facing stay fixed, re-read the screen for sign/NPC text before assuming a navigation wedge.

## Current route
- We have Squirtle Lv12.
- Oak's Parcel delivery and Pokédex progression are complete.
- Real progression is north from Viridian onto Route 2 / Viridian Forest, not Route 22.
- From Viridian City upper-left, the real north exit is **not** the sign at `(19,2)`. The verified city-to-Route-2 warp is from about `(17,0)` pressing `up`, which lands on Route 2 around `(7,71)`.
- From Route 2 upper area, the gatehouse is **not** reached by pressing `up` on the visible top row at `y=48`; that entire row is a trap across all tested west-side positions.
- Reliable Route 2 gatehouse approach from the Viridian landing `(7,71)`: go north to about `y=57`, west to about `x=3`, north to about `y=48`, then move **right to `(8,48)`**, go **up to `(8,46)`**, then continue around the building frontage to the remembered `y=44` row.
- Exact south gatehouse entrance (turn 199 refinement): from around `(8,46)`, the successful frontage path reconnects to the true entrance row, and the actual warp is reached from roughly `(3,44)` by pressing `up` into the gatehouse.
- Turn 185 anti-stall check: from Viridian upper-left sign area, a fully different probe succeeded by leaving the sign corridor, moving down to `(19,14)`, then using the known upper-city lane rightward. The small house/school in upper Viridian is enterable from about `(21,16)` by stepping `up`; it is accessible but non-progressing.
- Turn 186 east-side anti-stall check: from the small house area, moving right reaches the Mart/rock-wall side. The long north fence row is still blocked from the east (`(27,14)` blocked upward), and the rock-wall lane beside the Mart is just ordinary city space, not the Route 2 entrance.

## Pallet / Lab quick notes
- Player's House 1F exit: around `(2,7)` on map `0x25`, pressing `down` exits.
- Pallet north exit trigger after game start is on the east side of the north path around map `0x00`, `x=10,y=1`.
- Oak's Lab is the southwest/left large building in Pallet.
- Right-side Pallet house can heal Squirtle via NPC interaction.
- Verified starter: Squirtle.
- On nickname screen, `START` declines naming.
- Oak parcel return: talking to Oak around `(4,2)` facing right advances the delivery scene.
- Lower-right lab NPC around `(8,11)` is only Oak's aide.

## Route 1
- South entrance around map `0x0c`, `(10,28)`: the north tile by sign/fence is blocked.
- East-side bottom strip reaches at least `x=17` but `up` there is blocked; grass trap.
- Clerk on the road is an NPC, not geometry.
- Verified northbound route: from about `(5,18)`, go north to `y=14`, east along the rock barrier to about `x=14`, north through the opening, continue north, then loop west near the top fence and go north at about `(11,0)` to warp into Viridian.
- Viridian south return uses the fence opening around `x≈20,y≈30`.
- Upper grass pocket around `(10..13,6..8)` is dangerous and easy to overprobe; bias west/north over the east dead end.

## Viridian City / Pokecenter
- City sign near `(16,17)`: `VIRIDIAN CITY - The Eternally Green Paradise`.
- East-side building labeled `MART` is the Poké Mart.
- There is a fence/rock barrier around `y=28`; many central/east `up` attempts from below are blocked.
- Usable crossing: from around `(22,28)`, move left to about `x=19`, then north to about `(19,25)`, then right.
- Pokecenter frontage lesson: from outside near `(22,26)`, repeated `up` can fail; moving farther right to about `(24,26)` and then using `left, up` enters.
- Nurse Joy position: stand at `(3,3)` facing up and press `A`; choose `HEAL`.
- After healing, if dialog gets sticky, use `B`; from `We hope to see you again!`, `B` plus `down` closes and steps away.
- Upper city: the broad north fence line near `y≈14` is blocked; to reach upper city from the east half, work left to around `(20,16)` and then up.
- Small house/school entrance is around `(21,15)`/`(21,16)`; from outside in front of the door, stepping `up` enters interior map `0x2b`.
- Northwest woman is only hint text about Viridian Forest/Pewter.
- West exit to Route 22 is at the far-left side; from about `(0,14)`, pressing `left` warps out.
- Route 22 far-east upper area can warp back into Viridian upper-left around `(0,17)` by pressing `right` near `(39,9)`.
- Upper-left `TRAINER TIPS` sign around `(19,2)` is multi-page text, not a trap; `B` can page/close it. After clearing, movement from `(19,2)` down to `(19,6)` and left to `(17,6)` is valid, but further left at `(17,6)` is blocked and repeated right from `(19,6)` is also blocked.
- Exact north exit discovery: from the upper-left corridor, go to about `(17,0)` and press `up` to warp to Route 2 at about `(7,71)`. This is the reliable return route; do not keep trying to force progress at the sign on `(19,2)`.
- East-side Mart / rock-wall probe: from the school area, rightward travel reaches at least `(34,15..23)` beside the rock wall. `(27,14) -> up` is blocked by the same long north fence. Being left of the Mart door around `(28,20)` is normal city frontage, not progression.

## Viridian Mart
- On entry, interior may show briefly before clerk parcel dialog; a short `wait` can be correct.
- Parcel flow: advance greeting, receive `OAK's PARCEL`, then clear final goodbye line.
- After parcel dialog, free position is around `(2,5)`; to exit, walk down to about `(2,7)` then use `right, down` to align with the door.

## Route 22
- Everything verified so far points to optional/non-progressing space.
- Lower road is traversable broadly but `up` is blocked across many tested positions; far-east upper grass also dead-ends.
- Grass there causes many low-value encounters; avoid exploratory grass steps when HP is low.

## Route 2
- Central lower path from about `(7,71)` is open north to about `y=57`.
- From `(7,57)`, going left reaches a west lane; taking that north works to about `(3,48)`.
- The visible top-west row around `y=48` is a repeated trap: from `(0..7,48)`, `up` is blocked.
- Avoid grinding the same top grass patch; it triggers wilds and reveals little.
- The apparent fence openings around `(x=3..11,y=62)` are fake from below; `up` is blocked there.
- Lower-left sign around `(5,66)` reads `ROUTE 2 / VIRIDIAN CITY - PEWTER CITY`.
- Real progress discovery: from the upper area, do **not** stop at `(3,48)`. Move right to about `(8,48)`, then north to `(8,46)`, then follow the building edge back left/up to the true gatehouse entrance row around `y=44`.
- Gatehouse south entrance exact tile: the actual warp is from about `(3,44) -> up` into `MAP_0x32`.
- Gatehouse north exit alignment: inside `MAP_0x32`, the correct north exit is along the top row near `x=5`; pressing `up` there exits to map `0x33` at about `(17,47)`.
- Practical transit rule: if you emerge onto Route 2 from Viridian at `(7,71)`, the reliable return path toward forest progress is north to `y≈57`, west to `x≈3`, north to `y≈48`, right to `x≈8`, up to `y≈46`, then across the frontage to the real entrance row and into the gatehouse.
- Forest-approach outdoor pocket (`0x33`) notes:
  - Start area near `(6,30)` is blocked above.
  - West reaches about `(2,30)` but only slightly downward there.
  - A lower route exists via the east/lower lanes, reaching at least `(14,43)` where an NPC says `They're out for POKEMON fights!`
  - South is blocked at `(14,43)`; stop hammering that tile.
  - Better progress came from the farther-right lane: from about `(15,39)`, moving right to `(17,39)` and then north reached at least `(17,34)`.
  - East side opens farther than first thought: reachable tiles include roughly `(19..23,40..43)` and farther east to around `(29,41)`.
  - North is blocked at least at `(22,40)`, `(23,40)`, and `(27,41)`.
  - The trainer at about `(26,33)` is a Bug Catcher who challenges immediately.
  - From around `(25,34)`, going north repeatedly reaches at least `(25,20)`; this is a real upper lane.
  - The upper pocket contains another Bug Catcher on the right side plus a sign near the upper center.
  - The entire top outside row around `y=1` from roughly `x=6..32` is a dead boundary; `up` does not enter the forest there.
  - Far-right boundary caps around `x=32`, with a downward lane on that side.
  - At about `(28,40)` facing left, the adjacent sprite/object is an interactable NPC; text includes `You should carry extras!`
  - From the mid-right/upper route, a clear lane reaches the upper clearing around `(25,8)`, but `up` is blocked there and the upper-left continuation to about `(17,8)` is also a dead corridor. A small south dip exists to `(17,10)`, but left from `(16,10)` is blocked. Do not keep treating the upper-left edge as the forest entrance.
  - A different upper-left route reaches another top-row clearing. Verified `(11,7) -> (11,3)` and top-row movement through about `x=6..13` at `y=1`, but `up` is still blocked across that row. The visible upper-left sign/tree cluster is misleading.
  - New synthesis: the unexplored value is now in descending from the upper-left/top-row clearings back into center-left lanes and checking for a hidden west/center opening, not in testing more `up` presses.
  - New center-left findings from turns 171-181:
    - From the upper-left top-row area, a valid descent runs roughly through `x=8` down to at least `y=21`.
    - At `(6,13)` and `(6,16)`, further `left` is blocked; at `(8,15)` and `(8,23)`, `right` is blocked.
    - Around `y=21`, left works to `(6,21)` but further left is blocked there.
    - Lower-left continuation exists through about `(6,23)` to `(3,23)`.
    - Around the trainer area, `(2,20) -> up` reaches `(2,19)`, but a second `up` is blocked; `(2,19) -> left` reaches `(1,19)` while `(2,19) -> right` is blocked.
    - The apparent object at `(2,19)` facing up is a Bug Catcher trainer, not scenery. After defeat, his text begins `I'm looking for the ...` and can be cleared with repeated `B`.
    - Important new connection: the center-left trainer corridor is not isolated. From the lower-left clearing around `(1..5,23)`, east/north routes reconnect back into the previously mapped upper-left lanes, including a climb through roughly `(7,23) -> (7,13) -> (6,11) -> (8,10) -> (6,10)`.

## Battle heuristics
- Early wild fights can sometimes be handled with repeated `A`, but blind mash is unsafe once move choice matters.
- Squirtle knows **Tackle**, **Tail Whip**, and **Bubble**. Visible order: **TACKLE / TAIL WHIP / BUBBLE**.
- If the move cursor is on **TAIL WHIP**, then `down + A` selects **BUBBLE**; plain `A` wastes a turn.
- In simple wild fights, deliberately choose a damaging move; `Tackle` is the safe manual fallback.
- With low HP, avoid unnecessary battles; fleeing is preferred.
- After a battle, visible text can linger after battle-state tools think it cleared; keep advancing until overworld is visibly back.
- If stuck in move or Pokémon submenu, `B` backs out cleanly to the main battle menu.
- Reliable flee pattern from messy wild-battle substates: use `B` until at the 4-option menu, then try `down, A` or `right, A`; if needed, `down, right, A` from FIGHT also works.
- After `Got away safely!`, one more `A` is usually needed, then a short `wait` for the white transition back to overworld.
- After `used TACKLE!` or Bubble text, a short `wait` often reveals the actual next state more cleanly than extra inputs.
- Route 2 trainer fights: Bubble animation/text can lag a long time; prefer `wait` first, then single `A` presses with longer settle instead of mashing.
- Kakuna often uses **Harden**; the main risk is wasting turns with Tail Whip or mashing through lag, not sudden KO.
- Strong recent lesson for early trainer fights with Squirtle: when text seems frozen, use a cadence of long waits (~60-120 frames) plus single `A` presses. This cleanly resolved Weedle/Kakuna text, poison ticks, level-up, and faint text better than mashing.
- Current nuisance to remember: the automated flee helpers have been unreliable in some wild-battle substates; manual `B` recovery to the main 4-option menu plus explicit RUN selection is safer.

## Player's House 2F lesson
- Prior wedge hypothesis was wrong.
- Long-hold movement revealed a much larger room than short presses suggested; visible room edges are not always true movement bounds.

## Tooling / harness notes
- `save_checkpoint` may fail with a filesystem 500 error; do not rely on it until one succeeds.
- `probe_path` is excellent for discovering the effect of long holds because it logs coordinates after each step.
- `walk_path_until_event` is better than raw batched movement when following a known route through grass or near warps because it stops on battle/map change.
- Old `clear_battle_text` is broken (`ctx.frame is not a function`); use the safer state-based replacement instead.
- In-game SAVE is distinct from harness checkpoints and appears usable as a fallback.
- The first `route2_to_forest_approach` macro was buggy because it tried to over-batch the route; prefer short stepwise movement macros that stop on battle/map change.
- Current safe checkpoint trio: `turn188_route2_start`, `turn189_route2_3_48`, and `turn196_route2_start_fullhp`.

arguments.content:
# Strategy playbook

## Early boot / intro
- If boot is blank white, `wait` a few seconds for logo/title.
- At title/menu, `start` or `a` proceeds.
- RAM location can be stale during boot/title; trust screenshot until gameplay begins.
- On player/rival naming screens, `start` accepts the default name.
- After intro, if spawned facing TV/SNES, `A` can reopen inspect text; clear it before moving.

## General
- Default d-pad press moves about one tile.
- Long-hold d-pad presses can reveal larger walkable areas than short probes suggest.
- A advances/confirms; B cancels; START opens menu.
- If a textbox is open, movement does nothing; clear text first.
- `B` often dismisses overworld textboxes more cleanly than `A`.
- Trust RAM for coordinates/state and screenshot for UI/world geometry.
- Probe systematically with RAM coordinates instead of retrying the same blocked tile.
- Adjacent sprites may be NPCs, not geometry; try `A` before assuming collision.
- Blank white/black screens during transitions often just need `wait`; check battle flag before panicking.
- When a battle just ended or is mid-transition, do not trust the partially rendered screen as overworld access.
- On route signs, `A` often leaves a textbox that blocks movement; clear with `B` before probing further.
- Pokecenter door transitions can leave a fully black screen briefly after RAM already reports a map change; wait ~60-90 frames.
- Outdoor-route NPCs near signs/trees can look like scenery; if `A` opens text, record tile + message.
- After trainer battles, the opponent's post-battle line can remain open on the overworld even though `battle: none`; clear that text before trying to move.
- If movement seems totally dead but RAM/facing stay fixed, re-read the screen for sign/NPC text before assuming a navigation wedge.

## Current route
- We have Squirtle Lv12.
- Oak's Parcel delivery and Pokédex progression are complete.
- Real progression is north from Viridian onto Route 2 / Viridian Forest, not Route 22.
- From Viridian City upper-left, the real north exit is **not** the sign at `(19,2)`. The verified city-to-Route-2 warp is from about `(17,0)` pressing `up`, which lands on Route 2 around `(7,71)`.
- From Route 2 upper area, the gatehouse is **not** reached by pressing `up` on the visible top row at `y=48`; that entire row is a trap across all tested west-side positions.
- Reliable Route 2 gatehouse approach from the Viridian landing `(7,71)`: go north to about `y=57`, west to about `x=3`, north to about `y=48`, then move **right to `(8,48)`**, go **up to `(8,46)`**, then continue around the building frontage to the remembered `y=44` row.
- Exact south gatehouse entrance (turn 199 refinement): from around `(8,46)`, the successful frontage path reconnects to the true entrance row, and the actual warp is reached from roughly `(3,44)` by pressing `up` into the gatehouse.
- Turn 185 anti-stall check: from Viridian upper-left sign area, a fully different probe succeeded by leaving the sign corridor, moving down to `(19,14)`, then using the known upper-city lane rightward. The small house/school in upper Viridian is enterable from about `(21,16)` by stepping `up`; it is accessible but non-progressing.
- Turn 186 east-side anti-stall check: from the small house area, moving right reaches the Mart/rock-wall side. The long north fence row is still blocked from the east (`(27,14)` blocked upward), and the rock-wall lane beside the Mart is just ordinary city space, not the Route 2 entrance.

## Pallet / Lab quick notes
- Player's House 1F exit: around `(2,7)` on map `0x25`, pressing `down` exits.
- Pallet north exit trigger after game start is on the east side of the north path around map `0x00`, `x=10,y=1`.
- Oak's Lab is the southwest/left large building in Pallet.
- Right-side Pallet house can heal Squirtle via NPC interaction.
- Verified starter: Squirtle.
- On nickname screen, `START` declines naming.
- Oak parcel return: talking to Oak around `(4,2)` facing right advances the delivery scene.
- Lower-right lab NPC around `(8,11)` is only Oak's aide.

## Route 1
- South entrance around map `0x0c`, `(10,28)`: the north tile by sign/fence is blocked.
- East-side bottom strip reaches at least `x=17` but `up` there is blocked; grass trap.
- Clerk on the road is an NPC, not geometry.
- Verified northbound route: from about `(5,18)`, go north to `y=14`, east along the rock barrier to about `x=14`, north through the opening, continue north, then loop west near the top fence and go north at about `(11,0)` to warp into Viridian.
- Viridian south return uses the fence opening around `x≈20,y≈30`.
- Upper grass pocket around `(10..13,6..8)` is dangerous and easy to overprobe; bias west/north over the east dead end.

## Viridian City / Pokecenter
- City sign near `(16,17)`: `VIRIDIAN CITY - The Eternally Green Paradise`.
- East-side building labeled `MART` is the Poké Mart.
- There is a fence/rock barrier around `y=28`; many central/east `up` attempts from below are blocked.
- Usable crossing: from around `(22,28)`, move left to about `x=19`, then north to about `(19,25)`, then right.
- Pokecenter frontage lesson: from outside near `(22,26)`, repeated `up` can fail; moving farther right to about `(24,26)` and then using `left, up` enters.
- Nurse Joy position: stand at `(3,3)` facing up and press `A`; choose `HEAL`.
- After healing, if dialog gets sticky, use `B`; from `We hope to see you again!`, `B` plus `down` closes and steps away.
- Upper city: the broad north fence line near `y≈14` is blocked; to reach upper city from the east half, work left to around `(20,16)` and then up.
- Small house/school entrance is around `(21,15)`/`(21,16)`; from outside in front of the door, stepping `up` enters interior map `0x2b`.
- Northwest woman is only hint text about Viridian Forest/Pewter.
- West exit to Route 22 is at the far-left side; from about `(0,14)`, pressing `left` warps out.
- Route 22 far-east upper area can warp back into Viridian upper-left around `(0,17)` by pressing `right` near `(39,9)`.
- Upper-left `TRAINER TIPS` sign around `(19,2)` is multi-page text, not a trap; `B` can page/close it. After clearing, movement from `(19,2)` down to `(19,6)` and left to `(17,6)` is valid, but further left at `(17,6)` is blocked and repeated right from `(19,6)` is also blocked.
- Exact north exit discovery: from the upper-left corridor, go to about `(17,0)` and press `up` to warp to Route 2 at about `(7,71)`. This is the reliable return route; do not keep trying to force progress at the sign on `(19,2)`.
- East-side Mart / rock-wall probe: from the school area, rightward travel reaches at least `(34,15..23)` beside the rock wall. `(27,14) -> up` is blocked by the same long north fence. Being left of the Mart door around `(28,20)` is normal city frontage, not progression.

## Route 22
- Everything verified so far points to optional/non-progressing space.
- Lower road is traversable broadly but `up` is blocked across many tested positions; far-east upper grass also dead-ends.
- Grass there causes many low-value encounters; avoid exploratory grass steps when HP is low.

## Route 2
- Central lower path from about `(7,71)` is open north to about `y=57`.
- From `(7,57)`, going left reaches a west lane; taking that north works to about `(3,48)`.
- The visible top-west row around `y=48` is a repeated trap: from `(0..7,48)`, `up` is blocked.
- Avoid grinding the same top grass patch; it triggers wilds and reveals little.
- The apparent fence openings around `(x=3..11,y=62)` are fake from below; `up` is blocked there.
- Lower-left sign around `(5,66)` reads `ROUTE 2 / VIRIDIAN CITY - PEWTER CITY`.
- Real progress discovery: from the upper area, do **not** stop at `(3,48)`. Move right to about `(8,48)`, then north to `(8,46)`, then follow the building edge back left/up to the true gatehouse entrance row around `y=44`.
- Gatehouse south entrance exact tile: the actual warp is from about `(3,44) -> up` into `MAP_0x32`.
- Gatehouse north exit alignment: inside `MAP_0x32`, the correct north exit is along the top row near `x=5`; pressing `up` there exits to map `0x33` at about `(17,47)`.
- Practical transit rule: if you emerge onto Route 2 from Viridian at `(7,71)`, the reliable return path toward forest progress is north to `y≈57`, west to `x≈3`, north to `y≈48`, right to `x≈8`, up to `y≈46`, then across the frontage to the real entrance row and into the gatehouse.
- Forest-approach outdoor pocket (`0x33`) notes:
  - Start area near `(6,30)` is blocked above.
  - West reaches about `(2,30)` but only slightly downward there.
  - A lower route exists via the east/lower lanes, reaching at least `(14,43)` where an NPC says `They're out for POKEMON fights!`
  - South is blocked at `(14,43)`; stop hammering that tile.
  - Better progress came from the farther-right lane: from about `(15,39)`, moving right to `(17,39)` and then north reached at least `(17,34)`.
  - East side opens farther than first thought: reachable tiles include roughly `(19..23,40..43)` and farther east to around `(29,41)`.
  - North is blocked at least at `(22,40)`, `(23,40)`, and `(27,41)`.
  - The trainer at about `(26,33)` is a Bug Catcher who challenges immediately.
  - From around `(25,34)`, going north repeatedly reaches at least `(25,20)`; this is a real upper lane.
  - The upper pocket contains another Bug Catcher on the right side plus a sign near the upper center.
  - The entire top outside row around `y=1` from roughly `x=6..32` is a dead boundary; `up` does not enter the forest there.
  - Far-right boundary caps around `x=32`, with a downward lane on that side.
  - At about `(28,40)` facing left, the adjacent sprite/object is an interactable NPC; text includes `You should carry extras!`
  - From the mid-right/upper route, a clear lane reaches the upper clearing around `(25,8)`, but `up` is blocked there and the upper-left continuation to about `(17,8)` is also a dead corridor. A small south dip exists to `(17,10)`, but left from `(16,10)` is blocked. Do not keep treating the upper-left edge as the forest entrance.
  - A different upper-left route reaches another top-row clearing. Verified `(11,7) -> (11,3)` and top-row movement through about `x=6..13` at `y=1`, but `up` is still blocked across that row. The visible upper-left sign/tree cluster is misleading.
  - New synthesis: the unexplored value is now in descending from the upper-left/top-row clearings back into center-left lanes and checking for a hidden west/center opening, not in testing more `up` presses.
  - New center-left findings from turns 171-181:
    - From the upper-left top-row area, a valid descent runs roughly through `x=8` down to at least `y=21`.
    - At `(6,13)` and `(6,16)`, further `left` is blocked; at `(8,15)` and `(8,23)`, `right` is blocked.
    - Around `y=21`, left works to `(6,21)` but further left is blocked there.
    - Lower-left continuation exists through about `(6,23)` to `(3,23)`.
    - Around the trainer area, `(2,20) -> up` reaches `(2,19)`, but a second `up` is blocked; `(2,19) -> left` reaches `(1,19)` while `(2,19) -> right` is blocked.
    - The apparent object at `(2,19)` facing up is a Bug Catcher trainer, not scenery. After defeat, his text begins `I'm looking for the ...` and can be cleared with repeated `B`.
    - Important new connection: the center-left trainer corridor is not isolated. From the lower-left clearing around `(1..5,23)`, east/north routes reconnect back into the previously mapped upper-left lanes, including a climb through roughly `(7,23) -> (7,13) -> (6,11) -> (8,10) -> (6,10)`.
  - Turns 201-209 refinement near the antidote sign / mid pocket:
    - From gate north start `(17,47)`, a real approach exists through the mid-right grass: `(17,43)` then right to about `(20,43)`, and `up` works to `(20,41)`.
    - Another valid lane climbs from about `(17,35)` to `(17,32)`, but `up` from `(17,32)` is blocked.
    - The nearby sign is **not** read from `(15,32)` facing up; the successful interaction was from about `(15,32)` **facing right**, yielding `For poison, use ANTIDOTE! Get it at POKE...`.
    - Around the sign choke, `up` is blocked from `(14,32)`, `(15,32)`, `(16,32)`, `(17,32)`, and `(18,32)`.
    - `(15,32) -> down -> (15,33)` and `(14,32) -> down -> (14,33)` are valid; this is a local shelf/choke, not the entrance.
    - Far-left upper lane note: from about `(2,30)`, left reaches `(1,30)` but `down` there is blocked.
    - Current search bias should move **east/southeast from the `(14..18,33..34)` zone** and test for a connection toward the already-known east-side lanes, rather than rechecking the blocked sign row.

## Battle heuristics
- Early wild fights can sometimes be handled with repeated `A`, but blind mash is unsafe once move choice matters.
- Squirtle knows **Tackle**, **Tail Whip**, and **Bubble**. Visible order: **TACKLE / TAIL WHIP / BUBBLE**.
- If the move cursor is on **TAIL WHIP**, then `down + A` selects **BUBBLE**; plain `A` wastes a turn.
- In simple wild fights, deliberately choose a damaging move; `Tackle` is the safe manual fallback.
- With low HP, avoid unnecessary battles; fleeing is preferred.
- After a battle, visible text can linger after battle-state tools think it cleared; keep advancing until overworld is visibly back.
- If stuck in move or Pokémon submenu, `B` backs out cleanly to the main battle menu.
- Reliable flee pattern from messy wild-battle substates: use `B` until at the 4-option menu, then try `down, A` or `right, A`; if needed, `down, right, A` from FIGHT also works.
- After `Got away safely!`, one more `A` is usually needed, then a short `wait` for the white transition back to overworld.
- After `used TACKLE!` or Bubble text, a short `wait` often reveals the actual next state more cleanly than extra inputs.
- Route 2 trainer fights: Bubble animation/text can lag a long time; prefer `wait` first, then single `A` presses with longer settle instead of mashing.
- Kakuna often uses **Harden**; the main risk is wasting turns with Tail Whip or mashing through lag, not sudden KO.
- Strong recent lesson for early trainer fights with Squirtle: when text seems frozen, use a cadence of long waits (~60-120 frames) plus single `A` presses. This cleanly resolved Weedle/Kakuna text, poison ticks, level-up, and faint text better than mashing.
- Current nuisance to remember: the automated flee helpers have been unreliable in some wild-battle substates; manual `B` recovery to the main 4-option menu plus explicit RUN selection is safer.
- For opening wild battles in this area, the common stable pattern is: one or two `A` presses with waits to finish the intro/send-out sequence, confirm the 4-option menu is really up, then select RUN explicitly. Do not assume RUN inputs sent too early will land.

## Player's House 2F lesson
- Prior wedge hypothesis was wrong.
- Long-hold movement revealed a much larger room than short presses suggested; visible room edges are not always true movement bounds.

## Tooling / harness notes
- `save_checkpoint` may fail with a filesystem 500 error; do not rely on it until one succeeds.
- `probe_path` is excellent for discovering the effect of long holds because it logs coordinates after each step.
- `walk_path_until_event` is better than raw batched movement when following a known route through grass or near warps because it stops on battle/map change.
- Old `clear_battle_text` is broken (`ctx.frame is not a function`); use the safer state-based replacement instead.
- In-game SAVE is distinct from harness checkpoints and appears usable as a fallback.
- The first `route2_to_forest_approach` macro was buggy because it tried to over-batch the route; prefer short stepwise movement macros that stop on battle/map change.
- Current safe checkpoint trio: `turn188_route2_start`, `turn189_route2_3_48`, and `turn196_route2_start_fullhp`.
- We keep repeating the same manual early-wild escape cadence in Route 2 grass; if this continues, replace it with a stricter opening-battle escape helper rather than relying on the flaky older flee macros.

- West exit to Route 22 is at the far-left side; from about `(0,14)`, pressing `left` warps out.
- Route 22 far-east upper area can warp back into Viridian upper-left around `(0,17)` by pressing `right` near `(39,9)`.
- Upper-left `TRAINER TIPS` sign around `(19,2)` is multi-page text, not a trap; `B` can page/close it. After clearing, movement from `(19,2)` down to `(19,6)` and left to `(17,6)` is valid, but further left at `(17,6)` is blocked and repeated right from `(19,6)` is also blocked.
- Exact north exit discovery: from the upper-left corridor, go to about `(17,0)` and press `up` to warp to Route 2 at about `(7,71)`.
- East-side Mart / rock-wall probe: from the school area, rightward travel reaches at least `(34,15..23)` beside the rock wall. `(27,14) -> up` is blocked by the same long north fence. Being left of the Mart door around `(28,20)` is normal city frontage, not progression.

## Route 22
- Everything verified so far points to optional/non-progressing space.
- Lower road is traversable broadly but `up` is blocked across many tested positions; far-east upper grass also dead-ends.
- Grass there causes many low-value encounters; avoid exploratory grass steps when HP is low.

## Route 2
- Central lower path from about `(7,71)` is open north to about `y=57`.
- From `(7,57)`, going left reaches a west lane; taking that north works to about `(3,48)`.
- The visible top-west row around `y=48` is a repeated trap: from `(0..7,48)`, `up` is blocked.
- Avoid grinding the same top grass patch; it triggers wilds and reveals little.
- The apparent fence openings around `(x=3..11,y=62)` are fake from below; `up` is blocked there.
- Lower-left sign around `(5,66)` reads `ROUTE 2 / VIRIDIAN CITY - PEWTER CITY`.
- Real progress discovery: from the upper area, do **not** stop at `(3,48)`. Move right to about `(8,48)`, then north to `(8,46)`, then follow the building edge back left/up to the true gatehouse entrance row around `y=44`.
- Gatehouse south entrance exact tile: the actual warp is from about `(3,44) -> up` into `MAP_0x32`.
- Gatehouse north exit alignment: inside `MAP_0x32`, the correct north exit is along the top row near `x=5`; pressing `up` there exits to map `0x33` at about `(17,47)`.
- Practical transit rule: if you emerge onto Route 2 from Viridian at `(7,71)`, the reliable return path toward forest progress is north to `y≈57`, west to `x≈3`, north to `y≈48`, right to `x≈8`, up to `y≈46`, then across the frontage to the real entrance row and into the gatehouse.
- Forest-approach outdoor pocket (`0x33`) notes:
  - Start area near `(6,30)` is blocked above.
  - West reaches about `(2,30)` but only slightly downward there.
  - A lower route exists via the east/lower lanes, reaching at least `(14,43)` where an NPC says `They're out for POKEMON fights!`
  - South is blocked at `(14,43)`; stop hammering that tile.
  - Better progress came from the farther-right lane: from about `(15,39)`, moving right to `(17,39)` and then north reached at least `(17,34)`.
  - East side opens farther than first thought: reachable tiles include roughly `(19..23,40..43)` and farther east to around `(29,41)`.
  - North is blocked at least at `(22,40)`, `(23,40)`, and `(27,41)`.
  - The trainer at about `(26,33)` is a Bug Catcher who challenges immediately.
  - From around `(25,34)`, going north repeatedly reaches at least `(25,20)`; this is a real upper lane.
  - The upper pocket contains another Bug Catcher on the right side plus a sign near the upper center.
  - The entire top outside row around `y=1` from roughly `x=6..32` is a dead boundary; `up` does not enter the forest there.
  - Far-right boundary caps around `x=32`, with a downward lane on that side.
  - At about `(28,40)` facing left, the adjacent sprite/object is an interactable NPC; text includes `You should carry extras!`
  - From the mid-right/upper route, a clear lane reaches the upper clearing around `(25,8)`, but `up` is blocked there and the upper-left continuation to about `(17,8)` is also a dead corridor. A small south dip exists to `(17,10)`, but left from `(16,10)` is blocked. Do not keep treating the upper-left edge as the forest entrance.
  - A different upper-left route reaches another top-row clearing. Verified `(11,7) -> (11,3)` and top-row movement through about `x=6..13` at `y=1`, but `up` is still blocked across that row. The visible upper-left sign/tree cluster is misleading.
  - New synthesis: the unexplored value is now in descending from the upper-left/top-row clearings back into center-left lanes and checking for a hidden west/center opening, not in testing more `up` presses.
  - New center-left findings from turns 171-181:
    - From the upper-left top-row area, a valid descent runs roughly through `x=8` down to at least `y=21`.
    - At `(6,13)` and `(6,16)`, further `left` is blocked; at `(8,15)` and `(8,23)`, `right` is blocked.
    - Around `y=21`, left works to `(6,21)` but further left is blocked there.
    - Lower-left continuation exists through about `(6,23)` to `(3,23)`.
    - Around the trainer area, `(2,20) -> up` reaches `(2,19)`, but a second `up` is blocked; `(2,19) -> left` reaches `(1,19)` while `(2,19) -> right` is blocked.
    - The apparent object at `(2,19)` facing up is a Bug Catcher trainer, not scenery. After defeat, his text begins `I'm looking for the ...` and can be cleared with repeated `B`.
    - Important new connection: the center-left trainer corridor is not isolated. From the lower-left clearing around `(1..5,23)`, east/north routes reconnect back into the previously mapped upper-left lanes, including a climb through roughly `(7,23) -> (7,13) -> (6,11) -> (8,10) -> (6,10)`.
  - Turns 201-209 refinement near the antidote sign / mid pocket:
    - From gate north start `(17,47)`, a real approach exists through the mid-right grass: `(17,43)` then right to about `(20,43)`, and `up` works to `(20,41)`.
    - Another valid lane climbs from about `(17,35)` to `(17,32)`, but `up` from `(17,32)` is blocked.
    - The nearby sign is **not** read from `(15,32)` facing up; the successful interaction was from about `(15,32)` **facing right**, yielding `For poison, use ANTIDOTE! Get it at POKE...`.
    - Around the sign choke, `up` is blocked from `(14,32)`, `(15,32)`, `(16,32)`, `(17,32)`, and `(18,32)`.
    - `(15,32) -> down -> (15,33)` and `(14,32) -> down -> (14,33)` are valid; this is a local shelf/choke, not the entrance.
    - Far-left upper lane note: from about `(2,30)`, left reaches `(1,30)` but `down` there is blocked.
    - Current search bias should move **east/southeast from the `(14..18,33..34)` zone** and test for a connection toward the already-known east-side lanes, rather than rechecking the blocked sign row.

## Battle heuristics
- Early wild fights can sometimes be handled with repeated `A`, but blind mash is unsafe once move choice matters.
- Squirtle knows **Tackle**, **Tail Whip**, and **Bubble**. Visible order: **TACKLE / TAIL WHIP / BUBBLE**.
- If the move cursor is on **TAIL WHIP**, then `down + A` selects **BUBBLE**; plain `A` wastes a turn.
- In simple wild fights, deliberately choose a damaging move; `Tackle` is the safe manual fallback.
- With low HP, avoid unnecessary battles; fleeing is preferred.
- After a battle, visible text can linger after battle-state tools think it cleared; keep advancing until overworld is visibly back.
- If stuck in move or Pokémon submenu, `B` backs out cleanly to the main battle menu.
- Reliable flee pattern from messy wild-battle substates: use `B` until at the 4-option menu, then try `down, A` or `right, A`; if needed, `down, right, A` from FIGHT also works.
- After `Got away safely!`, one more `A` is usually needed, then a short `wait` for the white transition back to overworld.
- After `used TACKLE!` or Bubble text, a short `wait` often reveals the actual next state more cleanly than extra inputs.
- Route 2 trainer fights: Bubble animation/text can lag a long time; prefer `wait` first, then single `A` presses with longer settle instead of mashing.
- Kakuna often uses **Harden**; the main risk is wasting turns with Tail Whip or mashing through lag, not sudden KO.
- Strong recent lesson for early trainer fights with Squirtle: when text seems frozen, use a cadence of long waits (~60-120 frames) plus single `A` presses. This cleanly resolved Weedle/Kakuna text, poison ticks, level-up, and faint text better than mashing.
- Current nuisance to remember: the automated flee helpers have been unreliable in some wild-battle substates; manual `B` recovery to the main 4-option menu plus explicit RUN selection is safer.
- For opening wild battles in this area, the common stable pattern is: one or two `A` presses with waits to finish the intro/send-out sequence, confirm the 4-option menu is really up, then select RUN explicitly.

## Player's House 2F lesson
- Prior wedge hypothesis was wrong.
- Long-hold movement revealed a much larger room than short presses suggested; visible room edges are not always true movement bounds.

## Tooling / harness notes
- `save_checkpoint` may fail with a filesystem 500 error; do not rely on it until one succeeds.
- `probe_path` is excellent for discovering the effect of long holds because it logs coordinates after each step.
- `walk_path_until_event` is better than raw batched movement when following a known route through grass or near warps because it stops on battle/map change.
- Old `clear_battle_text` is broken (`ctx.frame is not a function`); use the safer state-based replacement instead.
- In-game SAVE is distinct from harness checkpoints and appears usable as a fallback.
- The first `route2_to_forest_approach` macro was buggy because it tried to over-batch the route; prefer short stepwise movement macros that stop on battle/map change.
- Current safe checkpoint trio: `turn188_route2_start`, `turn189_route2_3_48`, and `turn196_route2_start_fullhp`.

arguments.content: Route 2 north-side outdoor pocket far-right probe (turn 213): starting from the east NPC area at about `(28,40)` after clearing the textbox, the right side is walkable farther than the earlier local stall suggested. Verified pathing included `(29,40)`, `(29,41)`, `(29,42)`, `(30,42)`, `(31,42)`, `(31,41)`, `(31,40)`, `(30,40)`, and farther right to `(32,40)`. From there, going down reaches `(32,41) -> (32,42) -> (32,43)`, but another `down` from `(32,43)` is blocked. The lower-right area also connects left through `(31,43)` and `(30,43)`, then back up through `(30,42) -> (30,41) -> (29,41) -> (29,40)`. So the far-right side is a connected outer lane with a hard east boundary at `x=32` and a blocked southward continuation at least from `(32,43)`.
