arguments.content:
# Strategy playbook

## Early boot / intro
- If boot is blank white, `wait` a few seconds for logo/title.
- At title/menu, `start` or `a` proceeds.
- RAM location can be stale during boot/title; trust screenshot until gameplay begins.
- On player/rival naming screens, `start` accepts the default name.
- After intro, if spawned facing TV/SNES, `A` can reopen inspect text; clear it before moving.

## General
- Default d-pad press moves about one tile.
- Long-hold d-pad presses can reveal larger walkable areas than short probes suggest.
- A advances/confirms; B cancels; START opens menu.
- If a textbox is open, movement does nothing; clear text first.
- `B` often dismisses overworld textboxes more cleanly than `A`.
- Trust RAM for coordinates/state and screenshot for UI/world geometry.
- Probe systematically with RAM coordinates instead of retrying the same blocked tile.
- Adjacent sprites may be NPCs, not geometry; try `A` before assuming collision.
- Blank white/black screens during transitions often just need `wait`; check battle flag before panicking.
- When a battle just ended or is mid-transition, do not trust the partially rendered screen as overworld access.
- On route signs, `A` often leaves a textbox that blocks movement; clear with `B` before probing further.
- Pokecenter door transitions can leave a fully black screen briefly after RAM already reports a map change; wait ~60-90 frames.
- Outdoor-route NPCs near signs/trees can look like scenery; if `A` opens text, record tile + message.
- After trainer battles, the opponent's post-battle line can remain open on the overworld even though `battle: none`; clear that text before trying to move.
- If movement seems totally dead but RAM/facing stay fixed, re-read the screen for sign/NPC text before assuming a navigation wedge.

## Current route
- We have Squirtle Lv12.
- Oak's Parcel delivery and Pokédex progression are complete.
- Real progression is north from Viridian onto Route 2 / Viridian Forest, not Route 22.
- From Viridian City upper-left, the real north exit is **not** the sign at `(19,2)`. The verified city-to-Route-2 warp is from about `(17,0)` pressing `up`, which lands on Route 2 around `(7,71)`.
- From Route 2 upper area, the gatehouse is **not** reached by pressing `up` on the visible top row at `y=48`; that entire row is a trap across all tested west-side positions.
- Reliable Route 2 gatehouse approach from the Viridian landing `(7,71)`: go north to about `y=57`, west to about `x=3`, north to about `y=48`, then move **right to `(8,48)`**, go **up to `(8,46)`**, then continue around the building frontage to the remembered `y=44` row.
- Exact south gatehouse entrance (turn 199 refinement): from around `(8,46)`, the successful frontage path reconnects to the true entrance row, and the actual warp is reached from roughly `(3,44)` by pressing `up` into the gatehouse.
- Turn 185 anti-stall check: from Viridian upper-left sign area, a fully different probe succeeded by leaving the sign corridor, moving down to `(19,14)`, then using the known upper-city lane rightward. The small house/school in upper Viridian is enterable from about `(21,16)` by stepping `up`; it is accessible but non-progressing.
- Turn 186 east-side anti-stall check: from the small house area, moving right reaches the Mart/rock-wall side. The long north fence row is still blocked from the east (`(27,14)` blocked upward), and the rock-wall lane beside the Mart is just ordinary city space, not the Route 2 entrance.

## Pallet / Lab quick notes
- Player's House 1F exit: around `(2,7)` on map `0x25`, pressing `down` exits.
- Pallet north exit trigger after game start is on the east side of the north path around map `0x00`, `x=10,y=1`.
- Oak's Lab is the southwest/left large building in Pallet.
- Right-side Pallet house can heal Squirtle via NPC interaction.
- Verified starter: Squirtle.
- On nickname screen, `START` declines naming.
- Oak parcel return: talking to Oak around `(4,2)` facing right advances the delivery scene.
- Lower-right lab NPC around `(8,11)` is only Oak's aide.

## Route 1
- South entrance around map `0x0c`, `(10,28)`: the north tile by sign/fence is blocked.
- East-side bottom strip reaches at least `x=17` but `up` there is blocked; grass trap.
- Clerk on the road is an NPC, not geometry.
- Verified northbound route: from about `(5,18)`, go north to `y=14`, east along the rock barrier to about `x=14`, north through the opening, continue north, then loop west near the top fence and go north at about `(11,0)` to warp into Viridian.
- Viridian south return uses the fence opening around `x≈20,y≈30`.
- Upper grass pocket around `(10..13,6..8)` is dangerous and easy to overprobe; bias west/north over the east dead end.

## Viridian City / Pokecenter
- City sign near `(16,17)`: `VIRIDIAN CITY - The Eternally Green Paradise`.
- East-side building labeled `MART` is the Poké Mart.
- There is a fence/rock barrier around `y=28`; many central/east `up` attempts from below are blocked.
- Usable crossing: from around `(22,28)`, move left to about `x=19`, then north to about `(19,25)`, then right.
- Pokecenter frontage lesson: from outside near `(22,26)`, repeated `up` can fail; moving farther right to about `(24,26)` and then using `left, up` enters.
- Nurse Joy position: stand at `(3,3)` facing up and press `A`; choose `HEAL`.
- After healing, if dialog gets sticky, use `B`; from `We hope to see you again!`, `B` plus `down` closes and steps away.
- Upper city: the broad north fence line near `y≈14` is blocked; to reach upper city from the east half, work left to around `(20,16)` and then up.
- Small house/school entrance is around `(21,15)`/`(21,16)`; from outside in front of the door, stepping `up` enters interior map `0x2b`.
- Northwest woman is only hint text about Viridian Forest/Pewter.
- West exit to Route 22 is at the far-left side; from about `(0,14)`, pressing `left` warps out.
- Route 22 far-east upper area can warp back into Viridian upper-left around `(0,17)` by pressing `right` near `(39,9)`.
- Upper-left `TRAINER TIPS` sign around `(19,2)` is multi-page text, not a trap; `B` can page/close it. After clearing, movement from `(19,2)` down to `(19,6)` and left to `(17,6)` is valid, but further left at `(17,6)` is blocked and repeated right from `(19,6)` is also blocked.
- Exact north exit discovery: from the upper-left corridor, go to about `(17,0)` and press `up` to warp to Route 2 at about `(7,71)`.
- East-side Mart / rock-wall probe: from the school area, rightward travel reaches at least `(34,15..23)` beside the rock wall. `(27,14) -> up` is blocked by the same long north fence. Being left of the Mart door around `(28,20)` is normal city frontage, not progression.

## Route 22
- Everything verified so far points to optional/non-progressing space.
- Lower road is traversable broadly but `up` is blocked across many tested positions; far-east upper grass also dead-ends.
- Grass there causes many low-value encounters; avoid exploratory grass steps when HP is low.

## Route 2
- Central lower path from about `(7,71)` is open north to about `y=57`.
- From `(7,57)`, going left reaches a west lane; taking that north works to about `(3,48)`.
- The visible top-west row around `y=48` is a repeated trap: from `(0..7,48)`, `up` is blocked.
- Avoid grinding the same top grass patch; it triggers wilds and reveals little.
- The apparent fence openings around `(x=3..11,y=62)` are fake from below; `up` is blocked there.
- Lower-left sign around `(5,66)` reads `ROUTE 2 / VIRIDIAN CITY - PEWTER CITY`.
- Real progress discovery: from the upper area, do **not** stop at `(3,48)`. Move right to about `(8,48)`, then north to `(8,46)`, then follow the building edge back left/up to the true gatehouse entrance row around `y=44`.
- Gatehouse south entrance exact tile: the actual warp is from about `(3,44) -> up` into `MAP_0x32`.
- Gatehouse north exit alignment: inside `MAP_0x32`, the correct north exit is along the top row near `x=5`; pressing `up` there exits to map `0x33` at about `(17,47)`.
- Practical transit rule: if you emerge onto Route 2 from Viridian at `(7,71)`, the reliable return path toward forest progress is north to `y≈57`, west to `x≈3`, north to `y≈48`, right to `x≈8`, up to `y≈46`, then across the frontage to the real entrance row and into the gatehouse.
- Forest-approach outdoor pocket (`0x33`) notes:
  - Start area near `(6,30)` is blocked above.
  - West reaches about `(2,30)` but only slightly downward there.
  - A lower route exists via the east/lower lanes, reaching at least `(14,43)` where an NPC says `They're out for POKEMON fights!`
  - South is blocked at `(14,43)`; stop hammering that tile.
  - Better progress came from the farther-right lane: from about `(15,39)`, moving right to `(17,39)` and then north reached at least `(17,34)`.
  - East side opens farther than first thought: reachable tiles include roughly `(19..23,40..43)` and farther east to around `(29,41)`.
  - North is blocked at least at `(22,40)`, `(23,40)`, and `(27,41)`.
  - The trainer at about `(26,33)` is a Bug Catcher who challenges immediately.
  - From around `(25,34)`, going north repeatedly reaches at least `(25,20)`; this is a real upper lane.
  - The upper pocket contains another Bug Catcher on the right side plus a sign near the upper center.
  - The entire top outside row around `y=1` from roughly `x=6..32` is a dead boundary; `up` does not enter the forest there.
  - Far-right boundary caps around `x=32`, with a downward lane on that side.
  - At about `(28,40)` facing left, the adjacent sprite/object is an interactable NPC; text includes `You should carry extras!`
  - From the mid-right/upper route, a clear lane reaches the upper clearing around `(25,8)`, but `up` is blocked there and the upper-left continuation to about `(17,8)` is also a dead corridor. A small south dip exists to `(17,10)`, but left from `(16,10)` is blocked. Do not keep treating the upper-left edge as the forest entrance.
  - A different upper-left route reaches another top-row clearing. Verified `(11,7) -> (11,3)` and top-row movement through about `x=6..13` at `y=1`, but `up` is still blocked across that row. The visible upper-left sign/tree cluster is misleading.
  - New synthesis: the unexplored value is now in descending from the upper-left/top-row clearings back into center-left lanes and checking for a hidden west/center opening, not in testing more `up` presses.
  - New center-left findings from turns 171-181:
    - From the upper-left top-row area, a valid descent runs roughly through `x=8` down to at least `y=21`.
    - At `(6,13)` and `(6,16)`, further `left` is blocked; at `(8,15)` and `(8,23)`, `right` is blocked.
    - Around `y=21`, left works to `(6,21)` but further left is blocked there.
    - Lower-left continuation exists through about `(6,23)` to `(3,23)`.
    - Around the trainer area, `(2,20) -> up` reaches `(2,19)`, but a second `up` is blocked; `(2,19) -> left` reaches `(1,19)` while `(2,19) -> right` is blocked.
    - The apparent object at `(2,19)` facing up is a Bug Catcher trainer, not scenery. After defeat, his text begins `I'm looking for the ...` and can be cleared with repeated `B`.
    - Important new connection: the center-left trainer corridor is not isolated. From the lower-left clearing around `(1..5,23)`, east/north routes reconnect back into the previously mapped upper-left lanes, including a climb through roughly `(7,23) -> (7,13) -> (6,11) -> (8,10) -> (6,10)`.
  - Turns 201-209 refinement near the antidote sign / mid pocket:
    - From gate north start `(17,47)`, a real approach exists through the mid-right grass: `(17,43)` then right to about `(20,43)`, and `up` works to `(20,41)`.
    - Another valid lane climbs from about `(17,35)` to `(17,32)`, but `up` from `(17,32)` is blocked.
    - The nearby sign is **not** read from `(15,32)` facing up; the successful interaction was from about `(15,32)` **facing right**, yielding `For poison, use ANTIDOTE! Get it at POKE...`.
    - Around the sign choke, `up` is blocked from `(14,32)`, `(15,32)`, `(16,32)`, `(17,32)`, and `(18,32)`.
    - `(15,32) -> down -> (15,33)` and `(14,32) -> down -> (14,33)` are valid; this is a local shelf/choke, not the entrance.
    - Far-left upper lane note: from about `(2,30)`, left reaches `(1,30)` but `down` there is blocked.
    - Search bias should move **east/southeast from the `(14..18,33..34)` zone** and test for a connection toward the already-known east-side lanes, rather than rechecking the blocked sign row.
  - Turn 216-219 breakthrough refinement:
    - From around `(18,34)`, the route `up, left, left, up, right, right` reaches the sign-row shelf around `(18,33)` / `(16,33)` but does not open north there.
    - A real continuation exists by dropping back through the lane to the left side: from the breach area, travel back toward about `(13,32)` / `(12,32)` and continue west.
    - From the left side near `(8,30)`, farther left/down/up movement opens a broader western clearing.
    - This western continuation reaches at least `(5,31)` and then about `(2,31)`; this is the newest promising unexplored area and should be probed next instead of the far-right dead end or the blocked sign row.

## Battle heuristics
- Early wild fights can sometimes be handled with repeated `A`, but blind mash is unsafe once move choice matters.
- Squirtle knows **Tackle**, **Tail Whip**, and **Bubble**. Visible order: **TACKLE / TAIL WHIP / BUBBLE**.
- If the move cursor is on **TAIL WHIP**, then `down + A` selects **BUBBLE**; plain `A` wastes a turn.
- In simple wild fights, deliberately choose a damaging move; `Tackle` is the safe manual fallback.
- With low HP, avoid unnecessary battles; fleeing is preferred.
- After a battle, visible text can linger after battle-state tools think it cleared; keep advancing until overworld is visibly back.
- If stuck in move or Pokémon submenu, `B` backs out cleanly to the main battle menu.
- Reliable flee pattern from messy wild-battle substates: use `B` until at the 4-option menu, then try `down, A` or `right, A`; if needed, `down, right, A` from FIGHT also works.
- After `Got away safely!`, one more `A` is usually needed, then a short `wait` for the white transition back to overworld.
- After `used TACKLE!` or Bubble text, a short `wait` often reveals the actual next state more cleanly than extra inputs.
- Route 2 trainer fights: Bubble animation/text can lag a long time; prefer `wait` first, then single `A` presses with longer settle instead of mashing.
- Kakuna often uses **Harden**; the main risk is wasting turns with Tail Whip or mashing through lag, not sudden KO.
- Strong recent lesson for early trainer fights with Squirtle: when text seems frozen, use a cadence of long waits (~60-120 frames) plus single `A` presses. This cleanly resolved Weedle/Kakuna text, poison ticks, level-up, and faint text better than mashing.
- Current nuisance to remember: the automated flee helpers have been unreliable in some wild-battle substates; manual `B` recovery to the main 4-option menu plus explicit RUN selection is safer.
- For opening wild battles in this area, the common stable pattern is: one or two `A` presses with waits to finish the intro/send-out sequence, confirm the 4-option menu is really up, then select RUN explicitly.
- Current live-state reminder for next action: if the battle screen still says `Go! A!`, do **not** send menu inputs yet; advance intro text first, then wait for the command menu before choosing RUN.

## Player's House 2F lesson
- Prior wedge hypothesis was wrong.
- Long-hold movement revealed a much larger room than short presses suggested; visible room edges are not always true movement bounds.

## Tooling / harness notes
- `save_checkpoint` may fail with a filesystem 500 error; do not rely on it until one succeeds.
- `probe_path` is excellent for discovering the effect of long holds because it logs coordinates after each step.
- `walk_path_until_event` is better than raw batched movement when following a known route through grass or near warps because it stops on battle/map change.
- Old `clear_battle_text` is broken (`ctx.frame is not a function`); use the safer state-based replacement instead.
- In-game SAVE is distinct from harness checkpoints and appears usable as a fallback.
- The first `route2_to_forest_approach` macro was buggy because it tried to over-batch the route; prefer short stepwise movement macros that stop on battle/map change.
- Current safe checkpoint trio: `turn188_route2_start`, `turn189_route2_3_48`, and `turn196_route2_start_fullhp`.
- Fresh useful forest-approach checkpoints now include `turn201_gate_north_start`, `turn212_kakuna_escaped_28_40`, `turn213_npc_cleared_28_40`, `turn216_start_18_35`, and `turn217_post_caterpie_12_32`.

arguments.content:
# Strategy playbook

## Early boot / controls
- If boot is blank white, `wait` a few seconds.
- At title/menu, `start` or `a` proceeds.
- On naming screens, `start` accepts defaults.
- A advances/confirms; B cancels; START opens menu.
- Default d-pad press moves about one tile.
- If a textbox is open, movement does nothing; clear it first.
- Trust RAM for coords/state and screenshot for UI/world geometry.
- `B` often closes overworld textboxes more cleanly than `A`.
- Blank white/black screens during transitions often just need `wait`.

## General exploration
- Probe systematically with RAM coords instead of retrying the same blocked tile.
- Adjacent sprites may be NPCs, not walls; try `A` before assuming collision.
- Long-hold d-pad presses can reveal more walkable area than short probes suggest.
- On signs/NPCs, clear lingering text before resuming movement.
- After trainer battles, overworld post-battle text can still be open even when `battle: none`; clear it before moving.

## Current progress
- Starter: Squirtle, now around Lv12.
- Oak's Parcel delivery and Pokédex progression are complete.
- Real progression is north from Viridian onto Route 2 / Viridian Forest, not Route 22.

## Pallet / Lab quick notes
- Player's House 1F exit: around `(2,7)` on map `0x25`, press `down`.
- Pallet north exit trigger after game start is on the east side of the north path around `x=10,y=1`.
- Oak's Lab is the southwest/left large building in Pallet.
- Right-side Pallet house can heal Squirtle.
- Verified starter: Squirtle.
- On nickname screen, `START` declines naming.
- Oak parcel return: talk to Oak around `(4,2)` facing right.

## Route 1
- Verified northbound route: from about `(5,18)`, go north to `y=14`, east along the rock barrier to about `x=14`, north through the opening, continue north, then loop west near the top fence and go north at about `(11,0)` to warp into Viridian.
- Viridian south return uses the fence opening around `x≈20,y≈30`.
- Upper grass pocket around `(10..13,6..8)` is dangerous and easy to overprobe.

## Viridian City / Pokecenter
- City sign near `(16,17)`: `VIRIDIAN CITY - The Eternally Green Paradise`.
- East-side building labeled `MART` is the Poké Mart.
- There is a fence/rock barrier around `y=28`; many central/east `up` attempts from below are blocked.
- Usable crossing: from around `(22,28)`, move left to about `x=19`, then north to about `(19,25)`, then right.
- Pokecenter frontage: from outside near `(24,26)`, use `left, up` to enter.
- Nurse Joy: stand at `(3,3)` facing up and press `A`; choose `HEAL`.
- After healing, `B` plus `down` closes the final text and steps away.
- Upper city: broad north fence line near `y≈14` is blocked; to reach upper city from the east half, work left to around `(20,16)` and then up.
- Small house/school entrance is around `(21,15)`/`(21,16)`; non-progressing.
- West exit to Route 22 is far left; from about `(0,14)`, pressing `left` warps out.
- Upper-left `TRAINER TIPS` sign around `(19,2)` is just a sign, not progression.
- Exact north exit to Route 2: from the upper-left corridor, go to about `(17,0)` and press `up` to warp to Route 2 at about `(7,71)`.

## Route 22
- Everything verified so far is optional/non-progressing.
- Lower road is broad but dead-ends; far-east upper grass also dead-ends.
- Avoid exploratory grass there when HP is low.

## Route 2 south section / gatehouse
- From Viridian landing `(7,71)`, central path is open north to about `y=57`.
- From `(7,57)`, go west to about `x=3`, then north to about `y=48`.
- The visible top-west row around `y=48` is a trap: from `(0..7,48)`, `up` is blocked.
- Do **not** stop at `(3,48)`. Move right to about `(8,48)`, then north to `(8,46)`, then follow the building frontage back to the true entrance row.
- Exact south gatehouse entrance: from about `(3,44)`, press `up` into `MAP_0x32`.
- Gatehouse north exit: inside `MAP_0x32`, the correct north exit is along the top row near `x=5`; pressing `up` exits to map `0x33` around `(17,47)`.

## Route 2 north-side outdoor pocket (`0x33`)
- Start area near `(6,30)` is blocked above.
- West reaches about `(2,30)` but only slightly downward there.
- A lower route exists via east/lower lanes, reaching at least `(14,43)` where an NPC says `They're out for POKEMON fights!`
- South is blocked at `(14,43)`; stop hammering that tile.
- Farther-right lane: from about `(15,39)`, right to `(17,39)` and north reaches at least `(17,34)`.
- East side opens through roughly `(19..23,40..43)` and farther east to around `(29,41)`.
- North is blocked at least at `(22,40)`, `(23,40)`, and `(27,41)`.
- Trainer at about `(26,33)` is a Bug Catcher who challenges immediately.
- From around `(25,34)`, going north repeatedly reaches at least `(25,20)`; this is a real upper lane.
- The entire top outside row around `y=1` from roughly `x=6..32` is a dead boundary; `up` does not enter the forest there.
- Far-right boundary caps around `x=32`; that outer lane is a dead end for entrance search.
- At about `(28,40)` facing left, the adjacent sprite is an NPC; text includes `You should carry extras!`
- Upper-left lesson: multiple top-row clearings near the visible tree/sign cluster are misleading; `up` is still blocked there.
- Center-left connection discovered earlier: lower-left clearing around `(1..5,23)` reconnects back into upper-left lanes; the left-side trainer at about `(2,19)` is a Bug Catcher, not scenery.
- Antidote sign / mid-pocket refinement:
  - From gate north start `(17,47)`, a real approach exists via `(17,43)` then right to about `(20,43)`, and `up` works to `(20,41)`.
  - Another lane climbs from about `(17,35)` to `(17,32)`, but `up` from `(17,32)` is blocked.
  - The sign is **not** read from `(15,32)` facing up; the successful interaction is from about `(15,32)` **facing right**, yielding `For poison, use ANTIDOTE! ...`.
  - `up` is blocked from `(14,32)` through `(18,32)`; this is a local shelf/choke, not the entrance.
- Turn 216-219 refinement:
  - From around `(18,34)`, the sign-row breach area around `(16..18,33)` is real but still does **not** open north.
  - A better continuation exists by moving back left through about `(13,32)` / `(12,32)` and continuing west.
  - From the left side near `(8,30)`, farther left/down/up movement opens a broader western clearing.
  - This western continuation reaches at least `(5,31)` and then about `(2,31)`; this is the newest promising unexplored area. Probe it next instead of the far-right dead end or the blocked sign row.

## Battle heuristics
- Early wild fights can sometimes be handled with repeated `A`, but blind mash is unsafe once move choice matters.
- Squirtle knows **Tackle**, **Tail Whip**, **Bubble**. Visible order: **TACKLE / TAIL WHIP / BUBBLE**.
- If the move cursor is on **TAIL WHIP**, then `down + A` selects **BUBBLE**; plain `A` wastes a turn.
- With low HP, avoid unnecessary battles; fleeing is preferred.
- If stuck in move or Pokémon submenu, `B` backs out cleanly to the main battle menu.
- Reliable flee recovery from messy substates: use `B` until at the 4-option menu, then try `down, A` or `right, A`; if needed, `down, right, A` from FIGHT also works.
- After `Got away safely!`, one more `A` is usually needed, then a short `wait` for the white transition.
- After `used TACKLE!` or Bubble text, a short `wait` often reveals the true next state more cleanly than extra inputs.
- Route 2 trainer fights: Bubble animation/text can lag a long time; prefer long waits (~60-120 frames) plus single `A` presses instead of mashing.
- Kakuna often uses **Harden**; the main risk is wasting turns or mashing through lag, not sudden KO.
- For opening wild battles in this area, the stable pattern is: one or two `A` presses with waits to finish the intro/send-out sequence, confirm the 4-option menu is really up, then select RUN explicitly.
- If the battle screen still says `Go! A!`, do **not** send menu inputs yet; advance intro text first, then wait for the command menu before choosing RUN.

## Tooling / harness notes
- `probe_path` is excellent for discovering stepwise movement effects.
- `walk_path_until_event` is better than raw batched movement when following known routes through grass or near warps because it stops on battle/map change.
- Old `clear_battle_text` is broken (`ctx.frame is not a function`); use the safer state-based replacement instead.
- The first `route2_to_forest_approach` macro was buggy because it over-batched movement; prefer short stepwise movement macros that stop on battle/map change.
- Useful checkpoints: `turn201_gate_north_start`, `turn212_kakuna_escaped_28_40`, `turn213_npc_cleared_28_40`, `turn216_start_18_35`, and `turn217_post_caterpie_12_32`.

Inside, going straight up to about `(5,1)` and then pressing `up` exits north into `Route 2 Gate (Viridian Forest south)` (map 0x33 / displayed name `Route 2 Gate (Viridian Forest south)`) at about `(17,47)`. This confirms the Route 2 east-side/gatehouse progression path was real and the earlier upper-grass trap probing was bypassable by reaching the building frontage near `(7..8,44)`.

data.name: update_playbook

arguments.content:
# Strategy playbook

## Early boot / controls
- If boot is blank white, `wait` a few seconds.
- At title/menu, `start` or `a` proceeds.
- On naming screens, `start` accepts defaults.
- A advances/confirms; B cancels; START opens menu.
- Default d-pad press moves about one tile.
- If a textbox is open, movement does nothing; clear it first.
- Trust RAM for coords/state and screenshot for UI/world geometry.
- `B` often closes overworld textboxes more cleanly than `A`.
- Blank white/black screens during transitions often just need `wait`.

## General exploration
- Probe systematically with RAM coords instead of retrying the same blocked tile.
- Adjacent sprites may be NPCs, not walls; try `A` before assuming collision.
- Long-hold d-pad presses can reveal more walkable area than short probes suggest.
- Dialog boxes block movement until dismissed with A.
- On signs/NPCs, clear lingering text before resuming movement.
- After trainer battles, overworld post-battle text can still be open even when `battle: none`; clear it before moving.
- If a map search stalls for many turns, consider whether the entire premise is wrong (wrong side of a gate, wrong building exit, optional side pocket).

## Current progress
- Starter: Squirtle, now around Lv12.
- Oak's Parcel delivery and Pokédex progression are complete.
- Real progression is north from Viridian onto Route 2 / Viridian Forest, not Route 22.
- Major correction: the Route 2 map `0x33` outdoor pocket reached by exiting the gatehouse north is **not** the Viridian Forest entrance. Stop searching that outdoor pocket for the forest warp.

## Pallet / Lab quick notes
- Player's House 1F exit: around `(2,7)` on map `0x25`, press `down`.
- Pallet north exit trigger after game start is on the east side of the north path around `x=10,y=1`.
- Oak's Lab is the southwest/left large building in Pallet.
- Right-side Pallet house can heal Squirtle.
- Verified starter: Squirtle.
- On nickname screen, `START` declines naming.
- Oak parcel return: talk to Oak around `(4,2)` facing right.

## Route 1
- Verified northbound route: from about `(5,18)`, go north to `y=14`, east along the rock barrier to about `x=14`, north through the opening, continue north, then loop west near the top fence and go north at about `(11,0)` to warp into Viridian.
- Viridian south return uses the fence opening around `x≈20,y≈30`.
- Upper grass pocket around `(10..13,6..8)` is dangerous and easy to overprobe.

## Viridian City / Pokecenter
- City sign near `(16,17)`: `VIRIDIAN CITY - The Eternally Green Paradise`.
- East-side building labeled `MART` is the Poké Mart.
- There is a fence/rock barrier around `y=28`; many central/east `up` attempts from below are blocked.
- Usable crossing: from around `(22,28)`, move left to about `x=19`, then north to about `(19,25)`, then right.
- Pokecenter frontage: from outside near `(24,26)`, use `left, up` to enter.
- Nurse Joy: stand at `(3,3)` facing up and press `A`; choose `HEAL`.
- After healing, `B` plus `down` closes the final text and steps away.
- Upper city: broad north fence line near `y≈14` is blocked; to reach upper city from the east half, work left to around `(20,16)` and then up.
- Small house/school entrance is around `(21,15)`/`(21,16)`; non-progressing.
- West exit to Route 22 is far left; from about `(0,14)`, pressing `left` warps out.
- Upper-left `TRAINER TIPS` sign around `(19,2)` is just a sign, not progression.
- Exact north exit to Route 2: from the upper-left corridor, go to about `(17,0)` and press `up` to warp to Route 2 at about `(7,71)`.

## Route 2 south section / gatehouse
- From Viridian landing `(7,71)`, central path is open north to about `y=57`.
- From `(7,57)`, go west to about `x=3`, then north to about `y=48`.
- The visible top-west row around `y=48` is a trap: from `(0..7,48)`, `up` is blocked.
- Do **not** stop at `(3,48)`. Move right to about `(8,48)`, then north to `(8,46)`, then follow the building frontage back to the true entrance row.
- Exact south gatehouse entrance: from about `(3,44)`, press `up` into `MAP_0x32`.
- Important correction: inside `MAP_0x32`, going straight up to the top-row exit leads to map `0x33`, an outdoor north-side Route 2 pocket that is **optional for now** and not the forest entrance search target.
- Next progression hypothesis: inspect the gatehouse interior for a side/center exit into Viridian Forest instead of automatically taking the north outdoor exit.

## Route 2 north-side outdoor pocket (`0x33`)
- This area has consumed many turns and should now be treated as a mapped optional side pocket, not the main path.
- Start area near `(6,30)` is blocked above.
- West reaches about `(2,30)` but only slightly upward; the west clearing sign area is a dead end.
- A lower route exists via east/lower lanes, reaching at least `(14,43)` where an NPC says `They're out for POKEMON fights!`
- Farther-right lane opens through roughly `(19..23,40..43)` and farther east to around `(29,41)`, but the far-right boundary dead-ends.
- Sign-row / antidote area around `(14..18,32)` is a local choke: `up` blocked across that row; read the antidote sign from about `(15,32)` facing right.
- Trainer at about `(26,33)` is a Bug Catcher who challenges immediately.
- The entire top outside row around `y=1` from roughly `x=6..32` is a dead boundary; `up` does not enter the forest there.
- Conclusion: stop spending turns here looking for the forest entrance.

## Battle heuristics
- Early wild fights can sometimes be handled with repeated `A`, but blind mash is unsafe once move choice matters.
- Squirtle knows **Tackle**, **Tail Whip**, **Bubble**. Visible order: **TACKLE / TAIL WHIP / BUBBLE**.
- If the move cursor is on **TAIL WHIP**, then `down + A` selects **BUBBLE**; plain `A` wastes a turn.
- With low HP, avoid unnecessary battles; fleeing is preferred.
- If stuck in move or Pokémon submenu, `B` backs out cleanly to the main battle menu.
- Reliable flee recovery from messy substates: use `B` until at the 4-option menu, then try `down, A` or `right, A`; if needed, `down, right, A` from FIGHT also works.
- After `Got away safely!`, one more `A` is usually needed, then a short `wait` for the white transition.
- After `used TACKLE!` or Bubble text, a short `wait` often reveals the true next state more cleanly than extra inputs.
- Route 2 trainer fights: Bubble animation/text can lag a long time; prefer long waits (~60-120 frames) plus single `A` presses instead of mashing.
- Kakuna often uses **Harden**; the main risk is wasting turns or mashing through lag, not sudden KO.
- For opening wild battles in this area, the stable pattern is: one or two `A` presses with waits to finish the intro/send-out sequence, confirm the 4-option menu is really up, then select RUN explicitly.
- If the battle screen still says `Go! A!`, do **not** send menu inputs yet; advance intro text first, then wait for the command menu before choosing RUN.

## Tooling / harness notes
- `probe_path` is excellent for discovering stepwise movement effects.
- `walk_path_until_event` is better than raw batched movement when following known routes through grass or near warps because it stops on battle/map change.
- Old `clear_battle_text` is broken (`ctx.frame is not a function`); use the safer state-based replacement instead.
- The first `route2_to_forest_approach` macro was buggy because it over-batched movement; prefer short stepwise movement macros that stop on battle/map change.
- Useful checkpoints: `turn201_gate_north_start`, `turn212_kakuna_escaped_28_40`, `turn213_npc_cleared_28_40`, `turn216_start_18_35`, `turn217_post_caterpie_12_32`, `turn221_west_clear_2_31`, and `turn224_post_pikachu_escape_18_32`.

data.name: update_playbook

arguments.content:
# Strategy playbook

## Early boot / controls
- If boot is blank white, `wait` a few seconds.
- At title/menu, `start` or `a` proceeds.
- On naming screens, `start` accepts defaults.
- A advances/confirms; B cancels; START opens menu.
- Default d-pad press moves about one tile.
- If a textbox is open, movement does nothing; clear it first.
- Trust RAM for coords/state and screenshot for UI/world geometry.
- `B` often closes overworld textboxes more cleanly than `A`.
- Blank white/black screens during transitions often just need `wait`.

## General exploration
- Probe systematically with RAM coords instead of retrying the same blocked tile.
- Adjacent sprites may be NPCs, not walls; try `A` before assuming collision.
- Long-hold d-pad presses can reveal more walkable area than short probes suggest.
- Dialog boxes block movement until dismissed with A.
- On signs/NPCs, clear lingering text before resuming movement.
- After trainer battles, overworld post-battle text can still be open even when `battle: none`; clear it before moving.
- If a map search stalls for many turns, consider whether the entire premise is wrong (wrong side of a gate, wrong building exit, optional side pocket).
- Use `probe_path` or `walk_path_until_event` to log exact coords through suspect doors/warps; this is better than inferring from screenshots.

## Current progress
- Starter: Squirtle, now around Lv12.
- Oak's Parcel delivery and Pokédex progression are complete.
- Real progression is north from Viridian onto Route 2 / Viridian Forest, not Route 22.
- Major correction: the Route 2 map `0x33` outdoor pocket reached by exiting the gatehouse north is **not** the Viridian Forest entrance. Stop searching that outdoor pocket for the forest warp.
- New hard confirmation: inside `MAP_0x32`, the top doorway at about `(5,1) -> up` simply loops to north-side Route 2 (`map 0x33`, settling near `(17,47)`). Pressing `down` from that outdoor south edge returns into the same gatehouse. This is just the south<->north Route 2 connector.

## Pallet / Lab quick notes
- Player's House 1F exit: around `(2,7)` on map `0x25`, press `down`.
- Pallet north exit trigger after game start is on the east side of the north path around `x=10,y=1`.
- Oak's Lab is the southwest/left large building in Pallet.
- Right-side Pallet house can heal Squirtle.
- Verified starter: Squirtle.
- On nickname screen, `START` declines naming.
- Oak parcel return: talk to Oak around `(4,2)` facing right.

## Route 1
- Verified northbound route: from about `(5,18)`, go north to `y=14`, east along the rock barrier to about `x=14`, north through the opening, continue north, then loop west near the top fence and go north at about `(11,0)` to warp into Viridian.
- Viridian south return uses the fence opening around `x≈20,y≈30`.
- Upper grass pocket around `(10..13,6..8)` is dangerous and easy to overprobe.

## Viridian City / Pokecenter
- City sign near `(16,17)`: `VIRIDIAN CITY - The Eternally Green Paradise`.
- East-side building labeled `MART` is the Poké Mart.
- There is a fence/rock barrier around `y=28`; many central/east `up` attempts from below are blocked.
- Usable crossing: from around `(22,28)`, move left to about `x=19`, then north to about `(19,25)`, then right.
- Pokecenter frontage: from outside near `(24,26)`, use `left, up` to enter.
- Nurse Joy: stand at `(3,3)` facing up and press `A`; choose `HEAL`.
- After healing, `B` plus `down` closes the final text and steps away.
- Upper city: broad north fence line near `y≈14` is blocked; to reach upper city from the east half, work left to around `(20,16)` and then up.
- Small house/school entrance is around `(21,15)`/`(21,16)`; non-progressing.
- West exit to Route 22 is far left; from about `(0,14)`, pressing `left` warps out.
- Upper-left `TRAINER TIPS` sign around `(19,2)` is just a sign, not progression.
- Exact north exit to Route 2: from the upper-left corridor, go to about `(17,0)` and press `up` to warp to Route 2 at about `(7,71)`.

## Route 2 south section / gatehouse
- From Viridian landing `(7,71)`, central path is open north to about `y=57`.
- From `(7,57)`, go west to about `x=3`, then north to about `y=48`.
- The visible top-west row around `y=48` is a trap: from `(0..7,48)`, `up` is blocked.
- Do **not** stop at `(3,48)`. Move right to about `(8,48)`, then north to `(8,46)`, then follow the building frontage back to the true entrance row.
- Exact south gatehouse entrance: from about `(3,44)`, press `up` into `MAP_0x32`.
- Important correction: inside `MAP_0x32`, going straight up to the top-row exit leads to map `0x33`, an outdoor north-side Route 2 pocket that is **optional for now** and not the forest entrance search target.
- The gatehouse interior top exit is now fully verified as a loop to the north pocket, not a hidden forest connector.
- Remaining hypothesis: the actual Viridian Forest entrance is elsewhere on Route 2 / north pocket, or requires a different overworld doorway than the repeatedly tested gatehouse top exit.

## Route 2 north-side outdoor pocket (`0x33`)
- This area has consumed many turns and should now be treated as a mapped optional side pocket, not the main path.
- Start area near `(17,47)`; the old route remains reproducible: north to `(17,43)`, right to `(20,43)`, up to `(20,41)`, then north lane via `(18,40..33)`.
- Start area near `(6,30)` is blocked above in some earlier landing contexts; use RAM/state, not screenshots alone.
- West reaches about `(2,30)` but only slightly upward; the west clearing sign area is a dead end.
- A lower route exists via east/lower lanes, reaching at least `(14,43)` where an NPC says `They're out for POKEMON fights!`
- Farther-right lane opens through roughly `(19..23,40..43)` and farther east to around `(29,41)`, but the far-right boundary dead-ends.
- Sign-row / antidote area around `(14..18,32)` is a local choke: `up` blocked across that row; read the antidote sign from about `(15,32)` facing right.
- Trainer at about `(26,33)` is a Bug Catcher who challenges immediately.
- The entire top outside row around `y=1` from roughly `x=6..32` is a dead boundary; `up` does not enter the forest there.
- Conclusion: stop spending turns here looking for the forest entrance.

## Battle heuristics
- Early wild fights can sometimes be handled with repeated `A`, but blind mash is unsafe once move choice matters.
- Squirtle knows **Tackle**, **Tail Whip**, **Bubble**. Visible order: **TACKLE / TAIL WHIP / BUBBLE**.
- If the move cursor is on **TAIL WHIP**, then `down + A` selects **BUBBLE**; plain `A` wastes a turn.
- With low HP, avoid unnecessary battles; fleeing is preferred.
- If stuck in move or Pokémon submenu, `B` backs out cleanly to the main battle menu.
- Reliable flee recovery from messy substates: use `B` until at the 4-option menu, then try `down, A` or `right, A`; if needed, `down, right, A` from FIGHT also works.
- After `Got away safely!`, one more `A` is usually needed, then a short `wait` for the white transition.
- After `used TACKLE!` or Bubble text, a short `wait` often reveals the true next state more cleanly than extra inputs.
- Route 2 trainer fights: Bubble animation/text can lag a long time; prefer long waits (~60-120 frames) plus single `A` presses instead of mashing.
- Kakuna often uses **Harden**; the main risk is wasting turns or mashing through lag, not sudden KO.
- For opening wild battles in this area, the stable pattern is: one or two `A` presses with waits to finish the intro/send-out sequence, confirm the 4-option menu is really up, then select RUN explicitly.
- If the battle screen still says `Go! A!`, do **not** send menu inputs yet; advance intro text first, then wait for the command menu before choosing RUN.

## Tooling / harness notes
- `probe_path` is excellent for discovering stepwise movement effects.
- `walk_path_until_event` is better than raw batched movement when following known routes through grass or near warps because it stops on battle/map change.
- Old `clear_battle_text` is broken (`ctx.frame is not a function`); use the safer state-based replacement instead.
- The first `route2_to_forest_approach` macro was buggy because it over-batched movement; prefer short stepwise movement macros that stop on battle/map change.
- Useful checkpoints: `turn201_gate_north_start`, `turn212_kakuna_escaped_28_40`, `turn213_npc_cleared_28_40`, `turn216_start_18_35`, `turn217_post_caterpie_12_32`, `turn221_west_clear_2_31`, `turn224_post_pikachu_escape_18_32`, `turn228_gatehouse_interior_5_3`, and `turn228_north_pocket_start_17_47`.

Find the real Viridian Forest entrance path from Route 2 using a genuinely different search premise than the gatehouse-top-door loop
If continuing in the north-side pocket, probe for an untested side doorway/warp or NPC clue instead of rewalking the same sign-row and west-clearing dead ends
Keep a fresh checkpoint before risky Route 2/forest probing
Use stable wild-battle escape cadence: clear intro with A+waits, then RUN explicitly

data.name: update_playbook

arguments.content:
# Strategy playbook

## Early boot / controls
- If boot is blank white, `wait` a few seconds.
- At title/menu, `start` or `a` proceeds.
- On naming screens, `start` accepts defaults.
- A advances/confirms; B cancels; START opens menu.
- Default d-pad press moves about one tile.
- If a textbox is open, movement does nothing; clear it first.
- Trust RAM for coords/state and screenshot for UI/world geometry.
- `B` often closes overworld textboxes more cleanly than `A`.
- Blank white/black screens during transitions often just need `wait`.

## General exploration
- Probe systematically with RAM coords instead of retrying the same blocked tile.
- Adjacent sprites may be NPCs, not walls; try `A` before assuming collision.
- Long-hold d-pad presses can reveal more walkable area than short probes suggest.
- Dialog boxes block movement until dismissed with A.
- On signs/NPCs, clear lingering text before resuming movement.
- After trainer battles, overworld post-battle text can still be open even when `battle: none`; clear it before moving.
- If a map search stalls for many turns, consider whether the entire premise is wrong (wrong side of a gate, wrong building exit, optional side pocket).
- Use `probe_path` or `walk_path_until_event` to log exact coords through suspect doors/warps; this is better than inferring from screenshots.
- When one branch is repeatedly proving to be a dead end, prefer rewinding to a clean checkpoint near the branch point rather than continuing from a deep exploratory leaf.

## Current progress
- Starter: Squirtle, now around Lv12.
- Oak's Parcel delivery and Pokédex progression are complete.
- Real progression is north from Viridian onto Route 2 / Viridian Forest, not Route 22.
- Major correction: the Route 2 map `0x33` outdoor pocket reached by exiting the gatehouse north is **not** the Viridian Forest entrance.
- Hard confirmation: inside `MAP_0x32`, the top doorway at about `(5,1) -> up` loops to north-side Route 2 (`map 0x33`, settling near `(17,47)`), and pressing `down` there returns into the same gatehouse.
- Strong working hypothesis now: `map 0x33` is the **north-of-forest / optional side pocket** (likely a later or post-forest area), so stop trying to force main progression from there.
- Therefore, next real search should focus back on **south Route 2 outdoor map `0x0d`** for a genuinely different doorway/entrance into Viridian Forest, not on the gatehouse top exit or the `0x33` outdoor pocket.

## Pallet / Lab quick notes
- Player's House 1F exit: around `(2,7)` on map `0x25`, press `down`.
- Pallet north exit trigger after game start is on the east side of the north path around `x=10,y=1`.
- Oak's Lab is the southwest/left large building in Pallet.
- Right-side Pallet house can heal Squirtle.
- Verified starter: Squirtle.
- On nickname screen, `START` declines naming.
- Oak parcel return: talk to Oak around `(4,2)` facing right.

## Route 1
- Verified northbound route: from about `(5,18)`, go north to `y=14`, east along the rock barrier to about `x=14`, north through the opening, continue north, then loop west near the top fence and go north at about `(11,0)` to warp into Viridian.
- Viridian south return uses the fence opening around `x≈20,y≈30`.
- Upper grass pocket around `(10..13,6..8)` is dangerous and easy to overprobe.

## Viridian City / Pokecenter
- City sign near `(16,17)`: `VIRIDIAN CITY - The Eternally Green Paradise`.
- East-side building labeled `MART` is the Poké Mart.
- There is a fence/rock barrier around `y=28`; many central/east `up` attempts from below are blocked.
- Usable crossing: from around `(22,28)`, move left to about `x=19`, then north to about `(19,25)`, then right.
- Pokecenter frontage: from outside near `(24,26)`, use `left, up` to enter.
- Nurse Joy: stand at `(3,3)` facing up and press `A`; choose `HEAL`.
- After healing, `B` plus `down` closes the final text and steps away.
- Upper city: broad north fence line near `y≈14` is blocked; to reach upper city from the east half, work left to around `(20,16)` and then up.
- Small house/school entrance is around `(21,15)`/`(21,16)`; non-progressing.
- West exit to Route 22 is far left; from about `(0,14)`, pressing `left` warps out.
- Upper-left `TRAINER TIPS` sign around `(19,2)` is just a sign, not progression.
- Exact north exit to Route 2: from the upper-left corridor, go to about `(17,0)` and press `up` to warp to Route 2 at about `(7,71)`.

## Route 2 south section / gatehouse
- From Viridian landing `(7,71)`, central path is open north to about `y=57`.
- From `(7,57)`, go west to about `x=3`, then north to about `y=48`.
- The visible top-west row around `y=48` is a trap: from `(0..7,48)`, `up` is blocked.
- Do **not** stop at `(3,48)`. Move right to about `(8,48)`, then north to `(8,46)`, then follow the building frontage back to the true entrance row.
- Exact south gatehouse entrance: from about `(3,44)`, press `up` into `MAP_0x32`.
- Important correction: inside `MAP_0x32`, going straight up to the top-row exit leads only to map `0x33`, an outdoor north-side Route 2 pocket that is optional for now and not the forest entrance search target.
- The gatehouse interior top exit is fully verified as only the south<->north Route 2 connector.
- Next south-Route-2 work should assume the real Viridian Forest entrance is a **different overworld doorway/warp on map `0x0d`**, not anything inside this connector gatehouse.

## Route 2 north-side outdoor pocket (`0x33`)
- Treat this as a mapped optional side pocket, not the main path.
- Start area near `(17,47)`; reproducible route: north to `(17,43)`, right to `(20,43)`, up to `(20,41)`, then north lane via `(18,40..33)`.
- West reaches about `(2,30..31)`; the west clearing sign area is a dead end.
- A lower route exists via east/lower lanes, reaching at least `(14,43)` where an NPC says `They're out for POKEMON fights!`
- Farther-right lane opens through roughly `(19..23,40..43)` and farther east to around `(29,41)`, but the far-right boundary dead-ends.
- Sign-row / antidote area around `(14..18,32)` is a local choke: `up` blocked across that row; read the antidote sign from about `(15,32)` facing right.
- Trainer at about `(26,33)` is a Bug Catcher who challenges immediately.
- The entire top outside row around `y=1` from roughly `x=6..32` is a dead boundary; `up` does not enter the forest there.
- Latest extra confirmation from turn 229: from around `(18,33)`, the right-side tree corridor loops down to about `(18,36)` and continuing south to about `(18,39)` also dead-ends; this is not a hidden entrance lane.
- Conclusion: stop spending turns here looking for the forest entrance.

## Battle heuristics
- Early wild fights can sometimes be handled with repeated `A`, but blind mash is unsafe once move choice matters.
- Squirtle knows **Tackle**, **Tail Whip**, **Bubble**. Visible order: **TACKLE / TAIL WHIP / BUBBLE**.
- If the move cursor is on **TAIL WHIP**, then `down + A` selects **BUBBLE**; plain `A` wastes a turn.
- With low HP, avoid unnecessary battles; fleeing is preferred.
- If stuck in move or Pokémon submenu, `B` backs out cleanly to the main battle menu.
- Reliable flee recovery from messy substates: use `B` until at the 4-option menu, then try `down, A` or `right, A`; if needed, `down, right, A` from FIGHT also works.
- After `Got away safely!`, one more `A` is usually needed, then a short `wait` for the white transition.
- After `used TACKLE!` or Bubble text, a short `wait` often reveals the true next state more cleanly than extra inputs.
- Route 2 trainer fights: Bubble animation/text can lag a long time; prefer long waits (~60-120 frames) plus single `A` presses instead of mashing.
- Kakuna often uses **Harden**; the main risk is wasting turns or mashing through lag, not sudden KO.
- For opening wild battles in this area, the stable pattern is: one or two `A` presses with waits to finish the intro/send-out sequence, confirm the 4-option menu is really up, then select RUN explicitly.
- If the battle screen still says `Go! A!`, do **not** send menu inputs yet; advance intro text first, then wait for the command menu before choosing RUN.

## Tooling / harness notes
- `probe_path` is excellent for discovering stepwise movement effects.
- `walk_path_until_event` is better than raw batched movement when following known routes through grass or near warps because it stops on battle/map change.
- Old `clear_battle_text` is broken (`ctx.frame is not a function`); use the safer state-based replacement instead.
- The first `route2_to_forest_approach` macro was buggy because it over-batched movement; prefer short stepwise movement macros that stop on battle/map change.
- Useful checkpoints: `turn201_gate_north_start`, `turn212_kakuna_escaped_28_40`, `turn213_npc_cleared_28_40`, `turn216_start_18_35`, `turn217_post_caterpie_12_32`, `turn221_west_clear_2_31`, `turn228_gatehouse_interior_5_3`, and `turn228_north_pocket_start_17_47`.

This confirms the apparent southward continuation in that local corridor is just another dead-end branch, not a hidden entrance toward Viridian Forest.

arguments.content: Route 2 south map 0x0d row-62 opening probe (turn 234): from sign area `(5,64)`, moving right along the lower path reaches at least `(10,64)`. Going up there reaches `(10,63)` then `(10,62)`, but further `up` is blocked. From `(9,62)` and `(11,62)`, `up` is also blocked. From `(11,62)`, repeated `right` is blocked, so the apparent right-side side opening by the tree column is not enterable from this lower lane. Conclusion so far: the visible left crossing lets you stand on row `y=62`, but the entire tested span `(8..11,62)` is still blocked north, and the tree-column approach on the right is also blocked from below.

arguments.name: route2_map33_is_viridian_forest_correction_turn235
arguments.content: Major correction (turn 235): the outdoor map previously mislabeled as a Route 2 north-side pocket (`map 0x33`) is almost certainly the actual **Viridian Forest interior/south approach**, not an optional dead-end Route 2 area. Evidence: entering from the south gatehouse top door places the player in a large wooded maze with wild forest species, Bug Catcher trainers, and the sign text `LEAVING VIRIDIAN FOREST / PEWTER CITY AHEAD` plus an Antidote trainer-tips sign area — all matching Viridian Forest. The repeated failure was not finding the entrance; it was misclassifying the forest itself and then searching for a different entrance. New plan: use the verified south Route 2 breach to the gatehouse, then treat `map 0x33` as the forest to be traversed northward toward Pewter.

data.name: update_playbook

arguments.content:
# Strategy playbook

## Early boot / controls
- If boot is blank white, `wait` a few seconds.
- At title/menu, `start` or `a` proceeds.
- On naming screens, `start` accepts defaults.
- A advances/confirms; B cancels; START opens menu.
- Default d-pad press moves about one tile.
- If a textbox is open, movement does nothing; clear it first.
- Trust RAM for coords/state and screenshot for UI/world geometry.
- `B` often closes overworld textboxes more cleanly than `A`.
- Blank white/black screens during transitions often just need `wait`.

## General exploration
- Probe systematically with RAM coords instead of retrying the same blocked tile.
- Adjacent sprites may be NPCs, not walls; try `A` before assuming collision.
- Long-hold d-pad presses can reveal more walkable area than short probes suggest.
- Dialog boxes block movement until dismissed with A.
- On signs/NPCs, clear lingering text before resuming movement.
- After trainer battles, overworld post-battle text can still be open even when `battle: none`; clear it before moving.
- If a map search stalls for many turns, consider whether the entire premise is wrong (wrong side of a gate, wrong building exit, optional side pocket).
- Use `probe_path` or `walk_path_until_event` to log exact coords through suspect doors/warps; this is better than inferring from screenshots.
- When one branch is repeatedly proving to be a dead end, prefer rewinding to a clean checkpoint near the branch point rather than continuing from a deep exploratory leaf.
- Big meta-lesson from Route 2: if a map contains the right ecology/trainers/signs for the target area, stop insisting it must be somewhere else. Re-evaluate the label, not just the path.

## Current progress
- Starter: Squirtle, now around Lv12.
- Oak's Parcel delivery and Pokédex progression are complete.
- Real progression is north from Viridian onto Route 2 and then through Viridian Forest.
- **Major correction (turn 235): map `0x33` is almost certainly the actual Viridian Forest interior/south approach, not an optional north-side Route 2 pocket.** The prior stall came from misclassifying the forest itself.
- Therefore the goal is no longer to hunt for a different south Route 2 entrance. The verified route to the gatehouse/top door is already the correct progression; next task is to traverse `0x33` northward toward Pewter.

## Pallet / Lab quick notes
- Player's House 1F exit: around `(2,7)` on map `0x25`, press `down`.
- Pallet north exit trigger after game start is on the east side of the north path around `x=10,y=1`.
- Oak's Lab is the southwest/left large building in Pallet.
- Right-side Pallet house can heal Squirtle.
- Verified starter: Squirtle.
- On nickname screen, `START` declines naming.
- Oak parcel return: talk to Oak around `(4,2)` facing right.

## Route 1
- Verified northbound route: from about `(5,18)`, go north to `y=14`, east along the rock barrier to about `x=14`, north through the opening, continue north, then loop west near the top fence and go north at about `(11,0)` to warp into Viridian.
- Viridian south return uses the fence opening around `x≈20,y≈30`.
- Upper grass pocket around `(10..13,6..8)` is dangerous and easy to overprobe.

## Viridian City / Pokecenter
- City sign near `(16,17)`: `VIRIDIAN CITY - The Eternally Green Paradise`.
- East-side building labeled `MART` is the Poké Mart.
- There is a fence/rock barrier around `y=28`; many central/east `up` attempts from below are blocked.
- Usable crossing: from around `(22,28)`, move left to about `x=19`, then north to about `(19,25)`, then right.
- Pokecenter frontage: from outside near `(24,26)`, use `left, up` to enter.
- Nurse Joy: stand at `(3,3)` facing up and press `A`; choose `HEAL`.
- After healing, `B` plus `down` closes the final text and steps away.
- Upper city: broad north fence line near `y≈14` is blocked; to reach upper city from the east half, work left to around `(20,16)` and then up.
- Small house/school entrance is around `(21,15)`/`(21,16)`; non-progressing.
- West exit to Route 22 is far left; from about `(0,14)`, pressing `left` warps out.
- Upper-left `TRAINER TIPS` sign around `(19,2)` is just a sign, not progression.
- Exact north exit to Route 2: from the upper-left corridor, go to about `(17,0)` and press `up` to warp to Route 2 at about `(7,71)`.

## Route 2 south section / gatehouse
- From Viridian landing `(7,71)`, the important lower-left breach is real.
- Lower-left sign area: sign around `(5,66)` says `ROUTE 2 / VIRIDIAN CITY - PEWTER CITY`.
- Key breakthrough: from sign area `(5,64)`, go left to about `(2,64)`, then work north/right to expose the real path upward; this leads back to the upper Route 2 area and then the gatehouse frontage.
- Verified climb from the breach can reach upper area around `(3,48)`, then east to `(8,48)`, north to `(8,46)`, left along the building frontage, then up to the south gatehouse entrance at about `(3,44)`.
- From gatehouse interior `MAP_0x32`, the upper-left aisle leads to `(5,1)`; pressing `up` from there enters map `0x33`.
- New interpretation: that top exit is the correct Viridian Forest progression.

## Viridian Forest / map 0x33
- Treat `map 0x33` as the actual Viridian Forest area entered from the south gatehouse.
- Entry from south gatehouse top door settles near `(17,47)`.
- Known reproducible early route from entrance area: north to `(17,43)`, right to `(20,43)`, up to `(20,41)`, then various lanes branch through the forest maze.
- Forest evidence already seen: Bug Catcher trainers, wild Caterpie/Weedle/Kakuna/Pikachu, and the antidote/forest-leaving signs.
- West clearing around `(2,30..31)` is a dead local branch, not the exit.
- Sign-row / antidote area around `(14..18,32)` is a local choke; north is blocked there.
- Trainer at about `(26,33)` is a Bug Catcher who challenges immediately.
- Latest correction: stop trying to “find the entrance”; focus on finding the **north exit** through the maze.

## Battle heuristics
- Early wild fights can sometimes be handled with repeated `A`, but blind mash is unsafe once move choice matters.
- Squirtle knows **Tackle**, **Tail Whip**, **Bubble**. Visible order: **TACKLE / TAIL WHIP / BUBBLE**.
- If the move cursor is on **TAIL WHIP**, then `down + A` selects **BUBBLE**; plain `A` wastes a turn.
- With low HP, avoid unnecessary battles; fleeing is preferred.
- If stuck in move or Pokémon submenu, `B` backs out cleanly to the main battle menu.
- Reliable flee recovery from messy substates: use `B` until at the 4-option menu, then try `down, A` or `right, A`; if needed, `down, right, A` from FIGHT also works.
- After `Got away safely!`, one more `A` is usually needed, then a short `wait` for the white transition.
- After `used TACKLE!` or Bubble text, a short `wait` often reveals the true next state more cleanly than extra inputs.
- Forest trainer fights: Bubble animation/text can lag a long time; prefer long waits (~60-120 frames) plus single `A` presses instead of mashing.
- Kakuna often uses **Harden**; the main risk is wasting turns or mashing through lag, not sudden KO.
- For opening wild battles in this area, the stable pattern is: one or two `A` presses with waits to finish the intro/send-out sequence, confirm the 4-option menu is really up, then select RUN explicitly.
- If the battle screen still says `Go! A!`, do **not** send menu inputs yet; advance intro text first, then wait for the command menu before choosing RUN.

## Tooling / harness notes
- `probe_path` is excellent for discovering stepwise movement effects.
- `walk_path_until_event` is better than raw batched movement when following known routes through grass or near warps because it stops on battle/map change.
- Old `clear_battle_text` is broken (`ctx.frame is not a function`); use the safer state-based replacement instead.
- The first `route2_to_forest_approach` macro was buggy because it over-batched movement; prefer short stepwise movement macros that stop on battle/map change.
- Useful checkpoints: `turn201_gate_north_start`, `turn212_kakuna_escaped_28_40`, `turn213_npc_cleared_28_40`, `turn216_start_18_35`, `turn217_post_caterpie_12_32`, `turn221_west_clear_2_31`, `turn228_gatehouse_interior_5_3`, `turn228_north_pocket_start_17_47`, and `turn235_route2_8_57_start`.

Keep a fresh checkpoint before deeper forest probing or trainer-heavy branches
Use stable wild-battle escape cadence: clear intro with A+waits, then RUN explicitly
If forest progress stalls, systematically map branches from the south entrance instead of relabeling the area incorrectly

- From `(26,36)`, continued probing north:

data.name: update_playbook

arguments.content:
# Strategy playbook

## Early boot / controls
- If boot is blank white, `wait` a few seconds.
- At title/menu, `start` or `a` proceeds.
- On naming screens, `start` accepts defaults.
- A advances dialog and confirms; B cancels; START opens menu.
- Default d-pad press moves about one tile.
- If a textbox is open, movement does nothing; clear it first.
- Trust RAM for coords/state and screenshot for UI/world geometry.
- `B` often closes overworld textboxes more cleanly than `A`.
- Blank white/black screens during transitions often just need `wait`.

## General exploration
- Probe systematically with RAM coords instead of retrying the same blocked tile.
- Adjacent sprites may be NPCs, not walls; try `A` before assuming collision.
- Long-hold d-pad presses can reveal more walkable area than short probes suggest.
- Dialog boxes block movement until dismissed with A.
- On signs/NPCs, clear lingering text before resuming movement.
- After trainer battles, overworld post-battle text can still be open even when `battle: none`; clear it before moving.
- If a map search stalls for many turns, consider whether the entire premise is wrong (wrong side of a gate, wrong building exit, optional side pocket).
- Use `probe_path` or `walk_path_until_event` to log exact coords through suspect doors/warps; this is better than inferring from screenshots.
- When one branch is repeatedly proving to be a dead end, prefer rewinding to a clean checkpoint near the branch point rather than continuing from a deep exploratory leaf.
- Big meta-lesson from Route 2: if a map contains the right ecology/trainers/signs for the target area, stop insisting it must be somewhere else. Re-evaluate the label, not just the path.

## Current progress
- Starter: Squirtle, now Lv12.
- Oak's Parcel delivery and Pokédex progression are complete.
- Real progression is north from Viridian onto Route 2 and then through Viridian Forest.
- **Major correction (turn 235): map `0x33` is the actual Viridian Forest interior/south approach, not an optional north-side Route 2 pocket.**
- Current task: continue traversing `0x33` northward toward Pewter / the forest's north exit.
- Current live state from recent probing reached the forest's upper-east side around `(32,11)` with full HP.

## Pallet / Lab quick notes
- Player's House 1F exit: around `(2,7)` on map `0x25`, press `down`.
- Pallet north exit trigger after game start is on the east side of the north path around `x=10,y=1`.
- Oak's Lab is the southwest/left large building in Pallet.
- Right-side Pallet house can heal Squirtle.
- Verified starter: Squirtle.
- On nickname screen, `START` declines naming.
- Oak parcel return: talk to Oak around `(4,2)` facing right.

## Route 1
- Verified northbound route: from about `(5,18)`, go north to `y=14`, east along the rock barrier to about `x=14`, north through the opening, continue north, then loop west near the top fence and go north at about `(11,0)` to warp into Viridian.
- Viridian south return uses the fence opening around `x≈20,y≈30`.
- Upper grass pocket around `(10..13,6..8)` is dangerous and easy to overprobe.

## Viridian City / Pokecenter
- City sign near `(16,17)`: `VIRIDIAN CITY - The Eternally Green Paradise`.
- East-side building labeled `MART` is the Poké Mart.
- There is a fence/rock barrier around `y=28`; many central/east `up` attempts from below are blocked.
- Usable crossing: from around `(22,28)`, move left to about `x=19`, then north to about `(19,25)`, then right.
- Pokecenter frontage: from outside near `(24,26)`, use `left, up` to enter.
- Nurse Joy: stand at `(3,3)` facing up and press `A`; choose `HEAL`.
- After healing, `B` plus `down` closes the final text and steps away.
- Upper city: broad north fence line near `y≈14` is blocked; to reach upper city from the east half, work left to around `(20,16)` and then up.
- Small house/school entrance is around `(21,15)`/`(21,16)`; non-progressing.
- West exit to Route 22 is far left; from about `(0,14)`, pressing `left` warps out.
- Upper-left `TRAINER TIPS` sign around `(19,2)` is just a sign, not progression.
- Exact north exit to Route 2: from the upper-left corridor, go to about `(17,0)` and press `up` to warp to Route 2 at about `(7,71)`.

## Route 2 south section / gatehouse
- From Viridian landing `(7,71)`, the important lower-left breach is real.
- Lower-left sign area: sign around `(5,66)` says `ROUTE 2 / VIRIDIAN CITY - PEWTER CITY`.
- Key breakthrough: from sign area `(5,64)`, go left to about `(2,64)`, then work north/right to expose the real path upward; this leads back to the upper Route 2 area and then the gatehouse frontage.
- Verified climb from the breach can reach upper area around `(3,48)`, then east to `(8,48)`, north to `(8,46)`, left along the building frontage, then up to the south gatehouse entrance at about `(3,44)`.
- From gatehouse interior `MAP_0x32`, the upper-left aisle leads to `(5,1)`; pressing `up` from there enters map `0x33`.

## Viridian Forest / map 0x33
- Treat `map 0x33` as the actual Viridian Forest area entered from the south gatehouse.
- Entry from south gatehouse top door settles near `(17,47)`.
- Known reproducible early route from entrance area: north to `(17,43)`, right to `(20,43)`, up to `(20,41)`, then various lanes branch through the forest maze.
- Forest evidence already seen: Bug Catcher trainers, wild Caterpie/Weedle/Kakuna/Metapod/Pikachu, and the antidote/forest-leaving signs.
- West clearing around `(2,30..31)` is a dead local branch, not the exit.
- Sign-row / antidote area around `(14..18,32)` is a local choke; north is blocked there.
- Trainer at about `(26,33)` is a Bug Catcher who challenges immediately.
- Strong recent progress came from the east side instead of the overprobed west/sign area.
- Verified east-side climb so far:
  - from roughly `(22,41)`, go right to `(26,41)`
  - then north to `(26,36)` and continue up to about `(26,32)` / `(25,30)`
  - from `(25,30)`, a long straight north corridor reaches `(25,22)` and then `(25,10)`
  - near the top, east is mostly blocked except one step to `(26,10)` and north there only reaches `(26,8)`
  - shifting west from the upper corridor allowed progress to `(18,7)`
  - latest known live state is farther east again near `(32,11)`, suggesting a significant upper-east branch remains to explore
- Practical next-step heuristic: from high/east forest coordinates, probe for north/west openings rather than dropping all the way back to the old central sign row.

## Battle heuristics
- Early wild fights can sometimes be handled with repeated `A`, but blind mash is unsafe once move choice matters.
- Squirtle knows **Tackle**, **Tail Whip**, **Bubble**. Visible order: **TACKLE / TAIL WHIP / BUBBLE**.
- If the move cursor is on **TAIL WHIP**, then `down + A` selects **BUBBLE**; plain `A` wastes a turn.
- With low HP, avoid unnecessary battles; fleeing is preferred.
- If stuck in move or Pokémon submenu, `B` backs out cleanly to the main battle menu.
- Reliable flee recovery from messy substates: use `B` until at the 4-option menu, then try `down, A` or `right, A`; if needed, `down, right, A` from FIGHT also works.
- After `Got away safely!`, one more `A` is usually needed, then a short `wait` for the white transition.
- After `used TACKLE!` or Bubble text, a short `wait` often reveals the true next state more cleanly than extra inputs.
- Forest trainer fights: Bubble animation/text can lag a long time; prefer long waits (~60-120 frames) plus single `A` presses instead of mashing.
- Kakuna often uses **Harden**; the main risk is wasting turns or mashing through lag, not sudden KO.
- For opening wild battles in this area, the stable pattern is: one or two `A` presses with waits to finish the intro/send-out sequence, confirm the 4-option menu is really up, then select RUN explicitly.
- If the battle screen still says `Go! A!`, do **not** send menu inputs yet; advance intro text first, then wait for the command menu before choosing RUN.
- Full HP is valuable in forest exploration because trainer branches may still be ahead; when healthy, prioritize mapping over grinding.

## Tooling / harness notes
- `probe_path` is excellent for discovering stepwise movement effects.
- `walk_path_until_event` is better than raw batched movement when following known routes through grass or near warps because it stops on battle/map change.
- Old `clear_battle_text` is broken (`ctx.frame is not a function`); use the safer state-based replacement instead.
- The first `route2_to_forest_approach` macro was buggy because it over-batched movement; prefer short stepwise movement macros that stop on battle/map change.
- Useful checkpoints: `turn201_gate_north_start`, `turn212_kakuna_escaped_28_40`, `turn213_npc_cleared_28_40`, `turn216_start_18_35`, `turn217_post_caterpie_12_32`, `turn221_west_clear_2_31`, `turn228_gatehouse_interior_5_3`, `turn228_north_pocket_start_17_47`, `turn235_route2_8_57_start`, `turn236_gatehouse_4_7`, `turn237_post_metapod_22_41`, `turn238_post_weedle_25_30`, `turn239_forest_25_22`.

arguments.content:
Viridian Forest upper-east progress through turn 239: after the major correction that map `0x33` is the real forest, the best northward progress came from the east side rather than the overprobed west/sign-row area.

Verified route pieces:
- From around `(22,41)`, moving `right x4` reached `(26,41)`.
- From there, moving `up x5` reached `(26,36)`.
- Continuing north from that branch was open up to about `(26,32)` and then `(25,30)`.
- From `(25,30)`, a long straight north corridor was verified all the way to `(25,22)`.
- From `(25,22)`, straight `up x12` reached `(25,10)`.
- Near the top, `right` was mostly blocked except for one step to `(26,10)`; from `(26,10)`, `up` only worked to `(26,8)` before blocking.
- Shifting west across the upper lane allowed progress to about `(20,8)`, then routing left/up reached `(18,7)`.
- Current live state reported at turn 240 is even farther east at `(32,11)` facing down, confirming there is a substantial upper-east branch still unexplored.

Interpretation:
- The east-side vertical corridor is the strongest known progress line toward the forest's north half.
- The old west clearing `(2,30..31)` and antidote/sign-row region `(14..18,32)` are local branches/chokes, not the main forward route.
- Next useful probe should continue from the upper-east state around `(32,11)`, testing north/west openings before retreating south.

Verified route pieces: `(32,11) -> up x2 -> (32,9) -> left x4 -> (28,9) -> up -> (28,8)` with further `up` blocked there; continuing left/down found an upper lane through `(25,9) -> (25,8) -> (24,8)`, but north was blocked at both `(25,8)` and `(24,8)`. Repositioning right/up then succeeded through `(31,7) -> (31,5) -> (32,5) -> (32,4) -> (31,4) -> (31,3) -> (32,3) -> (32,2) -> (31,2)`. From there, going left to `(27,2)` and up reached `(27,1)`, and the full top row is walkable eastward at least to `(31,1)`, but `up` is blocked at both `(27,1)` and `(31,1)`. Conclusion: this upper-east branch opens into a high rectangular clearing with a walkable top row, but no north exit on the tested right/top edge; future progress from this branch should probe west/south connectors from the upper lane rather than keep pressing into the top boundary.
